Thank you for the magic words Vin :D

ok guys it is just that they have added the text "weather station" and are using the code name for the station rather than using the name at the top of the page - which is more logical and makes sense. It falls bang on the data set for the day forecasts. Just a line to strip it out :D

The actual data is just fine.

make a small 1 line change in ..../conkywx/lib/**parsewxdata.pl**

in this file there is a function **currdatahtml** look for this code towards the bottom of the function.

```

        if (/start_daily_fct/../end_daily_fct/) {
            if ( $_ =~ /\|/ ) {
                $_ = (convert_fc_units( $_ ));
            }
            if ( $_ =~ /day/ ) {
                $_ = (get_short_day( $_ ));
            }
            $_ =~ s/of a //;
            $_ =~ s/of //;
            push(@temp_out_day, "$_");
        }

```

Add this line in bold blue.

```

        if (/start_daily_fct/../end_daily_fct/) {
            [COLOR=#0000ff]**next if (/weather station/);**[/COLOR]
            if ( $_ =~ /\|/ ) {
                $_ = (convert_fc_units( $_ ));
            }
            if ( $_ =~ /day/ ) {
                $_ = (get_short_day( $_ ));
            }
            $_ =~ s/of a //;
            $_ =~ s/of //;
            push(@temp_out_day, "$_");
        }

```

Let me know how it goes.

---

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-05-30
> **paramvir said:**
> Let me know how it goes.
Perfection!  :)

I'm still getting "A.A.H." for the Moonrise output, but that may simply be an anomaly.

One thing I might mention...

IMO, one of the Golden Rules of writing .pl files is... don't mix <spaces> and <tabs>.  Use one or the other to indent code.

So, when I edited the '/usr/share/conkywx/lib/parsewxdata.pl' file, I used the <tab> key to indent the added line.

Also, when I was done editing/saving the .pl file, I:
[LIST=1]
[*]Did a 'killall .conkyrc' in CLI
[*]Ran 'conkywx' and selected Option 3 (FORCE update conkywx data)
[*]Restarted .conkyrc
[/LIST]


[INDENT][[IMG]http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-30-may-2013-1(650x520).png[/IMG]]("http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-30-may-2013-1.png")

*Experimental "Light Dubstep" Station (Icecast Radio)  Mock Graphic [/INDENT]


Excellent job, Paramvir, as usual!

One thing I might mention...

IMO, one of the Golden Rules of writing .pl files is... don't mix <spaces> and <tabs>.  Use one or the other to indent code.

So, when I edited the '/usr/share/conkywx/lib/parsewxdata.pl' file, I used the <tab> key to indent the added line.

Also, when I was done editing/saving the .pl file, I:

Excellent job, Paramvir, as usual!

And, thanks, for the quick response...  ;)

Thank You and Welcome :D

Yes I use tabs as well - well I guess we know we think a lot alike and mostly same time too he he :P

Yes force update is the one to use else you will need to wait for the next cycle of update.

A.A.H. is Always Above Horizon - the Moon actually rose last night - so no Moon rise time for today and will set today for your location. You may also see it during the Moon set time if you have no Moon set then = AAH with respect to the 24 hours day. In the polar regions it can last for days and then there is also A.B.H - always below horizon - same idea. Same goes for the Sun - but in the higher latitudes.

cheers

### Post by Cvetan on 2013-06-01

### Post by paramvir on 2013-06-01
> **Cvetan said:**
> When i first wrote the post it was about 23:00 on the friday, and it showd the same. :D

Can you tell me how to get icons? I would like to change this font characters to those icon sets.

Yes that change to the next day happens well before midnight - this is a wunderground thing.

for your images here is some data from the images template - you will need to change the values for -p option.

```

${image [D2I] -p 22,635 -s 34x34}${image [D3I] -p 82,635 -s 34x34}${image [D4I] -p 141,635 -s 34x34}${image [D5I] -p 198,635 -s 34x34}

```

### Post by paramvir on 2013-06-01
> **Cvetan said:**
> This -p option is positionof the images in pixels right? And where from it starts counting from the previous section of conky or from where? And the -s is size in pixels right?

Yes the -s option is pixels

### Post by paramvir on 2013-06-01
> **dtokarz said:**
> Here ya go!  :)

Thanks for the file... :)

OK - I had actually made a message to you asking you to wait for the next update - I knew it was the url which had 33606 id number - just before I posted your message had a look at the url in your log file HAAAAAA - got the pesky little url... can't get away from me lol :D

your url
```

[SIZE=3]Location="http://www.wunderground.com/cgi-bin/findweather/getForecast?query=33606&sp=KFLTAMPA46"[/SIZE]

```

url you should use
```

[SIZE=3]Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:33606.1.99999"[/SIZE]

```

You get this url from the history page of KFLTAMPA46 - this also works
```

[SIZE=3]http://www.wunderground.com/cgi-bin/findweather/getForecast?query=27.946%2C-82.464&sp=KFLTAMPA46[/SIZE]

```
It is not something you as a user will realise - so not an error - this is an auto generated url - but it also adds the station ID.

You get the same station KTPF - this will change perhaps as wund rotates stations.

let me know if this fixes the issue ;)

cheers

---

> Thanks for the file... :)

your url
```

[SIZE=3]Location="http://www.wunderground.com/cgi-bin/findweather/getForecast?query=33606&sp=KFLTAMPA46"[/SIZE]

```

url you should use

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-06-01
> **paramvir said:**
> HAAAAAA - got the pesky little url... can't get away from me lol :D
Good job, detective (on all the fixes)!  :D

Allow me to interject something off-topic here...

I noticed that somebody (above) is still using version 8 of my calendar code.

The cal code is currently sitting at version 9 (yes, it took me 9 tries to get it right).  ;)

I've been running the "experimental" version 9 cal code for many months, but I didn't realize (until I just looked) that it required some maintenance, updating, commenting, and so forth, and so on.  It was due to "expire" (go stale) at the end of June 2013.

Here is the latest update:

```
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color3}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -80}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color7}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jun.2013 - Dec.2014 (use mono fonts only)
## Manually tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jun 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color3}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jun.2013 - Dec.2014 (use mono fonts only)
## Manually tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
# ${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Sep 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Dec 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color3}/'}
```

Hopefully, the "commenting" explains it all...

That said, it never occurred to me, until today, that the "pesky" months with six calendar weeks are different for weeks starting on Sunday, and weeks starting on Monday.  Nobody complained, and I never noticed it. Sorry!

Anyway, I think I pegged it, for the next year_and_a_half (2013/2014).

If there are any problem(s), let me know.


**June 2013 / First Day of Week, Sunday / Six Calendar Weeks**

[INDENT][[IMG]http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-cal9-1st-day-sunday.png[/IMG]]("http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-cal9-1st-day-sunday.png")[/INDENT]


**June 2013 / First Day of Week, Monday / Five Calendar Weeks**

[INDENT][[IMG]http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-cal9-1st-day-monday.png[/IMG]]("http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-cal9-1st-day-monday.png")[/INDENT]

Okay, back on-topic...  ;)

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-06-01
> **Cvetan said:**
> @vindsl

I see you also use weather with icons. Can you shown me how to set that part? 
I can't figure out that part by any way. :(
The icon sets are in the default folder(s) location: /usr/share/conkywx/images

And, I select the icon set in:  /home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf

```
<SNIP>

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Choose weather icon set
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [1] Enable icon set: org 12 by bharathp666 (Paramvir Likhari remix)
# [2] Enable icon set: origin unknown
# [3] Enable icon set: tick weather icons by xiao4
# [4] Enable icon set: Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
wx_iconset=2

<SNIP>
```

That's about it.

Not quite sure why yours aren't working... :(

**EDIT**

Just thinking...

If you're displaying metric stats, you would set the icons in:  /home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/vindsl-metric-conkywx.conf

* I suppose it goes without saying but, the path should include your username, not mine.  :D

Example:  /home/<USERNAME>/.config/conkywx/

### Post by aquarius18 on 2013-06-03
Where are my arrows?? :(

Just installed a copy of Mint 15 on one of my drives. All went well with conky and conkywx - BUT for the trend arrows! They just won't show up at all......

Got this running on a 12.04, a 13.04 and a Mint 14 install - no probs whatsoever.

The problem child is running the latest conkywx (0.9.3) and conky 1.9.x (definitely the latest)

Checked

- syntax of my templates --> ok
- installation of Arrows.otf --> ok
- location of Arrows.otf font --> ok
- paths to conkywx.conf and template files --> ok
- .conkyrc file for any syntax errors --> ok

Ran conkywx in terminal, selection 4 and entered path to conkywx.conf file and I am getting this:

>     Enter your config file path/name.
    Followed by [ENTER]:/home/frank/.conky/weather/conkywx.conf
/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh: line 627: /home/frank/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal/trends/forecast_trends-conkywx.conf-frank: Permission denied
/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh: line 631: /home/frank/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal/trends/forecast_trends-conkywx.conf-frank: No such file or directory
/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh: line 686: nowtime1 -  : syntax error: operand expected (error token is "-  ")

**************************************************************
  DEBUG Information
**************************************************************
  DEBUG info for config file:
  /home/frank/.conky/weather/conkywx.conf
  
  Please check the log file 
  /tmp/wx-frank-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wund-debug.log
  and attach the file with your message if required.
 
**************************************************************


If running the same command as sudo, then there is no error msg :idea:

Soooo ... checked permissions of /home/frank/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal/trends/forecast_trends-conkywx.conf-frank and the lot is set to root!!! (wasn't me...)

Checking if the file /usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh exists: yes, gives me a positive result - it does exist!

So ..... I changed the permissions of the /home/frank/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal/trends directory to 755 and the files within to 644 and .... YOU GUESSED IT: my arrows are back!! :guitar:

Now, considering that this directory is created upon installation without the user's intervention, me thinks that there may be a bug in the install files when invoked during setup???? :-k

What do you think guys?

Cheers / Frank

(I am having another one of foodoo's Biers now!)

---

Soooo ... checked permissions of /home/frank/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal/trends/forecast_trends-conkywx.conf-frank and the lot is set to root!!! (wasn't me...)

Checking if the file /usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh exists: yes, gives me a positive result - it does exist!

So ..... I changed the permissions of the /home/frank/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal/trends directory to 755 and the files within to 644 and .... YOU GUESSED IT: my arrows are back!! :guitar:

Now, considering that this directory is created upon installation without the user's intervention, me thinks that there may be a bug in the install files when invoked during setup???? :-k

What do you think guys?

Cheers / Frank

(I am having another one of foodoo's Biers now!)

### Post by paramvir on 2013-06-04
> **AK44 said:**
> @paramvir - sorry to post here but couldn't attach pics on the blog - here's a screenshot of 'top' showing conkywx.sh holding a huge process for a long time. This is causing my CPU to run hot as well. Thoughts?



Interesting :) - Kill conkywx.sh and restart your conky script in a terminal to see what messages you get. Is this happening when ever you run conkywx or one off - other issues may be involved. 

I do not see wget, perl or magellan processes running and the rest conkywx.sh is the only thing.

Do this only if you have conkywx still taking up the cpu - open conkywx.sh and uncomment "set -x" near the top of the file and at the bottom before exit add "set +x" and then run your conky script in the terminal -  This will give a full raw dump of the execution process like a manga mega debug dump - if it is hanging somewhere you will see it and you will just need to copy that text off the terminal and plonk it onto the reply to this message ;)

let me know what you find :)

### Post by AK44 on 2013-06-08
Just switched to KDE (Cinnamon was getting on my nerves) and everything works fine with one annoying exception. When conky starts up the conky window is moved down from the top of the screen. If I simply open .conkyrc and save it (no changes) it bumps it back up to where I set it. I'm guessing I'm missing something easy but what?

---

### Post by paramvir on 2013-06-08
> **AK44 said:**
> Just switched to KDE (Cinnamon was getting on my nerves) and everything works fine with one annoying exception. When conky starts up the conky window is moved down from the top of the screen. If I simply open .conkyrc and save it (no changes) it bumps it back up to where I set it. I'm guessing I'm missing something easy but what?

You will need to change the own_window_type - try override or one of the other options - a good resource for all options is the conky docs at [http://conky.sourceforge.net/docs.html](http://conky.sourceforge.net/docs.html)

             **                 own_window_type             **         
if own_window is yes, you may specify type         normal, desktop, dock, panel or override (default: normal).         Desktop windows are special windows that have no window         decorations; are always visible on your desktop; do not         appear in your pager or taskbar; and are sticky across all         workspaces. Panel windows reserve space along a desktop         edge, just like panels and taskbars, preventing maximized         windows from overlapping them. The edge is chosen based on         the alignment option. Override windows are not under the         control of the window manager. Hints are ignored. This type         of window can be useful for certain situations.

Type conkywx and select the DEBUG option and check that log file.

Desktop windows are special windows that have no window         decorations; are always visible on your desktop; do not         appear in your pager or taskbar; and are sticky across all         workspaces. ### Post by paramvir on 2013-06-17
> **LamboDroid said:**
> Sure, here it is. Thanks for the help. Your a genius :P

here is the problem ;)

```

${image /tmp/wx-l${offset -2}AMbodroid-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/nowCond.png -p 15,507 -s 46x46}${image /tmp/wx-l${offset -2}AMbodroid-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/daily-2.png -p 22,713 -s 34x34}${image /tmp/wx-l${offset -2}AMbodroid-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/daily-3.png -p 82,713 -s 34x34}${image /tmp/wx-l${offset -2}AMbodroid-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/daily-4.png -p 141,713 -s 34x34}${image /tmp/wx-l${offset -2}AMbodroid-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/daily-5.png -p 198,713 -s 34x34}${image /tmp/wx-l${offset -2}AMbodroid-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/nowmoon.png -p 190,620 -s 45x45}

```

Solution:
in your conkywx config file you have 
[code]
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process these final template changes before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the changes in single quotes as shown
template_cleanup=( 
[COLOR=#0000ff][B]    'AM:${offset -2}AM'
    'PM:${offset -2}PM'
[/B][/COLOR]    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waning Gibbous:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
  )
[\code]


[code]
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process these final template changes before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the changes in single quotes as shown
template_cleanup=( 
[COLOR=#0000ff][B]    ' AM:${offset -2}AM'
    ' PM:${offset -2}PM'
[/B][/COLOR]    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waning Gibbous:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
  )
[\code]

The program is matching the **am** in your id and making the change .

**option 1:** Those 2 lines in bold blue. First add a space between **'AM:**... so it becomes **' AM**:... do it for both the lines AM and PM.
Now try and see if the images are back - if fine then go with it. 

**option2:** Delete the 2 lines and see how it looks - if fine then go with it.

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-07-03
> **davesbrain said:**
> Strange calendar alignment.  Today is Wed July 3rd.   How can I get all the columns to line up properly?
Don't know what's going on there, but you might want to try this...

```
${voffset 30}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color5}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color5}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -80}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color8}${alignc 99}2${font}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jun 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color3}/'}

```

Works on this machine.  ;)

---

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-07-03
BTW, I tweaked the Time/Calendar sections a while back.

Here's what I'm currently using...

```
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset -9}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -5}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color3}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color3}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -80}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color7}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jun.2013 - Dec.2014 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jun 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color3}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jun.2013 - Dec.2014 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
# ${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Sep 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Dec 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color3}/'}

```

Fantastic ;)

Regarding the moon and the sun - they do not rise and set in the same way.

Just a small explanation :D

Moon has the same side always facing the Earth - this is a habit of most satellite moons - or so I have understood. Our Sun spins at a certain rate which gets adjusted every leap year. Oh and there is nothing as Dark side of the Moon - that is just Pink Floyd lol :D - That side is just the side we cannot see from Earth!!!

Our moon has 1 lunar month of 29d 12h 44m 03s [2.86s actually] - this is solar time - so in solar 24 hours - that is the actual time for a lunar month.

Which means that there will be times when within your 24 solar hours the moon may not set or rise.

Soooo - it may rise today and set tomorrow - you get A.A.H ~ always above horizon - no setting time for the moon today!

Or you may have moon set today and rise another day but not the day you are looking at - A.B.H. ~ always below horizon.

Our bellowed Sun also has these issues - but in the polar latitudes. ;)

 That is why you do not have that Moonset thing - that happened that day - now both rise and set times fall within your solar 24 hour day.

cheers :D

---

### Post by paramvir on 2013-07-18
I have just scratched a script for getting music information - you already have this in the VinDSL template - but I wanted a single line - no matter what player I am using - I use smplayer and vlc - or both at the same time ;)

There are 2 ways you can use it
[LIST=1]
[*]template1 path-to-file/music_sorter.sh 
[*]template1 path-to-file/music_sorter.sh "SMPlayer" 
[/LIST]

In the 1st case - if you have configured the 2 player in the script they get activated and you get Alerts kind of display but with your players marked with :: as separator on the same single line.

In the second case you define the player and it will work like you have the long script at present.


music_sorter.sh
[PHP]
#!/bin/bash

UserChoice="$1"

if  [[ ! $UserChoice ]]; then
    ## Single scroller for multiple players
    # add the name of the player as seen in the xlsclients output
    players=( "SMPlayer" "VLC media player" )
    # add name here as you would like to see in the scrolling text
    short_player_name=( "SMPlayer" "VLC" )
    
    for c in ${!players[@]}
    do
        get_track=$(/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "${players[$c]}" )
        if [[ $get_track ]]; then
            get_track="::${short_player_name[$c]}:: ${get_track/Name: /}"
            g_track+=( "${get_track/- "${players[$c]}"/ }" )
        fi
    done
    g_track=${g_track[@]/#/ | }
    g_track=${g_track/|/}
else
    ## Scroller for each player as defined
    get_track=$(/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "$UserChoice" )
    if [[ $get_track ]]; then
        get_track="${get_track/Name: /}"
        g_track=( "${get_track/- "$UserChoice"/ }" )
    fi
    g_track="${g_track
[*]}"
fi

echo "${g_track}"

exit 0

[/PHP]


my line for the single scroller
```

${if_empty ${exec ${template1}}}${voffset 2}${color4}${hr 2}${else}\
${voffset 2}${font Dejavu Sans:bold:size=10}${color1}Media Playing ${color4}${voffset -2}${hr 1}
${voffset 4}${color3}${if_match ${execi 10 expr length "$(${template1})" }>=34}${color3}${scroll 34 10 ${execi 10 ${template1}}}
${color4}${hr 2}${color3}${else}${alignc}${execi 10 ${template1}}
${color4}${hr 2}${font}${endif}${endif}

```

NJoy :D

for l in ${!nowdata[@]}

                        trend1="Ealier"

                elif [[ ${nowdata[$l]} -gt ${nowstored[$l]} ]]; then

                        trend1="Later"

                    fi
                    trend_arrow1=$( echo -e $risingtrend )
                else
                    trend1="-"
                    trend_arrow1="-"
                fi
            fi

            trend+=( $trend1 )
            trend_arrow+=( $trend_arrow1 )
        done
    local time_diff2=$(( nowtime1 - ${trends_store[0]} ))
    local time_diff_time=$(( nowtime1 - ${trends_time_store[0]} ))

### Post by paramvir on 2013-07-20
> **Cvetan said:**
> Here is the debug file. Had to change extension since forum didn't allow with .log to be uploaded.

Many thanks for that file. I had to finally use manual data to re-create that error - and found that perhaps we had a 2 decimal value where normally we expect 1 decimal value. So a value like 0.08 came in which got cleaned to 08.

That really helped me for the next version which is mostly in perl - where a leading 0 gets taken as an octal value lol - you have to watch that leading zero ;)

```

    ft_data=( "$Pressure" "$Temp_now" "$wind_speed_trend" "$Humidity" "$uv2" "$Dew_Point" "$Visibility" )
    # ft_data=( "1020 hpa" "16°C" "0.08 km/h" "70" "0.7 : low" "0°C" "0.7 km" )
    shopt -s extglob
    for l2 in ${!ft_data[@]}
    do
        ft_data[$l2]=${ft_data[$l2]/°*}        # clean deg symbol
        ft_data[$l2]=${ft_data[$l2]/ *}        # clean spaces
[COLOR=#0000ff]**        ft_data[$l2]=${ft_data[$l2]//[.]/}     # clean the rest**[/COLOR]
[COLOR=#ff8c00]**        ft_data[$l2]=${ft_data[$l2]##+(0)}     # clean leading zero**[/COLOR]
        ft_data[$l2]=${ft_data[$l2]:-0}        # 0 if value is 0
    done
    # msgout "${ft_data
[*]}"

    # changes 24 hours
    ft2_time=( $sun_rise $sun_set $moon_rise $moon_set "$length_visible_light" "$length_day" $percentilluminated )
    # ft2_time=( 0656 1813 0 0059 1204 1116 00 )
    for l1 in ${!ft2_time[@]}
    do
        ft2_time[$l1]=${ft2_time[$l1]/always*/NA}     # clean the rest
[B]        [COLOR=#0000ff]ft2_time[$l1]=${ft2_time[$l1]//[.:h m]/}     # clean the rest[/COLOR]
        [COLOR=#ff8c00]ft2_time[$l1]=${ft2_time[$l1]##+(0)}         # clean leading zero[/COLOR]
[/B]        ft2_time[$l1]=${ft2_time[$l1]:-0}             # 0 if value is 0
    done
    shopt -u extglob



```

At present you have the blue line some where below the orange line - just move it like you see above and you will not get that error again.

While you will not get an error - I know the result will not give you correct trend when the value changes - 0.08 is less than 1 if that is the next value but it will show reverse trend as 8 is greater than 1. This has been an issue and is fixed in the next version. Weekends I get more time to look into all this sooooo :D

cheers

    Conkywx Weather Program Assistant
    ---------------------------------
    Current version of conkywx on your system is: 0.9.3
    Enter your option number: 
      1 Quick HELP with conkywx weather program
      2 Refer to offline conkywx WIKI Manual in your browser
      3 FORCE update conkywx data
      4 Get detailed DEBUG information for conkywx
      5 Reset Forecast trend information
      6 View Trend data table
      
      q to quit
      followed by [ENTER]:

```

### Post by paramvir on 2013-08-16
> **lordsyrinex said:**
> Hello all!  Read over a good chunk of the 108 pages (someone should update page 1 with new links) :) and have gotten this thing running about where I want it minus a few text tweaks here and there.


Greetings my Lord ;)

Yes and I am considering including this fabulous template as one of the templates in conkywx - but we all need to find that elusive time factor sorted out and of course the full inbox Vin has...

Assuming your weather section in the conkyrc file looks something like below - you need to focus on editing the config file and template file specified in red.

First sort out the Location - do not include the xml_ID - leave it commented out.

Consider this - you say that the locations you had originally before you put your Location - it worked fine??? Go back to that state and then rework your changes.

What version of conkywx are you running. 

The first page cannot be changed due to the forum rules in place :)

Type conkywx at the terminal you will get a conkywx assistant menu - use option 2 to view the help wiki in your browser. The live version is being updated all the time and may not apply to your version.

If all else fails - send me the debug information and the config and template files

```

##################################
##    CONKYWX WEATHER SCRIPT    ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DejaVu Sans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
[SIZE=3]${execpi 900 /usr/bin/conkywx **[COLOR=#0000cd]-c[/COLOR][COLOR=#ff0000] path/config file [/COLOR][COLOR=#0000cd]-t[/COLOR][COLOR=#ff0000] path/template file[/COLOR]** |[/SIZE]
####
## Time (convert AM/PM -> am/pm)
sed -e 's/AM'/'\$\{offset 1\}am/g' -e 's/PM'/'\$\{offset 1\}pm/g'|
####
## Windspeed [WS]
sed -e 's/Calm mph'/'\$\{offset 8\}Calm/g'|
####
## Moon Phases [MP]
sed -e 's/New Moon'/'\$\{offset -13\}New Moon/g'|
sed -e 's/Waxing Crescent'/'\$\{offset -3\}Waxing/g'|
sed -e 's/First Quarter'/'\$\{offset -3\}First Qtr/g'|
sed -e 's/Waxing Gibbous'/'\$\{offset -3\}Waxing/g'|
sed -e 's/Full Moon'/'\$\{offset -8\}Full Moon/g'|
sed -e 's/Waning Gibbous'/'\$\{offset -3\}Waning/g'|
sed -e 's/Last Quarter'/'\$\{offset -3\}Last Qtr/g'|
sed -e 's/Waning Crescent'/'\$\{offset -3\}Waning/g'
} ${font}

```

cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2013-08-17
> **lordsyrinex said:**
> It appears I have spoken too soon.  While I was able to get it to work once it appears that once it had refreshed it went back to the N/A and CoR % displays that were showing up before.

No worries ;)

the config file you have is for an earlier version of conkywx - things change :D

have attached the version for 0.9.3 conkywx - a copy of this file is in /usr/share/conkywx/examples

It is updated to your Location and other stuff - just check if things look wonky ;)

You should not use any xml_ID station since your location is being updated with the NWS data. The local stations are not producing data all the time. The next version of conkywx checks this and informs the user and also uses the main data only if such a condition exists.

Even though the stations nearby are geographically not that far - surrounding islands change the winds considerably - rest of the data will be more or less the same.

Let me know if this sorts the issue.

cheers

> **PJs Ronin said:**

1.   Empty ya Mailbox damnit!

Normal service will now resume. ty[/FONT][/COLOR][/SIZE]
Hi PJs Ronin,

Since you brought it up, I suppose I should offer a word of explanation.  I'm an enigma to a lot of ppl.  ;)

**Bullet #1**: My Mailbox has constantly been full, ever since someone talked me into posting my Conky script in "The Big Thread", so called.  It doesn't matter if I empty my inbox or not.  A few days later, it's full again, and ppl expect a reply.  Unfortunately, I don't have time to reply to PMs.  My options are to disable PM, or let it fill up.  I *think* it's less rude to let it fill up, than to disable it, and/or empty it and leave ppl hanging, waiting for a reply.

Actually, that's why 42dorian started this thread.  His inbox was constantly full too, trying to help ppl with my script.

I DO check this thread, several times a day.  I just don't have time to leave a suitable reply, most of the time.  This has earned me the reputation as "the guy that doesn't support his own work" which isn't entirely true.  Really, I'm the guy that doesn't have time to support his own work.  That's where the community comes into play.  So far, it's worked out okay.  It isn't a perfect arrangement, but it's the best I can do.

Paramvir has been a gift from God.  I don't know where he finds the time, but he's done an excellent job!

**Bullet #2**:  My production sites are hosted by the world's best web service provider, JaguarPC.  It took me years to find an ASP that would tolerate my modified, resource-intensive, hacks.  What I do is take standard CMSs and apps, like Coppermine Photo Gallery, WordPress, PHP-Nuke, et cetera, and re-write the code to my own satisfaction.  

My problem has always been, I get too much traffic for a shared hosting environment.  Their problem is, my web site(s) have taken down every shared server they've put me on, because of the traffic.  Really, I need to go the COLO or DEDICATED server route, but I can't afford it.

Currently, I'm getting around 100,000 page views a day on my main site (give_or_take 25,000) and support informed me that I'm bring down the whole node (again) e.g. I need to find a way to drive traffic away from my site(s)

Soooo, I had to shut down my site for a week or so, while I figured out a way to drive 'em away.  That's why you were getting the 403 (deny from all) error.  I was blocking all traffic, expect to my picture and exim servers.

I disabled some features on my production site, and currently have it up, right now.  I'm closely monitoring the server load.  If it starts spiking, I'll need to shut it down again, and try something else.

Anyway, hope that explains the situation(s).

Back on topic...

---

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-08-17
Hi Paramvir,

Yes, that sounds good.  Let me work on it.

#1 priority, this weekend, is monitoring my web server load.  Last week, it was spiking to 65-ish, at times.  It's motorboating around 1-ish right now (with some features disabled) which is great -- spiking to sub-2s -- which is acceptable.  It concerns me, however, that I'm still using 6.5 million inodes.  It bumping up against the soft limit.

### Post by Max_Runia on 2013-09-21
Hey VinDSL I really love your work on conky. I am having a problem with the moon displaying wrong, not sure what is causing it. I'll attach a screenshot.[https://docs.google.com/file/d/0B9c9p1S3hvs1TE54WVJ6SEhVY2s/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/file/d/0B9c9p1S3hvs1TE54WVJ6SEhVY2s/edit?usp=sharing)

As you can see the moon says 92% and Waning which is correct but the MoonPhases font is displaying the wrong Character. Not sure why this is. To be honest, I really like your latest screenshots where instead of using the MoonPhases font it looks like you are using images of the moon, like this... 
[http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-11-sep-2013-1.png](http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-11-sep-2013-1.png)

How did you do that? Thank you for writing such a great script!

---

### Post by paramvir on 2013-09-28
> **Max_Runia said:**
> I am having a problem with the moon displaying wrong, not sure what is causing it.
As you can see the moon says 92% and Waning which is correct but the MoonPhases font is displaying the wrong Character. Not sure why this is.

Hello Max :)

This question is regarding conkywx and so I am the one to answer your question... ;)

Yes that is a bug - Also I wonder which version of conkywx are you using - if it is 0.9.3 - the current version - you should use the option moon_outline=1 in the conkywx config file - this will enable the outline moon font characters -  In this set you get a ring and the moon characters are inside that ring.

The reason for using this is - the one without the ring is a bit wobbly - the font characters shift about and do not stay in a centre line axis if you please. There is a thought to disable the other font characters all together but then some soul may just want to use that set. Both sets are contained within the same font.

The next mega version of conkywx is under development and testing - I am also looking at the fact that Northern and Southern hemisphere view the moon differently - The images and fonts should reflect that. 

cheers

${font Ubuntu:bold:size=28}${color4}Ubuntu 12.10${offset 8}${font}[COLOR=#000000]

Does anybody know why I can't use the UbuntuTitle font in conky? I can easily use it in a libreoffice document but not in the conky.

[/COLOR][IMG]http://i43.tinypic.com/34qjpsx.png[/IMG]
[COLOR=#000000]
[/COLOR]```

${font Ubuntu:bold:size=28}${color4}Ubuntu 12.10${offset 8}${font}[COLOR=#000000]
[/COLOR]${font ubuntu-title:bold:size=28}${color4}Ubuntu 12.10${offset 8}${font}

```[COLOR=#000000]

As you can see there is no difference between Ubuntu and UbuntuTitle fonts using conky, but the difference is clear using libre office.



Also I'm wondering why I can't display the "am/pm" indicator next to the time:

[/COLOR]```
${voffset -4}${fontRadioSpace:size=32}${color3}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc7}${time %l:%M%p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc-1}${time %l:%M%p}${endif}${endif}${font}

```
[COLOR=#000000]
Thanks a lot.[/COLOR]

This seems to be something that often occurs: UbuntuTitle font doesn't work properly in Conky. I almost gave up but then I found this UbuntuTitle-package from here [https://wiki.ubuntu.com/UbuntuTitle](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/UbuntuTitle) and now, I don't know how or why but it works just fine.

The last weird problem I'm trying to solve is why I can't display am/pm information next to the time. Does anybody have a clue?

Anyway, here's the result, following Dorian/VinDSL instruction:


[IMG]http://i39.tinypic.com/whl5sn.png[/IMG]

And here's the weather-conky that I'm trying to setting up all by myself (although VinDSL's style is source of inspiration). :)

[IMG]http://i40.tinypic.com/2a0b14h.jpg[/IMG]

Last thing I'd like to ask: is there a way to display songs information using Audacious, instead of Banshee/Rhythmbox?
Thank you in advance.

### Post by aquarius18 on 2013-10-05
> **aquarius18 said:**
> Hi guys,

long time no talk - simply cos all was working a.ok. up until yesterday.

Since last night I am getting this in the 'WEATHER' heading:

WEATHER**[COLOR=#ff0000]- -Report-Buy a Station[/COLOR]** | blah blah ....

It used to read WEATHER Bellvista | blah blah ....

Bellvista being the station name nearest to us.

URI: [http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:00000.3.95566?MR=1](http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:00000.3.95566?MR=1)


WTF is going on here? Is it me (my script that miraculously changed) or is it Wunderground?

[IMG]http://isonetwork.net.au/conkywx_problem.png[/IMG]

Thanks guys

Frank

Well, got this working again -- to some satisfaction (see post #1115).

Modified the metric graphic template and it now looks like this:

[IMG]http://isonetwork.net.au/conkywx20131005.png[/IMG]

```
#################################################
## VinDSL | rev. 12-11-19 11:11 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
#################################################
## Modified by Frank (aquarius18) 05 Oct 2013  ##
##  METRIC WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 0.9.3  ##
#################################################
##
## filename and location: /home/frank/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-metric-weather-graphic-template.sh
##
## Show last update time in WX heading
${voffset -15}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color3}${goto 60} - ${offset 2}${color3}[**[COLOR=#ff0000]LN[/COLOR]**]${offset 2}${color6}updated:${offset 3}${color9}[**[COLOR=#ff0000]LU[/COLOR]**]${color8}${voffset -2}${offset 8}${hr 1}${font}
## Current Temperature, Temp Range and Wind
${voffset 9}${font Arrows:size=28}${goto 100}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset -43}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color cyan}${offset 8}[PT]${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.4}${offset 10}${color5}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color5}${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}${voffset -20}${goto 246}${font DroidSans:size=7.4}${color3}[WS] km/h${font}
${voffset -20}${goto 145}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.75}${color6}[D1T]${font}
${voffset 0}${goto 145}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.3}${color5}CoR${offset 3}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -45}${goto 130}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color6}I${font}${voffset -30}${goto 188}${font ConkyWindNESW:size=41.6}${color6}[BF]${font}
## Current conditions, Sunrise and Sunset
## ${voffset 2}${font Ubuntu:size=11}${color cyan}${offset 4}[CT]${font} --> removed current conditions to make space for icon
${voffset 25}${goto 130}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color5}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 6}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color5}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
## Rainfall, Humidity and Pressure
${voffset 25}${goto 17}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.55}${color5}Rain Fall${offset 1}:${goto 80}${color6}[RF]${offset 2}mm${font}${voffset 12}${goto 17}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.55}${color5}Humidity${offset 1}:${goto 80}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 17}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.55}${color5}Barometer${offset 1}:${goto 80}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}
## Moonrise, Moonset and Moonphase
${voffset -42}${goto 160}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.55}${color5}Moonrise${offset 1}:${goto 230}${color6}[MR]${font}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 160}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.55}${color5}Moonset${offset 1}:${goto 230}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 160}${font DroidSansFallback:size=7.55}${color5}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${goto 230}${color6}[FM]${font}
## Forecast Heading
${voffset 5}${goto 17}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${color5}[D2D]${goto 78}[D3D]${goto 138}[D4D]${goto 193}[D5D]${goto 248}[D6D]${font}
## Co-ordinates of Images
${image [PIC] -p 22,610 -s 44x44}${image [D2I] -p 20,758 -s 32x32}${image [D3I] -p 78,758-s 32x32}${image [D4I] -p 135,758 -s 32x32}${image [D5I] -p 190,758 -s 32x32}${image [D6I] -p 245,758 -s 32x32}
## Forecast
${voffset -12}${goto 17}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}${color3}[D2T]${goto 78}[D3T]${goto 138}[D4T]${goto 193}[D5T]${goto 248}[D6T]${font}
${voffset -2}${goto 17}${font DroidSans:size=8.45}${color5}CoR${offset 3}${color3}[D2P]${goto 77}${color5}CoR${offset 3}${color3}[D3P]${goto 138}${color5}CoR${offset 3}${color3}[D4P]${goto 193}${color5}CoR${offset 3}${color3}[D5P]${goto 248}${color5}CoR${offset 3}${color3}[D6P]${font}
```

Replaced the original SN with [COLOR=#ff0000]**LN**[/COLOR] and LF with **[COLOR=#ff0000]LU[/COLOR]**.

It doesn't show the actual name of the station, but it does the trick.

If I comment out the xml_ID=IQUEENSL218 in the conkywx.conf file then I get the current temperature showing N/A :mad:

Hope this helps anyone with the same problem.

Just wondering if anyone else in my neck of the woods (or anywhere else on the globe for that matter) experiences the same Wunderground BS??

@paramvir
haven't seen that elusive email yet mate????

Cheers / Frank

Last thing I'd like to ask: is there a way to display songs information using Audacious, instead of Banshee/Rhythmbox?

yes there is an easy way to do it with Conky....

here is a screenshot showing my Audacious information at the bottom of the
Left display....

the Right display is my Weather information from Conkywx....

here is a sample of my Conky code to display my Audacious information....

```
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Audaciuos Music Player
#--------------------------------------------------------------------
${font Dejavu Sans:bold:size=10}${color1}Audacious Music Player ${color4}${hr 2} $font${color3}
$color ${audacious_title 250}
 ${color green}${audacious_bar 10,250 } $color
 ${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${alignr}${audacious_status}
```

good luck...

Tommy

### Post by Cavsfan on 2013-10-10

I modified it a bit so that it fits my screen and is on the bottom right like VinDSL's script does with all the other music players and also keeps the same font/color scheme.


```
##################################

##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -70}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 2}$font$
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${color green}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${alignr}${audacious_status}

```

Are you using **$endif** at the end of your **$if_running** statement?

### Post by Cavsfan on 2013-10-11
> **stinkeye said:**
> Are you using **$endif** at the end of your **$if_running** statement?

> **NM5TF said:**
> + 10 Stinkeye...think you nailed it...:p

Tommy

+ 100 You are right [COLOR=#000000]*Tommy,*[/COLOR] Stinkeye nailed it on the head! Should've known it was something like that.
The code below works well with VinDSL's conky when running Audaciuos. It even has the green bar. Pretty sweet! 
Thanks *Tommy*!


```
##################################
##     Audaciuos                ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -70}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 2}$font$
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${color green}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${alignr}${audacious_status}${endif}

```

### Post by NM5TF on 2013-10-13
@3GyXCEc....you are welcome....

if you want the Audacious info to ALWAYS be showing, then leave it like
you have it now....

if you want the Audacious info to ONLY show when it is playing, then add
these 2 lines of code:

at the beginning add this
```
${if_running audacious}
```

at the end add this
```
${endif}
```

thanx to @Stinkeye & @CavsFan for that tip...

Tommy

### Post by Cavsfan on 2013-10-17
When I open the package with Ubuntu Software Centre to install the package it says this:

> Enable the conkywx config file option Dynamic_Moon=1 for trend graphs to work you need to install GnuPlot.

I've installed GnuPlot and everything else I believe but am unable to find the config file with **Dynamic_Moon=1** in it.
Could you point out where that file is?
Thanks :)

---

### Post by paramvir on 2013-10-17
> **Cavsfan said:**
> When I open the package with Ubuntu Software Centre to install the package it says this:
I've installed GnuPlot and everything else I believe but am unable to find the config file with **Dynamic_Moon=1** in it.
Could you point out where that file is?
Thanks :)


The config file in all cases is the one used in the examples at /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx.conf - or - in the conkywx stand alone directory / examples sub directory.

GnuPlot is used for the graphs for temperature, pressure and dew point. The elements are [GT], [GP] and [GD] respectively when you want to integrate them into your template. This information is missing in the wiki copy with conkywx program but updated on the main one :p

Dynamic Moon will require ImageMagick. At the bottom of the conkywx.conf file - you will also need to comment out one section and un-comment another - when you see the conkywx.conf file - you will see the comments I have added. It is something to do with the way Ubuntu is displaying colour now - 12.04 works fine with original figures but 13.04 onwards do not - this is FYI. ;)

Let me know how it goes :)

### Post by paramvir on 2013-10-18
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Not real sure what I have. It says error next to the alert. I installed GnuPlot which installed Imagemagick and a whole lot of other stuff.
This is on 12.04:
[ATTACH=CONFIG]247029[/ATTACH]


That is Great :D - have a look at the default templates mentioned in short-conkyrc.sh + conky-restart.sh + ../examples/conkywx.conf - there are many changes in the config file - and also refer to the release notes in the conkywx directory.

The "Error" word only comes if there is an unknown weather element. Weather Alerts are not part of that system.

The template probably needs tweaking - can you send me the template, your conkywx config file and the debug log.

You can run the conkywx default template and see how it comes out - /usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh

### Post by NM5TF on 2013-10-18
in whichever template you are using look for something like this just before
the TEXT area...

[CODE
# location of wxalert file - conkywx tmp directory for the used conkywx config file
# IMPORTANT do not comment this line - even if you do not use it
#-----------------------------------------------------------
template3 /tmp/wx-param-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert]
[/CODE]

of course you will replace the user "param" with your correct user name...

see if that doesn't fix the error message...

Tommy

Not sure if this is what you requested or not. My brain hurts! :)

EDIT: I did fix the CONKYWX ----------------------------------------- line with the alert and error. I commented it out in /home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-imperial-weather-graphic-template.sh
and uncommented it out where it originally was in the .conky file. The files on this post have been fixed to reflect that change.

Here is my conky file: 
```
###############################################
## VinDSL | rev. 12-11-19 11:55 | ver. 0.7.9 ##
###############################################
##          November 2012 Series             ##
###############################################

## ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

####
## Development Platforms (current)
#
#  Ubuntu 10.10 'Maverick Meerkat' (GNOME 2.28 - Conky 1.8.0-1)
#  Ubuntu 13.04 'Raring Ringtail'  (GNOME-SHELL - UNITY - LXDE - Conky 1.9.0-2)
#  Screen Resolution: 1920x1200x24 (DELL UltraSharp 1907FP)
#
## Tips n' Tricks: conky 1.8.1 will no longer be supported. RIP :)

####
## Prerequisites (required)
#
#  conky-all 1.8.0-1 or 1.9.0-2
#  Conkywx 0.9.3 (Paramvir Likhari)
#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor

####
## Installed fonts (required)
#
#  Arrows (JLH Fonts - not included in link below)
#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva - not included in link below)
#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
# 
## Tips n' Tricks from: Mr. Peachy, djyoung4, and 42dorian (Thanks!)
## Most necessary fonts can be downloaded here: http://ompldr.org/vOHdoag
## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

####
## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
#
use_xft yes
xftfont DroidSans:size=8.75
xftalpha 0.1

####
## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
#
override_utf8_locale yes

####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
#
text_buffer_size 4352

####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 700

####
## Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.
#
background yes

####
## Update interval in seconds.
#
update_interval 2.0

####
## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
## Zero makes Conky run forever.
#
total_run_times 0

####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
####
## Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
#
own_window_argb_visual yes
own_window_argb_value 255

####
## Force images to redraw when they change.
#
imlib_cache_size 1

####
## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
#
double_buffer yes

####
## Draw shades?
#
draw_shades yes
default_shade_color 330

####
## Draw outlines?
#
draw_outline no

####
## Draw borders around text?
#
draw_borders no

####
## Draw borders around graphs?
#
draw_graph_borders no

####
## Print text to stdout?
## Print text in console?
#
out_to_ncurses no
out_to_console no

####
## Text alignment.
#
alignment top_right

####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
#minimum_size 245 1394
minimum_size 245 1894
maximum_width 1200 
####added by me
## Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
max_user_text 81920

####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 245

####
## Gap between text and screen borders.
#
gap_x 6      # Left/Right
gap_y 32  # Top/Bottom

####
## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
#
short_units yes

####
## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
#
pad_percents 0

####
## Pad spacing between text and borders.
#
border_inner_margin 4

####
## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
#
top_name_width 10

####
## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
## Set to yes, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
#
no_buffers yes

####
## Set to yes, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
#
uppercase no

####
## Number of cpu samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
cpu_avg_samples 2

####
## Number of net samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
net_avg_samples 2

####
## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
## Only affects certain objects.
#
use_spacer right

####
## My colors (suit yourself)
#
color0 White        #FFFFFF
color1 Ivory        #FFFFF0
color2 Ivory2        #EEEEE0
color3 Ivory3        #CDCDC1
color4 Tan1        #FFA54F
color5 Tan2        #EE9A49
color6 Gray        #7E7E7E
color7 AntiqueWhite4    #8B8378
color8 DimGray        #696969
color9 Tomato        #FF6347
# colorX OrangeRed    #FF4500

#####
## Load Lua for shading (optional)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
lua_load ~/.conky/draw_bg.lua
lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

####
## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua
lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_testing.lua
lua_draw_hook_post main_bars

####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
template1 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf"
template2 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-metric-conkywx.conf"
template3 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-imperial-weather-dingbat-template.sh"
template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-imperial-weather-graphic-template.sh"
template5 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-metric-weather-dingbat-template.sh"
template6 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-metric-weather-graphic-template.sh"

TEXT
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}1${offset 1}2${offset 1}.${offset 0}0${offset 0}4${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
####
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
${voffset -2}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
# ${voffset 0}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:size=8}${color4}Pre-Alpha${font}
####
## Uncomment to display YOUR custom graphic image (upper-right corner)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -52}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/ABC.jpg -p 180,0 -s 52x52 -n}
##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################
${voffset 7}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}SYSTEM${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec lsb_release -sd | sed -e 's/development/dev/g'}${alignr}${pre_exec lsb_release -sr}${font}
${voffset 2}${offset -2}${font OpenLogos:size=12}${color2}Z${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 3}${sysname}${offset 3}${kernel}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${machine}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nVidia ID (nvidia-smi version - dynamic)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${alignr}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for 'nvidia-current' driver ID (DPKG version - hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec dpkg --status nvidia-current | grep Version | cut -f 1 -d '-' | sed 's/[^.,0-9]//g'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-19'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (any card / any driver - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Video Card Product Name${alignr}Driver Version${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU frequency (hybrid)
#${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Quad Q9550${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Quad Q9550${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}2.83${offset 1}GHz${font}
####
## Uncomment for System Uptime (hybrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}q${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}System${offset 3}Uptime${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${uptime_short}${font}
## uncoment for grub version
${voffset 2}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Grub${offset 3}Version${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec grub-install -v | grep grub-install | cut -c21-42}${font}

${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color0}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color0}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -80}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color7}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${voffset -70}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}

##################################

#${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt | head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################

${if_running rhythmbox}
${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##    BANSHEE (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running banshee}
${voffset -26}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}BANSHEE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f1- -d " "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##   GUAYADEQUE (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running guayadeque}
${voffset -36}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}GUAYADEQUE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}
${voffset -47}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     Audaciuos                ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -70}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 2}$font$
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${alignr}${audacious_status}${endif}
```

Here is my vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf file:
```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Wund Weather Config File | VinDSL | rev. 12-11-19 08:53 | ver. 0.7.9
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# THIS PROGRAM IS FOR PERSONAL AND PRIVATE USE ONLY
# CONTACT WUNDERGROUND.COM FOR PROFESSIONAL SOLUTIONS
# CAVEAT: No guarantee that this program will work on all platforms,
# Use At Your Own Risk.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# File path: "/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf"
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Instructions/examples: "/usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx.conf"
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use XML data from wunderground
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: If enabled, you must supply the XML ID of the station (below)
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_xml=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# XML ID
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Only needed, if you "use_xml=1" (above)
# # Scottsdale, AZ, Equestrian Manor
# xml_ID=KAZSCOTT39
# # Indian Harbour Beach, FL
# xml_id=KFLSATEL4
# # Chandler, AZ, Cooper and Frye
# xml_ID=KAZCHAND36
# # Fairbanks, AK, Skyflight Airstrip
# xml_ID=KAKFAIRB35
# # Colville, WA, Colville Mountain
# # Indian Harbour Beach, FL
xml_ID=KFLINDIA12

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Location settings
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# # Scottsdale, Arizona, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:85250.1.99999"
# # Indian Harbour Beach, FL
Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:32937.3.99999"
# # Chandler, Arizona, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:85224.1.99999"
# # Fairbanks, Alaska, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:99701.1.99999"
# # Colville, Washington, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:99114.1.99999"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Units to use and Downloaded
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enter the units to get and use, centigrade or Fahrenheit 
# [SI] metric
# [IM] imperial/english
Units_to_Use=IM
Units_downloaded=IM

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Show units, use short date, show notification
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] disable
# [1] enable
Units_show=0        # If you want to see units like km/h, mph, etc
use_short_date=1    # 'Sunday, 27' will be shortened to 'Sun 27'
show_notification=1    # Every update will display a popup notification

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Path/image to background image
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
path_to_background_image="$cwxpath/images/bgrounds"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  How do you want the present condition to look
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Capitalize The First Letter Of All Words, In The Present Condition
# [1] All UPPERCASE
# [2] all lowercase
# [3] Capitilize the first letter/first word only, in the present condition
pc_what_case=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use blacklist to manipulate Present Condition text
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_pcondition_blacklist=1    

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Words you want to add to/remove from Present Condition text
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
pcondition_blacklist=(
     Freezing
     pellet
     pellets
     Precipitation
     Small
    "and:+"
    "heavy:hvy"
    "light:lt"
    "Low Drifting"
    "Patches of"
    "Severe:svr"
    "Thunderstorms:t-storms"
    "Thunderstorm:t-storms"
    "Widespread:ws"
    "with:+"
  )

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process the final template before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_template_cleanup=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process these final template changes before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the changes in single quotes as shown
template_cleanup=( 
    'AM:${offset -2}AM'
    'PM:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waning Gibbous:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
  )

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use UPPERCASE or lowercase AM PM or am pm for 12 hour time format
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] ALL UPPERCASE (EXAMPLE: 03:00 PM)
# [1] all lowercase (example: 03:00 pm)
# [2] Hide am pm (example: 03:00)
# [3] all lowercase with no padding/spacing (example: 3:00pm)
# [4] Use custom time format. State the format in custom_time_format
# NOTE: This will only work if Time_format=12 (below)
show_lowercase_ampm=4

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Set custom time format - see man date in a terminal to get more options
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the time format in single quotes as shown
custom_time_format='+%l:%M %p'

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Time format to use 12 or 24 hour format
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [12] AM or PM (example: 03:00 PM)
# [24] 24 hour format (example: 15:00)
Time_format=12

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Show deg symbol in forecast temps
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Hide all deg symbols (example: 95/63)
# [1] Show all deg symbols (example: 95°/63°)
# [2] Show all deg symbols, depending on Unit_to_Use (metric: 35°/17°) (imperial: 95°/63°)
# [3] Show deg symbol ONLY, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°)
# [4] Show deg symbol AND unit, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°F)
show_degsym_fc_temp=3

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Characters to use for forecast trends
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rising trend arrow
risingtrend='c'
#
# Falling trend arrow
fallingtrend='d'
#
# Steady trend arrow
steadytrend='Q'

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Choose weather icon set
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [1] Enable icon set: org 12 by bharathp666 (Paramvir Likhari remix)
# [2] Enable icon set: origin unknown
# [3] Enable icon set: tick weather icons by xiao4
# [4] Enable icon set: Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
wx_iconset=2

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Moon font characters with or without outline ring
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Default was formerly "simple" e.g. no outline ring
# [0] Disable outline and use simple
# [1] Use outline and disable simple
moon_outline=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Allow Conkywx to check for latest version release
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Never check for a new version of Conkywx
# [1] Check for a new version, every 24 hours
enable_new_version_check=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Today date format - used in last fetch
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
today_date_format="%A %b, %d %Y"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimum time the script allows the wesite to be polled
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: This setting MUST BE LESS THAN the fetch frequency in your Conky script.
#
# Uncomment for Conky scripts that fetch data every 900 seconds (15 minutes).
minimum_fetch_time=870
#
# Uncomment for testing purposes. Fetches fresh data every time Conky is restarted.
# minimum_fetch_time=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# --- DO NOT TOUCH SECTION ---
# URLs used in this program
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
main_url="http://www.wunderground.com/global/stations/"
xml_url="http://api.wunderground.com/weatherstation/WXCurrentObXML.asp?ID=" 
```
