```

I finally could see the problem when I ran conkywx -c my config file -t my template file in the terminal and tracked the error codes...;)

Here's the screen shot and thanks again for all the help:

[https://db.tt/GMQg24hK](https://db.tt/GMQg24hK)

Xubuntu 13.10, Conky 1.9.0, 1680X1050,

Ok - my sleeves are rolled up - lets get to it...

Actually me is pretty tied up - sorting 70 plus languages... well...

Your conkywx.conf is wrong version. It perhaps will get you showing some of the stuff but will not work for the version and get you sad-nessssss...

You need to look for fonts - if you do not have the one here like me ;) - change them to the ones similar - use mono font for mono font - you get my drift :)

This is what you should be using:~
```

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Wund Weather Config File | Paramvir | rev. 13-10-19 05:10 | ver. 1.0.0
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## THIS PROGRAM IS FOR PERSONAL AND PRIVATE USE ONLY
## CONTACT WUNDERGROUND.COM FOR PROFESSIONAL SOLUTIONS
## CAVEAT: No guarantee that this program will work on all platforms,
## Use At Your Own Risk.

## To comment an option means adding # character at the start of that line
## this line is a comment starting with a # character

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Location settings - REQUIRED
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## # Your Location ???
**Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:97351.4.99999"**

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## xml_ID to use XML data from wunderground - OPTIONAL
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## # Commented - fix and use
# xml_ID=KFLINDIA12

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## User position
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Enter Latitude, Longitude and t_zone (time zone) in decimal format
## Comment the position if you want to use wunderground data co-ordinates
## This position will be in 1 decimal place.
## To get more accurate information for your precise location the coordinates
## can be picked by using Google or http://www.findlatitudeandlongitude.com
## Latitude="37.456254° N"
## Longitude="88.867862° E"


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## How to handle the time zone values
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Let conkywx calculate the time difference
## [2] Pick up the time zone values from wunderground
## OR - To Use custom time zone. State the time zone here
t_zone_pick=2


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Units to use and Downloaded
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Enter the units to get and use, centigrade or Fahrenheit
## [SI] metric
## [IM] imperial/english
Units_to_Use=IM
Units_downloaded=IM


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## comment option to disable, defining variable to 0 also works
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] enable
## if you want to see units like km/h etc in conky
## Units_show=1

## Sunday, 27 will become Sun 27
use_short_date=1

## Every update a popup notification shows
show_notification=1

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Weather Alerts
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Note: Active alerts at present time are only for the US.
## They will NOT warn you if a Tropical Storm is coming your way.
## Enable show_wx_Tropical_alerts for that.
show_wx_Active_alerts=1
show_wx_Tropical_alerts=1

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Allow Conkywx to check for latest version release
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [0] Never check for a new version of Conkywx
## [1] Check for a new version, every 24 hours
enable_new_version_check=1

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Moon font characters with or without outline ring
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## NOTE: Default was formerly "simple" e.g. no outline ring
## [0] Disable outline and use simple
## [1] Use outline and disable simple
moon_outline=1

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Dynamic Moon - using ImageMagick
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## View Moon image as it appears outside
## [1] use your local time, [2] use station local time for calculation
## Needs Imagemagick installed
Dynamic_Moon=2

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Font to Image - using ImageMagick
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## convert ConkyWeather font to 3D images - state the colour here.
## Use only for Image Templates
## COMMENT these options for using in font templates
## COMMENT these options for using regular weather images in image templates
## Needs Imagemagick installed
## to see List of Color Names visit http://www.imagemagick.org/script/color.php
## Colour can be stated in any of the following formats - enclosed in single quotes
## example for colour *lime* - 'lime' '#0f0' '#00ff00' 'rgb(0,255,0)' 'rgb(0,100%,0)'
fti_now='#fed577'
fti_wind='DarkSeaGreen1'
fti_fct='lime'

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Trend Graphs - using GnuPlot
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## We have ability to generate graphs for temperature, pressure and dewpoint
## You need gnuplot to be installed
## comment these colour options do disable
## colour can be in names or '#fed577' format if name gives white output
## This seed data is generated by wunderground and rest is recorded by conkywx
graph_temp='red'
graph_pressure='green'
graph_dewpoint='cyan'


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  How do you want the present conditon to look like
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Capitalize The First Letter Of All Words, In The Present Condition [Default]
## [2] All UPPERCASE
## [3] all lowercase
## [4] Capitilize the first letter/first word only, in the present condition
pc_what_case=1


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Add words you want to remove from Present Condition text in this list
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this variable Block if not required
pcondition_blacklist=(
    Freezing
    pellet
    pellets
    Precipitation
    Small
    'and:+'
    'heavy:hvy'
    'light:lt'
    'Low Drifting'
    'Patches of'
    'Severe:svr'
    'Thunderstorms:t-storms'
    'Thunderstorm:t-storms'
    'Widespread:ws'
    'with:+'
)

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Process the final template before showtime
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this variable Block if not required
template_cleanup=(
    'AM:${offset -2}AM'
    'PM:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waning Gibbous:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
)

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  LANGUAGE to see data in
##  Current Supported Languages:
#    it [Italian]    - Message & Language Translation by "Night Train"
#    es [Spanish]    - Language Translation by "Paramvir" needs proofing
#    pt [Portuguese]    - Language Translation by "Paramvir" needs proofing
#    de [German]        - Language Translation by "Paramvir" needs proofing
#    fr [French]        - Language Translation by "Paramvir" needs proofing
#    English is default and needs no setting
## Comment this variable if not required
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## language_to_use="it"

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Use UPPERCASE or lowercase AM PM or am pm for 12 hour time format
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] ALL UPPERCASE (EXAMPLE: 03:00 PM)
## [2] all lowercase (example: 03:00 pm)
## [3] Hide am pm (example: 03:00)
## [4] all lowercase with no padding/spacing (example: 3:00pm)
## [5] Use 24 hour time format
## OR - To Use custom time format. State the format here
## To get more date formats see man strftime
## Time_format='%-I:%M %p'
Time_format='%l:%M %p'

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Show time with month and day for Moon Phases
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Show month day time (example: May 18 10:06 am)
##      The time will follow the Time_format settings above in Time_format.
## [2] Show only month n day (EXAMPLE: May 18)
## OR - To Use custom time format. State the format here
## To get more date formats see man strftime
## moon_time='%d %b %R'
moon_time=1

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Today date format - used in last fetch
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
today_date_format='%A %b, %d %Y'

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## show deg symbol in forecast temps,
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Hide all deg symbols (example: 95/63)
## [2] Show all deg symbols (example: 95°/63°)
## [3] Show all deg symbols, depending on Unit_to_Use (metric: 35°C/17°C) (imperial: 95°F/63°F)
## [4] Show deg symbol ONLY, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°)
## [5] Show deg symbol AND unit, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°F) [Default]
show_degsym_fc_temp=4

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## characters to use for forecast trends
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Rising trend arrow
risingtrend='c'
##
## Falling trend arrow
fallingtrend='d'
##
## Steady trend arrow
steadytrend='Q'


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Choose weather icon set
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Enable icon set: org 12 by bharathp666 (Paramvir Likhari remix)
## [2] Enable icon set: origin unknown
## [3] Enable icon set: tick weather icons by xiao4
## [4] Enable icon set: Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
wx_iconset=2

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Minimum time the script allows the website to be polled
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
minimum_fetch_time=870

## Uncomment for testing purposes. Fetches fresh data every time Conky is restarted.
## minimum_fetch_time=10
## minimum_fetch_time=3600

## Font to image internal settings - do not comment - change only if required
## These setting changes may be required for some Systems
## ONLY USED if you are using fti [ font to image] above - do not comment these
## you can change these values if rendered images are not to your liking
## 3d_depth = normalize level % - 0-max depth, 50-flat, 50 - 100 inverse 3D, only whole values no decimal
## line_thickness = strokewidth value can use decimal values
## image_thickness = blur sigma value can use decimal values
## brightness = tint % only whole values no decimal
## if your images are looking rather bright or white then perhaps you can use the below commented values

fti_now_3d_depth='5%'
fti_now_line_thickness='2'
fti_now_image_thickness='6'
fti_now_brightness='110%'

fti_fct_3d_depth='20%'
fti_fct_line_thickness='2'
fti_fct_image_thickness='6'
fti_fct_brightness='115%'

fti_wind_3d_depth='15%'
fti_wind_line_thickness='1'
fti_wind_image_thickness='2'
fti_wind_brightness='115%'

## For Ubuntu 13+ and Mint Systems

## fti_now_3d_depth='8%'
## fti_now_line_thickness='3'
## fti_now_image_thickness='2'
## fti_now_brightness='70%'

## fti_fct_3d_depth='15%'
## fti_fct_line_thickness='3'
## fti_fct_image_thickness='2'
## fti_fct_brightness='100%'

## fti_wind_3d_depth='5%'
## fti_wind_line_thickness='1'
## fti_wind_image_thickness='1'
## fti_wind_brightness='110%'  


```


Use this for Template:
```

#################################################
## VinDSL | rev. 13-10-21 12:09 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
#################################################
## IMPERIAL WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 1.0.0 ##
#################################################
${voffset 0}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color6}${alignc -5}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -19}${offset 2}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.4}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}
${voffset -40}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.75}${color6}[D1T]${font}
${voffset 0}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.3}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -45}${goto 188}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color6}I${font}
${voffset 6}${font Ubuntu:size=22.75}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
${voffset 7}${alignc 3}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
${voffset 8}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FM]${font}
[COLOR=#0000ff]**### This for font to image**[/COLOR]
${if_existing [BI]}${voffset -30}${image [BI] -p 10, 615 -s 60x60}${else}${voffset -65}${goto 16}${font ConkyWindNESW:size=41.6}${color6}[BF]${font}${endif}
${voffset -38}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
${voffset 23}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] mph${voffset -1}${goto 203}[MP]${font}
** [COLOR=#0000ff]### This section handles the Gnuplot stuff[/COLOR]**
${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}${goto 30}Temp${goto 120}Baro${goto 210}Dew
${voffset -1}${image [GT] -p 18,705 -s  50x10}${image [GP] -p 107,705 -s  50x10}${image [GD] -p 190, 705 -s  50x10}
${voffset 9}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
${image [PIC] -p 10,  500 -s 46x46}${image [MIC] -p 195, 620 -s 45x45}${image [D2I] -p 22,  735 -s 34x34}${image [D3I] -p 82,  735 -s 34x34}${image [D4I] -p 141, 735 -s 34x34}${image [D5I] -p 198, 735 -s 34x34}
${voffset 20}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.5}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}

```

In your main conkyrc file use this at the right places above the **TEXT** where you have the **template**# [# is number] listed. The conkywx file here is called **vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf - make sure you have the name correct.**
```

template7 /tmp/wx-tom-**vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf**-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert

```

In the weather section use this
```

##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
# ${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
[COLOR=#0000ff]**### This handles the weather alerts**[/COLOR]
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} \
${color8}Alert: ${if_match ${execi 60 expr length "$(cat ${template7})"}>=32}\
${color5}${scroll 30 10 ${execi 60 cat ${template7}}}${else}\
${color5}${execi 60 cat ${template7}}${endif}${endif} ${color4}${hr 2}${font}${color8}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (DINGBAT font set).
#${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template3}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
[COLOR=#0000ff]**### This is the call to conkywx make sure the template numbers match with your declarations before the TEXT **[/COLOR]
${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for METRIC Weather Stats (DINGBAT font set).
# ${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template2}" -t "${template5}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for Metric Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
# ${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template2}" -t "${template6}"}${font}

```

That should get you on...

cheers

I feel we need to make a collaborative effort and get this fantabulous VinDSL templates as part of conkywx and also a wiki to go with that - so when you install conkywx you also get the option of using this template. ### Post by Cavsfan on 2013-12-06
> **paramvir said:**
> I feel we need to make a collaborative effort and get this fantabulous VinDSL templates as part of conkywx and also a wiki to go with that - so when you install conkywx you also get the option of using this template. As per the DEB and other distro installation file formats you cannot mess around with local home directories - we have many work arounds but I feel it will be rather simple to just include all that and since we spend so much time for help desk activities for the template if we have one unified location it will be help full  for people wanting to use this template.

People - thoughts on this please... :)

I agree. VinDSL's conky is that good! It should be in a wiki. I know that would be too much for me though. Any body else feel like they have the capacity to do that?

> **pcgeek2t said:**
> Yes, the Conkywx_Font_Assistant.sh is mentioned in the wikki provided during the install (file:///usr/share/conkywx/conkywxwiki.html),  
Thanks for the link, it will let me move on without chasing this down....

Thanks again
I

Good deal!
Glad I could help.

### Post by pcgeek2t on 2013-12-07
paramvir, cavsfan and all.

Here's the latest update, after the last round of suggestions things really came together.

[IMG]https://db.tt/NtI876Hw[/IMG]

My .conkyrc
```
###############################################
## VinDSL | rev. 12-11-19 11:55 | ver. 1.0.0 ##
###############################################
##          Tom's 12-2-13                    ##
###############################################

## ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

####
## Development Platforms (current)
#
#  Ubuntu 14.04 'Trusty Tahr'  (GNOME 3.8 - Conky 1.9.0-3)
#  Screen Resolution: 1920x1200x24 (Hanns-G 28" monitor)
#
## Tips n' Tricks: conky 1.8.1 will no longer be supported. RIP :)

####
## Prerequisites (required)
#
#  conky-all 1.8.0-1 or 1.9.0-3
#  Conkywx 1.0.0 (Paramvir Likhari)
#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor

####
## Installed fonts (required)
#
#  Arrows (JLH Fonts - not included in link below)
#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva - not included in link below)
#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
# 
## Tips n' Tricks from: Mr. Peachy, djyoung4, and 42dorian (Thanks!)
## Most necessary fonts can be downloaded here: [http://ompldr.org/vOHdoag](http://ompldr.org/vOHdoag)
## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

####
## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
#
use_xft yes
xftfont DroidSans:size=8.75
xftalpha 0.1

####
## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
#
override_utf8_locale yes

####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
#
text_buffer_size 4352

####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 800

####
## Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.
#
background yes

####
## Update interval in seconds.
#
update_interval 2.0

####
## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
## Zero makes Conky run forever.
#
total_run_times 0

####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
####
## Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
#
own_window_argb_visual yes
own_window_argb_value 255

####
## Force images to redraw when they change.
#
imlib_cache_size 1

####
## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
#
double_buffer yes

####
## Draw shades?
#
draw_shades yes
default_shade_color 330

####
## Draw outlines?
#
draw_outline no

####
## Draw borders around text?
#
draw_borders no

####
## Draw borders around graphs?
#
draw_graph_borders no

####
## Print text to stdout?
## Print text in console?
#
out_to_ncurses no
out_to_console no

####
## Text alignment.
#
alignment top_right

####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
#minimum_size 245 1394
minimum_size 445 1894
maximum_width 1200 
####added by me
## Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
max_user_text 114688

####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 280

####
## Gap between text and screen borders.
#
gap_x 10     # Left/Right
gap_y 40  # Top/Bottom

####
## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
#
short_units yes

####
## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
#
pad_percents 0

####
## Pad spacing between text and borders.
#
border_inner_margin 4

####
## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
#
top_name_width 10

####
## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
## Set to yes, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
#
no_buffers yes

####
## Set to yes, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
#
uppercase no

####
## Number of cpu samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
cpu_avg_samples 2

####
## Number of net samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
net_avg_samples 2

####
## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
## Only affects certain objects.
#
use_spacer right

####
## My colors (suit yourself)
#
color0 White        #FFFFFF
color1 Ivory        #FFFFF0
color2 Ivory2        #EEEEE0
color3 Ivory3        #CDCDC1
color4 Tan1        #FFA54F
color5 Tan2        #EE9A49
color6 Gray        #7E7E7E
color7 AntiqueWhite4    #8B8378
color8 DimGray        #696969
color9 Tomato        #FF6347
# colorX OrangeRed    #FF4500

#####
## Load Lua for shading (optional)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
#lua_load ~/.conky/draw_bg.lua
#lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

####
## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua
lua_draw_hook_post main_bars

####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
template1 "/home/tom/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf"
template4 "/home/tom/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-imperial-weather-graphic-template.sh"
template7 /tmp/wx-tom-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert

TEXT
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}1${offset 1}2${offset 1}.${offset 0}0${offset 0}4${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -70}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
####
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
${voffset -2}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
# ${voffset 0}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:size=8}${color4}Pre-Alpha${font}
####
## Uncomment to display YOUR custom graphic image (upper-right corner)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -52}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/ABC.jpg -p 180,0 -s 52x52 -n}
##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################
${voffset 7}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}SYSTEM${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec lsb_release -sd | sed -e 's/development/dev/g'}${alignr}${pre_exec lsb_release -sr}${font}
${voffset 2}${offset -2}${font OpenLogos:size=12}${color2}Z${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 3}${sysname}${offset 3}${kernel}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${machine}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nVidia ID (nvidia-smi version - dynamic)
#${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${alignr}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for 'nvidia-current' driver ID (DPKG version - hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec dpkg --status nvidia-current | grep Version | cut -f 1 -d '-' | sed 's/[^.,0-9]//g'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-19'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (any card / any driver - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}AMD Radeon HD 4550${alignr}Driver Version${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU frequency (hybrid)
#${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Quad Q9550${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}AMD${offset 3}AthlonII${offset 3}X2${offset 3}245${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}2.9${offset 1}GHz${font}
###### Uncomment for System Uptime (hybrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}q${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}System${offset 3}Uptime${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${uptime_short}${font}
## uncoment for grub version
${voffset 2}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Grub${offset 3}Version${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec grub-install -v | grep grub-install | cut -c21-42}${font}
##################################
##          PROCESSORS          ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}PROCESSORS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}%${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}%${font}
#${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%${font}
#${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%${font}
##################################
##            MEMORY            ##
##################################
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}MEMORY${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}l${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 3}RAM${goto 97}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${mem}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${memmax}${alignr}${memperc}%${font}
##################################
##             HDD              ##
##################################
${voffset 14}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}HDD${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 10}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}ROOT${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${fs_used /}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /}%${font}
#${voffset 7}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}HOME${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${fs_used /media/tom/home}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /media/tom/home}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /media/tom/home}%${font}
${voffset 16}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}SDISK USB${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${fs_used /media/tom/SDISK}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /media/tom/SDISK}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /media/tom/SDISK}%${font}

##################################
##         TEMPERATURES         ##
##################################
#${voffset 10}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}CPU/GPU TEMPERATURES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 1 Temp: $alignr${execi 600 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
#${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 2 Temp: $alignr${execi 600 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
#${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 3 Temp: $alignr${execi 600 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
#${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 4 Temp: $alignr${execi 600 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
#${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${execi 10 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F
##################################
##         TOP PROCESSES        ##
##################################
${voffset 14}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TOP PROCESSES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 6}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 1}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 1}${alignr}${top_mem mem 1}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 2}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 2}${alignr}${top_mem mem 2}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 3}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 3}${alignr}${top_mem mem 3}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 4}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 4}${alignr}${top_mem mem 4}%${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 5}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 5}${alignr}${top_mem mem 5}%${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 6}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 6}${alignr}${top_mem mem 6}%${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 7}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 7}${alignr}${top_mem mem 7}%${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 8}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 8}${alignr}${top_mem mem 8}%${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 9}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 9}${alignr}${top_mem mem 9}%${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 10}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 10}${alignr}${top_mem mem 8}%${font}
##################################
##          NETWORK             ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}NETWORK${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}T${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Download${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totaldown eth0}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${downspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}N${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Upload${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totalup eth0}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${upspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 4}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}LAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${addr eth0}${font}
#${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}Demo mode
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
# ${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
### This handles the weather alerts
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} \
${color8}Alert: ${if_match ${execi 60 expr length "$(cat ${template7})"}>=32}\
${color5}${scroll 30 10 ${execi 60 cat ${template7}}}${else}\
${color5}${execi 60 cat ${template7}}${endif}${endif} ${color4}${hr 2}${font}${color8}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (DINGBAT font set).
#${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template3}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
### This is the call to conkywx make sure the template numbers match with your declarations before the TEXT 
${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for METRIC Weather Stats (DINGBAT font set).
# ${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template2}" -t "${template5}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for Metric Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
# ${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template2}" -t "${template6}"}${font}
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset 25}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -8}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color0}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 10}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 75}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color0}${alignc 80}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color0}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 75}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 75}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -75}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color7}${alignc 115}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${voffset -70}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
# ${voffset -68}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 1 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running rhythmbox}
${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt | head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 2 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
#${if_running rhythmbox}
#${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##    BANSHEE (Experimental)    ##
##################################
#${if_running banshee}
#${voffset -26}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}BANSHEE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset -10}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f1- -d " "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##   GUAYADEQUE (Experimental)  ##
##################################
#${if_running guayadeque}
#${voffset -33}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}GUAYADEQUE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
#${if_running vlc}
#${voffset -55}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     Audaciuos                ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -70}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 2}$font$
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${alignr}${audacious_status}${endif}
```

wx-wu-vindsl-imerial-weather-graphic-template.sh
```
#################################################
## VinDSL | rev. 13-10-21 12:09 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
#################################################
## IMPERIAL WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 1.0.0 ##
#################################################
${voffset 0}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color6}${alignc -5}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -19}${offset 8}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.4}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}
${voffset -35}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.75}${color9}[D1T]${font}
${voffset -15}
${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.3}${color4}CoR${offset 5}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -40}${goto 188}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color9}I${offset 10}${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:bold:size=22.75}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
${voffset 7}${alignc 3}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
${voffset 8}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FM]${font}
### This for font to image
${if_existing [BI]}${voffset -10}${image [BI] -p 6, 625 -s 60x60}${else}${voffset -65}${goto 16}${font ConkyWindNESW:size=41.6}${color6}[BF]${font}${endif}
${voffset -30}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
${voffset 13}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] mph${voffset -1}${goto 203}[MP]${font}
 ### This section handles the Gnuplot stuff
${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}${goto 30}Temp${goto 120}Baro${goto 210}Dew
${voffset -1}${image [GT] -p 18,735 -s  50x10}${image [GP] -p 107,735 -s  50x10}${image [GD] -p 190,735 -s  50x10}
${voffset 9}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
${image [PIC] -p 10,  530 -s 46x46}${image [MIC] -p 210, 630 -s 45x45}${image [D2I] -p 22,  765 -s 34x34}${image [D3I] -p 82,  765 -s 34x34}${image [D4I] -p 141, 765 -s 34x34}${image [D5I] -p 198, 765 -s 34x34}
${voffset 20}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.5}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}
${voffset -1}${goto 24}${font  DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D2P]${goto  84}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D3P]${goto 144}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D4P]${goto 201}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D5P]${font}
```

Everything is working well but I need a couple of pointers:

The daily forcast next to the current temp (in orange) could someone give me pointers on where to set the coordinates for the graphic.  I think I've moved every graphic in the setup, but can't seem to find this one??

Also I am getting an error on load when I run conky & in the terminal:

```
Conky: '/home/tom/.conkyrc' modified, reloading...
Conky: failed to handle template 'template3' with args '(null)'
Conky: failed to handle template 'template2' with args '(null)'
Conky: failed to handle template 'template5' with args '(null)'
Conky: failed to handle template 'template2' with args '(null)'

Conky: drawing to created window (0x1600002)

```

I have searched the .conkyrc and the only references to these templates are commented out.  Should I worry about this?

Thanks again for all the help from the forum!  What a great resource! :p

I doubt that I have the knowledge to create the wiki paramvir suggested, but would gladly contribute my time to supporting the effort in some way?

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-12-08
First of all, an explanation of my absence is probably in order.  :)

Oct-Mar is always an extremely busy period in my 'real' life. Example:  Without boring you with too many gruesome details (I'm sure everyone else is busy too) I worked a 32-hour day, last Wednesday (went to work Wednesday morning, left work Thursday afternoon). I passed-out for 8 hours at the house, then worked 24-hours on Friday (went to work Friday morning, left work Saturday morning).

Not complaining, mind you.  I chose this crazy life of chaos and mayhem -- wouldn't have it any other way.  I'm just offering this as an apologia (defense of inaction).

In Mar-Oct my 'real' life is quite boring, with a few business trips around the USA, sprinkled with various legal actions and proceedings, contract compliance management, and so forth, and so on.  My only thrill is trying to navigate airport concourses in a timely manner, and getting the WIFI to work in convention halls and hotel rooms.  LoL! 

Anyway, let's light_this_candle...  ;)  
 
> **paramvir said:**
> re @VinDSL Templates

OK fine - I can handle the documentation - BUT - at least you guys or @VinDSL can send me the 4 templates [latest].

Are these the correct set in the fonts.zip - I have them so please por favor confirm.
Hi Paramvir,

I like your idea(s) about a separate Wiki page.  I actually started doing that a while back, when 42dorian was exiled from the forums.  But, alas, it remains an unfulfilled desire.  Now that there is some interest, and some potentially active participants, maybe this is the time to proceed with it.  :)

I'm up for anything you want to do -- add a section(s) to ConkyWX, or whatever. Up to you, my man. You're the mover_and_shaker of the group, with seemingly boundless energy and unlimited abilities!  My only interest is having a good looking Conky setup , and sharing it with other ppl, in a form that they can understand.

I have a different way of coding, as do you.  Anything we can do to explain our methods and madness will be a plus, IMO.

I'm on my 'new' Dell ATG D630 laptop right now -- soon to be my new 'road warrior'.  I installed Conky/ConkyWX, of course.  So, let me do a quick listing of the files in my '.conky/fonts' folder...

```
About
Ahem.ttf
Arrows.ttf
cutouts.ttf
DroidSans-Bold.ttf
DroidSansFallback.ttf
DroidSansJapanese.ttf
DroidSansMono.ttf
DroidSans.ttf
DroidSerif-BoldItalic.ttf
DroidSerif-Bold.ttf
DroidSerif-Italic.ttf
DroidSerif-Regular.ttf
KR A Round.ttf
monof55.ttf
monof56.ttf
MoonPhases.ttf
openlogos.ttf
PIZZADUDEBULLETS.ttf
Radiofbi.ttf
Radiofb.ttf
Radiofc.ttf
Radiofi.ttf
Radiof.ttf
STYLBCC_.TTF
Ubuntu-BI.ttf
Ubuntu-B.ttf
Ubuntu-LI.ttf
Ubuntu-L.ttf
Ubuntu-MI.ttf
Ubuntu-M.ttf
Ubuntu-RI.ttf
Ubuntu-R.ttf
UbuntuTitleBold.ttf
UbuntuTitleRegular.ttf
Ubuntu-Title.ttf
VinDSL_Conky_Fonts.zip
VinDSL_Font_List.txt
Weather.ttf
```

Okay, there she blows...

We'll need to compare it to your list.

There has always been an Ubuntu Bold font missing, in the packages that users have compiled.  It's the font I use in the header, for the Ubuntu version.  So, this is probably missing from your list.  I don't know if this bold Ubuntu title font is even available online any longer, but I do have it included in the zip file, listed above.

That bold title font would be a good addition to the Wiki, yes?  A reason for ppl to visit it...  :)

Alrighty, then... Let me see if I can find the 'empty' Wiki nest.  If I remember correctly, I started adding 42dorian's original post to the Wiki e.g. converting it from vBulletin format to Wiki format (where it would be editable/update-able).  I *think* I started working on it, saved it, but never completed it nor published it.

I'll go track it down, and see where it stands. Maybe you can take a look at it, and see if it suits the need(s).  If not, I can always add a Wiki section to VinDSL.com (when I get the time).  I'm running it on MediaWiki (same software Wikipedia uses), but I would prefer to use the Ubuntu Wiki.  I'm already getting too much traffic on my production server, in Atlanta, and I would need to add Squid to MediaWiki, to avoid crashing the whole node like a house_of_cards.  Right now, I'm just using MediaWiki to serve pics, so caching isn't required.  ;)

BBL with the URI...

**EDIT**

[https://wiki.ubuntu.com/VinDSL](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/VinDSL)

Blank canvas to play with...  :D

I'm up for anything you want to do -- add a section(s) to ConkyWX, or whatever. Up to you, my man. You're the mover_and_shaker of the group, with seemingly boundless energy and unlimited abilities!  My only interest is having a good looking Conky setup , and sharing it with other ppl, in a form that they can understand.

I have a different way of coding, as do you.  Anything we can do to explain our methods and madness will be a plus, IMO.

I'm on my 'new' Dell ATG D630 laptop right now -- soon to be my new 'road warrior'.  I installed Conky/ConkyWX, of course.  So, let me do a quick listing of the files in my '.conky/fonts' folder...

```

Arrows.ttf
MoonPhases.ttf
[B] UbuntuTitleBold.ttf
UbuntuTitleRegular.ttf[/B]
Weather.ttf

```

### Post by VinDSL on 2013-12-17
Hi Cavsfan,

Not sure about the weather icon set.  Generally speaking, it all depends on whether they're included in ConkyWX, or you're trying to add them to ConkyWX.  If they're part of the ConkyWX, it should be a simple matter.  If you're trying to add them to ConkyWX, not so easy (if memory serves).

On truncating 'Full Moon' -> 'Full' (my preference), and turning 'Am/Pm' -> 'AM/PM', this is how I handled it:

```
<SNIP>

File path: "/home/username/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf"

<SNIP>

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process these final template changes before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the changes in single quotes as shown
template_cleanup=( 
    'Am:${offset -2}AM'
    'Pm:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:Calm'
    'Calm mph:Calm'
    'Calm:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Mph:mph'
    'Km/h:km/h'
    '°F:°'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full Moon:${offset 5}Full${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning.*:${offset -5}Waning'
    'Waxing.*:${offset -5}Waxing'
  )

<SNIP>

```

Mind you, this is all preliminary.  I haven't had a chance to test practically anything, yet.

I'm doing a fresh install of Peppermint 4 Re-Spin, on my 'new' (new to me) Dell Latitude ATG D630, and I got to the point where it was time to install Conky/ConkyWX.

I'll need to go through all of the scripts and make sure they work with ConkyWX 1.0.0-1 -- spacing, alignment -- that sort of stuff.

---

Hi VinDSL and Thanks for your quick response. I know that you have been very busy.
I used the above to change my Am/Pm to AM/PM and that worked great. :)

But, I did like you had done and stripped the time off of the Full Moon and now "Full Moon" displays fine without a need to make it just "Full".

Yet I am still trying to use that icon set. I don't think I am using any of the ones in my picture above. 

I do see this in /etc/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh:
[PHP]set_variables () {
    # var_messages
    set_config

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Data constants
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    imagepath="$cwxpath/images"

    case $wx_iconset in
    1)    wp1=01    ;;
    2)    wp1=02    ;;
    3)    wp1=03    ;;
    4)    wp1=04    ;;
    *)    wp1=01    ;;
    esac

    iwxpath="$imagepath/wxicons-$wp1"
    imoonpath="$imagepath/moonicons" [/PHP]

I'd like to hit that wp1=01 which is the image set I mentioned. Then I would know how to change it to any of them, but I do not see where that variable gets passed in from. 

I've enjoyed stretching the mind over this for most of the day and tying to figure it out myself but, I am at a loss for where that one variable comes in from.
I have not tried to add anything to ConkyWX, so this should be included. I am apparently not using that parameter at this time.

Thanks as always for your time. :)

### Post by paramvir on 2013-12-18
> **Cavsfan said:**

Hello there ;)

This has mention in many places - even in my blog see the conkywx config file also and posts [**#1152**]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&p=12820336#post12820336") and [**#1253**]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&p=12865899#post12865899") 

```

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Font to Image - using ImageMagick
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## convert ConkyWeather font to 3D images - state the colour here.
## Use only for Image Templates
[COLOR=#0000ff][B]## COMMENT these options for using in font templates
## COMMENT these options for using regular weather images in image templates
[/B][/COLOR]## Needs Imagemagick installed
## to see List of Color Names visit http://www.imagemagick.org/script/color.php
## Colour can be stated in any of the following formats - enclosed in single quotes
## example for colour *lime* - 'lime' '#0f0' '#00ff00' 'rgb(0,255,0)' 'rgb(0,100%,0)'
fti_now='#fed577'
fti_wind='DarkSeaGreen1'
fti_fct='lime'

```


You are not alone - even I do not read stuff most of the time - so no worries ;-)

the next version of conkywx will be version 2.0 !!!

you will be able to do the following - besides others :
[LIST]
[*]give a path to your set of weather images - the naming convention will have to follow conkywx format. 
[*]new windrose images and also ability to give path to your own set [since you do not want to share your set with us mere mortals]  - ehh :D 
[*]choose the data units you want to download in - [IM] or [SI] 
[*]Unicode compliance - required for supporting languages. 
[*]so many languages to choose from ... 
[*]every system comes pre-installed with iconv - if not you will need to install it - as part of libiconv i think - you will need to find for your version. 
[*]fribidi - if you use right to left language [ farsi arabic etc ] - probably already installed if you use those linguas as your locale - or on Ubuntu it is known as libfribidi-bin 
[*]and much more ... 
[/LIST]

Most of all I cannot change or install - common human behaviour lol ha ha ha ha ha - the only time and I say again - the only time you realise what **RTFM** is when you write stuff and no one reads it lol

cheers

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2013-12-18
> **paramvir said:**
> Yes you can use the wx_iconset if you comment the fti_* options - try it :)

Finally I get it. Comment out the fti_* colors and use this in **~/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf**.

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Choose weather icon set
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Enable icon set: org 12 by bharathp666 (Paramvir Likhari remix)
## [2] Enable icon set: origin unknown
## [3] Enable icon set: tick weather icons by xiao4
## [4] Enable icon set: Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
wx_iconset=2
```

That was set to 1 by me I guess. As I believe you had it set to 2 by default.
I had tried setting that to 2 but could not figure out why it didn't change. I just had to comment out the colors. Thanks for the brain exercise! :) I like how you do not simply give me the answers. :)

[ATTACH=CONFIG]248714[/ATTACH]

Now to move the sun down a few pixels and it will be perfect. Luckily I know how to do that. Learning is always good. We can't have closed minds that is for sure. ;)
Thanks!

Soon I have everything all out of wack. So I just copy everything from Trusty into Mint and everything is worse. I procede to align everything once again.
This time making sure I work from the top down because if you make changes at the bottom and work up everything below needs tweaked again.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2013-12-19
Precise was a piece of cake. Just commenting out the the 3 lines and setting **wx_iconset=2** in **~/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf**. It was 1 so that must be the default.
This picture illustrates why **VinDSL**'s conky with **paramvir**'s weather is the absolute ultimate. You can surf the internet or whatever and still see what is going on at the right side of the screen.
Without the Unity bar of course which uses up another inch or so on the left.

[[img]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2013-12-191.png[/img]](http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2013-12-191.php)

Pretty sweet once again! I thought about changing the colors but then I'd have to change them every time I change the wallpaper and I do that a lot. I think I'll just keep the grey and white colors that **VinDSL** used.
It looks like your moon status is being truncated. In **~/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf** you could put this to fix it.
```

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Process the final template before showtime
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this variable Block if not required
template_cleanup=(
    'Am:${offset -2}AM'
    'Pm:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    '[COLOR=#ff0000]Waning Gibbous[/COLOR]:${offset -3}[COLOR=#ff0000]Waning[/COLOR]'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
)
```

That should help I think.

---

### Post by paramvir on 2013-12-21
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Pretty sweet once again! I thought about changing the colors but then I'd have to change them every time I change the wallpaper and I do that a lot. I think I'll just keep the grey and white colors that **VinDSL** used.
It looks like your moon status is being truncated. In **~/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf** you could put this to fix it.
```

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Process the final template before showtime
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this variable Block if not required
template_cleanup=(
    'Am:${offset -2}AM'
    'Pm:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    '[COLOR=#ff0000]Waning Gibbous[/COLOR]:${offset -3}[COLOR=#ff0000]Waning[/COLOR]'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
)
```

That should help I think.

Alloha :D

What you are doing is fine !!! as you require those offsets for the moon condition.

Element MP3 handles those conditions - like I said you have those offsets so - what you are doing is correct - but - maybe using ${alignc somevalue}[MP3] could work - we are using the moon's illumination centred over the moon isn't it :p - just a thought...

MP3 gives the moon phase as following ( this should make into the wiki huaahahaha )
```

    First Quarter  -> First Qtr
    Full Moon      -> Full Moon
    New Moon       -> New Moon
    Last Quarter   -> Last Qtr
    Waning Crescent-> Waning
    Waning Gibbous -> Waning
    Waxing Crescent-> Waxing
    Waxing Gibbous -> Waxing

```

End of the day - what ever makes you happy ;)

cheers

---

Buongiorno :)

You obviously know a lot more than I do but I had "Full Moon" displaying as "Full Moo". Until VinDSL pointed me to the above code. I also had to strip the time off of the Full Moon date as the status "Waning" etc. was displayed over top of the time. This might only affect VinDSL's conky which displays on the right side of the screen.
I measure it approximately 250 pixels wide.

I am good with seeing Full versus Full Moon. :)

I tried to make it a little wider but gave up when I realized it was above my skill set and above my pay grade. ;) I would not want to change a thing about it I love it just like it is!

But, thanks for pointing out the MP3 element. Many people could benefit from using that.

Thanks! :)

### Post by sparker256 on 2014-01-09
This will get your conky back untill there is a better solution.

in /usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh

line 444 

Change from 

		get_data_file

to

#		get_data_file

This will prevent it from looking for weather underground untill this is resolved.

You will need to be root.

Bill

Hey foodoo man ;)

Hi Sean - Sorry to hear about the break in !!! 

there was never a 0.9.7 version - use 1.0.0 - Version ONE onwards everything was sorted internally in perl - so never needed to write to text file as such. For basics the older config file will work but to add newer goodies you need to update the conkywx.conf to the latest.

See post [#1253]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&p=12865899#post12865899")   which has the config file for version 1.0.0 and modifications to the weather part of the VinDSL template.

let me know how it goes...

I noticed in an older version that two "Magellan" text files were put in the /tmp folder but in the latest version no "Magellan" files,
can you tell me if i'm missing a template and/or a wrong call-up ?

I'm using the old style of call-up in my .conkyrc looks like this:

${execpi 900 conkywx -c /etc/conkywx/conkywx.conf -t  /usr/share/conkywx/examples/wx-wu-vindsl-template.sh}${font}

Not sure if this will help but this is out of my ~/.conkyrc.vindsl file:

```
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} \
${color2}Alert: ${if_match ${execi 60 expr length "$(cat ${template7})"}>=32}\
${color5}${scroll 30 10 ${execi 60 cat ${template7}}}${else}\
${color5}${execi 60 cat ${template7}}${endif}${endif} ${color6}${hr 2}${font}${color8}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}

```

[ATTACH=CONFIG]249359[/ATTACH]

Yes, the template 7 is the alert and the way it is in the example I posted is how to get scrolling alerts. Parmavir explained that to me; it took a while for me to "get it". :lol:

The folder in the tmp directory is the same name for everyone with the username being the only difference.
Mine is named **/tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39 **.

I wish I could have some German bier! My son is in the Army stationed over there and he loves many things about that country, the bier being one of them. :)

### Post by paramvir on 2014-01-10
> **foodoo said:**
> 

time for some German "Bier" haha :-)

my question is do you have two text files called "Magellan ?
these two files contain the astro info.

oh the penny has just dropped the template 7 is for the conkywx Alert :-)

Yes "Bier" is goot for health lol

I do not output the Magellan data to a file - it is captured into hash things and played around. So mostly there has been no need to output data to files.

But - this is what you will need to do : In the **/usr/share/conkywx/lib/parsewx.pl** look for the **sub get_magellan**, look for **foreach(@magellan_tday)** loop - see the code:

```

    [COLOR=#0000cd]**open (out_data, '>', "$temppath/magellan") or die "$LMsg{no_open_file} magellan: save $!";**[/COLOR]
    foreach(@magellan_tday) {
        chomp;
        $_ =~ s/"//g;
        next if ($_ =~ /^\s*$|^#/);
        my ($var, $value) = split(/\s*=\s*/, $_, 2);
        $Mag_2day{$var} = $value;
        **[COLOR=#0000cd]print out_data "$var=\"$value\"\n";[/COLOR]**
    }
    **[COLOR=#0000cd]close (out_data);[/COLOR]**


```

You will need to insert the blue text into the file at these locations. This will give you the same output as you had with earlier versions of conkywx - There were 2 files as Magellan computes for today and tomorrow - my guess is you will require data for present day.

have a blast and let me know if this helps :lol:

cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2014-01-11
> **foodoo said:**
> Hi Guys thanks for your help,

I got the "magellan" file showing in the /tmp folder now :-)
got one more question haha i'm not so good with editing the ouput of sed calls

this is my call:
${execpi 1800 sed -n 1p /tmp/wx-foo-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/magellan}

and this is the output:
sun_rise="08:07"
I would like to edit this show just to show the time and not the rest.

any ideas Seany

Do something like this

```

${execpi 1800 sed -n 1p /tmp/wx-foo-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/magellan | sed 's/.*=//; s/"//g;' }

```


> **foodoo said:**
> Funny i've got the moon displaying upside down haha

If you have Dynamic_Moon enabled in the config file - you will see a moon image that spins - tries to give an actual representation of how the moon would look outside when visible :D at any time. The spin also changes if you are on the southern hemisphere.

If not then the moon is displayed at an angle not the traditional vertical split.

cheers

### Post by foodoo on 2014-01-11
Hi Paramvir,

Thanks for the tips been messing with this for a few hours, tried the sed editing trick in terminal works perfectly then tried it in the .Lua script but
the script has an epic fail and spits out an error something like cant "draw_fig" blah blah so in my madness i came up with this idea in my conky loader.

I know you will laugh because there must be a way of pushing this into one text file instead of four haha:

#!/bin/bash
( sleep 20 && conky /home/foo/.conkyrc ) &
( sleep 30 && conky -c /home/foo/scripts/.conkyrc_thermometer ) &
( sleep 40 &&
sed -n 1p /tmp/wx-foo-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/magellan | sed 's/.*=//; s/"//g;' >> /tmp/sunrise.txt &&
sed -n 3p /tmp/wx-foo-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/magellan | sed 's/.*=//; s/"//g;' >> /tmp/sunset.txt &&
sed -n 10p /tmp/wx-foo-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/magellan | sed 's/.*=//; s/"//g;' >> /tmp/moonrise.txt &&
sed -n 12p /tmp/wx-foo-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/magellan | sed 's/.*=//; s/"//g;' >> /tmp/moonset.txt ) &
( sleep 60 && conky -c /home/foo/scripts/.conkyrc_sun_moon ) &
exit

and then it was easy to call from the new text files from the .lua like this:

--get sunrise
sunrise=conky_parse("${execi 1800 sed -n 1p /tmp/sunrise.txt}")
--get sunset
sunset=conky_parse("${execi 1800 sed -n 1p /tmp/sunset.txt}")
--get moonrise
moonrise=conky_parse("${execi 1800 sed -n 1p /tmp/moonrise.txt}")
--get moonset
moonset=conky_parse("${execi 1800 sed -n 1p /tmp/moonset.txt}")

and bingo! got what i wanted :-)

Thanks mate i think i've earnt a beer haha!
