### Post by jdallara on 2014-01-25
Hi Sean,

  Yes it is the weather config script.  It gets called explicitly from .conkyrc.  Its the only place that fetch is defined.  The comments say to make sure the duration is less than the fetch duration in the conky script, but there isn't anything defined in any of those scripts.  I just want to make sure that changing this value isn't going to break something else.

```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Wund Weather Config File | VinDSL | rev. 12-11-19 08:53 | ver. 0.7.9
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# THIS PROGRAM IS FOR PERSONAL AND PRIVATE USE ONLY
# CONTACT WUNDERGROUND.COM FOR PROFESSIONAL SOLUTIONS
# CAVEAT: No guarantee that this program will work on all platforms,
# Use At Your Own Risk.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# File path: "/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf"
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Instructions/examples: "/usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx.conf"
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use XML data from wunderground
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: If enabled, you must supply the XML ID of the station (below)
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_xml=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# XML ID
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Only needed, if you "use_xml=1" (above)
# # Scottsdale, AZ, Equestrian Manor
# xml_ID=KAZSCOTT39
# # Chandler, AZ, Cooper and Frye
# xml_ID=KAZCHAND36
# # Fairbanks, AK, Skyflight Airstrip
# xml_ID=KAKFAIRB35
# # Colville, WA, Colville Mountain
# xml_ID=KWAEVANS3
# # Ripon, CA, North Ripon
xml_ID=KCARIPON3

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Location settings
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# # Scottsdale, Arizona, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:85250.1.99999"
# # Chandler, Arizona, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:85224.1.99999"
# # Fairbanks, Alaska, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:99701.1.99999"
# # Colville, Washington, General Vicinity
Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:95366.1.99999"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Units to use and Downloaded
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enter the units to get and use, centigrade or Fahrenheit 
# [SI] metric
# [IM] imperial/english
Units_to_Use=IM
Units_downloaded=IM

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Show units, use short date, show notification
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] disable
# [1] enable
Units_show=0        # If you want to see units like km/h, mph, etc
use_short_date=1    # 'Sunday, 27' will be shortened to 'Sun 27'
show_notification=1    # Every update will display a popup notification

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Path/image to background image
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
path_to_background_image="$cwxpath/images/bgrounds"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  How do you want the present condition to look
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Capitalize The First Letter Of All Words, In The Present Condition
# [1] All UPPERCASE
# [2] all lowercase
# [3] Capitilize the first letter/first word only, in the present condition
pc_what_case=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use blacklist to manipulate Present Condition text
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_pcondition_blacklist=1    

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Words you want to add to/remove from Present Condition text
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
pcondition_blacklist=(
     Freezing
     pellet
     pellets
     Precipitation
     Small
    "and:+"
    "heavy:hvy"
    "light:lt"
    "Low Drifting"
    "Patches of"
    "Severe:svr"
    "Thunderstorms:t-storms"
    "Thunderstorm:t-storm"
    "Widespread:ws"
    "with:+"
  )

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process the final template before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_template_cleanup=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process these final template changes before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the changes in single quotes as shown
template_cleanup=( 
    'AM:${offset -2}AM'
    'PM:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waning Gibbous:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
  )

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use UPPERCASE or lowercase AM PM or am pm for 12 hour time format
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] ALL UPPERCASE (EXAMPLE: 03:00 PM)
# [1] all lowercase (example: 03:00 pm)
# [2] Hide am pm (example: 03:00)
# [3] all lowercase with no padding/spacing (example: 3:00pm)
# [4] Use custom time format. State the format in custom_time_format
# NOTE: This will only work if Time_format=12 (below)
show_lowercase_ampm=4

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Set custom time format - see man date in a terminal to get more options
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the time format in single quotes as shown
custom_time_format='+%l:%M %p'

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Time format to use 12 or 24 hour format
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [12] AM or PM (example: 03:00 PM)
# [24] 24 hour format (example: 15:00)
Time_format=12

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Show deg symbol in forecast temps
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Hide all deg symbols (example: 95/63)
# [1] Show all deg symbols (example: 95°/63°)
# [2] Show all deg symbols, depending on Unit_to_Use (metric: 35°/17°) (imperial
: 95°/63°)
# [3] Show deg symbol ONLY, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°)
# [4] Show deg symbol AND unit, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°F)
show_degsym_fc_temp=3

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Characters to use for forecast trends
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rising trend arrow
risingtrend='c'
#
# Falling trend arrow
fallingtrend='d'
#
# Steady trend arrow
steadytrend='Q'

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Choose weather icon set
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [1] Enable icon set: org 12 by bharathp666 (Paramvir Likhari remix)
# [2] Enable icon set: origin unknown
# [3] Enable icon set: tick weather icons by xiao4
# [4] Enable icon set: Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
wx_iconset=2

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Moon font characters with or without outline ring
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Default was formerly "simple" e.g. no outline ring
# [0] Disable outline and use simple
# [1] Use outline and disable simple
moon_outline=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Allow Conkywx to check for latest version release
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Never check for a new version of Conkywx
# [1] Check for a new version, every 24 hours
enable_new_version_check=1

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Today date format - used in last fetch
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
today_date_format="%A %b, %d %Y"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimum time the script allows the wesite to be polled
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: This setting MUST BE LESS THAN the fetch frequency in your Conky script.
#
# Uncomment for Conky scripts that fetch data every 900 seconds (15 minutes).
minimum_fetch_time=870
#
# Uncomment for testing purposes. Fetches fresh data every time Conky is restart
ed.
# minimum_fetch_time=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# --- DO NOT TOUCH SECTION ---
# URLs used in this program
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
main_url="http://www.wunderground.com/global/stations/"
xml_url="http://api.wunderground.com/weatherstation/WXCurrentObXML.asp?ID="
```

Thanks,

Jon.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-01-26
I was wondering why my cpu/gpu core temps weren't updating very frequently. I noticed they rarely ever change even when recordmydesktop was using up 100% of one of my cores.

Figured this out:
The update_interval controls the quickest anything can check and so I set the cpu/gpu ${execi to 2 to fetch the temperature every 2 seconds. 
I like that a lot better. :) 

.conkyrc.vindsl:
```
####
## Update interval in seconds.
#
update_interval 2.0

...

##################################
##         TEMPERATURES         ##
##################################
${voffset 15}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}CPU/GPU TEMPERATURES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 1 Temp: $alignr${execi [COLOR=#ff0000]2[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 2 Temp: $alignr${execi [COLOR=#ff0000]2[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 3 Temp: $alignr${execi [COLOR=#ff0000]2[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 4 Temp: $alignr${execi [COLOR=#ff0000]2[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${execi [COLOR=#ff0000]2[/COLOR] nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F


```

### Post by VinDSL on 2014-02-02
> **paramvir said:**
> OK here is an update on lua - yesss - VinDSL template has driven me into the deep waters of lua. :D
Heh!  Lua is interesting, isn't it?!?!?  

IMO, Lua should be used as you would salt n' pepper (e.g. food seasoning) -- a little bit goes a long way -- but, that's just me.  ;)

As an aside, I finally installed Conkywx on my Ubu +1 desktop box, today...

```
vindsl@Zuul:~$ apt-cache policy conkywx
conkywx:
  Installed: 1.0.0-1
  Candidate: 1.0.0-1
  Version table:
 *** 1.0.0-1 0
        100 /var/lib/dpkg/status
vindsl@Zuul:~$
```

I've been putting off doing a 'fresh install' of 'Trusty'.  Actually, I've been putting off doing a fresh install since 2011.  LoL!  :D

I've always been able to put 'Humpty Dumpty' back together again, between releases, but alas I finally bit the bullet and wiped everything except '/home'. 

This is the last machine in my stable to get Conkywx 1.x.  Great job, Paramvir!  Worked on all of my portables, and the +1 test box.

Look... a blank canvas :mrgreen:


[INDENT][[img]http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-2-feb-2014-2(650x520).png[/img]]("http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-2-feb-2014-2.png")[/INDENT]


Don't ask me why, but I decided to add the GPU temp to the (nVidia) graphic card description -- only to find out there's a bug when calling 'nvidia-settings' from CLI.  

This error message was reproduced every 2 seconds in the Conky output (when opening Conky from a terminal) and dragging the whole machine down...

```
ERROR: Error querying target relations
ERROR: Error querying target relations
ERROR: Error querying target relations
ERROR: Error querying target relations
ERROR: Error querying target relations
ERROR: Error querying target relations
ERROR: Error querying target relations
ERROR: Error querying target relations
```

It's easily reproducible...

```
vindsl@Zuul:~$ nvidia-settings -q gpucoretemp -t | awk '{s+=$1}END{print s/NR}' RS=" "

ERROR: Error querying target relations

33
vindsl@Zuul:~$ 

```

After scratching my head for awhile, I *think* I discovered an undocumented feature in 'nvidia-settings' (not mentioned in --help / haven't checked MAN)...

```
--verbose=**[COLOR="#FF0000"]none[/COLOR]**
```

Adding it to the command gets rid of the error output...

```
vindsl@Zuul:~$ nvidia-settings -q gpucoretemp -t **[COLOR="#FF0000"]--verbose=none[/COLOR]** | awk '{s+=$1}END{print s/NR}' RS=" "
33
vindsl@Zuul:~$
```

I took the nice, clean output and added it to Conky, like so...

```
<SNIP>


##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################


<SNIP>


####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color3}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT**[COLOR="#FF0000"]${offset 3}(${offset 2}${execpi 2 nvidia-settings -q gpucoretemp -t --verbose=none | awk '{s+=$1}END{print s/NR}' RS=" "}°${offset 1})[/COLOR]**${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-20'}${font}


<SNIP>
```

You can see the result in the screenie above, if you're interested.  ;)

I don't know if others are suffering from this (assumed) 'nvidia-settings' bug, but maybe this hack will work for them, too.

Onward and upward!

**EDIT**

Bwahahahaha!  I need to practice my reading comprehension...  :D

>   -V VERBOSE, --verbose=VERBOSE
      Controls how much information is printed.  Valid values are **[COLOR="#FF0000"]'none' (do not print status messages)[/COLOR]**,
      'errors' (print error messages), 'deprecations' (print error and deprecation messages), 'warnings'
      (print error, deprecation, and warning messages), and 'all' (print error, deprecation, warning and
      other informational messages).  By default, 'deprecations' is set..

There are many methods available to monitor the GPU temp.  I tried some of the conventional way(s), but kept getting two numbers.

For instance:

```
vindsl@Zuul:~$ nvidia-settings -t -q GPUCoreTemp

ERROR: Error querying target relations

33
33
vindsl@Zuul:~$

```

ERROR: Error querying target relations

33
33
vindsl@Zuul:~$

I suppose this is why I'm seeing two numbers...

```
vindsl@Zuul:~$ nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp

ERROR: Error querying target relations


  Attribute 'GPUCoreTemp' (Zuul:0.0): 32.
    'GPUCoreTemp' is an integer attribute.
    'GPUCoreTemp' is a read-only attribute.
    'GPUCoreTemp' can use the following target types: X Screen, GPU.
  Attribute 'GPUCoreTemp' (Zuul:0[gpu:0]): 32.
    'GPUCoreTemp' is an integer attribute.
    'GPUCoreTemp' is a read-only attribute.
    'GPUCoreTemp' can use the following target types: X Screen, GPU.

vindsl@Zuul:~$
```

The results are probably hardware-dependent.  That's my best *guess*.

BTW, I'm not suggesting that anyone add this to their script.  I'm just toying with the idea -- and 'speaking' out loud...  ;)

### Post by PotatoHead007 on 2014-02-03
hey VinDSL :P I really like your scripts, but have run into a wall. I made a post about it [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2203004](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2203004) could you please address it? Or must i post here?

### Post by foodoo on 2014-02-03
Hello PotatoHead007,

Take a look @  [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=40](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=40)    post #400
this will show you how to display all 4 cores in the bar graph correctly :-)

Many thanks Sean

---

### Post by foodoo on 2014-02-03
Hi vinDSL,

I too had this problem and used:   [COLOR=#000000]nvidia-settings -q gpucoretemp | grep '):'| cut -c 43-44[/COLOR][COLOR=#ff0000] | sed '/^$/d'[/COLOR]

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=138](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=138)     post#1372

Cheers Sean

PS Before I upgraded to Ubuntu 13.10 I didn't have this problem, now I've changed to Ubuntu-Gnome 13.10
I had to play with the "cut" values.

foo@foo:~$ nvidia-settings -q gpucoretemp | grep '):'| cut -c 43-44 | sed '/^$/d'
64
foo@foo:~$

${voffset -10}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color6}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 15}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color0}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color0}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -70}${goto 35}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color7}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${voffset -60}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
# ${voffset -68}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 1 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
#${if_running rhythmbox}
#${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt | head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 2 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running rhythmbox}
${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##    BANSHEE (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running banshee}
${voffset -26}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}BANSHEE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -10}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f1- -d " "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##   GUAYADEQUE (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running guayadeque}
${voffset -33}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}GUAYADEQUE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}
${voffset -55}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     Audaciuos                ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -70}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}$font$
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${alignr}${audacious_status}${endif}
```

#################################################
## VinDSL | rev. 13-10-21 12:09 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
#################################################
## IMPERIAL WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 1.0.0 ##
#################################################
${voffset 0}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color6}${alignc -5}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -15}${offset 2}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.4}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}
${voffset -40}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.75}${color6}[D1T]${font}
${voffset 0}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.3}${voffset -4}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -38}${goto 188}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color6}I${font}
${voffset 6}${font Ubuntu:size=22.75}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
${voffset 7}${alignc 3}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
${voffset 8}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 9}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 9}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 9}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 9}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FM]${font}${voffset 9}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}U${offset 1}V${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[UV]${font}
${if_existing [BI]}${voffset -55}${image [BI] -p 10, 745 -s 60x60}${else}${voffset -70}${goto 16}${font ConkyWindNESW:size=41.6}${color6}[BF]${font}${endif}
${voffset -38}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
${voffset 28}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS]${voffset 1}${goto 210}[MP]${font}
${voffset 5}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}${goto 30}Temp${goto 120}Baro${goto 210}Dew
${voffset -1}${image [GT] -p 17, 774 -s  50x10}${image [GP] -p 107, 774 -s  50x10}${image [GD] -p 190, 774 -s  50x10}
${voffset 9}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
${image [PIC] -p 10, 610 -s 46x46}${image [MIC] -p 195, 685 -s 45x45}${image [D2I] -p 22, 799 -s 34x34}${image [D3I] -p 82, 804 -s 34x34}${image [D4I] -p 141, 799 -s 34x34}${image [D5I] -p 198, 799 -s 34x34}
${voffset 20}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.5}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}

and finally **vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf:**
```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Wund Weather Config File | VinDSL | rev. 12-11-19 08:53 | ver. 0.7.9
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# THIS PROGRAM IS FOR PERSONAL AND PRIVATE USE ONLY
# CONTACT WUNDERGROUND.COM FOR PROFESSIONAL SOLUTIONS
# CAVEAT: No guarantee that this program will work on all platforms,
# Use At Your Own Risk.


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# File path: "/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf"
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Instructions/examples: "/usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx.conf"
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use XML data from wunderground
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: If enabled, you must supply the XML ID of the station (below)
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_xml=1


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# XML ID
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Only needed, if you "use_xml=1" (above)
# # Scottsdale, AZ, Equestrian Manor
# xml_ID=KAZSCOTT39
# # Chandler, AZ, Cooper and Frye
# xml_ID=KAZCHAND36
# # Fairbanks, AK, Skyflight Airstrip
# xml_ID=KAKFAIRB35
# # Colville, WA, Colville Mountain
# xml_ID=KWAEVANS3
# # Frisco, TX
xml_ID=KTXDALLA25


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Location settings
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# # Scottsdale, Arizona, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:85250.1.99999"
# # Chandler, Arizona, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:85224.1.99999"
# # Fairbanks, Alaska, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:99701.1.99999"
# # Colville, Washington, General Vicinity
# Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:99114.1.99999"
# # Frisco, TX, General Vicinity
Location="http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:75033.1.99999"


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Units to use and Downloaded
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enter the units to get and use, centigrade or Fahrenheit 
# [SI] metric
# [IM] imperial/english
Units_to_Use=IM
Units_downloaded=IM


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Show units, use short date, show notification
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] disable
# [1] enable
Units_show=0        # If you want to see units like km/h, mph, etc
use_short_date=1    # 'Sunday, 27' will be shortened to 'Sun 27'
show_notification=1    # Every update will display a popup notification


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Path/image to background image
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
path_to_background_image="$cwxpath/images/bgrounds"


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  How do you want the present condition to look
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Capitalize The First Letter Of All Words, In The Present Condition
# [1] All UPPERCASE
# [2] all lowercase
# [3] Capitilize the first letter/first word only, in the present condition
pc_what_case=0


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use blacklist to manipulate Present Condition text
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_pcondition_blacklist=1    


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Words you want to add to/remove from Present Condition text
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
pcondition_blacklist=(
     Freezing
     pellet
     pellets
     Precipitation
     Small
    "and:+"
    "heavy:hvy"
    "light:lt"
    "Low Drifting"
    "Patches of"
    "Severe:svr"
    "Thunderstorms:t-storms"
    "Thunderstorm:t-storms"
    "Widespread:ws"
    "with:+"
  )


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process the final template before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] no
# [1] yes
use_template_cleanup=1


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process these final template changes before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the changes in single quotes as shown
template_cleanup=( 
    'AM:${offset -2}AM'
    'PM:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waning Gibbous:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
  )


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use UPPERCASE or lowercase AM PM or am pm for 12 hour time format
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] ALL UPPERCASE (EXAMPLE: 03:00 PM)
# [1] all lowercase (example: 03:00 pm)
# [2] Hide am pm (example: 03:00)
# [3] all lowercase with no padding/spacing (example: 3:00pm)
# [4] Use custom time format. State the format in custom_time_format
# NOTE: This will only work if Time_format=12 (below)
show_lowercase_ampm=4


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Set custom time format - see man date in a terminal to get more options
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the time format in single quotes as shown
custom_time_format='+%l:%M %p'


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Time format to use 12 or 24 hour format
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [12] AM or PM (example: 03:00 PM)
# [24] 24 hour format (example: 15:00)
Time_format=12


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Show deg symbol in forecast temps
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Hide all deg symbols (example: 95/63)
# [1] Show all deg symbols (example: 95°/63°)
# [2] Show all deg symbols, depending on Unit_to_Use (metric: 35°/17°) (imperial: 95°/63°)
# [3] Show deg symbol ONLY, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°)
# [4] Show deg symbol AND unit, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°F)
show_degsym_fc_temp=2


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Characters to use for forecast trends
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rising trend arrow
risingtrend='c'
#
# Falling trend arrow
fallingtrend='d'
#
# Steady trend arrow
steadytrend='Q'


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Choose weather icon set
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [1] Enable icon set: org 12 by bharathp666 (Paramvir Likhari remix)
# [2] Enable icon set: origin unknown
# [3] Enable icon set: tick weather icons by xiao4
# [4] Enable icon set: Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
wx_iconset=2


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Moon font characters with or without outline ring
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Default was formerly "simple" e.g. no outline ring
# [0] Disable outline and use simple
# [1] Use outline and disable simple
moon_outline=1


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Allow Conkywx to check for latest version release
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Never check for a new version of Conkywx
# [1] Check for a new version, every 24 hours
enable_new_version_check=1


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Today date format - used in last fetch
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
today_date_format="%A %b, %d %Y"


## We have ability to generate graphs for temperature, pressure and dewpoint
## You need gnuplot to be installed
## comment these colour options do disable
## colour can be in names or '#fed577' format if name gives white output
## This seed data is generated by wunderground and rest is recorded by conkywx
graph_temp='red'
graph_pressure='green'
graph_dewpoint='cyan'


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimum time the script allows the wesite to be polled
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: This setting MUST BE LESS THAN the fetch frequency in your Conky script.
#
# Uncomment for Conky scripts that fetch data every 900 seconds (15 minutes).
minimum_fetch_time=870
#
# Uncomment for testing purposes. Fetches fresh data every time Conky is restarted.
# minimum_fetch_time=0


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# --- DO NOT TOUCH SECTION ---
# URLs used in this program
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
main_url="http://www.wunderground.com/global/stations/"
xml_url="http://api.wunderground.com/weatherstation/WXCurrentObXML.asp?ID="
```

Does anyone have thoughts on what's broken?

Thanks!

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-02-03
If you are interesting in Fahrenheit Nvidia temperatures:

```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'
114.8

Here's the line from the conky:
```
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${execi 2 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F

### Post by VinDSL on 2014-02-04
> **Paul_Whitehurst said:**
>  I'm having a problem with the CoR forecast line in ConkyWX, after [D3P], things break. I usually see CoR${}o for the third day and conky spews "Conky: unknown variable" on the command line.

Does anyone have thoughts on what's broken?
Usually one of these settings needs to be increased...

```
####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
**[COLOR="#FF0000"]## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.[/COLOR]**
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
#
text_buffer_size **[COLOR="#FF0000"]4352[/COLOR]**

####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 600

[B][COLOR="#0000CD"]####
## Maximum size of user text buffer, e.g. for layout below TEXT line (default is 16384)
## Increase this, as needed, to accommodate large config files with a great deal of text. 
## Tips n' Tricks from: Cavsfan
#
max_user_text 32768[/COLOR][/B]
```

I'd try increasing the 'text_buffer_size', first.  ;)

**EDIT**

I was re-reading your '.conkyrc' code and noticed the 'Cavsfan Trick' is missing.

Make sure to add the 'max_user_text' section.  I'll highlight the code in blue (above).

---

### Post by Paul_Whitehurst on 2014-02-04
Thanks a lot, increasing text_buffer_size fixed the problem!

Everything looks great now.

####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.

[COLOR=#0000ff]maximum_width 1200 [/COLOR]
[COLOR=#ff0000]####added by me

max_user_text 114688[/COLOR]


####

[/COLOR]

> **VinDSL said:**

```
####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
**[COLOR=#FF0000]## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.[/COLOR]**
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!

**EDIT**

I was re-reading your '.conkyrc' code and noticed the 'Cavsfan Trick' is missing.

Make sure to add the 'max_user_text' section.  I'll highlight the code in blue (above).

VinDSL, it looks like Paul_Whitehurst did have the **max_user_text** setting like I did in my conky.
See the red lines. That is exactly like I had mine but I changed it to be like yours is.
I had set mine to the 114688 value because Raring would not display the Audacious code at the bottom that Tommy provided no matter what I set that value to.
It seemed to work great in Trusty, Precise and Mint 13 so I left it at that value.

Paul_Whitehurst, you'll probably want to comment out or delete that first **maximum_width 1200**. I had set that and forgot about it.
I'm pretty sure the 2nd one overrides it any way. I commented out the first one and the line above it and uncommented out the line above that and it still works great:

```
####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
[COLOR=#0000FF]minimum_size 245 1394
#minimum_size 445 1894
#maximum_width 1200[/COLOR] 

####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 245
```

EDIT: addendum

I left it like this and it works fine.

```
####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
#minimum_size 245 1394

####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 245
```

### Post by VinDSL on 2014-02-09
> **PJs Ronin said:**
> Also, having great difficulty turning off the 'C' in the current conditions... any ideas?
In your .conf file (example: vindsl-metric-conkywx.conf) try adding:

```
'°C:°'
```

...to the 'template_cleanup' code.

It should look something like this:

```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Process these final template changes before showtime
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Be sure to quote the changes in single quotes as shown
template_cleanup=( 
    'Am:${offset -2}AM'
    'Pm:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:Calm'
    'Calm mph:Calm'
    'Calm:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Mph:mph'
    'Km/h:km/h'
    '°F:°'
   **[COLOR="#FF0000"] '°C:°'[/COLOR]**
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full Moon:${offset 5}Full${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning.*:${offset -5}Waning'
    'Waxing.*:${offset -5}Waxing'
  )

```

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-02-22
Hello Spyros,

That is a very beautiful background wallpaper.

It would be very helpful if you showed us what template you are using...we could
help you easier that way...terminal is complaining about not being able to read a
template file...

I don't see the ALERTS showing in your screenshot, so it maybe [COLOR="#00FF00"]template7[/COLOR] that is 
causing your problem...does it look like this ???

```
template7 /tmp/wx-[COLOR="#00FF00"]param[/COLOR]-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert
```

if it does, then you need to change /wx-[COLOR="#FF0000"]param[/COLOR]-vindsl to YOUR user name...

to change the wx icons is very easy also...open the [COLOR="#00FFFF"]conkywx.conf[/COLOR] file and look for this:

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Choose weather icon set
## [COLOR="#00FF00"]Note: to view these icon sets - you need to comment fti_* options below[/COLOR]
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Enable conkywx icon set org 12 by bharathp666 complete set redone by me 24 icons
## [2] Enable icon set origin not known
## [3] Enable icon set tick weather icons by xiao4
## [4] Enable icon set Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
## OR - To Use custom icon set. State full path here in single quotes.
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
```

 you are using the 3D FTI images, so then you need to comment them out like this:

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Font to Image
## convert ConkyWeather font to 3D images - state the colour here.
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Use only for Image Templates
## COMMENT these options for using in font templates
## COMMENT these options for using regular weather images in image templates
## Needs Imagemagick installed
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## to see List of Color Names visit http://www.imagemagick.org/script/color.php
## Colour can be stated in any of the following formats - enclosed in single quotes
## example for colour *lime* - 'lime' '#0f0' '#00ff00' 'rgb(0,255,0)' 'rgb(0,100%,0)'
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
[COLOR="#00FF00"]#[/COLOR] fti_now='#fed577'
[COLOR="#00FF00"]#[/COLOR] fti_wind='DarkSeaGreen1'
[COLOR="#00FF00"]# [/COLOR]fti_fct='lime'

```

this will allow you to chose a different icon set...

let us know how it works for you....we are all here to help

tommy

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-02-25
Hi to all.  By the time I have made it here through the first 1400-odd messages, I feel almost as if I have met some of you.  I just thought I should pay some tribute to the work that has been done on making Conky accessible, almost easy, and in this case beautiful as well!  Thanks especially to ***vindsl*** for creation and design aesthetics ***42dorian*** for a clear delineation of the methods needed/used, and to ***paramvir*** for extending the possibilities far beyond the expected!

I was amazed at how straight forward the process actually was to create something livable - and although my definition of straight forward may be odd - the whole process (including having been through the whole thread (and some time on the conky weather thread too!) and setting up my own has taken only a day and a half of 'free' time!

[IMG]http://i60.tinypic.com/wk0lye.png[/IMG]

I had to squeeze a lot of features out of mine (only 768 vertical resolution) but I'll happily post a shot of where my setup is- as soon as I figure out the correct methodology here- though I doubt I'll be able to leave it 'as-is' for long!

BTW - I'm another 'experienced' user - as opposed to an 'experienced user' - the senior's discounts await!

---

### Post by VinDSL on 2014-02-26
> **freebird54 said:**
> [...] ***42dorian*** for a clear delineation of the methods needed/used [...]

I was amazed at how straight forward the process actually was to create something livable - and although my definition of straight forward may be odd - the whole process (including having been through the whole thread (and some time on the conky weather thread too!) and setting up my own has taken only a day and a half of 'free' time!
Thanks, freebird54!

42dorian is a genius (literally).  I really miss him.  It wasn't his (stated) intention, but he inadvertently made the best Conky tutorial on the web, IMO.

That said, you must be a fast learner.  Day-n'-a-half is pretty amazing.  Took me 9 tries (over several weeks) just to get the calendar right,  LoL  :D

Anyway, thanks for the kind words.  Glad you're having fun with it...

---

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-02-26
I suspect my days of fast learning are behind me... but fortunately I can still read quite quickly!  I suspect that another reason for the 'speed' is that your methodology for placing things feels quite 'normal' and 'professional' to me (unlike some opinions I have seen here :) ) - as it closely matches the ways in which I did similar things 'back when'.  For a number of years one of my jobs was to 'match' letter layouts (provided by design houses, usually on Macs) for hi-speed variable-laden printing. Matching font sizes, leading and kerning - along with correct image sizing and placement in an environment not unlike conky made me feel at home there!  It even made perfect sense of your 8.6 sized fonts.  :)

I still intend to figure out a way to add in some additional 'features' (such as CPU & HDD temps, Precip accumulations and so on) but I'll have to deconstruct some along the way as I have already started to compromise the aesthetic from the need to vertically squeeze things... I guess squeezing usually makes things wider after all.....

Anyway - thanks again for the framework, and I'll keep you updated with progress (if any) as I go...

Full screeny:

[IMG]http://i58.tinypic.com/imos3r.png[/IMG]

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-02-27
OK freebird......did NOT mean to get you upset with earlier comments...:frown:

in your conky.rc file look for this line & modify it to fit your system...seems to work
for most folks...you may have to play with the cut -c 2-9 parts to work...[-o<

```

##################################
##         TEMPERATURES         ##
##################################
${voffset 15}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}CPU/GPU TEMPERATURES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 1 Temp: $alignr${execi 2 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 2 Temp: $alignr${execi 2 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 3 Temp: $alignr${execi 2 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 4 Temp: $alignr${execi 2 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${execi 2 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F

```

I do not use this template myself, so just guessing here...

maybe VinDSL or Cavsfan will correct me if incorrect..

tommy

p.s. I would ***never*** even try to assume 42dorian's place...

However - I've got better alignment on the bar graphs, room for the temps of everything, and I'm back to a 4-day forecast even though I stuck the current day forecast with the others.  Not sure what's next! 

Keep up the good work - and I'll just keep on freezing here... I think the Fahrenheit would be -2 or so.  Which reminds me - I had to allow a few terms that were killed off in the scripts I got - like 'freezing' and 'pellets'.  They come up a bit too often here - and one NEEDS to know about them! (I had the joy of a week without power and heat over Christmas due to 'freezing' rain  :)

Back to the Bier!

[IMG]http://i61.tinypic.com/fsaj5.png[/IMG]

[IMG]http://i59.tinypic.com/10ibg35.png[/IMG]

correctly...

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-03-01
OK @freebird.....

this morning I opened my email & there was a nice message from Paramvir :)

I asked him your question about the debug file...and of course I was wrong
about the data not being there ;)

here is a snippet of his mail that should answer your concerns

"yes you use debug and that has the data - you also have the original data file which was downloaded - just open that in a browser and you will have the information. actually if you just open that html and look at the display you have on conky - you know where you stand......"

soooo... what does this mean ???

basically, open a terminal & type conkywx.....select option number 4 to set the debug mode....it will save
the data to a file you can peruse...

he has been very busy modifying the perl code that runs conkywx as he has now gotten rid
of the need to use imagemagick & gnuplot to generate the wx icons & trend graphs...he has
gone with Lua exclusively, but needs to figure how to bind perl to conkywx functions...a very
daunting task he says...

he also sent me the latest build to test before release to the World....I will install it later....looking
forward to the new magic stuff ;)

some of the screenshots he sent are magnificient !!!


tommy

p.s. "busy people" here...not me...retired in 2007 after 46+ years working in electronics for da Gov't...now spend time doing "honey do's" for Wife...and playing with Linux of course !!!

and Yes....Germany has a DIN standard for almost everything, including Bier...if you are served less than 0.5 Liter
in a 0.5 L mug, you can send it back...there is a mark in the mug that MUST be met...after the head goes down...

Conky: drawing to created window (0x3800002)

  Resource id in failed request:  0x3800003
  Serial number of failed request:  184
  Current serial number in output stream:  186
```

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-03-09
Never mind the above error. I googled it and found that this fixed it and it did indeed. :)

```
sudo apt-get install mesa-utils
```

So I'm good.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-03-09
> **freebird54 said:**
> Now if I can just figure out how to add in 2 more lines in the weather section, and find the wxalert file to switch the clock to a scroller when present, and....  :)

I can't help you with getting 2 more lines :p but the alert is like this:

```
####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
template1 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf"
template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-imperial-weather-graphic-template.sh"
[COLOR=#FF0000]template7 /tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert[/COLOR]

```

You will just have to change cavsfan to match your login name. The folder name is the same for everyone.
This is just above TEXT.

Then in the weather section:
```
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} \
${color2}Alert: ${if_match ${execi 60 expr length "$(cat ${template7})"}>=32}\
${color5}${scroll 30 10 ${execi 60 cat ${template7}}}${else}\
${color5}${execi 60 cat ${template7}}${endif}${endif} ${color6}${hr 1}${font}${color8}

```

That should make the alerts scroll if there is more than one I believe or if there is 1 long one.
If there is no alert it will just be a line.

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-03-10
Thanks cavsfan - I didn't have that (and have put it in for now - hopefully to see how it runs).  My problem, however, (which I did not describe!) was that I could not find the wxalert file - not even in the directory as shown.  When I went trolling through the .conf file in usr/share I found out (presumably) why no wxalert file - alerts were not turned on, so conkywx did not create the file!  Got the code inserted, and the file now generates, and maybe soon I'll get the scroll to work with it (2 alerts at the moment.)

[IMG]http://i60.tinypic.com/xlfrxk.png[/IMG]

Anyway - be it noted - Unity runs on compiz still (even on Trusty) - presumably because Mir is not yet ready for primetime.  So - the source of your trouble is interaction 'elsewhere' I guess.  Conky runs fine on this same machine on Trusty (I'm on 3.13.0.13 here) - though I haven't got all the sensors running yet.  I DID have one weird 'screen tearing' incident on startup - but haven't seen it since it got all the way up...

Hope you find it!

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-03-10

very strange indeed.....have never had or seen this problem unless reloading conkywx....did you by chance
reload conkywx from Paramvir's blog....or from Software Center ??  

the .deb files use Paramvir's default settings that match ***his display*** so they probably don't match 
anyone else's display; hence the need to adjust accordingly...

I ***assume*** you are using VinDSL's imperial-weather-graphics-template.sh  

this controls where each element is placed on your display as you most likely know by now....mine never
changes unless I change it myself, or install new conkywx version...

strange business if it changes without intervention...

tommy

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-03-13
Much appreciated - especially as I haven't 'seen' him here in a while :)  It is always a surprise how much more work it is to prepare a program for 'distribution' than it is to just get it to work!  All the extra bounds checking, the id10t proofing, and the misuse preparation, the exceptions for the guy who runs it on something outdated - aah - the memories! (a guy trying to run my multi-line BBS program on a pair of floppy drives comes to mind! :) )

Actually - it might be helpful for him to know that I had conky quit on both 14.04 and 12.04.  It is not verified - but  it seems to be related to continual operation with xml data error active, and NOT affected by xml=1 OR xml=0 (although I haven't yet tried commenting out the xml_ID).  Perhaps it is even stopped intentionally?  At first I thought it was a 14.04 anomaly - but last night it went down the same way on Precise too... and suggested sending the crash data in to the devs.

Anyway - thanks to all - and appreciation in all directions too!  Now to find out if I can find a way to improve the scrolling.... paramvir may not be the only one trying to integrate with conky!  You would think a little old-fashioned C code could come up with something using an overlapping viewport....

PS: Been checking - commenting out the xml_ID stops the loading of the xml data file - and SHOULD stop the deaths I noted on Trusty....

Maybe this will help:

```
####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 700

####
## Maximum size of user text buffer, e.g. for layout below TEXT line (default is 16384)
## Increase this, as needed, to accommodate large config files with a great deal of text. 
## Tips n' Tricks from: Cavsfan
#
max_user_text 98304
```

That's about all I can see concerning font limitations. The 2nd part is something I had to add to get the whole conky to display without getting cutoff.

BTW I did have to adjust the whole conky getting everything a little closer together to make room for Tommy's Audacious code at the bottom. 
So, it depends on what you want to show and how tight you want it to display but it was possible for me to get about a half inch or so more room at the bottom.

[ATTACH=CONFIG]251134[/ATTACH]

No matter how much I increased the **max_user_text** I could not get it to go any further down my screen. I have about 1/4 inch that I cannot use.
I have a 28" monitor 1920x1200.

As for the 'missing bottom' thing - one thing that changes from machine to machine (and maybe from build to build?) is adjusted with this:

```
####
## Gap between text and screen borders.
#
gap_x 6	  # Left/Right
gap_y 32  # Top/Bottom

####

```

maybe that's worth investigating?

### Post by paramvir on 2014-03-17
Hello People :D

I did write a message a few days back but never got to posting it and today it is too late...

I can be emailed you know :D

I have never so far used max specials or the max_user_text - I have always used text_buffer_size - it is a value in bytes and the default value is 256 - increasing this has always resolved my crisis - I still have to pounce on VinDsl template and then can give a final verdict :D

As for as xml station is concerned - comment out xml_ID if you are not going to use it - in fact - for some time now anything not required is to be commented out. This was required as programming languages lua, Perl etc etc take a value of zero as nil value and they may also look at it as text zero in which case it is not nil - so comment the bugger - then it is definitely nil.

Yes It takes time to make it idiot proof like I wrote to Tommy and at the same time make it usable.

I have had feedback for sometime regarding the xml station issue - but when i test it from my place it works out. Sometimes there is a delay and I also have no connection from conkywx - specially the German locations - but those times the locations themselves are not reachable. I use Tor-Dns normally but switch to others  like my ISP or google or Opendns to confirm.

To have a fallback weather site - what about if we do not use xml station - or is the main site data also in question - this has happened ;) - so to have a fallback data from another site - we would need one that has a fairly regular update - same as wunderground main site. In my view - we can wait and see - wunderground is good - it is going through enormous changes - perhaps these issues are a fall out because of that. I am open to suggestions ... my sites are set on intellicast ???

cheers

Good to see you back here - maybe it means that significant progress has been made :)

Anyway - what I pointed out about conky dying when the xml data was bad was more a note about a difference I found in Trusty vs Precise than anything else -- conkywx behaved fine AFAIK. As for the reaction when xml=0, I assumed that there was a problem as you describe with being sure what the data would be - testing the result!  Commenting out all reference works fine. By the way, if no 'backup' sites exist, it is not a big deal for the system to be down -- unless wunderground goes away entirely, conkywx handles the 'fail' just fine on my setup.  All that happens is that the data is missing (blank) including values, forecast days and temps, and pictures.  Perfectly sensible, and it recovers fine when the data retrieval goes again. Conky sails through it all uncaring and unknowing because everything is handled....
[IMG]http://i57.tinypic.com/nco3zs.png[/IMG]
as you see!
Are you still taking requests for 'features' in 2.0?  Was I right that ceiling information is available next to the CLD information?  Am I going to need a second monitor just to run and display all the new possibilities? 

On another note, if I can find enough info to hang together a little 'C' program, what capabilities would it need to interact with conkywx/conky? Could/should it handle large, multi-line messages (like the "Special Weather Statement" we keep getting for our alerts?  Should its interaction stay on stdio (standard shell style in and out)? Preliminary thoughts indicate it should need and x,y for top-left and bottom-right, perhaps font and colour, and scroll speed factor.....   It's time I tried SOMETHING useful, and I haven't seen a callable scroller as yet...

Have a g:):)d day!

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-03-18
@Tommy, It makes no difference whether I'm in Unity or Gnome Flashback w/compiz it still does the same. I'm good with differences between different versions on Linux OSs.

@freebird54, I also had to comment out **xml_id**. I'm just using location right now and it works fantabulous! I believe having both worked at one time but went awry at some point and Paramvir showed me how to fix it.

Another thing: today for some odd reason when I booted into Trusty in place of my weather was an error message something like **${vof}f** and I could not figure out how to fix it. It had an error in terminal output something about unknown variable vof (repeated over and over again).

I swapped all of the weather files and that was not it. So, I pulled the .conkrc file from Mint 13 and it worked great although it was skewed as the placement is different as mentioned.

I even found out how to compare the 2 files and tried to see what the difference was with the **diff** command and could not see the difference.

So I had backed up my .conkyrc file on trusty and copied and pasted it into the working conky file that was running and everything went back to normal! :confused: I was like wuuuuuuuu...

It looked good until I logged out and back in. The same error **${vof}f** was displayed. I was thinking KMN! :P

So, I started to go though making the conky on Mint work on Trusty. I had to rearrange everything. Then it dawned on me. I just cut everything out except the CONKYWX part and pasted that into the file from the one I had backed up then everything worked.

I installed some updates and a new kernel and rebooted and everything is back to normal.

```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ uname -a
Linux cavsfan-MS-7529 3.13.0-18-generic #38-Ubuntu SMP Mon Mar 17 21:40:06 UTC 2014 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

```

But, I swear conky can cause headaches, night sweats, anxiety attacks, hot dog fingers, exploding eyeballs... Well maybe I'm exaggerating but you know how frustrating it can be when you cannot figure out something.

as you see!
Are you still taking requests for 'features' in 2.0?  Was I right that ceiling information is available next to the CLD information?  Am I going to need a second monitor just to run and display all the new possibilities? 

On another note, if I can find enough info to hang together a little 'C' program, what capabilities would it need to interact with conkywx/conky? Could/should it handle large, multi-line messages (like the "Special Weather Statement" we keep getting for our alerts?  Should its interaction stay on stdio (standard shell style in and out)? Have a g:):)d day!

No - Single monitor will do just fine ;) 

I do not include ceiling info as I never really felt it was an issue as it does not help us in any way unless it would assist you in figuring if it is going to break and give us some degree of rain or precipitation... or if you are flying ... there are multiple layers of clouds. Basically it can be included - everything is do able given the inclination to do so ha ha ha ha :D

I have written a scroller in lua - but it does not yet scroll like the credits at the end of programming (TV, Cinema) - It does not give spaces at the end of text like conky scroller - it is a continuous scroller with some extra features. The scroll speed is based on the update interval of conky and the group of characters you move the scroll text with.

The alerts link to another web page - which is not handled - the idea is to give the user information that there is a situation and visiting the website will give him better understanding. If we pick that information and present it on the desktop it may or may not include other relevant and critical info which the user might miss.

cheers

Yeah - only for flying - or walking up high hills I suppose!  Anyway - VFR minimums require staying 500 feet below the clouds - and you often require 1000 feet minimums over built up areas - so a 1500 feet ceiling (453 m +) is rather needed!

As for the extended statements, it occurs to me to wonder if a 'hot spot' can be specified in conky - such that you can tie a mouse event to it. Use it to call up your browser on a CONKYWX heading click - with the wunderground ID already specified - so you can pick up your warnings etc perhaps?  I can't imagine it being worthwhile to try to scroll the amount of text we regularly get in a 'Special Weather Statement' here at conky refresh rates!

Probably not - nothing is ever that simple...

### Post by paramvir on 2014-03-19
> **freebird54 said:**
> Yeah - only for flying - or walking up high hills I suppose!  Anyway - VFR minimums require staying 500 feet below the clouds - and you often require 1000 feet minimums over built up areas - so a 1500 feet ceiling (453 m +) is rather needed!

As for the extended statements, it occurs to me to wonder if a 'hot spot' can be specified in conky - such that you can tie a mouse event to it. Use it to call up your browser on a CONKYWX heading click - with the wunderground ID already specified - so you can pick up your warnings etc perhaps? Probably not - nothing is ever that simple...

That would be so nice to have simplicity in everything - but we can make it appear simple ...

There are some threads on the net about "interactive conky" - they use different approaches - I have not really looked at them ... but yes that is a good point.

One can have their location as a bookmark and once they see an alert they just use that bookmark to visit their location. You can also use a key binding and have the location show up by just pressing a key sequence. We just use **xdg-open url** and add a key binding and voila - we have a working solution. ;)

cheers

---

There is quite a lot to understand about setting up a working conky of any kind - and even more for a Vindsl special!  I came in to this only a couple of weeks ago - and I read through the entire thread picking up hints and tips.  However - not EVERYONE needs to do ALL that :)

SO - the minimum to have it work for you is to read, CAREFULLY and in detail, the first post in particular.  When you understand that - you will have most of the difficulties solved.  Then - you find the newest complete script you can, and modify it as needed to fit your situation.  I grabbed a script from about page 75 - but there are newer ones later (maybe someone else kept track as to where?) but little changed after the ones I got apart from a few tweaks to the weather area.  Amazingly, nearly everything mentioned by 42dorian still works!

Good luck.  And remember, you have to make it fit your screen, you have to add the fonts as needed, and you have to set the conkywx to look for your weather where you are  :)  Here is my current setup - as you can see I had to lose some features to fit in a screen only 768 pixels tall...

[IMG]http://i60.tinypic.com/2qjx5qv.png[/IMG]

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-03-23
Nice post freebird54. I didn't know where to begin and you did a good job pointing Szokolai_Gyrgy in the right direction.

To add to what freebird54 has said, you will need to install lm-sensors - **sudo apt-get install lm-sensors** and then once installed enter in terminal **sudo sensors-detect** and answer Yes to all of the questions. 
That will pull some of the things needed from your particular system that is used in the conky. I also got my templates from this post on page 75 too. [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=75&p=12389760#post12389760](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=75&p=12389760#post12389760)

Like freebird54 said much of what you can display depends on the resolution size of your monitor. Mine is 1920x1200 so I have plenty of room. 
There is a lot of tweaking and adjusting to be done once you have gotten it close to where you want to be. 
You will also want to install screenruler - **sudo apt-get install screenruler** as it measures your screen in pixels.

Also when you make a change to a file you need to click on save twice on the .conkyrc file. 
If you make a change to another file besides the .conkyrc file you will need to have both open, clicking save once on the other file and then clicking save twice on the .conkyrc file to see the change and it's effect. 
We've all spent many hours on this to get it just like we want it but it's worth every minute. :)

Here is what mine looks like and it is named .conkyrc.vindsl - my startup command is **conky -c /home/cavsfan/.conkyrc.vindsl --pause=10** (where cavsfan is your name)
This will also work: **conky -c ~/.conkyrc.vindsl --pause=10** 
But if you just want to run it from terminal **conky -c /home/cavsfan/.conkyrc.vindsl** is all that is needed to see the output in terminal.
And this too **conky -c ~/.conkyrc.vindsl** will work as well.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-03-23174609.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-03-23174609.php")

I hope you can understand our English and good luck to you. :)

---

### Post by paramvir on 2014-03-24
OK one of the reasons for xml error - probably is that xml data is over 1 hour older than the main site data. In other words the xml data is fairly stale - in which case you will get a xml error. This may happen if the site is not a rapid fire update site.

But if you cannot do without that data - as in the case of Tommy - here is a work around.

[ATTACH=CONFIG]251423[/ATTACH]

As you can see this is the xml page with weather history - you see the link marked with that red line. Use that url as the main location url and comment out the xml_ID line in the config file.

Let me know how it works out...

cheers

---

thanx Param....once again you save us from despair :P

tommy

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-03-25
> **Cavsfan said:**
> 
....
Also when you make a change to a file you need to click on save twice on the .conkyrc file. 
If you make a change to another file besides the .conkyrc file you will need to have both open, clicking save once on the other file and then clicking save twice on the .conkyrc file to see the change and it's effect. 
We've all spent many hours on this to get it just like we want it but it's worth every minute. :)


IF the above procedure sounds odd or complex (even though it is actually easy and fast) then you could do what I do - I send conky a restart message whenever I have a change in ANY file I'm working on and want to check.  The command is 
```
sudo killall -SIGUSR1 conky

```
What I did is to add it as an alias to my 'bash_aliases' file as follows"
```

alias crstart='sudo killall -SIGUSR1 conky'

```
which means  I just enter '**crstart**' in the terminal window to restart using the new file versions - and after the first time, it is just **<UP ARROW>** and **<ENTER>**  :)

Tailor made for the lazy...

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-03-25
> **NM5TF said:**
> very true...and I also re-learned  how use my bash_alias files....brought back memories from 
longer ago than I care to think about....used Unix commands a lifetime ago it seems...:P

tommy

Ha! Yeah I used Unix commands on this server I had to deal with that communicated with the mainframe I programmed. That was only about 15 or so years ago. :p

If you haven't seen the dozens of times I've posted this, pardon me if you have but someone on this forum was nice enough to show me these aliases for updating via cli commands.
Not sure if you remember Ranch Hand in this forum but he called update manager update mangler and told me to use cli commands instead. I'm sure update manager has gotten smarter over the years 
but at least using cli you don't see partial updates. 

Anyway here is what I was shown to put in ~/.bashrc:

```
# update aliases
alias ud='sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade && sudo apt-get clean'
alias ud2='sudo apt-get dist-upgrade'
alias ud3='sudo apt-get autoremove'
```

I added the last one as I seen it was needed.

Hate to get off topic but that's all I got. :)

---

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-03-25
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Ha! Yeah I used Unix commands on this server I had to deal with that communicated with the mainframe I programmed. That was only about 15 or so years ago. :p

If you haven't seen the dozens of times I've posted this, pardon me if you have but someone on this forum was nice enough to show me these aliases for updating via cli commands.
Not sure if you remember Ranch Hand in this forum but he called update manager update mangler and told me to use cli commands instead. I'm sure update manager has gotten smarter over the years 
but at least using cli you don't see partial updates. 

Anyway here is what I was shown to put in ~/.bashrc:

```
# update aliases
alias ud='sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade && sudo apt-get clean'
alias ud2='sudo apt-get dist-upgrade'
alias ud3='sudo apt-get autoremove'
```

I added the last one as I seen it was needed.

Hate to get off topic but that's all I got. :)


We'll stay off topic for a moment :)  Here's a couple I use - mostly because of other OS's and hardwired fingers....

```

alias cls='clear'
alias list='ls -la | more'
alias md='mkdir'
alias copy='cp'
alias dir='ls -l'
alias dirs='ls -lR | more'
alias ren='mv'
alias move='mv'
alias type='cat'
alias x='exit'
alias restart='sudo shutdown -r now'
alias fontreload='sudo fc-cache -v'
alias crstart='sudo killall -SIGUSR1 conky'

```

I never understood how *nix boxes could possibly have **mkdir** rather than **md** - everything else is terse even when it is unneeded! :)
 Have a good day - and back to our regularly scheduled....

Thanks for the kind words - but the SIGUSR1 thing I picked up from a cruise elsewhere - maybe AskUbuntu?  I assume that it is faster because it is conky doing an internal 'flush' rather than being TERMinated and restarted by something external.  Same result (I think) with **sudo killall -1 conky** if you happen to be typing it 'live' ...

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-03-26
Just curious about the need for sudo in that command to restart conky.

```
killall -SIGUSR1 conky
```

seems to work as well as 

```
sudo killall -SIGUSR1 conky
```

without the need to enter password or am I wrong?

---

### Post by jdallara on 2014-03-28
Conky runs in your user space, therefore you don't need to sudo (i.e. you can kill your own processes, but not others, without su capabilities).

Jon.

---
