### Post by freebird54 on 2014-04-09
It occurs to me that I should have also posted a look at what the 'old' system has now, along with the modified code to make it happen (for those not used to modifying **vindsl**'s creations :)

A message or so earlier you could see that the RYTHMBOX entry (with added lines from Free Bird the song) shows when Rythmbox is running, and the section becomes FREEBIRD and shows more lines from the song when silence reigns.  I do this because nature abhors a vacuum, and I'm not too fond of even using one myself!

On the lower res screen (768 vertical res) I don't have room for Rhythmbox at all, so I switch out TIME when the music plays, as you see here:

[IMG]http://i58.tinypic.com/a3ieqo.png[/IMG]                           [IMG]http://i59.tinypic.com/2ret8ck.png[/IMG]

and the code for the affected sections...

```
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${if_running rhythmbox}
${voffset -10}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format "%tt  |  %ta"| head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format "%tt  |  %ta" --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format "%tt  |  %ta" --no-start}${font}${endif}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format "%at  |  %te  |  %td "| head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format "%at  |  %te  |  %td " --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format "%at  |  %te  |  %td " --no-start}${font}${endif}
${else}${voffset 1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -2}${font RadioSpace:size=24}${color8}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
${endif}
##################################
##   CALENDAR (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running rhythmbox}${voffset -14}${else}${voffset -10}${endif}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 14}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -6}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color3}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -78}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color7}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2014"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color1}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color1}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
# ${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Dec 2012"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jun 2013"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color3}/'##################################

```

Have a g:):)d day!

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-04-12155402.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-04-12155402.php")

This in Trusty Flashback (with Compiz) and I'm in love. ;)

OK that issue happens if there is no Clouds data ie no clouds - fixed...

I wonder why a conf error - Tommy can you check that line - it would come if there is a space in-between like so

```
minimum_fetch_time= 1800
``` get rid of that and it should be fine.

### Post by jdallara on 2014-04-20
Hello,

  Just wanted to add my screen shot.  Nothing fancy but I did add 2 font files for the nVidia and Intel logos in the system section:

```
${voffset 2}${font IllustrateIT:size=10}${color2}Y${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color3}${offset 5}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia/'}${alignr}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font Logoz\-Librariez:size=10}${color2}e${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color3}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Duo${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
```

### Post by paramvir on 2014-04-21
> **aquarius18 said:**
> What - the fire extinguisher went up in smoke???? :lol::lol:

More seriously: how do you get 2 conkys displayed on the screen? Sort of what Tommy and Paramvir are doing on their screens.... one top right and one top left.

I scrunched around this loooong thread to try and find the recipe for multiple .conkyrc files, so far to no avail.

Cheers / Frank


A lot of people use the default ~/.conkyrc file - I do not :wink:

```

conky -c ./examples/lua-wx-test.sh &
conky -c ./examples/short-02-conkyrc.sh & 
```

you use **-c** option to give conky the conkyrc to use - the **&** at the end - shell backgrounds the process.

I am using 4 of these - lua animated clock, system information, default conkywx and conkywx with lua widgets [trying to make something out of it]  ;-)

cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2014-04-21
> **aquarius18 said:**
> Aye Piloto! :tongue:
 Where would I pop that code? As command into Startup Apps?? :confused: Pardon my ignorance, in this regard I am like Schulz: "I know noffinggg!"

Cheers / Frank

Ohla Amigo mio  lol - have a look at my scripts

```

/usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh
/usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh 
```

basically you add the line(s) to a script file - make it executable and run it - it will start conky with the conkyrc files you give it with the **-c** option ;-) - it is soo simple - IF you know - other wise everything is like "I know noffinggg!" :lolflag: 

To make a file executable you could use the file manager and right click and select make it executable or something like dat ...

You will see lines that kill conky and then start new instances of conky - there are other ways - mentioned earlier but they do not go well with arch linux. You will need to stop running instances of conky - otherwise you will have multiple instances running. Give us a shout if you need further info ... ;-)

Njoy

---

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-04-21
> **aquarius18 said:**
> Aye Piloto! :tongue:
 Where would I pop that code? As command into Startup Apps?? :confused: Pardon my ignorance, in this regard I am like Schulz: "I know noffinggg!"

Cheers / Frank

do this...

```
sudo gedit /usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh

```

open conky-restart.sh.....you can select from a number of different options.....

```
#!/bin/bash
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export path statements for use in *conky* config file
# if you move this file else where then give the full path for WX_DIR variable
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# WX_DIR="/usr/share/conkywx"; export WX_DIR

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# stop existing conky processes
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# if [[ "$(pidof conky)" ]] ; then killall conky; fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# run conky instances
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 conky -c ./examples/conky-system.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/wx-wu-vindsl-conkyrc.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/expanded-conkyrc.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/short-conkyrc.sh &

# sleep 1
conky -c ./examples/lua-wx-test.sh &
conky -c ./examples/short-02-conkyrc.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/conkywx-now-conkyrc.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/scroller-conkyrc.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/slim-conkyrc.sh &


exit
```

as you can see, there 3 different conky's running....

the sys monitor & 2 conkywx's....

try them all until you find a favorite...

tommy

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-04-21
I guess I did it the hard way. When I had VinDSL's conky running and started up Paramvir's it killed VinDSL's. I could never get them both running at the same time either.

What I did was create a script, cmod +x and chown root:root and put that in my startup commands.

This is on Trusty.

[PHP]#!/bin/sh
sleep 25
/usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh
sleep 20
conky -c /home/cavsfan/.conkyrc.vindsl[/PHP]

It works everytime. :)

---

But, hey don't worry about it I think I'll be OK. :p

### Post by paramvir on 2014-04-21
> **Cavsfan said:**
> I guess I did it the hard way. When I had VinDSL's conky running and started up Paramvir's it killed VinDSL's. I could never get them both running at the same time either.

What I did was create a script, cmod +x and chown root:root and put that in my startup commands.

This is on Trusty.

[PHP]#!/bin/sh
sleep 25
/usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh
sleep 20
conky -c /home/cavsfan/.conkyrc.vindsl[/PHP]

It works everytime. :)

You really do not need those sleep commands unless you need time for your net to come up

[PHP]#!/bin/sh
sleep 25
/usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh &
conky -c /home/cavsfan/.conkyrc.vindsl &
[/PHP]

should work fine as well. ;)

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-04-26
> **Cavsfan said:**
> What I meant was if it shows up there on that list, you need to have the right location in that file. I think that is what Tommy was saying.
If the file shows in that window, it has to be correct for your conky to display the right weather for your location.

cheers :)

@CavsFan is correct....when installing Conkywx you need to set your location in the .conf files to get
the correct weather for ***your location***.....otherwise it will be set to whatever location Paramvir
was last using for testing purposes....sometimes it will be New Delhi, India....sometimes it will be
New Mexico, USA....possibly other locations...

depending upon which conkywx display you are using, the files to edit could be 1 or more of the following:

```
/usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf
```
```
/usr/share/conkywx/examples/lua-display.conf
```
```
/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf
```
```
/usr/share/conkywx/examples/scroller-conkywx.conf
```

have fun with the new & improved Conkywx

tommy

OK, thanks for the Black and White explanation. Good. 
I knew something was going to break sooner or later.
Might I also suggest a stripped down version of Conkywx to accompany the usual thing? I don't know about most people but I for sure never put too much faith in overly detailed weather forecasts, over many, many days. Those people with 7-day forcasts and cockpit flight controls with all the weather instrumentation are really just going for a ride, pardon the pun.

So this is me. I went from the expanded-template down to the now-template (to 2-3 days max) and couldn't be more pleased. This seems more useful to me, less information overload with better specificity and low maintenance.

### Post by paramvir on 2014-04-30
Thank you people for the kind words... :lol:

For the VinDSL Template included with conkywx - here are a few words ...

There are 2 templates - one with dingbats  and one with graphics. The unit shown is changed basis the config file option Units_to_use and also [UU] element which also has the units C or F. Basis this the mph and km/h under the windvane changes.

The main vindsl-conkyrc file also works on similar idea but has a template9 which the user can change to C or F - this has to be capital and the other information such as temperatures etc get changed auto-magically ;-)

The original content is there in comments in the vindsl-conkyrc. The config file is also up to date for version 2.

Also the lua stuff is using conkywx.lua - such as bar graphs and background. The max_user_text which was 81920 is now 20000 and the text_buffer_size was about 4800 or there abouts is now 6200. I have not used the music stuff at the bottom of template for lack of time - you may need to change these values when you bring them into action and please do let me know what changes you make so I can test and add them in for the next update...

This should reduce a lot of queries about where in the world do we find that awesome template called VinDSL template ha :lol:

We now have a unified location for VinDSL template and fonts !!! - Documentation shall follow in due course... the template is really well documented as it is...

cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2014-04-30
> **awoltering said:**
> Thanks for making it work again!!

I`ve been playing with it but I have one problem with the windvane, it doesn`t show up! al the other lua-parts are working.
When I look in the ```
/tmp/wx-arno-arno-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39
``` folder there is a picture for the windvane but it isn`t shown in the conky, as you can see in my screenshot.

Fantastic - take a look at the vindsl files [4 of them] in the examples folder - that shows you how to get the animated windvane working

```

####    Windvane
${lua main -n showcond -p 10, 620 -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 2 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }

```

you will need to adjust for your display.

In fact have a look at the vindsl-conkyrc - that will give you the display you see in conkywx image you see in my release post

cheers

But still I don`t have the windvane? I`m using the following files:

[B]/home/arno/.conkyrc

[/B]```
###############################################
## VinDSL | rev. 12-11-19 11:55 | ver. 0.7.9 ##
###############################################
##          November 2012 Series             ##
###############################################


## ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##


####
## Development Platforms (current)
#
#  Ubuntu 10.10 'Maverick Meerkat' (GNOME 2.28 - Conky 1.8.0-1)
#  Ubuntu 13.04 'Raring Ringtail'  (GNOME-SHELL - UNITY - LXDE - Conky 1.9.0-2)
#  Screen Resolution: 1920x1200x24 (DELL UltraSharp 1907FP)
#
## Tips n' Tricks: conky 1.8.1 will no longer be supported. RIP :)


####
## Prerequisites (required)
#
#  conky-all 1.8.0-1 or 1.9.0-2
#  Conkywx 2.0.0 (Paramvir Likhari)
#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor


####
## Installed fonts (required)
#
#  Arrows (JLH Fonts - not included in link below)
#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva - not included in link below)
#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
#
## Tips n' Tricks from: Mr. Peachy, djyoung4, and 42dorian (Thanks!)
## Most necessary fonts can be downloaded here: http://ompldr.org/vOHdoag
## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv


####
## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
#
use_xft yes
xftfont DroidSans:size=8.75
xftalpha 0.1


####
## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
#
override_utf8_locale yes


####
## Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.
#
background yes


####
## Update interval in seconds.
#
update_interval 2.0


####
## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
## Zero makes Conky run forever.
#
total_run_times 0


####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
# own_window_type normal
# own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
own_window_type override
####
## Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
#
# own_window_argb_visual yes
# own_window_argb_value 255


####
## Force images to redraw when they change.
#
imlib_cache_size 1


####
## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
#
double_buffer yes


####
## Draw shades?
#
draw_shades yes
default_shade_color 330


####
## Draw outlines?
#
draw_outline no


####
## Draw borders around text?
#
draw_borders no


####
## Draw borders around graphs?
#
draw_graph_borders no


####
## Print text to stdout?
## Print text in console?
#
out_to_ncurses no
out_to_console no


####
## Text alignment.
#
alignment top_right


####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
#minimum_size 245 1394
minimum_size 245 1080
maximum_width 1200




####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
#
text_buffer_size 6200


####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 700


####added by me
## Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
# max_user_text 81920
max_user_text 20000


####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 245


####
## Gap between text and screen borders.
#
gap_x 6      # Left/Right
gap_y 32  # Top/Bottom


####
## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
#
short_units yes


####
## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
#
pad_percents 0


####
## Pad spacing between text and borders.
#
border_inner_margin 4


####
## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
#
top_name_width 10


####
## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
## Set to yes, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
#
no_buffers yes


####
## Set to yes, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
#
uppercase no


####
## Number of cpu samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
cpu_avg_samples 1


####
## Number of net samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
net_avg_samples 1


####
## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
## Only affects certain objects.
#
use_spacer right


####
## My colors (suit yourself)
#
color0 White            #FFFFFF
color1 Ivory            #FFFFF0
color2 Ivory2           #EEEEE0
color3 yellow           #CDCDC1
color4 Tan1             #FFA54F
color5 cyan             #EE9A49
color6 Gray             #7E7E7E
color7 AntiqueWhite4    #8B8378
color8 DimGray          #696969
color9 Tomato           #FF6347
# colorX OrangeRed        #FF4500


#####
## Load Lua for shading (optional)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
lua_load /usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/draw_bg.lua
# lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg


####
## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_testing.lua
# lua_draw_hook_post main_bars


####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
#template0 "\"/usr/share/conkywx/""
template1 "\"/home/arno/.conky/conkywx/arno-conkywx.conf\""
####
## Choose Graphic or Dingbat template
template4 "\"/home/arno/.conky/conkywx/arno-weather-graphic-template.sh\""
# template4 "\"$WX_DIR/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh\""
####
template7 /tmp/wx-arno-arno-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert


## To switch between Imperial and Metric change template9 to F for Imperial or C for Metric values - template4 file does not require any change - it changes based on Units_to_Use in the vindsl-conkywx.conf file
template9 C


TEXT
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}1${offset 1}2${offset 1}.${offset 0}0${offset 0}4${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
####
##  Uncomment For Arch Linux
#${voffset 0}${font OpenLogos:size=24}${color2}B${font}${voffset -20}${goto 50}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=24}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' }${font}
####
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
${voffset -2}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
# ${voffset 0}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:size=8}${color4}Pre-Alpha${font}
####
## Uncomment to display YOUR custom graphic image (upper-right corner)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -52}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/ABC.jpg -p 180,0 -s 52x52 -n}


##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################
${voffset -7}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}SYSTEM${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec lsb_release -sd | sed -e 's/development/dev/g'}${alignr}${pre_exec lsb_release -sr}${font}
${voffset 2}${offset -2}${font OpenLogos:size=12}${color2}Z${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 3}${sysname}${offset 3}${kernel}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${machine}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nVidia ID (nvidia-smi version - dynamic)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${alignr}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for 'nvidia-current' driver ID (DPKG version - hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec dpkg --status nvidia-current | grep Version | cut -f 1 -d '-' | sed 's/[^.,0-9]//g'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-19'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (any card / any driver - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Video Card Product Name${alignr}Driver Version${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU frequency (hybrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec cat /proc/cpuinfo | grep 'model name' | uniq | cut -f 2 -d ':' | sed 's/.\{10\}$//' | sed 's/.tm.//;s/.\(.*\)/\1/'}${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
#${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Quad Q9550${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
#${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Multi${offset 3}Core${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
####
## Uncomment for System Uptime (hybrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}q${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}System${offset 3}Uptime${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${uptime_short}${font}
## uncoment for grub version
# ${voffset 2}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Grub${offset 3}Version${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec grub-install -v | grep grub-install | cut -c21-42}${font}
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}PROCESSORS+TEMPS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}%${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}%${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 10 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 10 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,145 -w 160 -s 1 -f cpu -a cpu1}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,160 -w 160 -s 1 -f cpu -a cpu2}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,175 -w 160 -s 1 -f cpu -a cpu3}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,190 -w 160 -s 1 -f cpu -a cpu4}
##################################
##            MEMORY            ##
##################################
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}MEMORY${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 6}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}l${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 3}RAM${goto 97}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${mem}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${memmax}${alignr}${memperc}%${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,259 -w 235 -s 1 -f memperc -a NA -l 1 -x 70}
##################################
##             HDD              ##
##################################
${voffset 12}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}HDD${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}ROOT${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${fs_used /}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /}% / ${fs_used_perc /}%${font}
${voffset 13}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}HOME${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${if_existing /home}${fs_used /home}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /home}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /home}% / ${fs_used_perc /home}%${font}${endif}
${voffset 13}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}4${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}SWAP${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${swap}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${swapmax}${alignr}${swapperc}%${font}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,311 -w 235 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a / -l 1 -x 70}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,337 -w 235 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a /home -l 1 -x 70}
##################################
##         TEMPERATURES         ##
##################################

# ${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 2 Temp: $alignr${execi 600 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
# ${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 3 Temp: $alignr${execi 600 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
# ${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU Core 4 Temp: $alignr${execi 600 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | awk '{print $3}' | cut -c2-9}
# ${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${execi 10 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F

${voffset 6}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 1}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 1}${alignr}${top_mem mem 1}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 2}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 2}${alignr}${top_mem mem 2}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 3}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 3}${alignr}${top_mem mem 3}%${font}

#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top_mem name 10}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top_mem mem_res 10}${alignr}${top_mem mem 8}%${font}
##################################
##          NETWORK             ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}NETWORK${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}T${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Download${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totaldown wlan0}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${downspeed wlan0}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}N${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Upload${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totalup wlan0}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${upspeed wlan0}${font}
${voffset 4}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}LAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${addr wlan0}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
#${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}ha ha
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}Alsdorf, Germany${if_existing ${template7}} ${color5}Alert:${font}${lua main -n scroller -f 12 -l 21 -s 5 -p 98,504 -o ${template7}}${else} ${color6}${hr 1}${endif}
${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -10}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color0}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color0}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color0}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -80}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color7}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
#${voffset -70}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
# ${execpi 60 my_cal=$(date +%-d); cal | sed 's/\<'$my_cal'\>/${color1}&${color3}/g'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${voffset -68}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}

```

[B]/home/arno/.conky/conkywx/arno-conkywx.conf

[/B]```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Wund Weather Config File | Paramvir | rev. 14-04-24 05:10 | ver. 2.0.0
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## THIS PROGRAM IS FOR PERSONAL AND PRIVATE USE ONLY
## CONTACT WUNDERGROUND.COM FOR PROFESSIONAL SOLUTIONS
## CAVEAT: No guarantee that this program will work on all platforms,
## Use At Your Own Risk.


## To comment an option means adding # character at the start of that line
## this line is a comment starting with a # character


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Location settings - REQUIRED
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## # Alsdorf, Germany
Location='http://www.wunderground.com/cgi-bin/findweather/hdfForecast?query=50.836%2C6.072&sp=INORDRHE127'


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## xml_ID to use XML data from wunderground - OPTIONAL
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------


## # Alsdorf, Germany
#xml_ID=INORDRHE127


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## User position
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Enter Latitude, Longitude and t_zone (time zone) in decimal format
## Comment the position if you want to use wunderground data co-ordinates
## This position will be in 1 decimal place.
## To get more accurate information for your precise location the coordinates
## can be picked by using Google or http://www.findlatitudeandlongitude.com
## Latitude="37.456254° N"
## Longitude="88.867862° E"




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## How to handle the time zone values
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Let conkywx calculate the time difference
## [2] Pick up the time zone values from wunderground
## OR - To Use custom time zone. State the time zone here
t_zone_pick=2


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Time Zone - set t_zone only if t_zone_pick=0
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this variable if not required
## t_zone="7.5"




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Units to use
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Enter the units to get and use, centigrade or Fahrenheit
## [SI] metric
## [IM] imperial/english
Units_to_Use=SI
# Units_to_Use=IM




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## comment option to disable, defining variable to 0 also works
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] enable
## if you want to see units like km/h etc in conky
## Units_show=1


## Sunday, 27 will become Sun 27
use_short_date=1


## Every update a popup notification shows
#show_notification=1


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Weather Alerts
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Note: Active alerts at present time are only for the US.
## They will NOT warn you if a Tropical Storm is coming your way.
## Enable show_wx_Tropical_alerts for that.
#show_wx_Active_alerts=1
#show_wx_Tropical_alerts=1


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Allow Conkywx to check for latest version release
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [0] Never check for a new version of Conkywx
## [1] Check for a new version, every 24 hours
enable_new_version_check=1


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Moon font characters with or without outline ring
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## NOTE: Default was formerly "simple" e.g. no outline ring
## [0] Disable outline and use simple
## [1] Use outline and disable simple
moon_outline=1


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Dynamic Moon - using ImageMagick
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## View Moon image as it appears outside
## [1] use your local time, [2] use station local time for calculation
## Needs Imagemagick installed
Dynamic_Moon=2






## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  How do you want the present conditon to look like
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Capitalize The First Letter Of All Words, In The Present Condition [Default]
## [2] All UPPERCASE
## [3] all lowercase
## [4] Capitilize the first letter/first word only, in the present condition
pc_what_case=1




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Add words you want to remove from Present Condition text in this list
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this variable Block if not required
pcondition_blacklist=(
    Freezing
    pellet
    pellets
    Precipitation
    Small
    'and:+'
    'heavy:hvy'
    'light:lt'
    'Low Drifting'
    'Patches of'
    'Severe:svr'
    'Thunderstorms:t-storms'
    'Thunderstorm:t-storms'
    'Widespread:ws'
    'with:+'
)


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Process the final template before showtime
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this variable Block if not required
template_cleanup=(
    'AM:${offset -2}AM'
    'PM:${offset -2}PM'
    'N/A:${offset 2}N/A'
    '${goto 206}100%:${goto 201}100%'
    'Calm km/h:${offset 8}Calm'
    'Calm mph:${offset 8}Calm'
    'First Quarter:${offset -6}First Qtr'
    'Full${font}:${offset -11}Full Moon${font}'
    'New Moon:${offset -13}New Moon'
    'Last Quarter:${offset -5}Last Qtr'
    'Waning Crescent:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waning Gibbous:${offset -3}Waning'
    'Waxing Crescent:${offset -3}Waxing'
    'Waxing Gibbous:${offset -3}Waxing'
)




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  LANGUAGE to see data in - English is default and needs no setting
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Current Supported Languages :
##  afrikaans, albanian, arabic, basque, belarusian, bulgarian,
##  catalan, cornish, croatian, czech, danish, deutsch,
##  dutch, espanol, esperanto, estonian, farsi, finnish,
##  french, frenchcanadian, gaelic, galician, greek, hebrew,
##  hindi, hungarian, icelandic, ido, indonesian, italian,
##  javanese, korean, latin, latvian, lithuanian, lowgerman,
##  macedonian, maltese, maori, marathi, nihongo, norwegian,
##  occitan, pashto, polish, portuguese, romanian, russian,
##  serbianlatin, simplifiedchinese, traditionalchinese, slovak,
##  serbian, slovenian, swahili, swedish, swiss, tagalog,
##  tatarish,thai, turkish, turkmen, ukrainian, urdu,
##  vietnamese, welsh, yiddishtransliterated, yiddishunicode
##  plautdietsch,


## Comment this variable if not required
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# language_to_use="arabic"


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  LANGUAGES Support for languages written Right to Left
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  You NEED fribidi installed for Right to Left languages
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Do NOT Comment this variable
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  if your choice of language is not listed below
##  please add it with a comma from the names listed above
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
RTL_lang="arabic,farsi,urdu,pashto,hebrew,yiddishunicode"




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Use UPPERCASE or lowercase AM PM or am pm for 12 hour time format
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] ALL UPPERCASE (EXAMPLE: 03:00 PM)
## [2] all lowercase (example: 03:00 pm)
## [3] Hide am pm (example: 03:00)
## [4] all lowercase with no padding/spacing (example: 3:00pm)
## [5] Use 24 hour time format
## OR - To Use custom time format. State the format here
## To get more date formats see man strftime
## Time_format='%-I:%M %p'
Time_format=5


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Show time with month and day for Moon Phases
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Show month day time (example: May 18 10:06 am)
##      The time will follow the Time_format settings above in Time_format.
## [2] Show only month n day (EXAMPLE: May 18)
## OR - To Use custom time format. State the format here
## To get more date formats see man strftime
## moon_time='%d %b %R'
moon_time=2


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Today date format - used in last fetch
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
today_date_format='%A %b, %d %Y'


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## show deg symbol in forecast temps,
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Hide all deg symbols (example: 95/63)
## [2] Show all deg symbols (example: 95°/63°)
## [3] Show all deg symbols, depending on Unit_to_Use (metric: 35°C/17°C) (imperial: 95°F/63°F)
## [4] Show deg symbol ONLY, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°)
## [5] Show deg symbol AND unit, after forecast pair (example: 95/63°F) [Default]
show_degsym_fc_temp=4


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## characters to use for forecast trends
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Rising trend arrow
risingtrend='c'
##
## Falling trend arrow
fallingtrend='d'
##
## Steady trend arrow
steadytrend='Q'




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Choose weather icon set
## Note: to view these icon sets - you need to comment Font ti Images options below
## Use wx_iconset if same icons for condition and day forecast comment wx_wind_day_set
## Use wx_wind_day_set if different icons for day forecast
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] Enable conkywx icon set org 12 by bharathp666 complete set redone by me 24 icons
## [2] Enable icon set origin not known
## [3] Enable icon set tick weather icons by xiao4
## [4] Enable icon set Sticker Weather Icons by Kortoik
## [5] Enable set Everyday weather images from the Internet Paramvir
## OR - To Use custom icon set. State full path here in single quotes.
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# wx_iconset='/home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/conkywx_1.0.1_source/images/Conky-Spectro/spectro/weather-photos'
# wx_iconset='/home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/conkywx_1.0.1_source/images/Conky-Spectro/spectro/wx-pics'
wx_iconset=2
wx_fcast_iconset=2




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Choose wind rose icon set
## Note: to view these icon sets - you need to use conkywx element [BI] with conky image element.
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] to [3] All icons made by Paramvir
## OR - To Use custom icon set. State full path here in single quotes.
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# wx_wind_iconset='/home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/images/windrose/01-sqr'
wx_wind_iconset=1




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Choose wind rose font set
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] to [4] All fonts are from font ConkyWindNESW
## OR - To Use custom font set. State characters in single quotes
## NOTE:: characters that need to be escaped with \ character
##     :: '\[', '\\', '\]', '\#', '\(', '\)'
##     :: characters that cannot be used are: $ and ' characters
##     :: has short cardinals, long cardinals, unknown, variable and not applicable characters
##     :: "N to NNW", unknown, variable and N/A characters
##     :: Total numbers of characters MUST be 69 including spaces excluding single quotes
##     :: "N to NNW" = 16 characters
##     :: "North to NORTH NORTH WEST" = 16 characters
##     :: unknown, variable and N/A = 3 characters
##     :: Total 16 + 16 + 3 = 35 => 35 * 2 = 70 => 70 -1 = 69 !!! (no space after last character)
##     :: If you are using escaped characters like '\[' - consider it as single character for counting
##     :: Remember wind is always stated from the direction it is blowing !
##     :: For North wind use the South pointing arrow
##     :: i.e. use opposite direction arrows from the wind direction.
##     :: sequence is "N to NNW", unknown, variable and N/A characters
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Example set - this is for ConkyWindNESW font option 3.
##  wx_wind_font_set='Y,Z,'\[','\\','\]',^,_,`,Q,R,S,T,U,V,W,X,'%','\#','-''
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
wx_wind_fontset=2




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Font to Images
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Convert ConkyWeather font to 3D images
## Please refer to conkywx wiki - now handled by lua




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## GnuPlot Graphs for temperature, pressure and dewpoint
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## We have ability to generate graphs for temperature, pressure and dewpoint
## Please refer to conkywx wiki - now handled by lua




## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Minimum time the script allows the website to be polled
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
minimum_fetch_time=850


## Uncomment for testing purposes. Fetches fresh data every time Conky is restarted.
## minimum_fetch_time=10
## minimum_fetch_time=3600

```

and [B]/home/arno/.conky/conkywx/arno-weather-graphic-template.sh

[/B]```
############################################################
## VinDSL | Paramvir | rev. 14-04-27 22:12 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
############################################################
## WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 2.0.0                     ##
############################################################
${voffset 0}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color6}${alignc -5}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -19}${offset 2}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.4}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}
${voffset -40}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.75}${color6}[D1T]${font}
${voffset 0}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.3}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -45}${goto 188}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color6}I${font}
${voffset 6}${font Ubuntu:size=22}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
${voffset 7}${alignc 3}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
${voffset 2}${alignc 3}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}
${voffset 8}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 82}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 83}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FM]${font}
####    Windvane
${lua main -n showcond -p 10, 620 -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 2 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
#${lua main -n showcond -p 10,620 -s 45 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 2 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
#${voffset -68}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 203}[MP]${font}
#${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}${goto 10}Temp${goto 60}Baro${goto 110}Dew${goto 160}Hum${goto 210}Wspd
#${lua main -n wxgraph -p 5,720 -d [GT] -c 0xEE4000:1}#
#${lua main -n wxgraph -p 55,720 -d [GP] -c 0xFF83FA:1}#
#${lua main -n wxgraph -p 105,720 -d [GD] -c 0x00FFFF:1}#
#${lua main -n wxgraph -p 155,720 -d [GH] -c 0x00FF00:1}#
#${lua main -n wxgraph -p 205,720 -d [GW] -c 0x00FF00:1}
${voffset 9}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
####    Present Condition  WX
${lua main -n showcond -p 10,525 -s 40 -w ci -t 1 -f [PIF] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [PIC] -j 0 }
####    Present Condition MOON
${lua main -n showcond -p 197, 650 -s 40 -w mi -f [MIF] -d [MROT] -i [MIC] }
####    Forecast Conditions
${lua main -n showcond -p 22,743 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D2F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D2I] }
${lua main -n showcond -p 82,743 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D3F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D3I] }
${lua main -n showcond -p 141,743 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D4F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D4I] }
${lua main -n showcond -p 198,743 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D5F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D5I] }
${voffset -40}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.5}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}
${voffset -1}${goto 24}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D2P]${goto 84}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D3P]${goto 144}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D4P]${goto 201}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D5P]${font}

```

he he ;) - this is applicable to all locations

use

```

own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager

```

comment out the override line - it will work fine !!!

cheers

```
####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
####
## Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
#
own_window_argb_visual yes
own_window_argb_value 255
```

### Post by os2 on 2014-05-01
^^^ Add the following list of sources to your Debian. It is called the Backports repository (BPO for short). In it you will find the latest stable kernel (3.13.10 at this time). I run everything Stable in Debian and if there is any reason to use something 'Bleeding Edge', I will compile from source.

```

deb http://http.debian.net/debian wheezy-backports main non-free contrib

```

No reason to not use Debian because of any of the reasons in the last few posts. Besides Arch Linux is run by Morons.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-05-05
So far I've discovered that this line needs to be added above the TEXT line in **.conkyrc.vindsl** file:

```
lua_load /usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua
```

In **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc** you will find the following line which does not work.

```
lua_load ./lib/conkywx.lua
```

I've got progress on the CPU and drive luas. Still no weather though. 

But that's progress I guess. :)

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-05-05
I think you'll find that the files you got from paramvir assumed you would be in the ***conkywx*** directory at the time of 'execution'.  In the case of my own system, I copied the entire contents of ***/usr/share/conkywx*** to ***~/.conky/conkywx***, and I run the startup script he provided (**./run-conkywx.sh**)from there.  In that location, there is a ***lib*** directory...

The change to specifying the entire path also works (provided it is an alteration or replacement of the original 'call') - so it matters not which way it is modified :) - so long as it *IS*.  I suggest keeping his file structure, though, as it simplifies finding things (like images and windvane backgrounds) and means you have a common 'starting point' for discussing any problems that might theoretically pop up.

Just my 2 cents worth - YMMV - hope it helps...

Now back to working on my 'panel' conky final details... :D

### Post by paramvir on 2014-05-06
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Thanks freebird54! I could not believe that cockpit conky! Amazing!!! :D

Got it ironed down now I think:

Just some minor mistakes.
And now just some major tweaking to do. :D



Yes freebird54 is correct...

Basically you can move everything related to data anywhere - but conkywx.lua and conkywx.sh need a definite path to the correct location. examples is the only folder that is movable - you need to then change the path for the files referenced in that folder.

If you give lua path /usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua and conkywx.sh path /usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh and then the locations for your config file and template everything should work fine.

There is an issue with windvane - work arounds - give full image path for the -i option - you have 21 compass images to choose from or remove the -i option completely for lua to draw the windvane.

In the mean time - if the other lua stuff is working like graphs then windvane should also work - I am using **os.getenv( "PWD" )** to get the current location of the program and infer the images location - but - some things do not seem to come out correct - though on my virtual systems they work. Need to wear my Sherlock Holmes hat ;-)

let me know how it goes

cheers

### Post by freebird54 on 2014-05-06
> **Cavsfan said:**
> 
```
############################################################
## VinDSL | Paramvir | rev. 14-04-27 22:12 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
############################################################
## WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 2.0.0                     ##
############################################################
[COLOR=#ff0000]####    Windvane
${lua main -n showcond -p 10, 620 -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 2 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${voffset -68}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}[/COLOR]
```



What I see is that you have not specified an image to work with  the **-i** should have **[LCI]** (which I think passes the setting from your conf file) rather than a number...  at least on my setups!  This also applies to **-a** I think, which has it passed by [**[LCAI]**

Give it a try - and cycle through the values in in the .conf to choose your look :)

here's the result....
```

[COLOR=#ff0000]####    Windvane
${lua main -n showcond -p 10, 620 -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i [LCI] -a [LCAI] -l 0xff0000:0.9 }[/color]

```
YMMV - and I make no representations as to the correctness of the rest of the call - I just reported the first difference I saw!

I'm amazed at how quickly he got version 2.0 up! If he needs testers I'm in there.

OK, this may seem kind of lame :lol: but since VinDSL made his new conky so condensed and efficent with combining the cpu usage with the temps 
and adding total upload/download to the network section I was having a hard time filling the screen. So here is what I came up with so far. 
I added a picture at the bottom. My wife is fond of cats and we have 2 so... the picture pretty much is true if you can see it. :lol: 
I had to compress the top stuff to make enough room for it and I wrapped some code around it that if Audacious is playing, that displays instead of the picture.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot1from2014-05-09.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot1from2014-05-09.php")

Then here is what it looks like when Audacious is playing. I added Output (stereo, etc.) and bitrate. While doing so when the song was not playing 
I was getting -1 for bitrate so after about 2 days I figured out how to get around that. At the same time when no song was playing Sample rate 
also showed as -1 so I added the same code to fix that. I didn't know for sure if I was ever going to get it done but finally it worked. :)

 [[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot214-05-09.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot214-05-09.php")

Here is the code of Tommy's that I modified (I feel like I plagiarised all of this stuff):

```
##################################
##     Audaciuos                ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -70}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}$font$
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate:${if_match ${audacious_frequency}==-1}${alignc} 0Hz${else}${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${endif}${alignr}${audacious_status}
Output: ${if_match ${audacious_channels}>1}Stereo ${else}Mono ${endif}${alignr}Bit Rate:#
${if_match ${audacious_bitrate}==-1} 0Kbs${else}${alignr} ${audacious_bitrate}${endif}${endif}
```

---

OK, this may seem kind of lame :lol: but since VinDSL made his new conky so condensed and efficent with combining the cpu usage with the temps 
and adding total upload/download to the network section I was having a hard time filling the screen. So here is what I came up with so far. 
I added a picture at the bottom. My wife is fond of cats and we have 2 so... the picture pretty much is true if you can see it. :lol: 
I had to compress the top stuff to make enough room for it and I wrapped some code around it that if Audacious is playing, that displays instead of the picture.

Then here is what it looks like when Audacious is playing. I added Output (stereo, etc.) and bitrate. While doing so when the song was not playing 
I was getting -1 for bitrate so after about 2 days I figured out how to get around that. At the same time when no song was playing Sample rate 
also showed as -1 so I added the same code to fix that. I didn't know for sure if I was ever going to get it done but finally it worked. :)

Here is the code of Tommy's that I modified (I feel like I plagiarised all of this stuff):



no problem Bro...no plagirizing here...it's FOSS...meant to be played with/improved by anyone...that's the
basic concept behind Linux...modify it all you want...):P

and yeah, the Cat comment is spot on....I got rid of mine when they kept chewing up the air lines to my
fish tanks....little furballs !!!

don't think Param is ready for testing yet...just throwing out some concepts for the future version...:D

if you want something to fill up some of that empty space I can send you a very kewl analog Lua clock....it's
fully animated, with moving gears & and hands.... you can change the clock face, gears, and hands to suit
your preferences....here is a screenie running under Mint 16.....

tommy

---

### Post by paramvir on 2014-05-09
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Thanks for the information Bro!

I'm amazed at how quickly he got version 2.0 up! If he needs testers I'm in there.

OK, this may seem kind of lame :lol: but since VinDSL made his new conky so condensed and efficent with combining the cpu usage with the temps 
and adding total upload/download to the network section I was having a hard time filling the screen. So here is what I came up with so far. 
I added a picture at the bottom. My wife is fond of cats and we have 2 so... the picture pretty much is true if you can see it. :lol: 
I had to compress the top stuff to make enough room for it and I wrapped some code around it that if Audacious is playing, that displays instead of the picture.


Yes there was an urgency once the website changed - 2.0 had to be pushed out ;)

VinDSL template fonts are really being downloaded quite fast - at least we now have a home for them :D

Some of the changes I made with the VinDSL template - changed the awk and cut into single sed - and a few similar tweaks... - moved the temps along with the cpu usage - HDD added the free and used % at the end... lots of scope - it is an excellent template ;-)

The Bargraphs for the memory and HDD need some tweaking - the red is not showing up correct - perhaps if you look at conkywx.lua and the function bargraph_styles - at the bottom there is a line

```

led_effect   = "e"
change this to ::
led_effect   = "r"

```

then the red shows up properly - I shall look into this later...

At present just concepts and ideas like tommy said and since Life pops in every now and then and spoils the fun :lolflag:

cheers

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-05-10
> **NM5TF said:**
> and yeah, the Cat comment is spot on....I got rid of mine when they kept chewing up the air lines to my
fish tanks....little furballs !!!

One cat who just happens to be 14 today I can be petting the heck out of him and then he will just bite me. :mad: I mean leave two puncture holes that bleed. Not kool! But can't just get rid of him. 
We have had him since he was about 6 weeks old. The other one's a girl and she's 6. They fight and play and keep each other going I guess. My wife want a Yorkie but not while we have these cats.
We had 2 dalmatians up until about 5 years ago and cats and dogs do not mix for reasons I cannot explain here ;) if you know what I mean.

I fixed the Audacious code in my previous post to align right the bitrate. I don't think I have enough room for that good looking clock you have.
Here is the small code to get the picture like I have:

```
##################################
##         Image                ##
##################################
${if_running audacious} ${else}#
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}ICAN HAZ CHEESEBURGER${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${offset -10}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/icanhascheeseburger3.jpeg -p 10,934 -s 240x200}${endif}
```

> **paramvir said:**
> Yes there was an urgency once the website changed - 2.0 had to be pushed out ;)

VinDSL template fonts are really being downloaded quite fast - at least we now have a home for them :D

Some of the changes I made with the VinDSL template - changed the awk and cut into single sed - and a few similar tweaks... - moved the temps along with the cpu usage - HDD added the free and used % at the end... lots of scope - it is an excellent template ;-)

The Bargraphs for the memory and HDD need some tweaking - the red is not showing up correct - perhaps if you look at conkywx.lua and the function bargraph_styles - at the bottom there is a line

```

led_effect   = "e"
change this to ::
led_effect   = "r"

```

then the red shows up properly - I shall look into this later...

At present just concepts and ideas like tommy said and since Life pops in every now and then and spoils the fun :lolflag:

cheers

I know what you mean Paramvir! I think you did a fantastic job! :D

I made the change you suggested and my Fantom 1TB USB drive now shows red in the last part. I have it too full. It only has 13% free space.
The rest are still green so, that is for telling you when your drive/memory is getting too full?

Thanks! :)

Hi paramvir and all  :)

Thought I should pop in here and give some well-deserved props -- great job on 2.0, bro!  

And, Kudos to everyone else, for your contributions to the project, too!!!  =d>

The 2.0 upgrade required very little tweaking.  All I needed to do is change the spacing on a single line in my script, and install:

```
Commit Log for Fri May  9 17:18:34 2014

Installed the following packages:

libhtml-parser-perl (3.71-1build1)
libhtml-tagset-perl (3.20-2)

```

Bingo!


[INDENT][[Img]http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-10-may-2014-1(650x520).png[/IMG]]("http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-10-may-2014-1.png")[/INDENT]


I've been busier than a one-arm wallpaper hanger, at work.  Plus, I was invited to be a 'Global Mod' in the Peppermint OS Forums.  

Heh!  Not enough hours in a day, you know?!?!?  ;)

I'm clawing my way back, slowly.  I can't believe I'm running an LTS (at the moment).  LoL!

Anyway, I just wanted to pop in here and let you know I haven't dropped off the face_of_the_earth.  I check this thread daily.  

Keep up the good work, all!

> **NM5TF said:**
> I don't know what is happening, but I keep getting duplicate posts...

That happened to me once. It happens when there is a pause when you click Submit Reply. Sometimes it will just sit there for a while.
I noticed that if you click it again it will double post. You just have to wait it out and it will post. It does the same with Review Post but there's no danger of a double post.
I've just learned that if I know I clicked it I wait until something happens. That *should* cure that problem.

Whoever said this is not rocket science was wrong. ](*,)This is on Trusty LTS 14.04.

I tried adding fan temps to be informative and also take up some room without a picture. I do like to know that my fans are working and it's nice to see the rpm too. 
I have many but only the first three are connected so they can be monitored. Fan 1 is the CPU fan.

So, based on this I figured "Aww how difficult could it be?" :rolleyes:

```
cavsfan@cavsfan-desktop:~$ sensors
coretemp-isa-0000
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 0:       +39.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)
Core 1:       +39.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)
Core 2:       +38.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)
Core 3:       +39.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)

f71882fg-isa-0a00
Adapter: ISA adapter
+3.3V:        +3.36 V  
in1:          +1.12 V  (max =  +2.04 V)
in2:          +0.92 V  
in3:          +0.77 V  
in4:          +0.98 V  
in5:          +1.10 V  
in6:          +0.91 V  
3VSB:         +3.36 V  
Vbat:         +3.31 V  
[COLOR=#ff0000]fan1:        2384 RPM
fan2:        2814 RPM
fan3:        2762 RPM[/COLOR]
fan4:           0 RPM  ALARM
temp1:        +31.0°C  (high = +85.0°C, hyst = +81.0°C)
                       (crit = +100.0°C, hyst = +96.0°C)  sensor = transistor
temp2:        +34.0°C  (high = +85.0°C, hyst = +81.0°C)
                       (crit = +95.0°C, hyst = +91.0°C)  sensor = thermistor
temp3:        +30.0°C  (high = +70.0°C, hyst = +68.0°C)
                       (crit = +85.0°C, hyst = +83.0°C)  sensor = transistor

```

${if_running audacious}
```

I take it out and it works, I put it in any where and "missing endif".

Here is what I want to add. BTW I deleted everything below TEXT and just had this code in the .conkyrc file and everything looked good but not when it's all together it breaks. :mad:

```
##################################
##          FAN SPEEDS          ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}FAN SPEEDS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan1' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan2' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan3' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
```

Any guidance would be much appreciated! I'm going to give up for now. ](*,)

I use so:

```
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}M${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}Fan 1: $alignr${platform [COLOR=#ff0000]f71882fg.2560[/COLOR] fan 1} RPM${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}M${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}Fan 2: $alignr${platform [COLOR=#ff0000]f71882fg.2560[/COLOR] fan 2} RPM${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}M${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}Fan 3: $alignr${platform [COLOR=#ff0000]f71882fg.2560[/COLOR] fan 3} RPM${font}
``` 

look at your system: "/sys/bus/platform/devices/[COLOR=#ff0000]f71882fg.2560[/COLOR][COLOR=#ff0000][/COLOR]"
replace it with something like "[COLOR=#ff0000]f71882fg.****[/COLOR]", as it is in your system. 


sorry, my english is not good.

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-05-14
@CavsFan got me to thinking about the use of images in conky...I had some room at the bottom
of VinDSL's system monitor, so I put an image there...but I didn't stop there...I modified my
Audacious player script so that if Audacious is running it reverts to the Audacious Player
info & the picture disappears.....the code is below if anyone wants it....

```
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Audacious Music Player
#--------------------------------------------------------------------

${if_running audacious}${font Dejavu Sans:bold:size=10}${color1}Audacious Music Player ${color4}${hr 2} $font${color3}
$color ${audacious_title 250}
${color green}${audacious_bar 10,250 } $color
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}    Sample Rate${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz${alignr}${audacious_status}
${else}${image /home/tommy3/Pictures/monument-valley.jpg -p 0,800 -s 310x70}
${endif}

```

of course you will have to specify the path to the image for ***your system***...and most likely adjust the image position 
& size to fit your own monitor...

use/modify it alll you desire..

screenie #1 is with picture
screenie #2 is with Audacious running

tommy

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-05-14
Tommy, I touched up my version of your Audacious code. The output displayed just because it was stopped. I fixed it to look for 1 = mono, and 2 = stereo and blank if it's stopped (value "-1").
(I needed the double lines to help fill my screen).

```
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -68}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}$font$
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${voffset 2}${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate:${if_match ${audacious_frequency}==-1}${alignc} 0Hz${else}${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${endif}${alignr}${audacious_status}
Output: ${if_match ${audacious_channels}==2}Stereo ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==1}Mono ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==-1} ${else}Unknown ${endif}${endif}${endif}${alignr}Bit Rate:#
${if_match ${audacious_bitrate}==-1} 0Kbs${else}${alignr} ${audacious_bitrate}${endif}${endif}
```


Also I guess today is the first time I have seen any weather alerts and wondered why it didn't display the line or the alerts.
Then I noticed that the alerts - green in color, which is pretty cool - was displaying 100 pixels or more above where it was supposed to be so I just had to tweak the Y component of the positioning of the alert:

 ```
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} ${color5}Alert:${font}${lua main -n scroller -f 12 -l 21 -s 5 -p 98,[COLOR=#ff0000]568[/COLOR] -o ${template7}}${else} ${color6}${hr 1}${endif}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
```

It scrolls and looks nice Paramvir!

The first screenie is with Audacious running but without a song playing and with the cursor pointing to the alert line.
The second is with a song playing and the Cairo Dock Impulse effect making the icons in the dock jump with the music.
It also has the Compiz effect Wizard going with the stars and fire etc.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-05-14134706.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-05-14134706.php")

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-05-14134845.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-05-14134845.php")

I guess the music wasn't fast enough to make the icons jump.

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-05-15
I had to make a couple of little tweaks in the weather on all of my Linux systems but no biggie:
1) The line beside CONKYWX was a couple pixels below where it should be.

```
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} ${color5}Alert:${font}${lua main -n scroller -f 12 -l 21 -s 5 -p 98,568 -o ${template7}}${else} [COLOR=#ff0000]${voffset -2}[/COLOR]${color6}${hr 1}${endif}
```
I just to add the part in red to fix that.

 2) Then since I had copied the contents of 
**/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf** to 
**/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf** so I could edit it without sudo.
 I had to change template7:
from this 
**template7 /tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert**
to this:
**template7 /tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert**

So I could see the tornado warning in my area. #-o

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-05-19
> **aquarius18 said:**
> Had plenty of animated (rotating) conky's yesterday - one of those Crownies must have been off ;) - should go and sue the brewery.........

But still, Paramvir may set us straight and tell us how to disable those unwanted vumeters in a more professional way.

well, since Param is busy making more eye kandy for us....even I could figure this out.....

open ***lua-template.sh*** and scroll down to these lines...

```
##---------- The present condition image --------------------------------------
#-------------------------
#-------------------------
# ${lua main -n showcond -p 0,200 -s 200 -w Ci -i [PIC] -j 1}
#${lua main -n showcond -p 2,197 -s 200 -w ci -t 1 -f [PIF] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [PIC] -j 1 }
#${lua main -n showcond -p 204,197 -s 200 -w mi -f [MIF] -d [MROT] -i [MIC] -j 1 }#

```

comment these lines to get rid of the duplicate Moon & Present Condition images....

and then scroll down to these lines....

```
##---------- Lua Thermometer and moon with back image -------------------------
# ${lua main -n thermometer -p 10,450 -t [PT] -l Temperature -s 1 -u [UU] }#
#${lua main -n vumeter -p 25,290 -d [VI] -u [UU] -o V -v h }#
# ${lua main -n vumeter -p 90,290 -d [UV] -u [UU] -o u -v h }#
# ${lua main -n vumeter -p 90,330 -d [HM] -u [UU] -o h -v h }#
#${lua main -n vumeter -p 225,290 -d [MV] -u [UU] -m [MP3] -o M -v h -g 0xBCEE68:1 }#

```

comment the lines pertaining to the ***vumeters***....

tommy

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-05-21
If any one is using Mint and wants the top to display a Mint logo, I found this. It only took me a couple of days to work the kinks out of it. :P

I noticed that on Mint the Ubuntu logo and name was still displayed so I thought I'd try to fix it.

I added this for color9:
```
color9 Green        #87CF3E
```

```
## Uncomment for Mint systems)
${voffset 32}${color9}${font conkysymbols:size=32}${voffset -35}u${font}${goto 59}${voffset -8}${font Neuropol:size=12.6}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'DESCRIPTION' | awk -F'"' '{print $2}'}${font}
${voffset -32}${offset 120}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
```

[[COLOR=blue]_Get the conkysymbol font here._[/COLOR]]("http://frugalware-art.org/content/show.php/Conky+Symbols?content=142593&PHPSESSID=ryngqtoxhzcxuby")

[[COLOR=blue]_Get the Neuropol font here._[/COLOR]]("http://www.dafont.com/neuropol.font")

If you do not want the conky version to display comment out that line and then you'll need to modify the voffset in the first line to get it to line up.

[[img]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-05-21160337.png[/img]](http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-05-21160337.php)
