from what I see in your code, you are only looking at the ***[COLOR="#FF0000"]top 3 Memory[/COLOR]*** processes.....is this what you want rather than
the "standard" readout of the top CPU & Mem processes ???

the standard readout would look like my screenie below....shows the top 5 CPU/MEM processes by PID, and
% of total.....

the code is as follows:

```
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# TOP PROCESSES top 5 running process sorted by cpu usage
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}TOP PROCESSES ${color4}${hr 2}
${color3}$font$font${goto 5}Process Name ${alignr}${goto 130}   PID ${goto 180}  CPU% ${goto 250} Mem%
${top name 1}${goto 130}${top pid 1} ${goto 180}${top cpu 1}   ${goto 250}${top mem 1}
$font${top name 2}${alignr} ${goto 130}${top pid 2}   ${goto 180}${top cpu 2}    ${goto 250}${top mem 2}
$font${top name 3}${alignr} ${goto 130}${top pid 3}   ${goto 180}${top cpu 3}    ${goto 250}${top mem 3}
$font${top name 4}${alignr} ${goto 130}${top pid 4}   ${goto 180}${top cpu 4}    ${goto 250}${top mem 4}
$font${top name 5}${alignr} ${goto 130}${top pid 5}   ${goto 180}${top cpu 5}    ${goto 250}${top mem 5}
```

screenie follows:

I was just wondering if I could put this in [COLOR=#ff0000]red[/COLOR] in what I posted above: (you have to scroll to the right to see the [COLOR=#ff0000]red[/COLOR])

```
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}PROCESSORS+TEMPS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${[COLOR=#ff0000]execi 1[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${[COLOR=#ff0000]execi 1[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${[COLOR=#ff0000]execi 1[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${[COLOR=#ff0000]execi 1[/COLOR] sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,172 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,190 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,207 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,223 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4}
```

Execi is defined as the same as exec but with specific interval. I just want it updated quicker if possible.

[http://conky.sourceforge.net/variables.html](http://conky.sourceforge.net/variables.html)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-06-18
> **NM5TF said:**
> I was going to suggest that earlier, but it seemed too obvious.....;)

LoL! :lol: haha

I changed it back to 2.0 seconds after I made the change described below because 1 second was freakishly fast.

All this time I was thinking those were the top CPU processes when they were the top MEM processes. So I tweaked the spacing of everything and changed that part to be CPU processes.
I managed to get all top 10 processes in there. :shock: I got rid of grub version as that was just taking up space. If I wanted to know that I know how to find out pretty readily. 

TOP and MEM conky elements both use the same components. TOP is CPU and MEM is well that is obvious. ;)

[http://conky.sourceforge.net/variables.html](http://conky.sourceforge.net/variables.html)

I changed it to this:

```
##################################
##      TOP CPU PROCESSES       ##
##################################
${voffset -24}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TOP CPU PROCESSES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 1}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 1}${alignr}${top cpu 1}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 2}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 2}${alignr}${top cpu 2}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 3}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 3}${alignr}${top cpu 3}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 4}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 4}${alignr}${top cpu 4}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 5}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 5}${alignr}${top cpu 5}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 6}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 6}${alignr}${top cpu 6}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 7}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 7}${alignr}${top cpu 7}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 8}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 8}${alignr}${top cpu 8}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 9}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 9}${alignr}${top cpu 9}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 10}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 10}${alignr}${top cpu 10}%${font}

```

Now it shows exactly what I wanted to see: the top processes using up the CPU.

### Post by paramvir on 2014-07-07
> **ramakanta said:**
> Finally my desktop conky looks like 

Temprature: ${hwmon 0 temp 1}°C

For some reason hwmon or platform is not working on some systems - work around

```

${execi 6 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/;s/\(.*\)\..*/\1/'}°C

```

Better solution with sed would be - as we do not need grep in this case :p
```

${execi 6 sensors | sed -n '/Core 0/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°C

```

This is for 1st core - change the zero to 1 for 2nd cpu and so on ...


cheers :)

### Post by paramvir on 2014-07-07
Here is the Processor cpu n gpu section. Take note of the sed usage and the nvidia data harvesting :D

```

# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# PROCESSORS cpu and gpu info
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}PROCESSORS ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}CPU1 ${execi 6 sensors | sed -n '/Core 0/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°C ${cpu cpu1}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 117,275 -w 90 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}$alignr ${cpugraph cpu1 12,90 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU2 ${execi 6 sensors | sed -n '/Core 1/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°C ${cpu cpu2}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 117,290 -w 90 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}$alignr ${cpugraph cpu2 12,90 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU3 ${execi 6 sensors | sed -n '/Core 2/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°C ${cpu cpu3}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 117,305 -w 90 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}$alignr ${cpugraph cpu3 12,90 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU4 ${execi 6 sensors | sed -n '/Core 3/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°C ${cpu cpu4}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 117,320 -w 90 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4}$alignr ${cpugraph cpu4 12,90 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}PCH: ${execi 6 sensors | sed -n '/PCH_CHIP_TEMP/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°C Fan: ${execi 6 sensors | sed -n '/fan2/{s/.*:[ ]*\([0-9]*\) RPM.*/\1/p}'} rpm ${alignr}Freq: ${freq_g cpu0}Ghz
GPU: ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="temperature.gpu" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}°C Fan: ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="fan.speed" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}% ${alignr}Mem: ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="memory.used" --format=csv,noheader,nounits} / ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="memory.total" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}mb

```

[ATTACH=CONFIG]254549[/ATTACH]

cheers

### Post by ramakanta on 2014-07-10

my conkyrc code..
```

# Create own window instead of using desktop (required in nautilus)
own_window yes
own_window_type override
own_window_hints below

# Use double buffering (reduces flicker, may not work for everyone)
double_buffer yes

# fiddle with window
use_spacer yes
use_xft no

# Update interval in seconds
update_interval 3.0

#Maximum Width of Window
maximum_width 320

# Minimum size of text area
# minimum_size 250 5

# Draw shades?
draw_shades no

# Text stuff
draw_outline no # amplifies text if yes
draw_borders no
font arial
uppercase no # set to yes if you want all text to be in uppercase

# Stippled borders?
stippled_borders 3

# border margins
border_margin 5

# border width
border_width 6

# Default colors and also border colors, grey90 == #e5e5e5
default_color FFFFCC

own_window_colour brown
own_window_transparent yes

#

# Text alignment, other possible values are commented
#alignment top_left
alignment top_right
#alignment bottom_left
#alignment bottom_right

# Gap between borders of screen and text
gap_x 12
gap_y 35
##gap_x 6      # Left/Right
#gap_y 32  # Top/Bottom


# &#732;TEXT  screen

TEXT
$color
${color CC9900}SYSTEM ${hr 2}$color
$nodename $sysname $kernel on $machine
#Hostname: $alignr$nodename
#Kernel: $alignr$kernel
Uptime: $alignr$uptime
#Temp: ${alignr}${acpitemp}C

${color CC9900}CPU ${hr 2}$color
Inte(R)Pentium(R) ACPIx86 Dual Core CPU E2180,2.0GHz

Total CPU:${color FF6600} ${cpu cpu0}% $color        CPU Freq: ${color #FF6600}  ${freq_g} GHz
${color 597DB2}${cpubar}$color
${cpugraph 000000 597DB2}
Core 1: ${freq 1} MHz      ${color #00ff00} Temprature:$color ${hwmon 0 temp 1}°C 
${color FFFF33}${cpu cpu1}% $color${color 597DB2}${cpubar cpu1}$color
Core 2: ${freq 2} MHz      ${color #00ff00} Temprature:$color ${hwmon 1 temp 2}°C 
${color FFFF33}${cpu cpu2}%$color ${color 597DB2}${cpubar cpu2}$color

NAME            PID     CPU%      MEM%
${color CCFFFF}${top name 1} ${top pid 1} ${top cpu 1} ${top mem 1}
${top name 2} ${top pid 2} ${top cpu 2} ${top mem 2}
${top name 3} ${top pid 3} ${top cpu 3} ${top mem 3}
${top name 4} ${top pid 4} ${top cpu 4} ${top mem 4}$color

${color CC9900}MEMORY ${hr 2}$color
RAM Used: ${mem}    RAM Free: ${memfree}/ ${memmax}
 RAM: $memperc%  ${color FF6600} ${membar 6}$color
Swap: $swapperc%   ${color FF6600} ${swapbar 6}$color

#${color CC9900}DISK ${hr 2}$color
#sdc5 ${fs_type} (Root): ${fs_free_perc /}% ${color FFFF33} ${fs_bar 6 /}$color
#sdc1 NTFS (Data): ${fs_free_perc /media/data}% ${color FFFF33} ${fs_bar 6 /media/data}$color

${color CC9900}FILESYSTEM ${hr 2}${color}
Root: ${alignr}${fs_free /} / ${fs_size /} 
${color FFFF33} ${fs_bar 6 /}$color
Home: ${alignr}${fs_free /home} / ${fs_size /home} 
${color FFFF33} ${fs_bar 6 /home}$color
#HDD0: ${alignr}${fs_free /media/HDD0} / ${fs_size /media/HDD0}
#${fs_bar 4 /media/HDD0}
#HDD1: ${alignr}${fs_free /media/HDD1} / ${fs_size /media/HDD1}
#${fs_bar 4 /media/HDD1}

${color CC9900}NETWORK (${addr ppp0}) ${hr 2}$color
Down: $color${downspeed ppp0}/s ${alignr}Up: ${upspeed ppp0}/s
${downspeedgraph ppp0 25,140 000000 ff0000} ${alignr}${upspeedgraph ppp0
25,140 000000 00ff00}$color
Total: ${totaldown ppp0} ${alignr}Total: ${totalup ppp0}
Inbound: ${tcp_portmon 1 32767 count} Outbound: ${tcp_portmon 32768
61000 count}${alignr}Total: ${tcp_portmon 1 65535 count}

#${color CC9900}LOGGING ${hr 2}$color
#${color 339900}${execi 30 tail -n3 /var/log/messages | fold -w50}$color



```

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-07-27
Well, I found the cause as to why I could not get the color to change no matter what I did. It's a little embarrassing though. :oops: I was trying to change the color on Precise and it would not change either.
Then I got to looking and found that I had copied about 3 conkys into the .conkyrc.vindsl file :oops: So when I changed the one on the top it read the one or two other ones which is why the color stayed the same.
One must watch that cntl+v closely. :p

It helps if you're not a [ATTACH=CONFIG]255094[/ATTACH] :p

So once I made sure I had one start and one end everything is hunky dory. :p

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-07-29
Ok so I got curious and noticed that the font symbol next to the fan speeds that I had added were the same as the PROCESSOR+TEMPS so I took a look at some other ones and found a suitable one.
I downloaded the Conky Symbol fonts [_here_]("http://scribusstuff.org/content/show.php/Conky+Symbols?content=142593") as they don't come standard. I put it in ~/.fonts.
This font symbol looks like a fan. But it made me adjust everything a bit because even though they are the same size as the other font, these are a little bigger.
Here is the code:
```
##################################
##          FAN SPEEDS          ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}FAN SPEEDS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font [COLOR=#FF0000]conkysymbols[/COLOR]:size=9.9}${color2}[COLOR=#ff0000]L[/COLOR]${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU FAN${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan1' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font [COLOR=#ff0000]conkysymbols[/COLOR]:size=9.9}${color2}[COLOR=#ff0000]L[/COLOR]${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan2' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font [COLOR=#ff0000]conkysymbols[/COLOR]:size=9.9}${color2}[COLOR=#ff0000]L[/COLOR]${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan3' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
```

Then I noticed that the things on the right under NETWORK were not flush against the right side even though there was a ${alignr} in it but I guess it wasn't just prior to the object. I moved it to that place and it did the job.

```
##################################
##          NETWORK             ##
##################################
${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}NETWORK${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}T${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Download${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totaldown eth0}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}[COLOR=#ff0000]${alignr}[/COLOR]${downspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}N${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Upload${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totalup eth0}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}[COLOR=#ff0000]${alignr}[/COLOR]${upspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}LAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}[COLOR=#ff0000]${alignr}[/COLOR]${addr eth0}${font}
#${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}[COLOR=#ff0000]${alignr}[/COLOR]${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}[COLOR=#ff0000]${alignr}[/COLOR]Demo mode
```

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-07-29115134.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-07-29115134.php")

I know it's not much but I thought I'd throw it out there. :)
I was able to copy and paste these changes into each of my 4 other Linux systems. But the touching up of the placement due to the increased size has to be done in each system.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-08-12
Ok, I have been thinking about this for a while now. I'm not sure if anyone else would care but I wanted to see the point release at the top if the Ubuntu version is a LTS version.
And after _*much*_ work on this one single line I think I've got it. I tried it on Precise 12.04 LTS and on Utopic 14.10 non-LTS and it seems to work, it took the *else* because I had to jack with it. :p

I realize this is a very minor thing but seeing 12.04.5 (for point release 5) instead of just 12.04 is what I was looking for and believe I found.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-08-12150025.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-08-12150025.php")

```
TEXT
${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 45 -c 0x000000:0.2 -p NA,NA}
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}1${offset 1}2${offset 1}.${offset 0}0${offset 0}4${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
[COLOR=#ff0000]${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${if_match "${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '9-11'}" == "LTS"} ${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '1-7'} ${else} ${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font} ${endif}${font}[/COLOR]
####
```

It is just that one red line which is fairly long. Wish I knew how to split lines but I'll not worry about that at this time.
I didn't take a pic of Utopic but it just displayed 14.10 so I think it works.
With all those awk, sed and cut commands I think I just lucked out because I barely understand what they do. :p

### Post by paramvir on 2014-08-13
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Well, I already found it doesn't work on 14.04 LTS as /etc/*release is different on this vs. 12.04.
Back to the drawing bored. :p

Maybe I'll figure this out but give me a couple of days.

:lolflag: What are friends for :guitar: 

Ok here is a script that figures the version number for any linux distro - using a different approach and a scripted file which should save a load of line dump.

To break lines in conky you can use the hash character "#" at the end of a line or for some "\" can also work but "#" works for all distros - I have used it in my templates....

Here is the script - I have called it osname.sh - you can call it what ever :p

[PHP]
#!/bin/bash

trim ()
{
    # clean out leading and trailing spaces in any variable
    shopt -s extglob
    local var=$1
    var=${var##+([[:space:]])}
    var=${var%%+([[:space:]])}
    shopt -u extglob
    echo -n "$var"
}


lsb1=$(perl -ne 'm?[\d\.]+? && print "$&"' /etc/issue)

lsb2=${lsb1//.}

# printf "%b\n" "$lsb1" >&2

# to handle distros that do not have a version number [rolling release] :D
if [[ $lsb2 -eq 0 || $lsb2 == "" ]]; then
    lsb1=$(perl -ne 'm?[\w\s\.\s]+? && print "$&"' /etc/issue)
fi

lsb1=$(trim "$lsb1")

printf "%b" "$lsb1"

[/PHP]

In this example it is placed in my lib folder under conkywx - you can place it again where ever ;)
This is using the same syntax as you are using - but you may need a small degree of tweakingssss

[PHP]
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}#
${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${pre_exec ./lib/osname.sh} ${font}
[/PHP]

tested on Mint 14, Ubuntu 9.10, 12.04, 14.04, Fedora 17, Arch linux

Earlier versions of Ubuntu were using lsb-release while the rest have been using os-release thingy - but - we do not use that anyway in my way :guitar: - so you had a difference in DISTRIB_RELEASE and PRETTY_NAME... and if you look at Mint - both the files refer to different systems - hence a standard way using /etc/issue and not /etc/issue.net as it does not exist on my working distro !!!


cheers ):P

---

```

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-08-13
> **paramvir said:**
> :lolflag: What are friends for :guitar: 

Ok here is a script that figures the version number for any linux distro - using a different approach and a scripted file which should save a load of line dump.

To break lines in conky you can use the hash character "#" at the end of a line or for some "\" can also work but "#" works for all distros - I have used it in my templates....

Here is the script - I have called it osname.sh - you can call it what ever :p

[PHP]
#!/bin/bash

trim ()
{
    # clean out leading and trailing spaces in any variable
    shopt -s extglob
    local var=$1
    var=${var##+([[:space:]])}
    var=${var%%+([[:space:]])}
    shopt -u extglob
    echo -n "$var"
}


lsb1=$(perl -ne 'm?[\d\.]+? && print "$&"' /etc/issue)

lsb2=${lsb1//.}

# printf "%b\n" "$lsb1" >&2

# to handle distros that do not have a version number [rolling release] :D
if [[ $lsb2 -eq 0 || $lsb2 == "" ]]; then
    lsb1=$(perl -ne 'm?[\w\s\.\s]+? && print "$&"' /etc/issue)
fi

lsb1=$(trim "$lsb1")

printf "%b" "$lsb1"

[/PHP]

In this example it is placed in my lib folder under conkywx - you can place it again where ever ;)
This is using the same syntax as you are using - but you may need a small degree of tweakingssss

[PHP]
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}#
${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${pre_exec ./lib/osname.sh} ${font}
[/PHP]

tested on Mint 14, Ubuntu 9.10, 12.04, 14.04, Fedora 17, Arch linux

Earlier versions of Ubuntu were using lsb-release while the rest have been using os-release thingy - but - we do not use that anyway in my way :guitar: - so you had a difference in DISTRIB_RELEASE and PRETTY_NAME... and if you look at Mint - both the files refer to different systems - hence a standard way using /etc/issue and not /etc/issue.net as it does not exist on my working distro !!!


cheers ):P

### Post by paramvir on 2014-08-13
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Thanks dude! :guitar:

Basically the idea was to not use so many tools when you can have a single tool - in my case I am using perl as it is way more suitable - but then we learn as we go along

I have 2 examples and you can change the blue coloured text as required to get the result. So you do not have to use cat, grep, awk etc... both are the same.

```

${pre_exec perl -ne 'if (/.***[COLOR=#0000ff]DESCRIPTION[/COLOR]**.*/)  {$_=~s/[^\d.]+//g; print "$_";}' /etc/*release }

or

${pre_exec perl -ne 'if (/.***[COLOR=#0000ff]PRETTY_NAME[/COLOR]**.*/)  {$_=~s/[^\d.]+//g; print "$_";}' /etc/*release }


```

In any event in the development version you perhaps will not find the issue file with version etc - it is a guess...
Many roads lead to Rome :lolflag:

---

### Post by paramvir on 2014-08-13
It also all depends what you want - just the number with or without the decimal or the full text after the "=" with quotes etc etc...

```

${pre_exec perl -ne 'if (/.*DESCRIPTION.*/) {$_=~s/.*="(.*?)"/$1/; print "$_";}' /etc/*release }

```

This will give you full text with LTS ;)

:popcorn:

### Post by paramvir on 2014-08-15
> **NM5TF said:**
> I tried Param's code under Debian 7.6 Wheezy....did NOT work....

but then neither did my code on previous page...

even tho both Ubuntu & Mint are based on Debian, there is some weirdness in it...
some things that work in Ubuntu or Mint just won't work in Debian for whatever reason...

That is correct I realised after googling - Debian has Description and not all capitals so we can modify the general syntax like so :: by making the pattern matching case insensitive - this will give you the number not the full text.

```

${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}#
${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4} #
${pre_exec perl -ne 'if ([COLOR=#0000cd]**/.*description.*/i**[/COLOR])  {$_=~s/[^\d.]+//g; print "$_";}' /etc/*release }${font}

```

For full text extraction you will do the same for the search part - add an "i" character
```

${pre_exec perl -ne 'if ([COLOR=#0000cd]**/.*description.*/i**[/COLOR]) {$_=~s/.*="(.*?)"/$1/; print "$_";}' /etc/*release }

```

Each distro has a slight nuance and one has to catch that - perhaps with some more looking into one can come with a more generic script.

Tommy does debian have /etc/issue file ??? all other distros I have seen have this file which has the release number included. But perhaps the development ones do not add the version number here yet.

cheers

---

### Post by paramvir on 2014-08-15
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Yeah I don't think this will work on a development version as it probably changes or it could change. But both the above will not work as this is what I have in 14.10 devel version:
```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ cat /etc/*release
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=14.10
DISTRIB_CODENAME=utopic
DISTRIB_[COLOR=#ff0000]DESCRIPTION[/COLOR]="Ubuntu Utopic Unicorn (development branch)"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="14.10 (Utopic Unicorn)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
[COLOR=#ff0000]PRETTY_NAME[/COLOR]="Ubuntu Utopic Unicorn (development branch)"
VERSION_ID="14.10"
HOME_URL="http://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="http://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"

```

It is OK with me though. I use LTS versions only except when there is a developmental version. I have 4 LTS versions - 2 Ubuntu and 2 Mint and the rolling developmental Ubuntu version. so I am good.

So I guess this road isn't going to Rome :lol: :lolflag:

But for any one else what you came up with will work nicely pulling point release on LTS systems and the version on non-LTS versions:
```
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4} ${pre_exec perl -ne 'if (/.*DESCRIPTION.*/)  {$_=~s/[^\d.]+//g; print "$_";}' /etc/*release }${font}
```

Thanks again Paramvir!

I wonder if any one else can confirm that that one line of code on a non-LTS system pulls the version.

He he he ok let me break things down a bit

Whether the line has LTS Ubuntu or Timbuktu on it - it does not matter...

Numbers extract only
[FONT=courier new]**if (/.*DESCRIPTION.*/i)**[/FONT] [[ here Perl is searching for a match with any line that has the word "description" - "i" makes it case insensitive - this is added in the new syntax]] [FONT=courier new]**{$_=~s/[^\d.]+//g; print "$_";}**[/FONT] this will pull out only numbers in this line and print it out. So if your line is [FONT=courier new]**DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 14.04 LTS .01"**[/FONT] - your result will be **[FONT=courier new]14.04.01[/FONT]**

Full text extract
first part is same as above:: [FONT=courier new]**{$_=~s/.*="(.*?)"/$1/; print "$_";}**[/FONT] this will only pick out data between the double quotes and give you **[FONT=courier new]Ubuntu 14.04 LTS .0[/FONT]1** from above line.

In other cases you may need to change "[FONT=courier new]**description**[/FONT]" with other search words like "[FONT=courier new]**release**[/FONT]" or "[FONT=courier new]**pretty_name**[/FONT]" or what ever.

You just need to do a [FONT=courier new]**cat /etc/*release***[/FONT] and pick the term and replace [FONT=courier new]"**description**[/FONT]".

Edit:
Oh and if you do want LTS text along with the version number use this
```

perl -ne 'if (/.*DESCRIPTION.*/i) {$_=~s/[^\d.]+//; $_=~s/"//g; print "$_";}' /etc/*release

```

cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2014-08-18
> **Robbobden said:**
> Howdy !  -  I've been using the legacy vindsl conky for several years.   I just downloaded conkywx 2.0 so I could get some weather data.   Things were looking really good until I added the Public IP line to 'Network'.  Now some icons and the weather trending graphs below are mis-placed.   I thought I might re-workd the wxtemp_template in the /tmp folder to move things around, but the data won't change.  I get a para telling me the data on disk has changed; reload or cancel.   So, it never gets changed.  I've attached a .jpg.   Advice anyone ?

Great job on the conkywx !!

RDL

Hi there

First - conkywx lua and conky images use direct placement - while rest of the conky information is relatively placed based on earlier directives.

Stuff in the temp folder will always change - you will need to make changes in the /use/share/conkywx/examples folder.

If you are using the vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh - make backup and then edit - look at the line that contains the **wxgraph** and **showcond** words - These are lines that need to be adjusted - the **-p** element - have a go and when u see it - you will know how to make changes and see how it looks...

let us know if this helps

cheers

---

### Post by paramvir on 2014-08-18
> **Cavsfan said:**
> I've seen instances where a # character before a line moves everything down a bit like it's still acting as a place holder even though you and I thought it was just a comment.
As when I tried to comment out the junk line I added and then added Parimvir's line. I had to delete my line or everything below was shifted down.

That is why I ask.

Yes Tommy is again correct  lol The # character at the end of the line - means no space character but end of line and it works as a line continuation character. If you are getting stuff shifting - check and adjust - may be a line some where is not properly formatted or an extra space character after # character...

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-08-19
nice try, but it's not a linear conversion....;)

it's true that 0 C = + 32 F...

however  100 C = + 212 F...

and to make it even more interesting  - 40 C = - 40 F :-k

to convert F to C you must do this....C = F - 32 X 5/9 

and to convert C to F do this........... F = C X 9/5 + 32

or just use an on-line converter...:lolflag:

tommy

I had the same problem with Mint Mate.....

you need to edit your /usr/share/conkywx/examples/short-02-conkyrc.sh file to expand the vertical size so it doesn't cut off
the forecast icons...try to increase the size of this line.....maybe go from 532 to something like 700 or 750:

```
${voffset -14}${lua main background 45 0xFF83FA 0.1 NA 532}
${voffset -14}${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x000000:0.2 -p NA,NA}
```

and then you might also have to edit the short-02-image-template.sh file to adjust the icon positions...

the first number after the -p is the Horizontal position...the 2nd number is the Vertical position....

and the numbers after the -s are the icon size in Pixels....

```
##---------- Forecast images --------------------------------------------------
${image [D2I] -p  15, 447 -s 40x40}#
${image [D3I] -p  80, 447 -s 40x40}#
${image [D4I] -p 145, 447 -s 40x40}#
${image [D5I] -p 210, 447 -s 40x40}#
${image [D6I] -p 275, 447 -s 40x40}
${image [D7I] -p 340, 447 -s 40x40}
${image [D8I] -p 405, 447 -s 40x40}
```

tommy

---

Ok he he ;) - I am writing this from my old gma500 thingy - finally did a total custom install - 12.04 LTS command line - then X and openbox - little thing has 1 gb ram and I have to be rather frugal he he ;)

To run presentation videos using mplayer2
```

#!/bin/bash


killall conky


mplayer -fs -af volnorm=2:0.75 -lavdopts threads=4 -zoom -framedrop -stop-xscreensaver "$@"


/home/param/bin/conkywx_2.0.0_sa/run-conkywx.sh & 

```

We do not have a propah gma500 driver to use the graphich chipset - but it works with 12.04.5 but not with 14.04 - so it is good to go - full screen HD video haaaaaaa

OK now the QnA part .....
if you get conky showing a part of the screen like in [COLOR=#000000]Utopic Mate - increanse the [/COLOR]text_buffer_size by 1000 and then see and adjust.

You should be able to adjust the desktop font size in the settings - some developer perhaps is using lower rez display or something... happens all the time.

I have noticed when changing Desktop Environment or Distro - sometimes the alignment goes turtle - changes made here bring things back to normal. Font size of 10 works great in XFCE4 Arch - maybe will be different for another DE - in Ubuntu - 9.5 works magic...

```

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Change the xftfont size value if the template is not
# appearing aligned
#-----------------------------------------------------------
xftfont DejaVu Sans Mono:size=9.5
# xftfont DroidSansFallback:size=10
# xftfont DejaVu Sans Mono:size=10
# xftfont FreeSerif:size=10
# xftfont FreeSans:size=10

```

I have various fonts here since I was testing Unicode and esoteric languages :p

Let me know how it goes ;)):P

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-08-28
Well after about a days work I've made progress on the VinDSL conky :D I'll get to Paramvir's conky next.
Here is what I've got so far:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot1328.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot1328.php")

I had to change this and it made it work without the audacious code showing up in error:
```
## Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
# max_user_text 20000
max_user_text 52768
```

Not to mention changing almost every font size there was. ;)

Also had a semi-ignorant question to ask. :lol:
These 2 files and 1 folder at the top here should be deleted right? They are from the earlier version of CONKYWX. I know everything is now in **/usr/share/conkywx**.

[ATTACH=CONFIG]256524[/ATTACH]

And I always loved me some South City Midnight Lady. :)

conkywx_internal and system.conf are still used - delete them and they get created again. I am using tmpfs for tmp folder and so does not survive a reboot - so - anything that needs to survive that - is in the ~/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal directory.

Living on the bleeding edge one learns to watch carefully at the updates - font system update may make fonts looking weird. i have not had a font issue for quite some time - perhaps a year or so but it happens...

> **paramvir said:**
> conkywx_internal and system.conf are still used - delete them and they get created again. I am using tmpfs for tmp folder and so does not survive a reboot - so - anything that needs to survive that - is in the ~/.config/conkywx/conkywx_internal directory.

Living on the bleeding edge one learns to watch carefully at the updates - font system update may make fonts looking weird. i have not had a font issue for quite some time - perhaps a year or so but it happens...

Thanks Paramvir! I'll leave them alone then. Yes, the fonts bit me on both developmental systems. But, it happens I see. I thought it was just me.
Thanks for the update! :)

### Post by paramvir on 2014-09-28
> **awoltering said:**
> Hi there,

somewhere on the line mijn conkywx produces a failure and and therefore Lua parts of conky won`t show up (see screenshot) 
when I run conkywx from the terminal with debugging on I can`t see anything wrong with the logfiles , but in the terminal conky gives the following error message:

```
Conky: llua_do_call: function conky_main execution failed: attempt to call a nil value
```

Is there somebody that knows what to do?
it could be that I had an update of Lua i have two version of it 5.1 and 5.2, should i remove one of them?

thanks in advance

Greetings ;-)

Lua being what lua is - it gives some error message at least - and this is the best it can do - with the desire to keep it lean and mean - anyway - it just means that some where you are calling lua function and you are not calling it correctly.

For starters - look at the conkywx/examples folder - see how lua is being called in short-02-conkyrc.sh

In your posted image one can see no lua output - but try running conky from a terminal. Just as conky starts - it will post some error and then keep posting this line of nil value later on. Post that information - just the lines of conky when it starts.

cheers

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-10-09
this may be a little off-topic as this is a Ubuntu forum/thread....8-[

I installed my 5th *nix distro a week or so ago.....Fedora 20

and of course, I wanted to install conkywx....I downloaded the RPM version from the wiki page with no problems...or so I thot....

whenever it was told to start all it showed was 1 line of text & a bunch of error messages complaining of an invalid config file....

after spending several days looking for the offending/missing culprit, I finally gave up & had to email the Master for help....thanx Param ):P

he traced it to a missing module in the perl section where it looks for the time....after searching for & installing the missing module
everything is now working except for the wlan info....need to find out how Fedora handles things differently from the Debian-based
distros....using wlan0 returns nothing in Fedora, but works perfectly in Debian-based distros.....need more research....

### Post by krasenpaskaa on 2014-10-12
I apologize for asking something that must seem super obvious but I have been reading and trying for days now and I just cant figure out how you guys got the weather working.

I am very new to conky so I don't really know what I am doing. I read the guide on this thread only to find that there did not seem to be any way to download the actual .conkyrc.

I read up more and it turns out that script is outdated so I find some new version of the script that uses the google api. Turns out that also is apparantly outdated.

I understand I am rambling but I am super confused right now and could really use some help, please :)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-10-12
> **krasenpaskaa said:**
> I apologize for asking something that must seem super obvious but I have been reading and trying for days now and I just cant figure out how you guys got the weather working.

I am very new to conky so I don't really know what I am doing. I read the guide on this thread only to find that there did not seem to be any way to download the actual .conkyrc.
I spent some more time and I eventually found some version of VinDSL's script that seems to work rather well for me, except that the weather part keeps spamming "conkyForecast not found".
I read up more and it turns out that script is outdated so I find some new version of the script that uses the google api. Turns out that also is apparantly outdated.
I then find something called conkywx but I can't figure out how to incorporate that with the VinDSL thing I got going.

I understand I am rambling but I am super confused right now and could really use some help, please :)

Install Paramvir's CONKYWX version 2 from here: [http://foreverquest.blogspot.com/2014/04/conkywx-two-released-conkywx-conky.html](http://foreverquest.blogspot.com/2014/04/conkywx-two-released-conkywx-conky.html)
Then in terminal you can enter conkywx and enter 2 to brink up Paramvir's conkywx wiki. A lot is explained there.

Then the new VinDSL conky files will be in /usr/share/conkywx/examples along with some of Paramvir's conkys.

You will need to modify the VINDSL .conkyrc file to suit your screen size etc. 

I could give you a copy of my files if you wanted them. I have a 1920x1200 pixel monitor.

You should have **conky-all** and **lm-sensors** installed and have ran **sudo sensors-detect** in terminal to pull some of the items conky uses.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2014-10-12134504.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2014-10-12134504.php")

Let us know if you need more info. :)

---

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-10-15
> **krasenpaskaa said:**
> I apologize for asking something that must seem super obvious but I have been reading and trying for days now and I just cant figure out how you guys got the weather working.

I am very new to conky so I don't really know what I am doing. I read the guide on this thread only to find that there did not seem to be any way to download the actual .conkyrc.
I spent some more time and I eventually found some version of VinDSL's script that seems to work rather well for me, except that the weather part keeps spamming "conkyForecast not found".
I read up more and it turns out that script is outdated so I find some new version of the script that uses the google api. Turns out that also is apparantly outdated.
I then find something called conkywx but I can't figure out how to incorporate that with the VinDSL thing I got going.

I understand I am rambling but I am super confused right now and could really use some help, please :)

I would save your current VinDSL configuration somewhere like a USB thumb drive...

download and install conkywx from here:

[http://foreverquest.blogspot.in/search/label/conkywx](http://foreverquest.blogspot.in/search/label/conkywx)

select the VinDSL templates from conkywx....load the USB drive and overwrite the conkywx files with your saved
files from the USB drive...save everything....restart conkywx...

the very helpful conkywx wiki is located here:

[https://bitbucket.org/plikhari/conkywx_pub/wiki/Home](https://bitbucket.org/plikhari/conkywx_pub/wiki/Home)

if you need further help, this is the BEST place to come for help...besides the frequent posters, the authors of
the scripts hang out here from time to time...:KS

tommy

### Post by NM5TF on 2014-10-19
you most likely have something like this to get your point release....

[CODE]
$alignr${color3}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'DESCRIPTION' | awk -F'"' '{print $2}'}
/CODE]

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-10-20
> **NM5TF said:**
> you most likely have something like this to get your point release....

[CODE]
$alignr${color3}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'DESCRIPTION' | awk -F'"' '{print $2}'}
/CODE]

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-10-20
Forgot - it was on Linux Mint 17 Qiana
```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ cat /etc/*release
DISTRIB_ID=LinuxMint
DISTRIB_RELEASE=17
DISTRIB_CODENAME=qiana
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Linux Mint 17 Qiana"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="14.04.1 LTS, Trusty Tahr"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 14.04.1 LTS"
VERSION_ID="14.04"
HOME_URL="http://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="http://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
cat: /etc/upstream-release: Is a directory

```

That last line is where it's coming from. 
/etc/upstream-release/lsb-release

```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ cat /etc/upstream-release/lsb-release
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=14.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=trusty
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 14.04 LTS"cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ 

```

It does not hurt anything. Guess I'll just leave it alone. Unless someone has an answer.

### Post by Al1000 on 2014-10-25
I thought it would be more interesting to get conky to display the user rather than the PID for the top running processes. But with some chromium-browser processes, conky says the user is root, whereas top in the terminal says it's me.

I don't understand why conky's output should be different from what top says. Does anyone know how I might get conky to display the user correctly?

Here is my conky.conf, and a screenshot of top reporting the users of all chromium-browser processes to be me, and conky reporting some of them to be root.

```
alignment top_right
background true
border_width 1
cpu_avg_samples 2
default_color green
default_outline_color white
default_shade_color white
draw_borders no
draw_graph_borders no
draw_outline no
draw_shades no
use_xft yes
xftfont Bitstream Vera Sans Mono:size=10
gap_x 5
gap_y 110
minimum_size 5 5
net_avg_samples 2
double_buffer yes
out_to_console no
own_window yes
own_window_argb_visual yes
own_window_argb_value 100
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
stippled_borders 0
update_interval 03.0
uppercase no
use_spacer none
show_graph_scale no
show_graph_range no

TEXT
${color}${scroll 16 $nodename - $sysname $kernel on $machine | }
${color white}$hr
${color}Uptime:$uptime
${color white}$hr
${color}RAM Usage:$mem/$memmax - $memperc% ${membar 7}
Swap Usage:$swap/$swapmax - $swapperc% ${swapbar 7}
${color white}$hr 
${color}CPU 1: ${cpu cpu1}% ${cpubar cpu1}
CPU 2: ${cpu cpu2}% ${cpubar cpu2}
Frequency: ${freq}MHz
CPU 1 Temp: ${hwmon 1 temp 1}C    CPU 2 Temp: ${hwmon 2 temp 1}C
${color white}$hr
${color}File systems: ${fs_free /}/${fs_size /} ${fs_bar 6 /}
${color white}$hr
${color}Networking:
Up:${upspeed eth1}k/s       Down:${downspeed eth1}k/s
${color white}$hr
${color}Processes:$processes     Running:$running_processes
${color white}$hr
${color}Name               CPU%   MEM%  USER
${top name 1} ${top cpu 1} ${top mem 1}  ${top user 1}
${top name 2} ${top cpu 2} ${top mem 2}  ${top user 2}
${top name 3} ${top cpu 3} ${top mem 3}  ${top user 3}
${top name 4} ${top cpu 4} ${top mem 4}  ${top user 4}
${top name 5} ${top cpu 5} ${top mem 5}  ${top user 5}
```

[ATTACH=CONFIG]257492[/ATTACH]

### Post by paramvir on 2014-10-26
> **NM5TF said:**
> you most likely have something like this to get your point release....

```

$alignr${color3}${pre_exec [COLOR=#0000ff]**cat**[/COLOR] /etc/*release | [COLOR=#008000]**grep**[/COLOR] 'DESCRIPTION' | [COLOR=#a52a2a]**awk**[/COLOR] -F'"' '{print $2}'}
/CODE]

Like they say many roads to a city [Rome ???] lol

Cheaper approaches perhaps ... :p

[code]
SED
$alignr${color3}${pre_exec sed -n 's/"//g;s/.*DESCRIPTION=//p' /etc/*release }

AWK
$alignr${color3}${pre_exec awk '$1 ~ /DESCRIPTION/ { gsub(/.*DESCRIPTION=|\"/,""); print}' /etc/*release }

```

cheers  :guitar:

```
##################################
##      TOP CPU PROCESSES       ##
##################################
${voffset -21}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}TOP CPU PROCESSES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Name${goto 100}CPU${goto 163}MEM${alignr}USER${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 1}${goto 100}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 1}${goto 160}${top cpu 1}%${alignr}${top user 1}${font}

> **paramvir said:**
> Like they say many roads to a city [Rome ???] lol

Cheaper approaches perhaps ... :p

```

SED
$alignr${color3}${pre_exec sed -n 's/"//g;s/.*DESCRIPTION=//p' /etc/*release }

AWK
$alignr${color3}${pre_exec awk '$1 ~ /DESCRIPTION/ { gsub(/.*DESCRIPTION=|\"/,""); print}' /etc/*release }

```

cheers  :guitar:


Both of those yield this:
```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ sed -n 's/"//g;s/.*DESCRIPTION=//p' /etc/*release
**Ubuntu Vivid Vervet (development branch)**
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ awk '$1 ~ /DESCRIPTION/ { gsub(/.*DESCRIPTION=|\"/,""); print}' /etc/*release
**Ubuntu Vivid Vervet (development branch)**
```

Which is good but that cannot be displayed in the space available.

This is what is displaying and it's all I have room for in VinDSL's conky:
```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'
**15.04**
```

I think that error was a fluke. I am not getting it any more in the new release Vivid.

### Post by aquarius18 on 2014-11-08
Just found this app, designed for WordPress and recently updated (2014-10-26). 

[https://wordpress.org/plugins/wunderground/](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wunderground/)

It connects to Wunderground API. 

Wondering if it is worth contacting the author of this app to get some more insight how they are fetching data from wunder? Contact via [https://profiles.wordpress.org/katzwebdesign/](https://profiles.wordpress.org/katzwebdesign/)

And this from OpenWeatherMap: [http://openweathermap.org/api](http://openweathermap.org/api)

Did a 7 day forecast for my place Caloundra to fetch an XML file: [http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast/daily?q=Caloundra&mode=xml&units=metric&cnt=7](http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast/daily?q=Caloundra&mode=xml&units=metric&cnt=7)

To do this for your own location (eg Berlin) replace Caloundra in the URL above with Berlin

JSON: [http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast?lat=-26.79&lon=153.1](http://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast?lat=-26.79&lon=153.1)

Cheers / Frank

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-12-28
Something else I've realized: while you are making changes to the VinDSL conky with say Audacious running at the bottom, 
by clicking on save on the .conkyrc file it cuts off about a half inch on my screen. Don't panic and think that stuff is being cut off 
at the bottom of the conky. When you boot up or logon the conky goes all the way from the top to the bottom on my screen.

However when I make changes to it and save it to refresh it or whether I restart it via **killall -SIGUSR1 conky** it will cut the bottom 1/2 inch off.

I sound like I'm repeating myself but what I've found that really works is when you are finished with changes, simply logout and back in to see if everything displays as desired.
It does indeed make a difference for some reason. Perhaps VinDSL could explain why that happens. I just know it does but do not know why.

That's a tip from a pawn in this here chess game. :)

PS: This was proven to me when I added the titles to the parts in Top CPU processes in Trusty. I hadn't added the titles because I thought it would cut off the bottom when Audacious displays but I added them and it does not.
Actually if my eyes are accurate it's about 23 pixels at the bottom and not 1/2 inch. But a lot will fit in 23 pixels. :)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-12-29

### Post by Cavsfan on 2014-12-30
I just tested on Vivid 15.04 the restart conky and the bottom 23 pixels (at least on my screen) disappears. I restarted the conky and the bottom disappeared so I gave it about 5 hours and it was still missing.

Only a logout or restart will restore the full conky all the way to the bottom. This will happen on every Linux version after Precise.

### Post by jdallara on 2015-01-02
Hello All,

  Quick question:  is there a way to assign the output from an execi function to a local variable?  I use execi to get my cpu temperatures that I'd like to use in two places and I want the same value to be used for both, which can't be guranteed if I use two execi calls.

CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 6 sensors -f | sed -n '/Core 0/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°F

Thanks,

Jon.

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-01-03
> **jdallara said:**
> Hello All,

  Quick question:  is there a way to assign the output from an execi function to a local variable?  I use execi to get my cpu temperatures that I'd like to use in two places and I want the same value to be used for both, which can't be guranteed if I use two execi calls.

CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 6 sensors -f | sed -n '/Core 0/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°F

Thanks,

Jon.

Here is how I get my cpu temps and more. You can probably cut out what you need. If template9 is C it pulls Celsius, if it's F it pulls Fahrenheit.  

```
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##
##################################
${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}PROCESSORS+TEMPS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,136 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,151 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,165 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 90,180 -w 120 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4} 
```

I take no credit for this code either. :)

---

### Post by jdallara on 2015-01-03
Guess I should have been clearer as to what I was looking for (was in a rush at the time).  So here is my code section:

```
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##
##################################
${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}PROCESSORS+TEMPS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 6 sensors -f | sed -n '/Core 0/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°F${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}%${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 6 sensors -f | sed -n '/Core 1/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°F${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}%${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 6 sensors -f | sed -n '/Core 2/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°F${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execi 6 sensors -f | sed -n '/Core 3/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°F${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2}GPU   ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="temperature.gpu" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}°C  ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="fan.speed" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}% ${nvidia gpufreq}MHZ ${alignr}Mem ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="memory.used" --format=csv,noheader,nounits} / ${execi 6 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="memory.total" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}M${font}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 128,175 -w 125 -s 4 -f cpu -a cpu1}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 128,192 -w 125 -s 4 -f cpu -a cpu2}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 128,209 -w 125 -s 4 -f cpu -a cpu3}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 128,226 -w 125 -s 4 -f cpu -a cpu4}

```

What I would like to do is be able to save the output from the sensors execi call and the ${cpu cpuN} call and be able to use them as input to the bargraph call.  I want to be able to have a display where I have 2 bargraphs, the top one showing the % usage and the bottom one showing the temp for each CPU.  See the attached screen shot.  Instead of the current bargraphs which are wide, I want to have 2 narrow ones like I'm using for the disk usage bar graphs.  To do this it would be useful to be able to use the same values for the text and the bargraphs since they can change quite rapidly, and it removes having to do 4 more system calls.

BTW, just started using Mint - Love it!

Thanks,
Jon.

[ATTACH=CONFIG]258980[/ATTACH]

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-01-05

Just curious, but why do you have a 8GB swap file? I have 1GB and it is never used not even 1% of it. It seems swap files are used on older computers and yours is much newer than mine.
I don't even show swap in my conky as I need that unused space for more important things.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2015-01-05103423.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2015-01-05103423.php")

---

### Post by jdallara on 2015-01-05
Force of habit.  For 25+ years its always been swap = memory.  The only time it gets used is when I'm running Windoze.  On the other hand, its a 1TB drive.  I have it show because its a flag something is wrong if it suddenly starts getting used, and I don't need the space for anything else right now.

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-01-05
> **jdallara said:**
> Force of habit.  For 25+ years its always been swap = memory.  The only time it gets used is when I'm running Windoze.  On the other hand, its a 1TB drive.  I have it show because its a flag something is wrong if it suddenly starts getting used, and I don't need the space for anything else right now.

Swap file in Linux and page file in Windows are 2 entirely different things. If you have 8GB mem and you suspend your Linux system and do not use hibernation you don't really need a swap file or have to have one. 
If you do hibernate then you do need a swap file. I have just 4GB of RAM and I have never seen my swap file used at all and I use suspend mode not hibernation.

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2211150](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2211150)

But it will not hurt to have a swap file.

---

### Post by jdallara on 2015-01-06
Actually HP-UX sys admin on workstations and data base servers for most of those 25 years.  When 16MB of memory and 100MB hard drive would cost a years salary (almost ;).  Can remember having to get the director of our division to approve a purchase order for a 500MB disk drive because at >$15K it was a capitol equipment purchase (akin to buying a large piece of machinery).  Swap files used to be used, but like you say, now a days using the swap file is an indication that you need another stick of RAM.

On another note, just changed from Ubuntu 14.10 to Linux Mint 17.1 and the CPU frequency doesn't get picked up by conky correctly again.  Is yours working correctly?

### Post by jdallara on 2015-01-06
Here is what I have:

```
${voffset 2}${font Logoz\-Librariez:size=10}${color2}e${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color3}${offset 5}${pre_exec more /proc/cpuinfo | grep -m 1 'model name' | awk '{print $4$5" "$6" "$8}'}${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
```

It worked before, but only after upgrading to Ubuntu 14.10.  The problem is that it is only reporting the 'factory' frequency.  The output of turbostat is closer:

```
jon@mothership:~$ sudo turbostat
cor CPU    %c0  GHz  TSC SMI    %c1    %c3    %c6    %c7 CTMP PTMP   %pc2   %pc3   %pc6   %pc7  Pkg_W  Cor_W GFX_W
         82.51 4.20 3.40   0   1.46   4.85   1.58   9.60   60   60   0.00   0.00   0.00   0.00  73.42  64.97  0.00
  0   0  43.82 4.20 3.40   0   3.64  13.71   4.50  34.32   42   60   0.00   0.00   0.00   0.00  73.42  64.97  0.00
  1   1  99.89 4.20 3.40   0   0.05   0.04   0.01   0.01   60
  2   2  91.62 4.20 3.40   0   1.24   3.62   1.06   2.46   55
  3   3  94.71 4.20 3.40   0   0.93   2.03   0.74   1.59   56
cor CPU    %c0  GHz  TSC SMI    %c1    %c3    %c6    %c7 CTMP PTMP   %pc2   %pc3   %pc6   %pc7  Pkg_W  Cor_W GFX_W
         82.39 4.20 3.40   0   1.51   4.70   1.32  10.09   62   62   0.00   0.00   0.00   0.00  71.98  63.55  0.00
  0   0  38.72 4.20 3.40   0   4.54  15.87   4.38  36.48   49   62   0.00   0.00   0.00   0.00  71.98  63.55  0.00
  1   1 100.00 4.20 3.40   0   0.00   0.00   0.00   0.00   62
  2   2  90.82 4.20 3.40   0   1.49   2.93   0.89   3.87   55
  3   3 100.00 4.20 3.40   0   0.00   0.00   0.00   0.00   57
```

The first line under the header is the overall usage stats while each additional line is each core.  You'll notice that the CPU frequency is 4.2GHz, not 3.4GHz which is what conky reports and is the non-turbo frequency from the factory (factory turbo is 3.8GHz).  Also, normally when one of the cores is running less than 90-100% it down clocks to 1.2GHz or less.  Here is the output of turbostat when I had it running under Ubuntu 14.10:

```
    Core     CPU Avg_MHz   %Busy Bzy_MHz TSC_MHz     SMI  CPU%c1  CPU%c3  CPU%c6  CPU%c7 CoreTmp  PkgTmp Pkg%pc2 Pkg%pc3 Pkg%pc6 Pkg%pc7 PkgWatt CorWatt GFXWatt 
       -       -    3359   81.78    4107    3406       0    0.79    4.03    1.67   11.73      56      56    0.00    0.00    0.00    0.00   55.70   48.27    0.00
       0       0    4107  100.00    4107    3406       0    0.00    0.00    0.00    0.00      56      56    0.00    0.00    0.00    0.00   55.70   48.27    0.00
       1       1    1115   27.14    4107    3406       0    3.14   16.11    6.70   46.90      42
       2       2    4107  100.00    4107    3406       0    0.00    0.00    0.00    0.00      50
       3       3    4107  100.00    4107    3406       0    0.00    0.00    0.00    0.00      49


```

Notice core 1 is running at 1.115GHz and the overall CPU frequency is 3.359GHz. At this time I had it running at 4.1GHz, not 4.2GHz as it is running now.   (The 3.406GHz under the TSC column is the cache memory frequency).  The other utility I've used is powertop which will display all the frequency states the cores were in during any given period.  It isn't working at all under Mint.

Thanks, Jon

### Post by paramvir on 2015-01-14
OK people new version is out [B]Conkywx THREE
[/B]
[[IMG]http://www.zimagez.com/miniature/conkywx2.jpg[/IMG]]("http://www.zimagez.com/zimage/conkywx2.php")  [[IMG]http://www.zimagez.com/miniature/conkywx1.jpg[/IMG]]("http://www.zimagez.com/zimage/conkywx1.php")

**Conkywx THREE - has many additions and changes - **type [SIZE=2][FONT=courier new]**conkywx --help**[/FONT][/SIZE] in a terminal to view improved help on a web browser

Many new things like CIMSS and wunderground storm images - CIMSS images have wind barbs for wind strength and direction.

Many sources for data - current and forecast.

Please refer to link **[http://foreverquest.blogspot.in/2015/01/conkywx-three-released-conkywx-conky.html](http://foreverquest.blogspot.in/2015/01/conkywx-three-released-conkywx-conky.html)**


Cheers

Thanks Sean ;)
It can take time - but not that much - which combination are you using - 15 possibilities huahahahaha :lolflag:

What does your today_date_format in conf file look like??? do not comment it - leave it as it is in default

I must say - wunderground can get pretty slow - intellicast, msn, metar are fast add xml_id and you are good to go. Nearly everyone is using METAR data for current conditions if they can help it - you should too - plusssss you will update faster than the websites - if you feel pws station will help like get you UV then use xml_ID  and for forecast use intellicast - in which case history data will also come from intellicast. Intellicast forecast data is coming from wunderground duh... msn has it's own sources so you have another perspective else we only have wunderground and METAR + pws as data source ;)


cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2015-01-15
> **jdallara said:**
> Guess I should have been clearer as to what I was looking for (was in a rush at the time).  So here is my code section:

```
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##
##################################
${execi 6 sensors -f | sed -n '/Core 0/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°F
${lua main -n bargraph -p 128,175 -w 125 -s 4 -f cpu -a cpu1}#

```

What I would like to do is be able to save the output from the sensors execi call and the ${cpu cpuN} call and be able to use them as input to the bargraph call.  I want to be able to have a display where I have 2 bargraphs, the top one showing the % usage and the bottom one showing the temp for each CPU.  See the attached screen shot.  Instead of the current bargraphs which are wide, I want to have 2 narrow ones like I'm using for the disk usage bar graphs.  To do this it would be useful to be able to use the same values for the text and the bargraphs since they can change quite rapidly, and it removes having to do 4 more system calls.

BTW, just started using Mint - Love it!

Thanks,
Jon.



OK Yes this is my part to answer :)

Hi Jon,

Yes the IRC info is spot on - you cannot assign variables - they will need to be separate execpi calls.

That bit of sed thing was done by me to reduce overheads - and lua takes conky variables internally and since we are unable to access some of the temperature information - we use sensors and exec calls.

Even if we call sensors from lua it will be 4 calls to the same single process. But from what we understand from conky docs - it will probably be cheaper than using conky to drive every update 4 times... Question is - how big a difference...

Not much in reality - it all boils down to your update_interval - you can clobber the cpu or let it live a few more heart beats - it is a single chip - so what if it has 4 or 8 cores - if one rises others will follow ;)

Just a food for thought :)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-01-15
> **mesilikas said:**
> My dear friends hi
Great day today our friend paramvir gave us a very good gift Thank you very much "Paramvir" :-)
but want a little help your old files in folder  "home/.config/ Conkywx" should extinguish and the new "conkywx 3.0...deb" will fix new folder? I do not understand the upgrade process i use now (wx-Wu-vindsl-**metric**-weather-graphic-template.sh) data.
Any help is valuable thank you take care! 
Spiros
Just the **home/.config/conkywx****/vindsl-conkywx.conf** needs to be modified. The contents of **/usr/share/conkywx** will be updated by installing the deb file.
But VinDSL's conky pulls the weather through conkywx 3.0 so no changes are necessary to the **~/.conkyrc.vindsl** file.

It will just pull the right weather instead of Na/Na. You'll need to update the **~/.config/conkywx/vindsl-conkywx.conf** file with **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf**.

You might want to do a side by side comparison.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-01-17
Just my 2 cents but I was seeing Waning Cr in VinDSL's conky so I changed the MP line in the config file to align right.

```
#${voffset 35}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 162}[MP]${font}
${voffset 35}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${alignr}[MP]${font}
```

So, now it says Waning Crescent. Plus it's not hard coded to a certain spot, it will stick to the side how ever long the text is.

---

### Post by dtokarz on 2015-01-18
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Just my 2 cents but I was seeing Waning Cr in VinDSL's conky so I changed the MP line in the config file to align right.

```
#${voffset 35}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 162}[MP]${font}
${voffset 35}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${alignr}[MP]${font}
```

So, now it says Waning Crescent. Plus it's not hard coded to a certain spot, it will stick to the side how ever long the text is.

Are you not using sed commands anymore to align and shorten each moon phase?  I will show you how I align mine...

```
${execpi 900 conkywx -c /home/dtokarz/.conky/conkywx.conf -t ${template1}|
####
## Moon Phases [MP]
sed -e 's/Calm mph'/'\$\{offset 8\}Calm/g' |
sed -e 's/New Moon'/'\$\{offset -13\}New Moon/g' |
sed -e 's/Waxing Crescent'/'\$\{offset -10\}Waxing/g' |
sed -e 's/First Quarter'/'\$\{offset -11\}First Qtr/g' |
sed -e 's/Waxing Gibbous'/'\$\{offset -10\}Waxing/g' |
sed -e 's/Full Moon'/'\$\{offset -16\}Full Moon/g' |
sed -e 's/Waning Gibbous'/'\$\{offset -10\}Waning/g' |
sed -e 's/Last Quarter'/'\$\{offset -7\}Last Qtr/g' |
sed -e 's/Waning Crescent'/'\$\{offset -10\}Waning/g' |
sed -e 's/TT:Falling'/'\$\{offset -4\}d/g' |
sed -e 's/TT:Rising'/'\$\{offset -5\}c/g' |
sed -e 's/TT:Steady'/'\$\{offset -8\}RQ/g' |
sed -e 's/TT:N\/A'/'/g'
}${font}
```

That should handle all moon phases that conkywx spits out.

set the conkywx config  file for VinDSL template
```


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## comment option to disable, defining variable to 0 also works
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] enable
## if you want to see units like km/h etc in conky
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
Units_show=1    <- comment this out - do not show units for this template - it has them in the template already la 


```

That did the trick! I didn't want to remove the "mph" from that line so this is much better and works well.
So, I gather that if that is uncommented it and Units_to_Use=IM will cause the WS element will pull the number of miles per hour as well as the text "mph".
And likewise with Units_to_Use=SI it would pull the number plus "km/h". Makes sense to me!
Thanks Bro! What did you think of my screenie? I'm not sure I want to see those images between June 1st and November 1st though. :p :lolflag:
That is hurricane season in these here parts. We get many who come off the west coast of Africa and "look like" they could be coming our way and then go somewhere else.
I try to avoid the weather on the news because these Florida forecasters seem to thrive on that, as if hoping one will come our way - "well, let's take a look at the tropics and see what we have".

### Post by VinDSL on 2015-01-29
> **deadflowr said:**
> Get well and best wishes for a full recovery, VinDSL.

Thx!

Funny story... wonder if this happens to other coders, staring death in the face...

I was drifting in n' out of consciousness in ICU the first week.  I began coding while I was unconscious, to kill the boredom.  I was in coding heaven.  Everything became crystal clear.  Occasionally, they would wake me up, and I couldn't find my mouse.  They thought I was hallucinating.  LeL

After several days of them trying to convince me I wasn't sitting at a computer, I started watching closely what was happening, and realized that I had internally programmed my brain to use a sort virtual input device, somewhat like a touchpad that didn't require touching -- a touchless touchpad, if you will. Worked great!  Problem was, I never could figure out where the virtual data was being saved.

The second week, I figured out a way to reset everything, and turn off the 'coding machine'.  That came as a great relief to the hospital staff. :p

Anyway, it'll be great to get to work on some real code... things I can share with others.  

Doesn't do a lot of good to have this stuff pigeon-holed in the back of your mind, like RoboCop. you know?

Thanks Paramvir for clarifying that.

This variable is in fact a 2 x 3 matrix:

```
default_wx=w:1
```

Extension is too complicated. Needs to be and kept simple.

Any idea what WU changed causing the last version to break?

---

### Post by paramvir on 2015-01-29
> **os2 said:**
> Thanks Paramvir for clarifying that.

This variable is in fact a 2 x 3 matrix:

```
default_wx=w:1
```

Extension is too complicated. Needs to be and kept simple.

Any idea what WU changed causing the last version to break?

WU forecast data is now in a flash wrapper called by a javascript - no text anywhere... anyway - that is history. WU cannot be accessed by text browsers for forecast. 

It is not complicated - 
[LIST]
[*]left of colon is current condition 
[*]right of colon is forecast. 
[*]2 stations or systems deliver only current conditions. 
[*]3 systems deliver current conditions and forecast. 
[*]WU and METAR do not do forecast 
[*]the balance 3 do both. 
[/LIST]
 
Cheers :)

---

[INDENT][[IMG]http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-29-jan-2015-2(650x520).png[/IMG]]("http://vindsl.com/images/vindsl-desktop-29-jan-2015-2.png")[/INDENT]


Upgrading was stupid simple :D

All I did was:
[LIST=1]
[*]Update from ConkyWX 2 => ConkyWX 3
[*]Specify w:1 in conkywx.sh 
[*]And, add my Intellicast ID to vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf 
[/LIST]

I'm sure this isn't the official way of doing it - I haven't RTFM yet - but it worked a treat.  LoL

Good going, man!

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-01-30
@Paramvir, I have a question. Is it ok to pull the weather updates every 15 minutes by changing this to 900 in all of the conf files you are using?

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Minimum time the script allows the wesite to be polled
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## NOTE: This setting MUST BE LESS THAN the fetch frequency in your Conky script.
## Uncomment for Conky scripts that fetch data every 900 seconds (15 minutes).
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
minimum_fetch_time=[COLOR=#ff0000]1800[/COLOR]


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## End of file
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------

```

I've tried that and didn't think it worked. Is there another conf file that needs changing perhaps in **/etc/share/conkywx** ?

Thank you

---

### Post by aquarius18 on 2015-01-31
> **Cavsfan said:**
> @Paramvir, I have a question. Is it ok to pull the weather updates every 15 minutes by changing this to 900 in all of the conf files you are using?

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Minimum time the script allows the wesite to be polled
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## NOTE: This setting MUST BE LESS THAN the fetch frequency in your Conky script.
## Uncomment for Conky scripts that fetch data every 900 seconds (15 minutes).
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
minimum_fetch_time=[COLOR=#ff0000]1800[/COLOR]


## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## End of file
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------

```

I've tried that and didn't think it worked. Is there another conf file that needs changing perhaps in **/etc/share/conkywx** ?

Thank you

If you want 15 mins update (minimum_fetch_time=900) then  in big-luamix-conkyrc.sh you need to set 

```
TEXT
${execpi **[COLOR=#ff0000]930[/COLOR]** "${template0}" -c "${template1}" -t "${template2}"} 
```

which is just 30 secs more than your 900 in the conf file.

Or set the first one to 830 and the second to 900.

### Post by paramvir on 2015-01-31
> **Cavsfan said:**
> @Paramvir, I have a question. Is it ok to pull the weather updates every 15 minutes by changing this to 900 in all of the conf files you are using?

Thank you

You have the correct ans from our dear Frank ... :)

But here is my 2 cents - not on the way to shorten the update interval but why reduce the update at all.

You need to be aware - at what frequency is the back ground data being updated. But then again even if it is being updated every few seconds - is the content changing? and if it is - then by how much over 1 hour - 1 hour is a good reference.

Now step in the trend graphs - they represent last 12 hours of weather data for a particular element.

Since I have an idea how the weather has behaved so far - I prefer to normally update no less than 30 min or 1800 sec.

CIMSS images get updated every 3 hours at the servers - and we are talking about serious storm trackers. 

Just do not clobber the website - 15 should be just the minimum - I would prefer a 30 min minimum

Another case is the update_interval in conkyrc - it becomes a serious resource hog - so keep things in perspective and use what ever makes you happy ;)


cheers ):P

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-02-02
I have just verified that your if statements are set to = true if the images are GT 10800 seconds...= 3 hours

```
# set -x
	if [[ $cimss_surface_image ]]; then
		if [[ $do_history -eq 1 ]]; then
			get_surface_image
		elif [[ -s $gsi_image ]]; then
			if [[ $(($(date +%s -r "$gsi_image") - $nowtime1)) -gt 10800 ]]; then
				get_surface_image
			fi
		else
			get_surface_image
		fi
	else
		rm $gsi_image 2> /dev/null
	fi

	if [[ $wund_surface_image ]]; then
		if [[ $do_history -eq 1 ]]; then
			get_wund_surface_image
		elif [[ -s $gwsi_image ]]; then
			if [[ $(($(date +%s -r "$gwsi_image") - $nowtime1)) -gt 10800 ]]; then
				get_wund_surface_image
			fi
		else
			get_wund_surface_image
		fi
	else
		rm $gwsi_image 2> /dev/null
	fi

```

I willl change them to gt 3600 to see if they will update at 1 hour intervals....

tommy


UPDATE: still shows 0000 UTC yesterday for GOES WEST winds....website shows correct date/time values
