### Post by NM5TF on 2015-02-02
VinDSL...


in your config file, look for something like this....

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## How to handle the time zone values
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Comment this option - conkywx will use your PC time zone information
## OR - To Use custom time zone. State the time zone here
##      Manual entry only rquired if you are tracking weather for other
##      locations not in your time zone or you will see the sun rise etc
##      relative to your time for that location.
## How to state: use + for East or - for West locations
## Format East: 7.5 or +0730 and West: -7.5 or -0730
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
t_zone_pick=-7

```

you might use the tz-7 as you are in the Mountain Time Zone....

---

### Post by VinDSL on 2015-02-02
Nice one!

This seems to have done the deed.  I double-checked the output against WU, and the times are correct now.

```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# How to handle the time zone values
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Manually enter the time zone value in the t_zone variable below
# [1] Let conkywx calculate the time difference
# [2] Pick up the time zone values from wunderground
t_zone_pick=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Time Zone
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Set "t_zone" only if "t_zone_pick=0" (above)
# Comment this variable if not required
t_zone="7.5"
```

I know it doesn't match up with the comments in your example, but it works.

Thx

```

The comments in that template indicate I should be using a negative number in my locale.

Perhaps it's a typo.  Regardless, all is calm on the western front. :D

### Post by paramvir on 2015-02-03
> **VinDSL said:**
> Nice one!

This seems to have done the deed.  I double-checked the output against WU, and the times are correct now.

```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# How to handle the time zone values
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# [0] Manually enter the time zone value in the t_zone variable below
# [1] Let conkywx calculate the time difference
# [2] Pick up the time zone values from wunderground
t_zone_pick=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Time Zone
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Set "t_zone" only if "t_zone_pick=0" (above)
# Comment this variable if not required
t_zone="7.5"
```

I know it doesn't match up with the comments in your example, but it works.

Thx


OK just to clear the air here :D - your conkywx is using the system time to figure out the time zone and not the t_zone. The config file variables are Hashed and so there is no reference to catch $UserConfig{t_zone} - even though it has a registered value. 

What is - however - being looked at is $UserConfig{t_zone_pick} - which is effectively undefined and so your pc time information is being used  ;) - it was meant to work like that - if you comment both the variables above - it will still work correctly.

Yes VinDSL - the config file does need an overhaul as it has redundant variables ;)

And Thanks Tommy - getting help testing does help add bits in :D


cheers ):P

Something I learned recently (if you didn't already know this), if you have multiple Linux systems on your system and do not want to have 
to boot to your "primary system" and re-install grub there when grub is updated to a new version on any system there is a way around that.
Just install grub to the PBR instead of MBR on every system that is not your primary system where your main grub is.
You can do this when inititially installing by choose "something else" and installing grub to the PBR there, Or you can run this command on 
your other systems so they are not in control of grub any more.

```
**sudo dpkg-reconfigure grub-pc**  or **sudo dpkg-reconfigure grub-efi-amd64**
```
You go through a few screens tabbing to OK then the last screen uncheck the drive sda, sdb, etc and check the partition and you will see a few errors but it will install.
You just have to remember where your grub is installed.

### Post by VinDSL on 2015-02-07
Thought I'd better update the 'Calendar Section' before time catches up with us...  :)

Here are the updates with linefeeds added ([COLOR="#FF0000"]**for illustration only - don't copy n' paste this code**[/COLOR])

```
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color3}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -80}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color6}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
[COLOR="#0000CD"][B]${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jan 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jul 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}
${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}[/B][/COLOR]
${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
[COLOR="#0000CD"][B]${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}
${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}[/B][/COLOR]
${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}

```

${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}
${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}[/B][/COLOR]
${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}

```


Here is the code, properly formatted...

```
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color3}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -80}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color6}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jan 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jul 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
# ${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}

```


Spelling of the months will need to be tweaked, of course, if you're using a language other than English ;)

Thank you for that! I plugged that in and of course had to adjust the vertical placement but it looks better than what I had before. :)

@Tommy, maybe Paramvir the weather wizard can find another site that does the pictures. I don't know but if it's possible he can do it. :)

### Post by aquarius18 on 2015-02-08
> **NM5TF said:**
> I highlighted Paramvir's relevant response above...

this seems to be the case here.....

I just looked at the CIMSS website....the latest image is from 0000 UTC....over 7 hours ago.....

we may be forced to live with it...until CIMSS does more frequent updates....if they ever do....

at least the IR data is current...

My cron job is set to run every 3 hours and, yes, I am getting updates within 1-2 hours after the pic was taken. IOW, the 0000UTC image appears on my desktop sometime between 0100 and 0200UTC. 

Cron here:

```
#!/bin/sh

set -e

# zoomed image from CIMSS
curl -A "Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 4.4.4; XT1045 Build/KXB21.14-L1.45) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/39.0.2171.59 Mobile Safari/537.36" http://tropic.ssec.wisc.edu/real-time/**[COLOR=#ff0000]austeast/winds/wgmsirZ.GIF[/COLOR]** > /tmp/wx-**[COLOR=#ff0000]frank[/COLOR]**-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gsi_image.gif 
```

Adjust the highlighted to match your requirement and add the appropriate timing.

If this works for you (and others) without problems then we may have an issue with the data feed.

Cheers / Frank

### Post by paramvir on 2015-02-09
OK To get a quick primer on global weather patterns - have a peep at this page

[http://tropic.ssec.wisc.edu/real-time/mimic-tpw/global2/main.html](http://tropic.ssec.wisc.edu/real-time/mimic-tpw/global2/main.html)

Even though the page is showing "Total Precipitable Water - Global" - there is much more - you notice how the direction of flow is different towards the poles and the Equator. 

You will also notice that there is a normal build up of dark maroon colour where the wind has had a free flow or long enough fetch - across the Pacific towards south east Asian islands and Indian ocean.

Notice that you also see the air flow pattern - wind - aha 

cheers ):P

No disputing that - never expected a 3 hourly update for Aus regions, and that's not the point either....

My obs are this:

1. upon booting I am getting the latest image, always - usually the 1800UTC edition

2. cron tab turned off yesterday for most of the day

3. 14-15 hours after booting I am still staring at the original 1800UTC image I had in the morning upon booting. Considering my time diff to UTC I would have expected the 0600UTC image at the end of the day

4. today, again turned cron off after booting and let the conkywx script do what it is supposed to do - but I went one step further: the image loaded upon booting was zoomed. After turning cron tab off, in the .conf file I changed my settings from cimss_surface_image=7:lz to cimss_surface_image=7:l (no zoom). So, by rights, conkywx should now display the big (not zoomed) picture...... but NO update after 8 hours!

Soooo - crontab on again.

Cheers ):P

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-03-06
Installed Vivid Mate yesterday as it's now an accepted flavor of Ubuntu and man do I have some font resizing to do with VinDSL's conky.
I can generally copy from one Ubuntu to the next but not Mate. It's like a different animal altogether. Everything as it was looking good on the other OSs is tiny on Mate. 

Oh, well been there a couple of times before so here goes...

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-03-07
After much wrangling with the beast :p on Vivid Mate 15.04 and about a day and a half I think I got it pretty close. 
I had to make every font there was bigger. I also had to adjust nearly everything there was to adjust. 
It was tiny and only a little more than half the screen before any adjustment.
But I did find an error I had made that perpetuated throughout all of my systems.
I had a typo on a ${font} in the Audacious code and now I no longer see "invalid variable" any more in the output in terminal.

I made it a dark background just for the adjustment process so I could see better.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot-mate-conky.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot-mate-conky.php")

I know it's not perfect but, wudda ya think?

Now on to the much easier to adjust (at least I'm hoping) Paramvir's Big-luamix conky.

---

### Post by VinDSL on 2015-03-07
Looks great, Cavsfan.  Kudos!

I had the same 'problem' a couple of years ago...  

I was playing around with a distro that had the Enlightenment WM -- Bodhi w/E16 or E17 -- if I remember correctly.

The entire UI layout was designed to be compact, which was nice, because you could cram a lot of stuff on the screen without having to vertically scroll.  It was especially nice on a 10" netbook display, for instance, but it required a LOT of adjustments to Conky.

Anyway, good job, man!  ;)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-03-07
> **VinDSL said:**

I had the same 'problem' a couple of years ago...  

I was playing around with a distro that had the Enlightenment WM -- Bodhi w/E16 or E17 -- if I remember correctly.

The entire UI layout was designed to be compact, which was nice, because you could cram a lot of stuff on the screen without having to vertically scroll.  It was especially nice on a 10" netbook display, for instance, but it required a LOT of adjustments to Conky.

Anyway, good job, man!  ;)

Thank you! Mate was like a moving target too. :lol: Ha! Every time I made a few changes I had to up the **max_user_text** thing.
Or else it would display stuff was mismatched in the Audacious code which I knew was not the case.

I could logoff and back on and something would change. Or even reboot and the positioning of the luas would be misaligned.
Even after I posted that picture and rebooted I had to realign the cpu and mem/hd luas! 

I think they have finally met their match though! :lol:

I went ahead and added Paramvir's great looking big lua mix and had to wrestle with that for a couple of hours.
I swear it was a moving target too although nothing compared to yours. At one point it looked like I had a lot more adjusting to do.
Then I looked back at it and it was pretty much lined up correctly.

Who knows what's going to happen after a reboot though. Hopefully nothing. ;)

While I was at it I learned a little bit about backgrounds and added one to your conky. It's very slight but I think it helps a tad while looking at it on this background.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot-both-mate-conkys.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot-both-mate-conkys.php")

### Post by TehPirate on 2015-03-15
This is a lovely conky but I'm severely failing to use the new version. The old version was easy to setup due to the tutorial. I've been searching through this thread for the past 8 hrs trying ot figure out how to set it up and it's either erroring in terminal or rendered wrong. I figured it was a copy-paste job I did wrong and wiped everything to start fresh to no avail. Any help?  elementary OS Freya - Ubuntu 14.04, Conky 1.9

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-03-15
> **TehPirate said:**
> This is a lovely conky but I'm severely failing to use the new version. The old version was easy to setup due to the tutorial. I've been searching through this thread for the past 8 hrs trying ot figure out how to set it up and it's either erroring in terminal or rendered wrong. I figured it was a copy-paste job I did wrong and wiped everything to start fresh to no avail. Any help?  elementary OS Freya - Ubuntu 14.04, Conky 1.9

Download Conkywx here: [https://bitbucket.org/plikhari/conkywx_pub/downloads](https://bitbucket.org/plikhari/conkywx_pub/downloads)

You'll need **conkywx_3.0.0-1_all.deb** for Ubuntu. There is also **VinDSL_Conky_Fonts_Assistant.tar.xz** to install the necessary fonts.

Once you install conkywx from Ubuntu software center or however you choose.
Type conkywx in terminal  then press 2 for on offline link explaining how the new version works.

[ATTACH=CONFIG]260640[/ATTACH]

You'll find the VinDSL conky files in /etc/share/conkywx/examples/.

That should get you started at least. :)

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-03-19
nice progress so far....almost done...just need to move some icons & graphs up a little...

I assume that you know how to edit the relevant template file below...

```
####    Windvane
${lua main -n showcond -p 10, [COLOR="#FF0000"]620[/COLOR] -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -f [BF] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 4 -a 1 -l  0xff0000:0.9 }
# ${lua main -n showcond -p 10, -s 60 -w Wi -f [BF] -d [WDD] -t 0 -e [WS] -i 17 -a 5 -j 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${voffset -68}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
${voffset 23}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 203}[MP]${font}
${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}${goto 10}Temp${goto 60}Baro${goto 110}Dew${goto 160}Hum${goto 210}Wspd
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 5,[COLOR="#FF0000"]720[/COLOR] -d [GT] -c 0xEE4000:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 55,[COLOR="#FF0000"]720[/COLOR] -d [GP] -c 0xFF83FA:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 105,[COLOR="#FF0000"]720[/COLOR] -d [GD] -c 0x00FFFF:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 155,[COLOR="#FF0000"]720[/COLOR] -d [GH] -c 0x00FF00:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 205,[COLOR="#FF0000"]720[/COLOR] -d [GW] -c 0x00FF00:1}
${voffset 9}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
```[COLOR="#000000"][/COLOR]

these highlighted values control the vertical placement of the windvane & trend graphs....

play with it a little more until you like it...

tommy

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-03-19
> **TehPirate said:**
> After some minor help, I've made progress. Weather is a lil bugged for me:  [http://gyazo.com/0e2c863b72593efdc2e509a4e58090dd](http://gyazo.com/0e2c863b72593efdc2e509a4e58090dd)

> **NM5TF said:**
> nice progress so far....almost done...just need to move some icons & graphs up a little...

I assume that you know how to edit the relevant template file below...

```
####    Windvane
${lua main -n showcond -p 10, [COLOR=#FF0000]620[/COLOR] -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -f [BF] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 4 -a 1 -l  0xff0000:0.9 }
# ${lua main -n showcond -p 10, -s 60 -w Wi -f [BF] -d [WDD] -t 0 -e [WS] -i 17 -a 5 -j 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${voffset -68}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
${voffset 23}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 203}[MP]${font}
${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}${goto 10}Temp${goto 60}Baro${goto 110}Dew${goto 160}Hum${goto 210}Wspd
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 5,[COLOR=#FF0000]720[/COLOR] -d [GT] -c 0xEE4000:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 55,[COLOR=#FF0000]720[/COLOR] -d [GP] -c 0xFF83FA:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 105,[COLOR=#FF0000]720[/COLOR] -d [GD] -c 0x00FFFF:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 155,[COLOR=#FF0000]720[/COLOR] -d [GH] -c 0x00FF00:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 205,[COLOR=#FF0000]720[/COLOR] -d [GW] -c 0x00FF00:1}
${voffset 9}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
```

these highlighted values control the vertical placement of the windvane & trend graphs....

play with it a little more until you like it...

tommy

I agree. Some tweaking with the vertical (y) placement, as Tommy has pointed out in red above and you are there. :)
The tweaking of VinDSL's weather template is a little touchy though. You'll find by moving one thing up a bit, some other items move along with it.
So, while moving one thing up, it may cause the necessity to move something else down. But, it's something we all go through to get it just right.

I am referring to this file, not sure where yours is maybe in **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh**
Mine is in **~/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh**. That's where it originally was and I just kept it there for convenience.

Just modify the **${voffset n}** (vertical offsets) parts and in the lua lines the ones after the -p that Tommy has in red.

I believe that /usr/share/conkywx/ directory comes with ownership privileges of (in my case) cavsfan:user.

I just changed it to cavsfan:cavsfan so I could save the files edited by **sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/*** and that worked for me.

However, you may have already figured that out or else it isn't a problem for you.

Since I said I'd show you my version of VinDSL's conky and our monitors are the exact same size, here it is from Trusty 14.04.2:

```
###############################################
## VinDSL | rev. 01-03-15 05:10 | ver. 3.0.0 ##
###############################################
##             January 2015 Series           ##
###############################################

## ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

####
## Development Platforms (current)
#
#  Ubuntu 12.04 'Precice Pangolin' (GNOME 3.4 - Conky 1.9.0)
#  Ubuntu 14.04 'Trusty Tahr'  (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Ubuntu 14.10 'Utopic Unicorn'  (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Screen Resolution: 1920x1080 (ASUS VN289QL)
#
## Tips n' Tricks: conky 1.8.1 will no longer be supported. RIP :)

####
## Prerequisites (required)
#
#  conky-all 1.8.0-1 or 1.9.0-2 or 1.9.0-4
#  Conkywx 2.0.0 (Paramvir Likhari)
#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor

####
## Installed fonts (required)
#
#  Arrows (JLH Fonts)
#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
#  ConkySymbols (Cavsfan - not included)
#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva)
#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
#  Neuropol (Mint systems only - not included)
#

## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

####
## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
#
use_xft yes
xftfont DroidSans:size=8.75
xftalpha 0.1

####
## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
#
override_utf8_locale yes

####
## Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.
#
background yes

####
## Update interval in seconds.
#
update_interval 2.0

####
## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
## Zero makes Conky run forever.
#
total_run_times 0

####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
####
## Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
#
# own_window_argb_visual yes
# own_window_argb_value 255

####
## Force images to redraw when they change.
#
imlib_cache_size 1

####
## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
#
double_buffer yes

####
## Draw shades?
#
draw_shades yes
default_shade_color 330

####
## Draw outlines?
#
draw_outline no

####
## Draw borders around text?
#
draw_borders no

####
## Draw borders around graphs?
#
draw_graph_borders no

####
## Print text to stdout?
## Print text in console?
#
out_to_ncurses no
out_to_console no

####
## Text alignment.
#
alignment top_right

####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
minimum_size 245 1025

####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
#
text_buffer_size 6456

####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 956

####added by Cavsfan
## Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
max_user_text 52768

####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 245

####
## Gap between text and screen borders.
#
gap_x 6      # Left/Right
gap_y 32  # Top/Bottom

####
## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
#
short_units yes

####
## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
#
pad_percents 0

####
## Pad spacing between text and borders.
#
border_inner_margin 4

####
## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
#
top_name_width 10

####
## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
## Set to yes, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
#
no_buffers yes

####
## Set to yes, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
#
uppercase no

####
## Number of cpu samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
cpu_avg_samples 2

####
## Number of net samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
net_avg_samples 2

####
## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
## Only affects certain objects.
#
use_spacer right

####
## My colors (suit yourself)
#
color0 White            #FFFFFF
color1 Ivory            #FFFFF0
color2 Ivory2           #EEEEE0
color3 yellow           #CDCDC1
#color4 Gold        #D4A017
#color4 DeepSkyBlue    #3BB9FF
#color4 DodgerBlue    #1589FF
#color4 DodgerBlue2    #157DEC
color4 Tan1        #FFA54F
#color4 green        #00FF00
#color4 Turquoise1    #52F3FF
#color4 Magenta        #FF00FF
#color4 cyan        #00FFFF
#color4 Red        #FF0000
color5 cyan             #EE9A49
color6 Gray             #7E7E7E
color7 AntiqueWhite4    #8B8378
color8 DimGray          #696969
color9 Tomato           #FF6347
# colorX OrangeRed        #FF4500

#####
## Load Lua for shading (optional)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
lua_load /usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/draw_bg.lua
# lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

####
## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_testing.lua
# lua_draw_hook_post main_bars

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export path statements for use in *conky* config file
# if you move this file else where then give the full path for WX_DIR variable
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## do not uncomment
#WX_DIR="/usr/share/conkywx"; #export WX_DIR

####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
template1 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-conkywx.conf"
####
## Choose Graphic or Dingbat template
template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh"
#template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh"
####
template7 /tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert

## To switch between Imperial and Metric change template9 to F for Imperial or C for Metric values - template4 file does not require any change - it changes based on Units_to_Use in the vindsl-conkywx.conf file
template9 F

TEXT
#${voffset -15}${lua main -n background -r 45 -c 0x000000:0.2 -p NA,NA}

##             LOGO             ##
##################################

####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic) Ubuntu systems
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4} ${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| awk -F'"' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '8-14'}${font}

## Uncomment for Mint systems)

####

${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Multi${offset 3}Core${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}

## uncoment for grub version

${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#

##################################
##          NETWORK             ##
##################################
${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}NETWORK${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}T${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Download${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totaldown eth0}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${downspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}N${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Upload${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totalup eth0}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${upspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}LAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${addr eth0}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
#${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}Demo Mode
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} ${color5}Alert:${font}${lua main -n scroller -f 12 -l 21 -s 5 -p 98,549 -o ${template7}}${else} ${voffset -2}${color6}${hr 1}${endif}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
${execpi 1830 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -12}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color6}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset -5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 7}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color4}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color4}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset -2}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset -2}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -79}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color6}${alignc 99}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2015"}${voffset -70}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jan 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jul 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
# ${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}

#${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt | head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################

##################################
${if_running rhythmbox}
${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##    BANSHEE (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running banshee}
${voffset -26}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}BANSHEE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f1- -d " "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##   GUAYADEQUE (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running guayadeque}
${voffset -36}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}GUAYADEQUE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}
${voffset -44}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  Audacious (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -73}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}$font$
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${voffset 3}${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${voffset 2}${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate:${if_match ${audacious_frequency}==-1}${alignc} 0Hz${else}${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${endif}${alignr}${audacious_status}
${voffset 0}Output: ${if_match ${audacious_channels}==2}Stereo ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==1}Mono ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}>2} audacious_channels${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==-1} ${else}Unknown ${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${alignr}Bit Rate:#
${if_match ${audacious_bitrate}==-1} 0Kbs${else}${alignr} ${audacious_bitrate}${endif}${endif}
```

I try to do what I say I will do. If you are going to use the fan speeds you will need the **ConkySymbols** fonts and they can be downloaded from here:

[http://gnome-look.org/content/show.php/Conky+Symbols?content=142593](http://gnome-look.org/content/show.php/Conky+Symbols?content=142593)

You'll have to change all of the file names to suit your system.

I also added a transparent background to mine. It is just above the TEXT line and is adjustable as far as being more or less transparent.
If you want a total transparent background just comment out the one line and uncomment out the other.

Cheers

PS: Here's my weather template for it also. You'll still have to do some adjusting to do. 

```
############################################################
## VinDSL | Paramvir | rev. 01-03-15 05:10 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
############################################################
## WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 3.0.0                     ##
############################################################
${voffset -3}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color6}${alignc -18}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -19}${offset 2}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.4}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}
${voffset -40}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.75}${color6}[D1T]${font}
${voffset 0}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.3}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -45}${goto 188}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color6}I${font}
${voffset 2}${font Ubuntu:size=22}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
${voffset 1}${offset 10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
${voffset -3}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FM]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}U${offset 1}V${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[UV]${font}
####    Windvane (without -i 19 pointing to a windvane image it draws one with lua)
#${lua main -n showcond -p 7, 713 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 19 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
### This leaves out the image so it draws one with lua
### ${lua main -n showcond -p 7, 713 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${lua main -n showcond -p 1, 649 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 13 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${voffset -55}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
#${voffset 22}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 192}[MP]${font}
${voffset 22}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset 0}${alignr}[MP]${font}
${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}${goto 10}Temp${goto 60}Baro${goto 110}Dew${goto 160}Hum${goto 210}Wspd
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 5,750 -d [GT] -c 0xEE4000:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 55,750 -d [GP] -c 0xFF83FA:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 105,750 -d [GD] -c 0x00FFFF:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 155,750 -d [GH] -c 0x00FF00:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 205,750 -d [GW] -c 0x00FF00:1}
${voffset 2}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
####    Present Condition  WX
${lua main -n showcond -p 3,553 -s 60 -w ci -t 1 -f [PIF] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [PIC] -j 0 }#
####    Present Condition MOON
${lua main -n showcond -p 185, 648 -s 60 -w mi -f [MIF] -d [MROT] -i [MIC] }#
####    Forecast Conditions
${lua main -n showcond -p 22,758 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D2F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D2I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 82,758 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D3F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D3I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 141,758 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D4F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D4I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 198,758 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D5F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D5I] }
${voffset 10}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.5}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 24}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D2P]${goto 84}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D3P]${goto 144}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D4P]${goto 201}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D5P]${font}#

```

### Post by TehPirate on 2015-03-19
Thanks for the help, seems to be all correct now though what's with the terminal error? [http://gyazo.com/42da302f8f442e2f5d219636ea38bc25](http://gyazo.com/42da302f8f442e2f5d219636ea38bc25) Gyazo doesn't play nicely and detects any transparancy as black.

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-03-20
> **TehPirate said:**
> Thanks for the help, seems to be all correct now though what's with the terminal error? [http://gyazo.com/42da302f8f442e2f5d219636ea38bc25](http://gyazo.com/42da302f8f442e2f5d219636ea38bc25) Gyazo doesn't play nicely and detects any transparancy as black.

Just uncomment out the line just below TEXT (${voffset -15}${lua main -n background -r 45 -c 0x000000:0.2 -p NA,NA}) and either delete the other one or comment it out.

That is an error that we all get. It's meaningless I believe Paramvir said.

Judging from sample rate showing up at the bottom and you do not have Audacious running you need to increase either **text_buffer_size** or **max_user_text**.

You should see some errors in the terminal output when you first start the conky up until you get it increased to the right amount. That is before you get the other errors in your picture.

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-03-31
A perfect piece of art from Paramvir and VinDSL and the minor tweaking by me.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-11
Hey, I think I may have stumbled upon something useful.  You know when you go away for several hours and put your system in suspend mode and you notice the weather hasn't been updated since before you left?

You want a simple way to get fresh weather up to the minute?

Add this to your .bashrc file:

If you're just using the magnificent VinDSL conky put this code in there:
```
#Refresh and restart conky
alias crrestart='killall conky && conky -c /home/cavsfan/.conkyrc.vindsl'
```

Of course modify as needed.

Or if your running one or more of magnificent Paramvir's conkys along with VinDSL's conky like I am enter this:
```
#Refresh and restart conky
alias crrestart='killall conky && /usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh'
```

Then instead of crestart to just restart the conky enter crrestart (or whatever you want to call it).

I guess we wouldn't want to run this to update changes to the conky files as it would force another download of the weather every time.

Just a once a day thing or whatever to refresh your weather instead of waiting until the next time it refreshes on it's own.

That is Paramvir's Big Luamix Conky consisting of these files

[LIST=1]
[*]/usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.sh 
[*]/usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh 
[*]/usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.conf (see below) 
[/LIST]
The 3rd file didn't exist I just copied the contents of ~/.config/conkywx/vindsl-conkywx.conf and saved it as /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.conf.

All the actual weather conf files should be the exact same. Or at least they work best that way.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-17
I'll throw my 2 cents in here. :p

It looks like you are missing some fonts for the VinDSL conky and it also looks like you are using the Big Luamix conky as I pointed you to originally.

Paramvir helped me with that as mine initially looked like yours. It is probably just a matter of changing the font size in /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.sh.
That plus some minor vertical re-alignment of some things.
My vanilla Ubuntu 14.04 uses one font size, my Mint 17 uses another and my Ubuntu Mate 15.04 uses another. :p 

```
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Change the xftfont size value if the template is not
# appearing aligned
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# xftfont DejaVu Sans:size=12
#xftfont DejaVu Sans Mono:size=9
# xftfont DroidSansFallback:size=10
# xftfont DejaVu Sans Mono:size=12
xftfont DroidSans:size=12:bold
# xftfont FreeSerif:size=15
# xftfont FreeSans:size=10
```

And as Paramvir mentioned it is just a matter of getting your conf file using your location and getting the template that uses the conf file pointing to the correct one.
That is why I created /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.conf from another conf file so it would not be overwritten and I was also keeping the files like named.
Your conf files are all going to be the same so you can use one or one for each conky, your choice. I have one for the VinDSL conky and another for the Big Luamix conky but they are identical.

Here is where in /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.sh that if points to the conf file:
```
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# template1 points to the conkywx config file
# single config file for both image and font templates
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# template1 "\"$WX_DIR/examples/conkywx-02.conf\""
# template1 ./examples/conkywx.conf
[COLOR=#ff0000]template1 ./examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.conf[/COLOR]
```

Here is where in VinDSL's conkyrc file (~/.conkyrc.vindsl) it points to the conf file:
```
####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
[COLOR=#ff0000]template1 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-conkywx.conf"[/COLOR]
####
## Choose Graphic or Dingbat template
template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh"
#template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh"
####
template7 /tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert

## To switch between Imperial and Metric change template9 to F for Imperial or C for Metric values - template4 file does not require any change - it changes based on Units_to_Use in the vindsl-conkywx.conf file
template9 F
```

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-18
> **Hazzabin said:**

Or make sure the other one is a copy of the first one.

You are likely to find the one that it currently points to has Delhi, India as it's location. That is where Paramvir lives and it comes with that location.

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-04-18
> **Hazzabin said:**
> Thanks for that info cavsfan, ok so which file should I be using to change from India to Australia, Brisbane

OK Hazzabin....here we go....

it seems that you are using the big-luamix-conkyrc.sh for your conkywx.....

open up that file with your favorite text editor....I use gedit myself....

look for these lines highlighted RED starting around line 87 or so....

```
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# template0 points to the main program conkywx
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# template0 "\"$WX_DIR/conkywx\""
template0 ./conkywx.sh

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# template1 points to the conkywx config file
# single config file for both image and font templates
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# template1 "\"$WX_DIR/examples/conkywx-02.conf\""
[COLOR="#FF0000"]template1 ./examples/conkywx-02.conf[/COLOR]

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# template2 points to the template to use
# comment the template you do not want to use
#-----------------------------------------------------------
#### font template section ---------------------------------
# template2 ./examples/short-02-font-template.sh
#### image template section --------------------------------
# template2 "\"$WX_DIR/examples/short-02-image-template.sh\""
template2 ./examples/big-luamix-template.sh
template9 20 * 20
imlib_cache_size 0

# template0 ./conkywx.sh
# template1 ./examples/lua-display.conf


#-----------------------------------------------------------
# location of wxalert file - conkywx tmp directory for the used conkywx config file
# IMPORTANT do not comment this line - even if you do not use it
#-----------------------------------------------------------
[COLOR="#FF0000"]template3 /tmp/wx-[COLOR="#00FF00"]tommy[/COLOR]-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert[/COLOR]

```

the entry labeled template1 shows that the config file is named [COLOR="#FF0000"]./examples/conkywx-02.conf[/COLOR]

this file contains the configuration info for conkywx that tells the program where you are located.....

also....change the green entry under template3 to whatever username you use...

again using your favorite text editor, open that file   ./examples/conkywx-02.conf

be certain that that this entry looks like this....if not, then change it...

```
[COLOR="#FF0000"]default_wx=w:3[/COLOR]
```

next you need to change the wunderground LOCATION  to yours.....this code will use the info from Brisbane In'tl Airport

```
[COLOR="#FF0000"]Location=http://www.wunderground.com/q/zmw:00000.1.94578[/COLOR]
```

you will need to comment out the entry for Delhi, India or you will still see Paramvir's forecast....

next you need to find this section...and then enter the info in RED.....

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Current Conditions + Forecast  - New Change since ver 2.0
## forecast http://www.msn.com weather ;-)
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------

[COLOR="#FF0000"]msn_location=http://www.msn.com/en-us/weather/today/Brisbane,Queensland,Australia/we-city--27.4685,153.023?q=brisbane-queensland&form=PRWKWB&iso=AU[/COLOR]

```

after all changes are made, SAVE the files and restart conkywx......you should be good to go for a start....

if you want to include the WX SAT pictures, you still have a ways to go.....

here is a screenie of my desktop with the WX SAT pix....

go here:  [http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot-1478.php](http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot-1478.php)

have fun with conkywx & let us know how it goes....

tommy

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-04-19
once more you will need to edit your -02.conf file to select the options pertinent to your location....

there are many choices....pick the ones that best suit your wants/needs....start here:

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Surface analysis image from CIMSS
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Default is upper level winds [option]:u
## For lower level winds use [option]:l
## You can get a "storm zoom" image [option]:uz or [option]:lz
## storm zoom is not available for areas [6] and [9]
## if you live closer to the coast then lower level may be better
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1] indian             [:l] lower [:lz] lower zoom [:u] upper [:uz] upper zoom
## [2] east pacific       [:l] lower [:lz] lower zoom [:u] upper [:uz] upper zoom
## [3] west pacific       [:l] lower [:lz] lower zoom [:u] upper [:uz] upper zoom
## [4] south east pacific [:l] lower [:lz] lower zoom [:u] upper [:uz] upper zoom
## [5] north atlantic     [:l] lower [:lz] lower zoom [:u] upper [:uz] upper zoom
## [6] east atlantic      [:l] lower [:u] upper
[COLOR="#FF0000"]## [7] east australia     [:l] lower [:lz] lower zoom [:u] upper [:uz] upper zoom[/COLOR]
## [8] west australia     [:l] lower [:lz] lower zoom [:u] upper [:uz] upper zoom
## [9] combo atlantic + pacific  [:l] lower [:u] upper
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
[COLOR="#FF0000"]cimss_surface_image=7:u[/COLOR]

```

notice that selection #7 is for Eastern Australia....where you live...

I selected option 7:u for the upper level winds....you may want to change that to 7:l since you
are near the coast......

go to the next section:

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Surface analysis image from wunderground
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Default is [:i] infrared image
## use 1:i or 1 to get infrared images for atlantic
## use 1:v to get visible images for atlantic
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1]  atlantic                [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [2]  caribbean               [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [3]  gulf of mexico          [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [4]  puerto rico             [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [5]  florida and the bahamas [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [6]  east pacific            [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [7]  west pacific            [:v] visible [:i] infrared
[COLOR="#FF0000"]## [8]  australia               [:v] visible [:i] infrared[/COLOR]
## [9]  north indian            [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [10] south indian            [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## [11] west african coast      [:v] visible [:i] infrared
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
[COLOR="#FF0000"]wund_surface_image=8:i[/COLOR]

```

select  option # 8:i for the Infrared SAT view of OZ.....

finally, go to the next section:

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Surface analysis image from Intellicast
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## [1]  Africa
## [2]  Africa North Western
## [3]  Africa Eastern
## [4]  Africa Western
## [5]  Africa South
## [6]  Asia
## [7]  Asia Russia
## [8]  Asia India
## [9]  Asia Indonesia
## [10] Asia Japan
## [11] Asia Middle East
## [12] Asia South Eastern 
## [13] Australia
[COLOR="#FF0000"]## [14] Australia North Eastern 
## [15] Australia South Eastern[/COLOR] 
## [16] Australia Western 
## [17] New Zealand
## [18] America North 
## [19] America Central 
## [20] America South 
## [21] America South Northern
## [22] America South Southern
## [23] America Carribean
## [24] America Brazil
## [25] America Mexico
## [26] Canada Central 
## [27] Canada Eastern 
## [28] Canada Western 
## [29] Europe
## [30] Europe North Eastern 
## [31] Europe North Western 
## [32] Europe Scandinavia
## [33] Europe South Eastern 
## [34] Europe South Western 
## [35] Europe United Kingdom
## [36] US Continental
## [37] US North East
## [38] US North West
## [39] US Great Plains
## [40] US Mid Atlantic
## [41] US Mid West
## [42] US Pacific
## [43] US South Central
## [44] US South East
## [45] US South West
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
[COLOR="#FF0000"]intel_surface_image=15[/COLOR]

```

select either option #14 or #15....whichever is correct.....

SAVE the changes....

almost there.......

next you will need to open /usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh

```

#!/bin/bash
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export path statements for use in *conky* config file
# if you move this file else where then give the full path for WX_DIR variable
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# WX_DIR="/usr/share/conkywx"; export WX_DIR

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# stop existing conky processes
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# if [[ "$(pidof conky)" ]] ; then killall conky; fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# run conky instances
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
conky -c ./examples/conky-system.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/vindsl-conkyrc &

# conky -c ./examples/expanded-conkyrc.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/short-conkyrc.sh &

# sleep 1
#conky -c ./examples/short-02-conkyrc.sh &

# conky -c ./examples/short-luamix-conkyrc.sh &

conky -c ./examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.sh &


# conky -c ./conky-mechanical-clock-master/conkyrc_clock1 &

# conky -c ./examples/conkywx-surface-wide-image.sh &

[COLOR="#FF0000"]conky -c ./examples/conkywx-surface-image.sh &[/COLOR]


```

uncomment the line in RED....

SAVE the changes....

restart conkywx......let us know how it works now....

tommy

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-22
> **Hazzabin said:**
> HEY big SURPRISE, installed in Ubuntu Mate 15.04 and without any adjustments to alignments or fonts I got this

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot-1479.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot-1479.php")

Much improved, but it should be bigger. The fonts you want to install in ~/.fonts.  If it doesn't exist as a hidden folder in your home directory, create it.
I put the **VinDSL_Conky_Fonts_Assistant.tar.xz** in that folder and unzipped them from there.
That is why you have letters displaying in your VinDSL conky at the far left side of each line instead of the font that that letter represents.

I think once installed. You just need to logout and log back in for them to take effect.

In [B]/usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh
[/B]
You'll have something like this:
```
###--------- NOTE: # character at the end of line is line break character -----
###--------- Backgrounds ------------------------------------------------------
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.8 -s 640,200 -p 1,5}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.8 -s 640,200 -p 1,210}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.7 -s 640,200 -p 1,415}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.7 -s 640,200 -p 1,620}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.7 -s 640,200 -p 1,825}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x555555:0.5 -s 640,200 -p 1,5}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x555555:0.5 -s 640,200 -p 1,210}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x555555:0.5 -s 640,200 -p 1,415}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x555555:0.5 -s 640,200 -p 1,620}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x555555:0.5 -s 640,200 -p 1,825}#

```

As it should show in the help file when you enter conkywx and enter 2, you will see that the **-s 640,200** represents size and **-p 1,5** represents placement.

The **-c 0x555555:0.5** represents how much transparency there is behind the conky.

I commented out the original lines and tweaked my transparency to what suits the background.

Also in **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.sh** you will see this as far as the font sizes are located and you just have to enter **:style=bold** after the font if you want it to be bold. 
That is what it took for me. I have a 1920x1080 monitor. So you will need to adjust the **-s 640,200** to correspond to the size of your screen. Between the placement and the size you can see that gives you a small gap in between the 5 parts.

```
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Change the xftfont size value if the template is not
# appearing aligned
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# xftfont DejaVu Sans:size=12
#xftfont DejaVu Sans Mono:size=9
# xftfont DroidSansFallback:size=10
# xftfont DejaVu Sans Mono:size=12
[COLOR=#FF0000]xftfont DroidSans:size=10:style=bold[/COLOR]
# xftfont FreeSerif:size=15
# xftfont FreeSans:size=10
```


This is on Trusty 14.04:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2015-04-22112547.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2015-04-22112547.php")

On some systems like Mate I've had to install this to get the whole thing to work. Then it took several times of rebooting for it to actually work.
I doubt you'd have to do this with regular Ubuntu though: **sudo cpan HTML::Entities**

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-04-24
Hazzabin....

it looks to me that all you need to do is to UNCOMMENT some items in the conkywx-surface-image.sh file

try this:

```
template1 /tmp/wx-mateto1504-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gsi_image.gif
template2 /tmp/wx-mateto1504-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gwsi_image.gif
```

this tells conkywx where to look for the images....

then you need to select what size image & where to place it by editing these lines:

```
TEXT
${if_existing ${template1}} ${image ${template1} [COLOR="#FF0000"]-p 240, 2 -s 450x300[/COLOR]}${else}No CIMSS Surface Analysis Image${endif}
${if_existing ${template2}} ${image ${template2} [COLOR="#FF0000"]-p 240, 305 -s 450x275[/COLOR]}${else}${goto 310}No Wund Surface Analysis Image${endif}

```

this code will put 2 images on your screen....

the [COLOR="#FF0000"]-p 240, 2[/COLOR] will put the 1st image 240 PIXELS from the left margin & 2 PIXELS down from the top margin of the surface-image box....
the [COLOR="#FF0000"]image size is 450 PIXELS X 300 PIXELS[/COLOR]....

the 2nd image will be 305 PIXELS down from the top of box....the image size will be 450 PIXELS X 275 PIXELS....

you will need to play with these values until you get them where you want them....

the next set of options will put much larger images on your screen....

again, you can play with the size & positions to get it like you want...

tommy

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-24
> **Hazzabin said:**
> Thanks cavsfan yes I got those images in tmp folder 

I looked here to see what suppose to happen or do

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/selection0033.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/selection0033.php")


and then followed through to here

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/selection0042.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/selection0042.php")

am I suppose to copypaste the above $ image thing into it, Sorry I'm not understanding what to do, geez getting late hope that makes sense

Tommy or Frank  would be of more help with the pictures. I had them at one time but now just have Paramvir's Big Lua Mix and VinDSL's conky.
But it's good to know you have them to work with. I see Tommy has already responded to helping you get the pictures going.

But adding the restart command to your **~/.bashrc** file is simpler than  entering **killall -SIGUSR1 conky** every time. Then you just have to enter **crestart** in terminal

```
#Restart conky
alias crestart='killall -SIGUSR1 conky'

```

But for some odd reason the bottom half inch or so will be gone after a restart. You have to either logoff and back on or I found another way to "refresh" the weather data like when you've been gone for some hours and want the current weather. It will also make the conky go all the way down to the bottom not like the restart conky. I added this also to the** ~/.bachrc** file:

```
#Refresh and restart all conkys
alias crefresh='killall conky && /usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh'

#refresh and restart just VinDSL's conky
alias cvrefresh='killall conky && conky -c /home/cavsfan/.conkyrc.vindsl'
```

Edit to match your situation. But when I come back from say 4 hours and want current weather I enter either **crefresh** for both conks or **cvrefresh** for just VinDSL's conky.

This is on my newly installed final release of Ubuntu Vivid Vervet Mate 15.04;

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot13ee6676e8379806e3ff16184c50d5f2.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot13ee6676e8379806e3ff16184c50d5f2.php")

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-24
Just wanted to mention as I forgot to in the previous post that I do not recommend using the refresh alias every time you make a change to conky as that will force a fresh weather fetch and if we do that too often the weather sites may do something like they did in the past and make it unavailable to us.

Thus complicating Paramvir's job. I know he permits your choice of several weather websites to use but I just don't recommend it. Maybe make the most changes you can and then logout and back in or something.

Just my 2 sense. :P

### Post by QIII on 2015-04-24
1.  Starting a thread does not confer ownership to the OP nor does is confer on the OP any special authority to dictate its path.  The Forums belong to the collective community.

2.  Only Staff can redirect threads to their original subject.  In this case the thread evolved to its current content rather than taking an abrupt turn off topic.  This thread has been shaped by the community to its current content and that seems to suit those who continue to post in it.

3.  Only Staff can close threads.  Forum members may use the "Report post" button to request that a thread be closed.  But in this case I doubt any staff member will bother to make the effort and disrupt any current discussion.

4.  Today is not three years ago.

5. Carry on without the territory marking.

#Restart conky
alias crestart='killall -SIGUSR1 conky'

```

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-25
> **42dorian2 said:**
> Okay, I gotta ask... Shouldn't the Conky Restart alias include the new conkywx work by Paramvir?  I know it calls the weather fresh every time, but, isn't it just simpler to have one restart for the whole thing, rather than separating conkywx from Conky?  That can confuse some people when they're tired.  Restart the wrong one, and you end up scratching your head.

Actually that command restarts any and all conkys you have running. The weather is not updated every time that command is initiated either. It is based on a timer in the conf file and the template.

This is from the VinDSL rc file:

```
${execpi **1800** ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font} 
```

1800 is 30 minutes - 1800÷60=30
