Glad it seems to be coming together. Don't forget to put a space before the & in red or it will error out.

As a man of my word, here is my VinDSL conky.rc file. On Mate I had to make major changes in the font sizes and positioning of everything, which sometimes goes with the territory. 
If you want it to be totally  transparent comment out the line just below TEXT and uncomment out the  other line. 
Or adjust the **-c 0x555555:0.5** part: higher numbers are less transparent, zeros are totally transparent. I change it based on the wallpaper. 
My screen is 1920x1080 so if yours is the same it should  fit perfectly, If not I'd trim some of the processes down from 10 (I  used to only display 3 until I resized everything).
I'll also include  the weather template for the conky since it will fit perfectly  otherwise you would have to figure out how to adjust each of the weather objects.
If you do it will only be the vertical positioning you will need to adjust - the y part of the x,y coordinates and voffsets. 

I fetch the weather every 15 minutes (900 seconds) and this needs to match what is in the bottom of the conf file with your locations, etc.
30 minutes is normal I believe (1800 seconds) but I like to keep track of the UV rays as I don't need any more skin cancer. It just happens to be 10.0 Very High at this moment.

```
###############################################
## VinDSL | rev. 01-14-15 05:10 | ver. 3.0.0 ##
###############################################
##             January 2015 Series           ##
###############################################

## ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

####
## Development Platforms (current)
#
#  Linux Mint 17.1 'Rebecca'    (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Ubuntu 14.04 'Trusty Tahr'   (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Ubuntu 15.04 'Vivid Vervet'  (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Screen Resolution: 1920x1080 (ASUS VN289QL)
#
## Tips n' Tricks: conky 1.8.1 will no longer be supported. RIP :)

####
## Prerequisites (required)
#
#  conky-all 1.8.0-1 or 1.9.0
#  Conkywx 3.0.0 (Paramvir Likhari)
#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor

####
## Installed fonts (required)
#
#  Arrows (JLH Fonts)
#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
#  ConkySymbols (Cavsfan Mint systems only - not included)
#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva)
#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
#  Neuropol (Cavsfan Mint systems only - not included)
#

## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

####
## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
#
use_xft yes
xftfont DroidSans:size=8.75
xftalpha 0.1

####
## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
#
override_utf8_locale yes

####
## Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.
#
background yes

####
## Update interval in seconds.
#
update_interval 2.0

####
## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
## Zero makes Conky run forever.
#
total_run_times 0

####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
#own_window_type override
####
## Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
#
# own_window_argb_visual yes
# own_window_argb_value 255

####
## Force images to redraw when they change.
#
imlib_cache_size 1

####
## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
#
double_buffer yes

####
## Draw shades?
#
draw_shades yes
default_shade_color 330

####
## Draw outlines?
#
draw_outline no

####
## Draw borders around text?
#
draw_borders no

####
## Draw borders around graphs?
#
draw_graph_borders no

####
## Print text to stdout?
## Print text in console?
#
out_to_ncurses no
out_to_console no

####
## Text alignment.
#
alignment top_right

####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
minimum_size 245 1000

####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
#
text_buffer_size 13936

####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 750

####added by Cavsfan
## Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
# max_user_text 81920
# max_user_text 20000
max_user_text 168224

####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 245

####
## Gap between text and screen borders.
#
gap_x 6   # Left/Right
gap_y 32  # Top/Bottom

####
## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
#
short_units yes

####
## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
#
pad_percents 0

####
## Pad spacing between text and borders.
#
border_inner_margin 4

####
## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
#
top_name_width 10

####
## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
## Set to yes, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
#
no_buffers yes

####
## Set to yes, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
#
uppercase no

####
## Number of cpu samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
cpu_avg_samples 2

####
## Number of net samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
net_avg_samples 2

####
## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
## Only affects certain objects.
#
use_spacer right

####
## My colors (suit yourself)
#
color0 White            #FFFFFF
color1 Ivory            #FFFFF0
color2 Ivory2           #EEEEE0
color3 yellow           #CDCDC1
#color4 DeepSkyBlue        #3BB9FF
#color4 DodgerBlue        #1589FF
#color4 DodgerBlue2        #157DEC
#color4 Tan1            #FFA54F
color4 Gold
#color4 green            #00FF00
#color4 Turquoise1        #52F3FF
#color4 Magenta            #FF00FF
#color4 cyan            #00FFFF
#color4 Red                #FF0000
color5 cyan             #EE9A49
color6 Gray             #7E7E7E
color7 AntiqueWhite4    #8B8378
color8 DimGray          #696969
color9 Tomato           #FF6347
# colorX OrangeRed      #FF4500

#####
## Load Lua for shading (optional)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
lua_load /usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/draw_bg.lua
# lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

####
## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_testing.lua
# lua_draw_hook_post main_bars

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export path statements for use in *conky* config file
# if you move this file else where then give the full path for WX_DIR variable
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## do not uncomment
#WX_DIR="/usr/share/conkywx"; #export WX_DIR

####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
template1 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-conkywx.conf"
####
## Choose Graphic or Dingbat template
template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh"
#template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh"
####
template7 /tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert

## To switch between Imperial and Metric change template9 to F for Imperial or C for Metric values - template4 file does not require any change - it changes based on Units_to_Use in the vindsl-conkywx.conf file
template9 F

TEXT
#${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 45 -c 0x000000:0.2 -p NA,NA}
${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x555555:0.5 -s 640,1025 -p 1,5}
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}1${offset 1}2${offset 1}.${offset 0}0${offset 0}4${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=113}${color2}v${font}${voffset -70}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=13}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${if_match "${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '9-11'}" == "LTS"} ${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '1-7'} ${else} ${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font} ${endif}${font}
####
##  Uncomment For Arch Linux
#${voffset 0}${font OpenLogos:size=24}${color2}B${font}${voffset -20}${goto 50}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=24}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' }${font}
####
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
${voffset 2}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=9.55}${color4}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
# ${voffset 0}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:size=8}${color4}Pre-Alpha${font}
####
## Uncomment to display YOUR custom graphic image (upper-right corner)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -52}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/ABC.jpg -p 180,0 -s 52x52 -n}

##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################
${voffset -5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}SYSTEM${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
# ${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec lsb_release -sd | sed -e 's/development/dev/g'}${alignr}${pre_exec lsb_release -sr}${font}
${voffset 2}${offset -2}${font OpenLogos:size=14}${color2}Z${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 3}${sysname}${offset 3}${kernel}${alignr -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${machine}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nVidia ID (nvidia-smi version - dynamic)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${font}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for 'nvidia-current' driver ID (DPKG version - hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec dpkg --status nvidia-current | grep Version | cut -f 1 -d '-' | sed 's/[^.,0-9]//g'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-19'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (any card / any driver - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Video Card Product Name${alignr}Driver Version${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU frequency (hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Quad Q9550${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Multi${offset 3}Core${alignr -7}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
####
## Uncomment for System Uptime (hybrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}q${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}System${offset 3}Uptime${alignr -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${uptime_short}${font}
## uncoment for grub version
#${voffset 2}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Grub${offset 3}Version${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec grub-install -V | grep grub-install | cut -c21-42}${font}
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##

${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}PROCESSORS+TEMPS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#

##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -8}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color6}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${voffset -6}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color4}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color4}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -67}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=68}${color4}${alignc 93}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${voffset -60}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.65}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)

#${if_running rhythmbox}
#${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt | head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 2 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running rhythmbox}
${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##    BANSHEE (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running banshee}
${voffset -26}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}BANSHEE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f1- -d " "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##   GUAYADEQUE (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running guayadeque}
${voffset -36}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}GUAYADEQUE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}
${voffset -44}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  Audacious (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -48}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}AUDACIOUS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${voffset 3}${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${voffset 2}${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate:${if_match ${audacious_frequency}==-1}${alignc} 0Hz${else}${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${endif}${alignr}${audacious_status}
${voffset 0}Output: ${if_match ${audacious_channels}==2}Stereo ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==1}Mono ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}>2} audacious_channels${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==-1} ${else}Unknown ${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${alignr}Bit Rate:#
${if_match ${audacious_bitrate}==-1} 0Kbs${else}${alignr} ${audacious_bitrate}${endif}${font}${endif}
```

Here's the weather template. You just have to change the F to a C above to get Celsius weather.

```
############################################################
## VinDSL | Paramvir | rev. 01-14-15 05:10| ver. GRAPHIC ##
############################################################
## WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 3.0.0                     ##
############################################################
${voffset -3}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color6}${alignc -18}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -12}${offset 2}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9.5}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}
${voffset -40}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.75}${color6}[D1T]${font}
${voffset 2}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.3}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -35}${goto 194}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color6}I${font}
${voffset 2}${font Ubuntu:size=22}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
${voffset 1}${alignc}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
${voffset -3}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FM]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}U${offset 1}V${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[UV]${font}
####    Windvane (without -i 19 pointing to a windvane image it draws one with lua)
#${lua main -n showcond -p 7, 713 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 19 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
### This leaves out the image so it draws one with lua
### ${lua main -n showcond -p 7, 713 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${lua main -n showcond -p 1,655 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 13 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${voffset -55}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
#${voffset 22}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 192}[MP]${font}
${voffset 22}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset 0}${alignr}[MP]${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}${goto 10}Temp${goto 60}Baro${goto 110}Dew${goto 160}Hum${goto 210}Wspd
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 5,759 -d [GT] -c 0xEE4000:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 55,759 -d [GP] -c 0xFF83FA:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 105,759 -d [GD] -c 0x00FFFF:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 155,759 -d [GH] -c 0x00FF00:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 205,759 -d [GW] -c 0x00FF00:1}
${voffset 8}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
####    Present Condition  WX
${lua main -n showcond -p 3,565 -s 60 -w ci -t 1 -f [PIF] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [PIC] -j 0 }#
####    Present Condition MOON
${lua main -n showcond -p 185, 650 -s 60 -w mi -f [MIF] -d [MROT] -i [MIC] }#
####    Forecast Conditions
${lua main -n showcond -p 22,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D2F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D2I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 82,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D3F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D3I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 141,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D4F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D4I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 198,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D5F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D5I] }
${voffset 17}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9.5}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}
${voffset -2}${goto 24}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.45}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D2P]${goto 84}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D3P]${goto 144}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D4P]${goto 201}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D5P]${font}#

```

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-04-27
> **Hazzabin said:**
> Ok, done installed, and many thanks mate

As when I've tried the weather in vindsl-conky I've gotten this problem, I do believe I have the fonts, least I've got as per that Font tz 

anyways here the issue

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotcrop.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotcrop.php")


[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/selection0121.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/selection0121.php")

It looks like you don't have nvidia-settings installed since you probably don't have an nVidia card I'm guessing. You'll have to change all of the lines that involve nvidia to something else.
Or delete those lines I'm not sure what to tell you. Maybe some one else has some ideas.

But, you have to make sure that the templates point to the files on your system and not mine. Like /media/cavsfan/Fantom is my USB drive.
It looks like you didn't change the weather templates to point to your files.

Just above TEXT is where the templates are defined;
```
####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
template1 "/home/[COLOR=#ff0000]cavsfan[/COLOR]/.config/conkywx/vindsl-conkywx.conf"
####
## Choose Graphic or Dingbat template
template4 "/home/[COLOR=#ff0000]cavsfan[/COLOR]/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh"
#template4 "/home/[COLOR=#ff0000]cavsfan[/COLOR]/.config/conkywx/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh"
####
template7 /tmp/wx-[COLOR=#ff0000]cavsfan[/COLOR]-vindsl-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert

## To switch between Imperial and Metric change template9 to F for Imperial or C for Metric values - template4 file does not require any change - it changes based on Units_to_Use in the vindsl-conkywx.conf file
template9 F 
```

Get those templates with the red in them to match your system. Some of them may be in totally different places than mine are. Then see what you have.

Then you'll have to roll up your sleeves like we all have done more than once and start doing some heavy duty re-alignment of the y (vertical) positioning of most everything. 
Start with the line just below the line that does match up well. When I first installed Mate the positioning was horrible and it took much tweaking and adjusting of font sizes and positioning to get it right.
And I mean starting at the top.

The nvidia card or lack thereof should only affect the 2nd line down from SYSTEM and the GPU temperature, so you can just enter something hard coded about onboard graphics if you like or comment out that line as well as the GPU temp line. That will cause everything below it to need re-aligned. But, since we chose this life we have to live it. :P

cavsfan, One thing I have noticed that on a couple of those codes you posted yours showed JANUARY 2015 in the headers, all of what I have are still April 2014

I've downloaded from the Params site, so I guess I'm missing something?

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/selection007.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/selection007.php")

Yep you are free to do what you please and like what you like and include what you want to include for sure! :P

I may have edited those 2 files with the date from Paramvir's site that now hosts all of VinDSL's stuff as well as the weather. [http://foreverquest.blogspot.in/2015/01/conkywx-three-released-conkywx-conky.html](http://foreverquest.blogspot.in/2015/01/conkywx-three-released-conkywx-conky.html)

But as long as you get this in terminal you are good to go:

```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ conkywx --version
3.0.0
```

Paramvir may have not updated the date and version on those but considering all the other hard work he did to put everything in one place and all the programming he did I would put that in the trivial column.

I got a message from someone who asked about my beautiful Mint 17 screen and he asked should he just start at page one. 
I said no that is outdated and pointed him in the right direction of what to install etcetera. I did mention the two fonts I added for Mint and where to get them from ConkySymbols and Neuropol.
But I said to get rolling and when he learns enough to be able to ask a question on here that's what he needs to do.

I finally agree that it's better if everyone learns their way on their own. About the only thing you need to do is search this thread for conkywx 3.0 and start from there. IMO :)

You're just not done yet Hazzabin.  You're doing fine.  You gotta stop calling things "Wrong" and "Mistakes"... if not for your own good, think of my SANITY, okay?  Please?  I don't know how many of us it IS driving crazy, but at least for MYSELF... Call your Conky what it REALLY is, okay?  "Incomplete."  There's nothing broken or in error, either by you, or by the programs involved, so, please.  Don't call what you've done "Broken", "In Error", or "A Mistake", or "Wrong"... Just call it "Not Done Yet", okay?  It's okay to not be HAPPY with it yet, but it's not broken.

You're.  Doing.  Fine.  Keep Going!

Me I kept my Vivid Mate intact and created another 10GB partition for Wiley. 

I went with the regular Ubuntu 15.04 ISO and then before updating anything did the **sudo sed -i 's/vivid/wily/g' /etc/apt/sources.list** command and then got updates.

I got that from ventrical's wiki for testers [https://wiki.ubuntu.com/U+1/tester-wiki#Sudo_Code_Definitions](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/U+1/tester-wiki#Sudo_Code_Definitions)
Which comes in handy. :)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-05-10
Yeah you're picking it up as well as the rest of the rest of us have. Everybody has to start somewhere and there is a huge learning curve.

### Post by aquarius18 on 2015-05-10
> **Cavsfan said:**
> ....The Mint one I customized a bit with the Mint green LM logo at the top instead of the white Ubuntu one. That took days.... And I cannot copy it to any other install; it doesn't work.

Solved the Mint logo a few years ago simply by doing this:

```
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment to display custom graphic image in place of logo
**[COLOR=#ff0000]${voffset 32}${image /home/frank/Pictures/linuxmint.png -p 0,-5 -s 300x78 -n}[/COLOR]**
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color0}v${font}${voffset -76}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.8}${color4}1${offset 3}1${offset 1}.${offset 1}1${offset 1}0${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color0}v${font}${voffset -76}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.8}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
####
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
# ${voffset -2}${goto 290}${font Ubuntu-B:size=8.5}${color0}15${font} 
```

Can copy the whole lot to another Mint install as long as I also copy the image to the same location.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-05-11
I really can't explain it or why it seems that the Mint VinDSL conky cannot be copied to another version or from another version but that has been my experience.
But, I added the conkysymbols.ttf font to get the Mint logo for the top and the neuropol.ttf to give the font for the "Linux Mint" line at the top as well as the fan symbols next to the fan speeds.

```
## Uncomment for Mint systems)
${voffset 32}${color9}${font conkysymbols:size=32}${voffset -35}u${font}${goto 59}${voffset -8}${font Neuropol:size=10.6}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'DESCRIPTION' | awk -F'"' '{print $2}'}${font}
${voffset -32}${offset 120}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
####
```

```
##################################
##          FAN SPEEDS          ##
##################################
${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}FAN SPEEDS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU FAN${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan1' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan2' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan3' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
```

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-05-11
Hazza...

you already have the first 2 WX images....the top image is showing winds aloft, and the 2nd image
is showing WX SAT views of clouds in the IR spectrum.....by comparing the 2 images you get a
detailed view of approaching WX...

the 3rd image is the current surface conditions for a given area....Barometric High/Low
areas of pressure + fronts passing thru.....this is still in testing mode & has not been released
for general use yet....

most likely will be in conkywx v 3.1  along with some other features being worked on...

patience grasshopper....

tommy

Hazza

This is what i have on all my conky's Linux Mint, Ubuntu with Unity and Ubuntu Mate:
```
####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
#### 
```

That has always worked for me and is from VinDSL's original conky. I have an old Nvidia Geforce 9800 GT.

The man page says that if you use own_window_type override it says it ignores the own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager

```
       own_window_type
              if own_window is yes, you may specify type normal, desktop, dock, panel or override (default: normal).  Desktop windows are special windows that have no window decorations; are always visible
              on your desktop; do not appear in your pager or taskbar; and are sticky across all workspaces. Panel windows reserve space along a desktop edge, just like panels and taskbars, preventing max&#8208;
              imized windows from overlapping them. The edge is chosen based on the alignment option. Override windows are not under the control of the window manager. Hints are ignored. This type of  win&#8208;
              dow can be useful for certain situations.

       own_window_hints undecorated,below,above,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
              If own_window is yes, you may use these window manager hints to affect the way Conky displays. Notes: Use own_window_type desktop as another way to implement many of these  hints  implicitly.
              _If you use own_window_type override, window manager hints have no meaning and are ignored._


```

I think it was just a fluke probably on the devel system. But if it happens again I'll remember to look at what I'm on.

Regards

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-05-23
> **Hazzabin said:**
> I decided to fill in my boring time with making adjustments to the 'Now' template and image, 2nd row from left is the 'Now' with 3 different weather basic info, I switched to a very dark background to all would show up better. 

 [[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/work021sml.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/work021sml.php")

Looking pretty sweet! You know you can also adjust the opacity with these parameters too if you want to go with a slightly lighter wallpaper but what is great is it's entirely up to you and what you want!

In VinDSL's conky I adjusted this:
```
TEXT
#${voffset -15}${lua main -n background -r 45 -c 0x[COLOR=#ff0000]000000:0.2[/COLOR] -p NA,NA}
${voffset -16}${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x[COLOR=#ff0000]333333:0.3[/COLOR] -s 260,1028 -p 1,1}
```

Zeros are total transparent and the higher the number the more opaque, 3 is fairly transparent.

In the Big Lua Mix big-luamix-template.sh Paramvir made the default 7 which is fairly opaque. But when I found out I could adjust it based on the wallpaper I use I did it.
```
###--------- NOTE: # character at the end of line is line break character -----
###--------- Backgrounds ------------------------------------------------------
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.8 -s 640,200 -p 1,5}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.8 -s 640,200 -p 1,210}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.7 -s 640,200 -p 1,415}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.7 -s 640,200 -p 1,620}#
#${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x777777:0.7 -s 640,200 -p 1,825}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c [COLOR=#ff0000]0x333333:0.3[/COLOR] -s 640,200 -p 1,5}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c [COLOR=#ff0000]0x333333:0.3[/COLOR] -s 640,200 -p 1,210}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c [COLOR=#ff0000]0x333333:0.3[/COLOR] -s 640,200 -p 1,415}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c [COLOR=#ff0000]0x333333:0.3[/COLOR] -s 640,200 -p 1,620}#
${lua main -n background -r 25 -c [COLOR=#ff0000]0x333333:0.3[/COLOR] -s 640,200 -p 1,825}#

```

You've probably seen this screen but it's with the 0x333333:0.3 setting:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2015-05-23115253.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2015-05-23115253.php")

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-05-24
Hazza....

try adjusting the following parameter:

```
#-------------------------------------------------------------------
# Colours used in this conkyrc and associated template
#-------------------------------------------------------------------
color1 Tan1
# color1 white
color2 Gray
color3 white
color4 DarkSlateGray
color5 yellow
color7 darkgrey
color8 cyan

maximum_width 470
[COLOR="#FF0000"]minimum_size 470 1[/COLOR]

use_xft yes
xftalpha 0.8
```

change the value to something other than 470 [COLOR="#FF0000"]1[/COLOR]

the conky documentation only mentions [COLOR="#FF0000"]width[/COLOR] as a variable,
but you can also change the vertical resolution....

no guarantees, but it's worth a try anyway....

tommy

### Post by donkarlos on 2015-06-17
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Glad it seems to be coming together. Don't forget to put a space before the & in red or it will error out.

As a man of my word, here is my VinDSL conky.rc file. On Mate I had to make major changes in the font sizes and positioning of everything, which sometimes goes with the territory. 
If you want it to be totally  transparent comment out the line just below TEXT and uncomment out the  other line. 
Or adjust the **-c 0x555555:0.5** part: higher numbers are less transparent, zeros are totally transparent. I change it based on the wallpaper. 
My screen is 1920x1080 so if yours is the same it should  fit perfectly, If not I'd trim some of the processes down from 10 (I  used to only display 3 until I resized everything).
I'll also include  the weather template for the conky since it will fit perfectly  otherwise you would have to figure out how to adjust each of the weather objects.
If you do it will only be the vertical positioning you will need to adjust - the y part of the x,y coordinates and voffsets. 

I fetch the weather every 15 minutes (900 seconds) and this needs to match what is in the bottom of the conf file with your locations, etc.
30 minutes is normal I believe (1800 seconds) but I like to keep track of the UV rays as I don't need any more skin cancer. It just happens to be 10.0 Very High at this moment.

```
###############################################
## VinDSL | rev. 01-14-15 05:10 | ver. 3.0.0 ##
###############################################
##             January 2015 Series           ##
###############################################

## ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

####
## Development Platforms (current)
#
#  Linux Mint 17.1 'Rebecca'    (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Ubuntu 14.04 'Trusty Tahr'   (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Ubuntu 15.04 'Vivid Vervet'  (Conky 1.9.0)
#  Screen Resolution: 1920x1080 (ASUS VN289QL)
#
## Tips n' Tricks: conky 1.8.1 will no longer be supported. RIP :)

####
## Prerequisites (required)
#
#  conky-all 1.8.0-1 or 1.9.0
#  Conkywx 3.0.0 (Paramvir Likhari)
#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor

####
## Installed fonts (required)
#
#  Arrows (JLH Fonts)
#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
#  ConkySymbols (Cavsfan Mint systems only - not included)
#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva)
#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
#  Neuropol (Cavsfan Mint systems only - not included)
#

## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

####
## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
#
use_xft yes
xftfont DroidSans:size=8.75
xftalpha 0.1

####
## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
#
override_utf8_locale yes

####
## Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.
#
background yes

####
## Update interval in seconds.
#
update_interval 2.0

####
## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
## Zero makes Conky run forever.
#
total_run_times 0

####
## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
#
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_type normal
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
#own_window_type override
####
## Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
#
# own_window_argb_visual yes
# own_window_argb_value 255

####
## Force images to redraw when they change.
#
imlib_cache_size 1

####
## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
#
double_buffer yes

####
## Draw shades?
#
draw_shades yes
default_shade_color 330

####
## Draw outlines?
#
draw_outline no

####
## Draw borders around text?
#
draw_borders no

####
## Draw borders around graphs?
#
draw_graph_borders no

####
## Print text to stdout?
## Print text in console?
#
out_to_ncurses no
out_to_console no

####
## Text alignment.
#
alignment top_right

####
## Minimum size of the text area.
## Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]
#
minimum_size 245 1000

####
## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
#
text_buffer_size 13936

####
## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
#
max_specials 750

####added by Cavsfan
## Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
# max_user_text 81920
# max_user_text 20000
max_user_text 168224

####
## Maximum width of the text area.
## Syntax: maximum_width [width]
#
maximum_width 245

####
## Gap between text and screen borders.
#
gap_x 6   # Left/Right
gap_y 32  # Top/Bottom

####
## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
#
short_units yes

####
## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
#
pad_percents 0

####
## Pad spacing between text and borders.
#
border_inner_margin 4

####
## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
#
top_name_width 10

####
## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
## Set to yes, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
#
no_buffers yes

####
## Set to yes, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
#
uppercase no

####
## Number of cpu samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
cpu_avg_samples 2

####
## Number of net samples to average.
## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
#
net_avg_samples 2

####
## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
## Only affects certain objects.
#
use_spacer right

####
## My colors (suit yourself)
#
color0 White            #FFFFFF
color1 Ivory            #FFFFF0
color2 Ivory2           #EEEEE0
color3 yellow           #CDCDC1
#color4 DeepSkyBlue        #3BB9FF
#color4 DodgerBlue        #1589FF
#color4 DodgerBlue2        #157DEC
#color4 Tan1            #FFA54F
color4 Gold
#color4 green            #00FF00
#color4 Turquoise1        #52F3FF
#color4 Magenta            #FF00FF
#color4 cyan            #00FFFF
#color4 Red                #FF0000
color5 cyan             #EE9A49
color6 Gray             #7E7E7E
color7 AntiqueWhite4    #8B8378
color8 DimGray          #696969
color9 Tomato           #FF6347
# colorX OrangeRed      #FF4500

#####
## Load Lua for shading (optional)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
lua_load /usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/draw_bg.lua
# lua_draw_hook_pre draw_bg

####
## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
## Set the path to your script here.
#
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/bargraph_small.lua
# lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_testing.lua
# lua_draw_hook_post main_bars

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export path statements for use in *conky* config file
# if you move this file else where then give the full path for WX_DIR variable
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## do not uncomment
#WX_DIR="/usr/share/conkywx"; #export WX_DIR

####
## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
#
template0 "/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh"
template1 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-conkywx.conf"
####
## Choose Graphic or Dingbat template
template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh"
#template4 "/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh"
####
template7 /tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert

## To switch between Imperial and Metric change template9 to F for Imperial or C for Metric values - template4 file does not require any change - it changes based on Units_to_Use in the vindsl-conkywx.conf file
template9 F

TEXT
#${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 45 -c 0x000000:0.2 -p NA,NA}
${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x555555:0.5 -s 640,1025 -p 1,5}
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}1${offset 1}2${offset 1}.${offset 0}0${offset 0}4${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=113}${color2}v${font}${voffset -70}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=13}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 174}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${if_match "${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '9-11'}" == "LTS"} ${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '1-7'} ${else} ${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font} ${endif}${font}
####
##  Uncomment For Arch Linux
#${voffset 0}${font OpenLogos:size=24}${color2}B${font}${voffset -20}${goto 50}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=24}${color4}${pre_exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' }${font}
####
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
${voffset 2}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=9.55}${color4}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
# ${voffset 0}${goto 181}${font Ubuntu-B:size=8}${color4}Pre-Alpha${font}
####
## Uncomment to display YOUR custom graphic image (upper-right corner)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -52}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/ABC.jpg -p 180,0 -s 52x52 -n}

##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################
${voffset -5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}SYSTEM${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
# ${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec lsb_release -sd | sed -e 's/development/dev/g'}${alignr}${pre_exec lsb_release -sr}${font}
${voffset 2}${offset -2}${font OpenLogos:size=14}${color2}Z${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 3}${sysname}${offset 3}${kernel}${alignr -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${machine}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nVidia ID (nvidia-smi version - dynamic)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${font}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${pre_exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for 'nvidia-current' driver ID (DPKG version - hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec dpkg --status nvidia-current | grep Version | cut -f 1 -d '-' | sed 's/[^.,0-9]//g'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-19'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (any card / any driver - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Video Card Product Name${alignr}Driver Version${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU frequency (hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Quad Q9550${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Multi${offset 3}Core${alignr -7}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
####
## Uncomment for System Uptime (hybrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}q${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}System${offset 3}Uptime${alignr -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${uptime_short}${font}
## uncoment for grub version
#${voffset 2}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}Grub${offset 3}Version${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${pre_exec grub-install -V | grep grub-install | cut -c21-42}${font}
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}PROCESSORS+TEMPS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 70,140 -w 145 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 70,153 -w 145 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 70,166 -w 145 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 70,179 -w 145 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4}
##################################
##          FAN SPEEDS          ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}FAN SPEEDS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU FAN${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan1' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan2' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan3' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
##################################
##            MEMORY            ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}MEMORY${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}l${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 3}RAM${goto 97}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${mem}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${memmax}${alignr}${memperc}%${font}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,279 -w 235 -s 1 -f memperc -a NA -l 1 -x 70}
##################################
##             HDD              ##
##################################
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}HDD${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}x${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}ROOT${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${fs_used /}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /}% / ${fs_used_perc /}%${font}
${voffset 11}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}${color0}${offset 4}FANTOM USB${goto 98}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${fs_used /media/cavsfan/Fantom}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /media/cavsfan/Fantom}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /media/cavsfan/Fantom}%${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,308 -w 235 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a /home -l 1 -x 70}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,329 -w 235 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a /media/cavsfan/Fantom -l 1 -x 70}
##################################
##      TOP CPU PROCESSES       ##
##################################
${voffset -18}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}TOP CPU PROCESSES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Name${goto 100}CPU${goto 163}MEM${alignr}USER${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10.5}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 1}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 1}${goto 137}${top pid 1}${goto 177}${top user 1}${alignr}${top cpu 1}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10.5}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 2}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 2}${goto 137}${top pid 2}${goto 177}${top user 2}${alignr}${top cpu 2}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10.5}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 3}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 3}${goto 137}${top pid 3}${goto 177}${top user 3}${alignr}${top cpu 3}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10.5}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 4}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 4}${goto 137}${top pid 4}${goto 177}${top user 4}${alignr}${top cpu 4}%${font}

${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10.5}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 8}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 8}${goto 137}${top pid 8}${goto 177}${top user 8}${alignr}${top cpu 8}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10.5}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 9}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 9}${goto 137}${top pid 9}${goto 177}${top user 9}${alignr}${top cpu 9}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10.5}${color0}${offset 5}${top name 10}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${top mem_res 10}${goto 137}${top pid 10}${goto 177}${top user 10}${alignr}${top cpu 10}%${font}
##################################
##          NETWORK             ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}NETWORK${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}T${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Download${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${totaldown eth0}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${alignr}${downspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}N${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Upload${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${totalup eth0}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${alignr}${upspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}LAN${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${alignr}${addr eth0}${font}
#${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${alignr}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=10}${color0}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${alignr}Giggity Giggity Goo!
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}CONKYWX${if_existing ${template7}} ${color5}Alert:${font}${lua main -n scroller -f 12 -l 21 -s 5 -p 98,563 -o ${template7}}${else} ${voffset -2}${color6}${hr 1}${endif}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -8}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color6}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${voffset -6}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color4}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color4}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -67}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=68}${color4}${alignc 93}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${voffset -60}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.65}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
# ${voffset -68}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
##################################
##         Image                ##
##################################
#${if_running audacious} ${else}#
#${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}ICAN HAZ CHEESEBURGER${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${offset -10}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/icanhascheeseburger3.jpeg -p 10,934 -s 240x200}${endif}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 1 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
#${if_running rhythmbox}
#${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:size=8.5}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt | head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 2 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running rhythmbox}
${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##    BANSHEE (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running banshee}
${voffset -26}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}BANSHEE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f1- -d " "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d " "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##   GUAYADEQUE (Experimental)  ##
##################################
${if_running guayadeque}
${voffset -36}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}GUAYADEQUE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}
${voffset -44}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color0}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 xlsclients -l | grep "VLC media player" | cut -c -9 --complement | rev | cut -c 19- | rev}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  Audacious (Experimental)    ##
##################################
${if_running audacious}
${voffset -48}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}AUDACIOUS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9}${color6}${audacious_title 250}
${voffset 3}${color4}${audacious_bar 10,250 }${color6}
${voffset 2}${audacious_position}/${audacious_length}  Sample Rate:${if_match ${audacious_frequency}==-1}${alignc} 0Hz${else}${alignc} ${audacious_frequency} Hz ${endif}${alignr}${audacious_status}
${voffset 0}Output: ${if_match ${audacious_channels}==2}Stereo ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==1}Mono ${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}>2} audacious_channels${else}${if_match ${audacious_channels}==-1} ${else}Unknown ${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${alignr}Bit Rate:#
${if_match ${audacious_bitrate}==-1} 0Kbs${else}${alignr} ${audacious_bitrate}${endif}${font}${endif}
```

Here's the weather template. You just have to change the F to a C above to get celcius weather.

```
############################################################
## VinDSL | Paramvir | rev. 01-14-15 05:10| ver. GRAPHIC ##
############################################################
## WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 3.0.0                     ##
############################################################
${voffset -3}${font RadioSpace:size=33}${color6}${alignc -18}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -12}${offset 2}${color6}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9.5}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color6}[FCTT]${font}
${voffset -40}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.75}${color6}[D1T]${font}
${voffset 2}${goto 203}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.3}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color6}[D1P]${font}
${voffset -35}${goto 194}${font KRARound:size=41.5}${color6}I${font}
${voffset 2}${font Ubuntu:size=22}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
${voffset 1}${alignc}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTSSF]${font}
${voffset -3}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color6}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color6}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[FM]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}U${offset 1}V${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color6}[UV]${font}
####    Windvane (without -i 19 pointing to a windvane image it draws one with lua)
#${lua main -n showcond -p 7, 713 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 19 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
### This leaves out the image so it draws one with lua
### ${lua main -n showcond -p 7, 713 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${lua main -n showcond -p 1,655 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 13 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
${voffset -55}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
#${voffset 22}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 192}[MP]${font}
${voffset 22}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset 0}${alignr}[MP]${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}${goto 10}Temp${goto 60}Baro${goto 110}Dew${goto 160}Hum${goto 210}Wspd
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 5,759 -d [GT] -c 0xEE4000:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 55,759 -d [GP] -c 0xFF83FA:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 105,759 -d [GD] -c 0x00FFFF:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 155,759 -d [GH] -c 0x00FF00:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 205,759 -d [GW] -c 0x00FF00:1}
${voffset 8}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.5}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
####    Present Condition  WX
${lua main -n showcond -p 3,565 -s 60 -w ci -t 1 -f [PIF] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [PIC] -j 0 }#
####    Present Condition MOON
${lua main -n showcond -p 185, 650 -s 60 -w mi -f [MIF] -d [MROT] -i [MIC] }#
####    Forecast Conditions
${lua main -n showcond -p 22,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D2F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D2I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 82,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D3F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D3I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 141,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D4F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D4I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 198,775 -s 34 -w ci -t 1 -f [D5F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D5I] }
${voffset 17}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9.5}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}
${voffset -2}${goto 24}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.45}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D2P]${goto 84}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D3P]${goto 144}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D4P]${goto 201}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D5P]${font}#

```

What do you get when you enter **sensors** in terminal?
```
cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ sensors
coretemp-isa-0000
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 0:       +41.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)
Core 1:       +41.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)
Core 2:       +39.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)
Core 3:       +39.0°C  (high = +76.0°C, crit = +100.0°C)

f71882fg-isa-0a00
Adapter: ISA adapter
+3.3V:        +3.36 V  
in1:          +1.11 V  (max =  +2.04 V)
in2:          +0.92 V  
in3:          +0.76 V  
in4:          +0.98 V  
in5:          +1.11 V  
in6:          +0.91 V  
3VSB:         +3.36 V  
Vbat:         +3.31 V  
fan1:        2347 RPM
fan2:        2814 RPM
fan3:        2712 RPM
fan4:           0 RPM  ALARM
temp1:        +33.0°C  (high = +85.0°C, hyst = +81.0°C)
                       (crit = +100.0°C, hyst = +96.0°C)  sensor = transistor
temp2:        +36.0°C  (high = +85.0°C, hyst = +81.0°C)
                       (crit = +95.0°C, hyst = +91.0°C)  sensor = thermistor
temp3:        +31.0°C  (high = +70.0°C, hyst = +68.0°C)
                       (crit = +85.0°C, hyst = +83.0°C)  sensor = transistor

```

That is from installing **lm-sensors** and running **sudo sensors-detect** and answering Yes to the questions. 
Then at the end definitely answer Yes to this:
```
To load everything that is needed, add this to /etc/modules:
#----cut here----
# Chip drivers
coretemp
f71882fg
#----cut here----
If you have some drivers built into your kernel, the list above will
contain too many modules. Skip the appropriate ones!

Do you want to add these lines automatically to /etc/modules? ([COLOR=#ff0000]yes[/COLOR]/NO)
```

Also the fan fonts are not included in the conky-assistant.

They are in **conkysymbols** font which can be downloaded and installed from here: [http://gnome-look.org/content/show.php/Conky+Symbols?content=142593]("http://gnome-look.org/content/show.php/Conky+Symbols?content=142593")

```
##################################
##          FAN SPEEDS          ##
##################################
${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}FAN SPEEDS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU FAN${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan1' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan2' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}FAN3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan3' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
```

I hope this helps. :)

It looks like you have just the cpu and chassis fans. - Es sieht aus wie Sie nur die CPU und Gehäuselüfter haben.

Try this: (Versuchen Sie das)
```
##################################
##          FAN SPEEDS          ##
##################################
${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}FAN SPEEDS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CPU FAN${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'CPU Fan Speed' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 0}${font conkysymbols:size=9.9}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}CHASSIS FAN${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Chassis Fan Speed' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}

```

Capitalization is your choice. Kapitalisierung ist Ihre Wahl.

The font is just the fan picture on the left side. Die Schrift ist nur der Lüfter Bild auf der linken Seite .

[ATTACH=CONFIG]262759[/ATTACH]

### Post by VinDSL on 2015-07-01
**[COLOR="#FF0000"]WARNING[/COLOR]**:  Vincent Cheng is on the move again...  :D

I just did an incremental Ubu 15.10 (Wily Werewolf) upgrade.  

Afterward, Conky was janky and throwing errors in CLI.  I noticed 'conky-all' had been upgraded to ver 1.10.x  

The workaround was to purge Conky 1.10.x  and re-install Conky 1.9.x (and pin 1.9.x).

If you need to download Conky 1.9.x, here's the link I used: [https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/conky-all/1.9.0-4](https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/conky-all/1.9.0-4)

Appears the config system has gone the lua way and you need to wrap you brain about this :-
wrap your config section in ```
conky.config = { ... }
```

wrap your TEXT section in ```
conky.text = [[ ... ]]
```

### Post by paramvir on 2015-07-03
We are now at **FOUR** - Conkywx 4.0.2 is OUT :guitar:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/conkywx402.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/conkywx402.php") [[IMG]http://www.zimagez.com/miniature/conkywx-402.jpg[/IMG]]("http://www.zimagez.com/zimage/conkywx-402.php") [[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/conkywx4020.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/conkywx4020.php")

cheers ;)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-07-04
I had trouble installing that conky-all tar-gz2 file. It kept saying it didn't see a configuration file or something like that so I just installed the deb file from here:

It has both 32 bit and 64 bit versions at the bottom and I got my conkys back. :guitar:

[https://packages.debian.org/jessie/conky-all](https://packages.debian.org/jessie/conky-all)

I love the simplicity of installing a deb file in Ubuntu with Ubuntu Software Center. 

Here is my Ubuntu Mate 15.10 screenie while I'm here:

[[img]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotf19abd98fa9bfd5200b07a7aa786b3b3.png[/img]](http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotf19abd98fa9bfd5200b07a7aa786b3b3.php)

Of course I had to do some tweaking again to add pollen in the VinDSL conky. One pixel at a time, one pixel at a time.... :p

But, much improved Pollen count added, a super sweet clock that you can customize with like 48 different faces, Seismic activity, and more LUA!!! It doesn't get any sweeter than this!!!

I have my seismic threshold set to 2.5 so I could see some of the earthquakes going on in the US plus I've added the states in the US as I have seen them.

Overall 120% job I'd say Paramvir did on this one!!! :guitar:
