### Post by paramvir on 2015-07-05
@cavsfan

Yesss your conky stuff is coming to life...

What about the Ubuntu desktop - perhaps the own_window_type needs to be tweaked... you are getting window outlines there.

The earth quake line needs to be updated - it has it's own back ground - check the conkywx-surface-image.sh for the newer options and you can add your US list to watch list as well ;)

Great stuff

Oh by the way - Arch has updated to 1.10 - but you are using the AUR version - which will probably stay at 1.9...

The new conky 1.10 is a good thing actually - it just needs time to be ironed out - they do say it is a partial convert to c++ - but from the changes it does seem to be there already. Perhaps it is just the cmake syntax that is hindering cairo bindings... All in good time ;)

cheers

conky-all set on hold.

cavsfan@cavsfan-MS-7529:~$ apt-mark showhold
conky-all

Reading package lists... Done

### Post by Cvetan on 2015-07-05
OK, i updated to conkywx 4, and now i am reading manual. Am i getting this right, no forecast from wunderground? All data used to be drawn from wunderground.

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-07-05
> **Cvetan said:**
> OK, i updated to conkywx 4, and now i am reading manual. Am i getting this right, no forecast from wunderground? All data used to be drawn from wunderground. 

Since my old templates are configures for wunderground.

You now have several different options. See  /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf for how to set it up.

This is how I have mine: I get current from Wunderground and forecast from Intellicast. Wunderground I believe is the only one that provides UV and pollen.
I think METAR will not; I know UV doesn't come from METAR data.

```
## main weather data source - building redundancy
## for current conditions use ::
##     wunderground [w], METAR [m]
## for forecast and/or current conditions use ::
##     intellicast [1] or weather [2] or msn [3]
## use of xml data remains unchanged See Note below at xml_ID
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
default_wx=w:1 
```


@Paramvir - **own_window_argb_visual yes** did the trick of getting rid of the outlines. It says you have to have a composite manager, which I do.

Did you go through the complete conf file and set everything for your location? You have to set all the values up for your location in any conf files you are using.
That includes latatude and longitude. Just look at /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf.

### Post by Cvetan on 2015-07-05
But it says that if i leave out longitude and latitude it will get it from weather site?

Do i have to enter config for all the services or just the one i use? For instance just weather.com, since i see in the example files there are entered values for all.


I managed to get it working. ;) I entered incorrect weather.com ID, i enter the part after the colon, that's why i got the wrong data. Thanks.

We all setup longitute and latititude; you can just search for your location on google and it will show the lat and long.

### Post by NM5TF on 2015-07-09
Karl..

editieren Sie die Datei genannt [COLOR="#FF0000"]/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-image.sh
[/COLOR]
edit [COLOR="#FF0000"]/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-image.sh[/COLOR]

replace [COLOR="#FF0000"]tommy[/COLOR] with [COLOR="#0000FF"]your user name.[/COLOR]..

ersetzen Sie meinen Benutzernamen ([COLOR="#FF0000"]Tommy[/COLOR]) durch [COLOR="#0000FF"]Ihren Benutzernamen[/COLOR]...
#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Load lua component of conkywx
#-----------------------------------------------------------
lua_load ./lib/conkywx.lua

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# location of image file - conkywx tmp directory for the used conkywx config file
# IMPORTANT do not comment this line - even if you do not use it
#-----------------------------------------------------------
template1 /tmp/wx-[COLOR="#FF0000"]tommy[/COLOR]-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gsi_image.gif
template2 /tmp/wx-[COLOR="#FF0000"]tommy[/COLOR]-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gwsi_image.gif
template3 /tmp/wx-[COLOR="#FF0000"]tommy[/COLOR]-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gisi_image.jpg
template7 /tmp/wx-[COLOR="#FF0000"]tommy[/COLOR]-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/seismic

imlib_cache_size 0

TEXT

> **donkarlos said:**

Hier noch mein aktueller Dektop



Great conky Karl...

Perhaps you did not realise but - conkywx does some German and Italian as well ;)

in location ```
/home/your user name/.config/conkywx.system.conf
```

you have this - you can un-comment deutsch to see messages in terminal in German.
```

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  LANGUAGE to see Messages in
#  Current Supported Languages:
#    italian     - Message & Language Translation by "Night Train"
#    deutsch     - Message & Language Translation by "Flowo"
#    English is default and needs no setting
# Comment this variable if not required
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# language_for_msgs="italian"
# language_for_msgs="deutsch"

```

Also you have this in the main conkywx config file you are using. un-comment the duetsch line to see the weather in German.
```

## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  LANGUAGE to see data in - English is default and needs no setting
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
##  Current Supported Languages :
##  deutsch, italian,
## Comment this variable if not required
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# language_to_use="deutsch"

```

You may not have everything translated - since this exercise was done for wunderground - and now we have other sites as well - but most of the times you will see correct information.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/conkywx0.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/conkywx0.php")

Enjoy ):P

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-07-17
40 seismic events vs 30 in Arch. In the conf file:
```
seismic_time_format='%d-%H:%M'
# seismic_time_format='%d-%I:%M %p'
# seismic_time_format='%d-%I:%M %P'
num_of_events=40
minimum_magnitude=2.5
alarm_magnitude=5
```

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2015-07-1715-25-17.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2015-07-1715-25-17.php")

### Post by 42dorian2 on 2015-07-25
Having a problem launching one of my Conky files.  The old conky help thread appears to be dead.  (Not this one.  The OLD one.)

.conkyrc.network
```
#-----Conky Settings
background yes
no_buffers yes
out_to_console no
top_cpu_separate no
cpu_avg_samples 2
net_avg_samples 3
total_run_times 0
update_interval 1

#-----Text Settings
uppercase no
override_utf8_locale yes
short_units no
pad_percents 0
text_buffer_size 1024
max_user_text 65536
use_xft yes
xftalpha 0.5
xftfont GpuTeks:bold:size=10

#-----Window Settings
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
own_window_type desktop

#-----Graphics Settings
double_buffer yes
draw_borders no
draw_graph_borders yes
draw_shades no
draw_outline no
max_specials 4096
imlib_cache_size 0

#-----Layout Settings
alignment bottom_right
gap_x 445
gap_y 65
maximum_width 600
minimum_size 320 100
use_spacer right

#-----Colors
default_color 808080          #Grey Default
default_outline_color ffffff  #White Outlines
default_shade_color c0c0c0    #Silver Shade
color0 ff0000                                    #Red
color1 ffff00                                    #Yellow
color2 ffffff                                    #White
color3 808080                                    #Grey
color4 008000                                    #Green
color5 c0c0c0                                    #Silver
color6 ffd700                                    #Gold
color7 000080                                    #Navy
color8 0000FF                                    #Blue
color9 404040                                    #DarkGrey

#-----Lua To Load

lua_draw_hook_pre conky_draw_netring

#-----TransponderAOE

#-----Network Info

${color2}${goto 115}${addr eth1}

${goto 115}|${goto 130}${color4}${downspeed pan1}${goto 190}/s${goto 205}${color2}|${goto 220}${color1}${upspeed pan1}${goto 275}/s${image ~/Conky/Icons/bluetooth.png -p 290,145 -s 20x20}

60 vnstat -m | awk '/Jul/{print $9}'}${goto 175}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Jul/{print $10}'}${else}${if_match ${time %m}==09}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Aug/{print $9}'}${goto 175}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Aug/{print $10}'}${else}${if_match ${time %m}==10}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Sep/{print $9}'}${goto 175}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Sep/{print $10}'}${else}${if_match ${time %m}==11}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Oct/{print $9}'}${goto 175}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Oct/{print $10}'}${else}${if_match ${time %m}==12}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Nov/{print $9}'}${goto 175}${execi 60 vnstat -m | awk '/Nov/{print $10}'}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font}${color2}${font}
${lua conky_draw_box}
${voffset -20}${color6}${hr 1}${color2} 
```

network.lua (Calls a data usage thing.)
```
 
require 'cairo'
function draw_ring_pos(co, across, down, rad, inlw, rstart, rend, bgr, bgg, bgb, bga, inr, ing, inb, ina, direction)

local angle_0=rstart*(2*math.pi/360)-math.pi/2
local angle_f=rend*(2*math.pi/360)-math.pi/2
local t_arc=(co/100)*(angle_f-angle_0)
-- Draw background ring
cairo_arc(cr,across,down,rad,angle_0,angle_f)
cairo_set_source_rgba(cr,bgr,bgg,bgb,bga)
cairo_set_line_width(cr,inlw)
cairo_stroke(cr)
-- Draw indicator ring
cairo_arc(cr,across,down,rad,angle_0,angle_0+t_arc)
cairo_set_source_rgba(cr,inr,ing,inb,ina)
cairo_stroke(cr)
end

function conky_draw_netring()
if conky_window == nil then return end
if cs == nil or cairo_xlib_surface_get_width(cs) ~= conky_window.width or cairo_xlib_surface_get_height(cs) ~= conky_window.height then
        if cs then cairo_surface_destroy(cs) end
        cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
    end
    if cr then cairo_destroy(cr) end
cr = cairo_create(cs)
local updates=tonumber(conky_parse('${updates}'))
if updates>=5 then
--NETRING SETUP-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
wtimer=(updates % 30)+1
months = {"Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun","Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"}
monthnum=tonumber(os.date('%m'))
monthname=os.date('%b')
day=tonumber(os.date('%d'))
if day<11 then
    if monthnum==1 then
    monthbeforenum=12
    else
    monthbeforenum=monthnum-1
    end
month=months[monthbeforenum]
else    
month=monthname
end
if updates==6 or wtimer==1 then    
co=(conky_parse("${exec vnstat -m | grep -n '" .. month .. "' | sed -n 1p | awk -F'|' '{print $3}'}"))
co=string.gsub(co," ","")
num=tonumber((string.gsub(co,"%a","")))
units=(string.gsub(co,"[%p%d]",""))
end
if num==nil then num=0 end
--max number--------------------------
if units=="GB" or units=="GiB" then max=100 end
if units =="MB" or units=="MiB"  then max=102400 end
if units =="KB" or units=="KiB"  then max=104857600 end
if max==nil then max=0 end
--position-----------------------------
across=275
down=230
--radius of ring-----------------------
rad=30
--ring width---------------------------
inlw=15
--start and end angles---------------
rstart=-135
rend=135
--color and alpha for indicator----
inr,ing,inb,ina=255,0,0,1
--background ring color and alpha-----
bgr,bgg,bgb,bga=255,255,0,1
--calculation, do not edit below---------
co=(num/max)*100
--draw the actual ring-----
draw_ring_pos(co, across, down, rad, inlw, rstart, rend, bgr, bgg, bgb, bga, inr, ing, inb, ina)
--END NETRING SETUP---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
--END SETUP---------------copy setup section and paste for additional rings----------
end
end

--################################################################################
--Box Function
--################################################################################
do

require "cairo"
local cs, cr = nil

local function conky_set_the_font(cr, fface, fsize, fbold)
        if fbold == 1 then
            cairo_select_font_face(cr, fface, CAIRO_FONT_SLANT_NORMAL, CAIRO_FONT_WEIGHT_BOLD)
        else
            cairo_select_font_face(cr, fface, CAIRO_FONT_SLANT_NORMAL, CAIRO_FONT_WEIGHT_NORMAL)
        end
        cairo_set_font_size(cr, fsize)
end

local function conky_draw_label(cr, extents, string, fred, fgreen ,fblue, falpha, tacross, down)
        cairo_set_source_rgba(cr, fred, fgreen, fblue, falpha)
        cairo_move_to(cr, tacross - (extents.width /2 ), down)
        cairo_show_text(cr, string)
end

function conky_draw_box(cs, cr)
    if conky_window == nil then return end
    if cs == nil or cairo_xlib_surface_get_width(cs) ~= conky_window.width or cairo_xlib_surface_get_height(cs) ~= conky_window.height then
        if cs then cairo_surface_destroy(cs) end
        cs = cairo_xlib_surface_create(conky_window.display, conky_window.drawable, conky_window.visual, conky_window.width, conky_window.height)
    end
    if cr then cairo_destroy(cr) end
    cr = cairo_create(cs)
    local updates = tonumber(conky_parse('${updates}'))
    --####################################################################################################
    if updates>5 then -- starts the display
    --####################################################################################################
        --Number text color
        local nred, ngreen, nblue, nalpha = 1, 1, 1, 1
        --Number text font
        local nfont = "GpuTeks"
        --Number text size
        local nfontsize = 20
        --Number font bold? Set to 1 for bold font
        local nfbold = 1
        --Number Label
        local numlabel="100"
        --###################################################
        --Unit text color
        local ured, ugreen, ublue, ualpha = 1, 1, 1, 1
        --Unit font
        local ufont = "GpuTeks"
        --Unit size
        local ufontsize = 20
        --Unit font bold? Set to 1 for bold font
        local ufbold = 1
        --Unit Label
        local unitlabel="GB"
        --###################################################
        --Text text color
        local txred, txgreen, txblue, txalpha = 1, 1, 1, 1
        --Text font
        local txfont = "GpuTeks"
        --Text size
        local txfontsize = 20
        --Text font bold? Set to 1 for bold font
        local txfbold = 1
        --Text Label
        local txtlabel="Total Allowed"
        --###################################################
        --indicator box enter 1 for box or 0 for no box
        local box = 1
        --use the following 2 numbers to adjust box position
        local xadjust = 230
        local yadjust = 190
        --set the box dimensions
        local width = 90
        local height = 90
        --box thickness
        local bthick = 2
        --box color
        local bxred, bxgreen, bxblue, bxalpha = 0, 0.5, 0, 1
        --###################################################
        --Today's Date Box ###############################
        --###################################################
        --position
        local down = yadjust + bthick
        --###################################################
        --gap from sides
        local gaph = 10
        --###################################################
        --gap from top of box
        local gaptb = 25
        --###################################################
        --gap between labels adjustment
        local gapla = 10
        --###################################################
        --variables (need to set font face, font size and text string in order to populate text extents structure with text measurements)
        -- Number
        local ext_numlabel = cairo_text_extents_t:create()
        conky_set_the_font(cr, nfont, nfontsize, nfbold)
        cairo_text_extents(cr, numlabel, ext_numlabel)
        -- Unit
        local ext_unitlabel = cairo_text_extents_t:create()
        conky_set_the_font(cr, ufont, ufontsize, ufbold)
        cairo_text_extents(cr, unitlabel, ext_unitlabel)
        -- Text
        local ext_txtlabel = cairo_text_extents_t:create()
        conky_set_the_font(cr, txfont, txfontsize, txfbold)
        cairo_text_extents(cr, txtlabel, ext_txtlabel)
        -- text across box center axis
        local tacross = xadjust + (width / 2)
        -- gap between lines (with 4 lines it will be 3 gaps)
        local gapl = (height - (bthick * 2) - (gaph * 2) - ext_numlabel.height - ext_unitlabel.height - ext_txtlabel.height - gapla) / 3
        --###################################################
        --print box 
        --###################################################
        --Number
        down = down + gapl + gaptb + ext_numlabel.height
        conky_set_the_font(cr, nfont, nfontsize, nfbold)
        conky_draw_label(cr, ext_numlabel, numlabel, nred, ngreen ,nblue, nalpha, tacross, down)
        --Unit
        down = down + gapl + ext_unitlabel.height
        conky_set_the_font(cr, ufont, ufontsize, ufbold)
        conky_draw_label(cr, ext_unitlabel, unitlabel, ured, ugreen , ublue, ualpha, tacross, down)
        --Text
        down = down + gapl + ext_txtlabel.height
        conky_set_the_font(cr, txfont, txfontsize, txfbold)
        conky_draw_label(cr, ext_txtlabel, txtlabel, txred, txgreen ,txblue, txalpha, tacross, down)
        --box around
        if box == 1 then
            cairo_set_source_rgba(cr, bxred, bxgreen, bxblue, bxalpha)
            cairo_rectangle(cr, xadjust, yadjust, width, height)
            cairo_set_line_width(cr, bthick)
            cairo_stroke(cr)
        end
        cairo_stroke(cr)
        --#################################################################################
    end--end if 5
end--end box function
cairo_destroy(cr)
cairo_surface_destroy(cs)
cr=nil
end
```

In Arch Linux I built the 1.9.0 version from the AUR so it'll never be automagically updated.

### Post by paramvir on 2015-07-28
Greetings Lonely Planet People huahahaha

I feel I have just come out of the wormhole :lolflag:

You know - you will see conky 1.10 is the way forward and it is faster. Past few days have been well spent taming the Dragon !!!

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/conky110cairo.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/conky110cairo.php")

There are a few quirks with the new version. To name one - if you have anything starting with ${if_what ever there must be a space before the closing bracket } and it works.

In the config section '--' works to comment text and in the text section '#' works for comments.

In my build of conky 1.10 - I have copied the convert.lua to /usr/bin/conky-convert.lua as an executable - run it anywhere and convert your config. Also it really helps to give a '.lua' extension to the new config file - the editors behave better ;) Reduced tons of dependencies and 3 patches - done away with conky docs altogether. 

All files in conkywx remain unchanged - except the conky config files need to be converted - and conky-convert.lua works fine. 

I called it conky-lua - perhaps conky-cairo would be better as lua is integrated anyways :D

As you can see the screenie :guitar:

cheers

There are a few quirks with the new version. To name one - if you have anything starting with ${if_what ever there must be a space before the closing bracket } and it works.

In the config section '--' works to comment text and in the text section '#' works for comments.

In my build of conky 1.10 - I have copied the convert.lua to /usr/bin/conky-convert.lua as an executable - run it anywhere and convert your config. Also it really helps to give a '.lua' extension to the new config file - the editors behave better ;) Reduced tons of dependencies and 3 patches - done away with conky docs altogether. 

All files in conkywx remain unchanged - except the conky config files need to be converted - and conky-convert.lua works fine. 

I called it conky-lua - perhaps conky-cairo would be better as lua is integrated anyways :D

### Post by paramvir on 2015-07-28
> **Cavsfan said:**
> Sweet but I don't like change! 

Sounds good to me but did you not have any problems with **own_window** functions. I heard that was buggy or something.

My dear - change is inevitable :lolflag: - it will happen and take you along with it like a tsunami huahahahaha

**own_window** ??? (type?) - No - I use override anyway lol - only on 14.04 I had to change them all to override and everything was sweet as honey ;)

Set ```
own_window = true,
```

**max_specials** - no longer in the source - it was introduced in Nov 2006 as a workaround for a bug - perhaps that has been addressed and is now no longer there - just remove it.

**pre_exec** - no longer in source - it was introduced in May 2004 - executes a shell command one time - but then so does **exec** - but **execp**, **execpi** and **execi** will execute each interval conky loops. I would think - that is why pre_exec is no longer there - streamlines stuff...

I can see that a whole lot of issues will prop up due to syntax errors. In the config section there is a comma at the end of each line - and many many more small nuances. That is why it is **very important** to use the conversion utility. They do use the standard disclaimer - but it works. :p

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/conky110-vindsl.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/conky110-vindsl.php")

cheers :guitar:

I'm RUSTY at all this, that's how long it has been since I was really into Conky!  :P  Hence why it's so weird for me to be asking for help.  I'M usually the one helping others.  This time?  Not so much.  :P

The stuff you're doing, paramvir, is fantastic.  I really think it looks great.  THAT said... as you can see... I kinda have my own Conky design that suits my style and tastes.  (Like the giant logo I have for a desktop, for example.)

Woooooow - you like Queen and use xfce - huaaaaaaa we are bros of the mind huahahahahahaha lol :guitar:

I have stopped using sensors - get the data from where it is stored - since with sensors you also need other stuff to get to the required data. 

if using lm-sensors use: ```
[SIZE=3]${execi 60 sensors | sed -n '/**Core 0**/{s/.*:[ ]*+\([0-9]*\).*/\1/p}'}°C[/SIZE]
``` for more cpu cores add this line again and change '**Core 0**' to '**Core 1**' and so on...

Yes we all loose information not relevant after some time - then one needs to dig in - Sherlock Holmes stuff ;)

I have installed a very light system with 12.04 - it works on poulsbo gma 500 thingy  - I do a base command line install and then add what is required - you will be surprised how much junk is left out. On older hardware that is sooo important - all those precious cpu cycles eaten by stuff you never need.

cheers ):P

### Post by 42dorian2 on 2015-07-28
Oh, I'm already doing something like that, paramvir.  The problem is... I forget how to get the PROGRAM sensors running.  :P  I haven't changed anything with my system, or with Conky, aside from the odd placement of one of them, in something like 4 years.  Suddenly, Saturday I had a problem with my Xubuntu 11.04 install, tried to run 12.04, and spent the next two days finding ways to get 12.04 running.  AFTER 14.04 dying on me twice.  It has been a long time, in technological terms, since I touched any of this.  I'm a total fog at this point.  Like a newbie, all over again!

Here's the conky script in question.  It literally JUST gets CPU Fan Speed and Temperature.  (I had an overheating issue once.  So I made myself a Conky to trigger when I thought it was happening again.)

.conkyrc.CPU
```

#-----Conky Settings
background yes
no_buffers yes
out_to_console no
top_cpu_separate no
cpu_avg_samples 2
net_avg_samples 3
total_run_times 0
update_interval 1

#-----Text Settings
uppercase no
override_utf8_locale yes
short_units no
pad_percents 0
text_buffer_size 1024
max_user_text 65536
use_xft yes
xftalpha 0.5
xftfont GpuTeks:bold:size=10

#-----Window Settings
own_window yes
own_window_transparent yes
own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
own_window_type normal

#-----Graphics Settings
double_buffer yes
draw_borders no
draw_graph_borders yes
draw_shades no
draw_outline no
max_specials 4096
imlib_cache_size 0

#-----Layout Settings
alignment top_middle
gap_x -430
gap_y 80
maximum_width 600
minimum_size 125 100
use_spacer right

#-----Colors
default_color 808080          #Grey Default
default_outline_color ffffff  #White Outlines
default_shade_color c0c0c0    #Silver Shade
color0 ff0000                                    #Red
color1 ffff00                                    #Yellow
color2 ffffff                                    #White
color3 808080                                    #Grey
color4 008000                                    #Green
color5 c0c0c0                                    #Silver
color6 ffd700                                    #Gold
color7 000080                                    #Navy
color8 0000FF                                    #Blue
color9 404040                                    #DarkGrey

#-----Required Fonts(For Use In This Conky. Google Search Or DaFont.com to Find Them)
#-----Diamond Fantasy
#-----TransponderAOE

TEXT
${font}${color2}CPU TEMP: ${color6}${hr 1}${color2}
${offset -10}${color0}${font ConkyWeather:size=40}E${font TransponderAOE:bold:size=20}${voffset -5}${goto 40}${color}${voffset -10}${execi 5 sensors |  awk '/temp1:/{print $2}'}
${font TransponderAOE:bold:size=20}${voffset 10}${goto 40}${color}${voffset -10}${execi 5 sensors |  awk '/fan1:/{print $2}'} RPM
${image /home/dorian/Conky/Icons/FanSymbol.png -p 5,65 -s 25x25}
${voffset -50}${color6}${hr 1}${color2}


```

I'm also getting this weird ghosting issue.  Every refresh is being written on top of the previous image, so new values are just blurring together with the previous value, and the whole thing just keeps getting bolder, and bolder, blurrier and blurrier.

### Post by paramvir on 2015-07-28
> **42dorian2 said:**
> Oh, I'm already doing something like that, paramvir.  The problem is... I forget how to get the PROGRAM sensors running.  :P  I haven't changed anything with my system, or with Conky, aside from the odd placement of one of them, in something like 4 years.  Suddenly, Saturday I had a problem with my Xubuntu 11.04 install, tried to run 12.04, and spent the next two days finding ways to get 12.04 running.  AFTER 14.04 dying on me twice.  It has been a long time, in technological terms, since I touched any of this.  I'm a total fog at this point.  Like a newbie, all over again!

Here's the conky script in question.  It literally JUST gets CPU Fan Speed and Temperature.  (I had an overheating issue once.  So I made myself a Conky to trigger when I thought it was happening again.)


I'm also getting this weird ghosting issue.  Every refresh is being written on top of the previous image, so new values are just blurring together with the previous value, and the whole thing just keeps getting bolder, and bolder, blurrier and blurrier.

OK for ghosting in xfce4 - you will need to delete a cache - had this issue many years ago... here is the link... conky session is being started 2 times - you need to remove the xfce cache and have just a single session started.
[http://wiki.xfce.org/faq#some_of_my_applications_are_always_started_when_i_login](http://wiki.xfce.org/faq#some_of_my_applications_are_always_started_when_i_login) 

So you are getting the desired info from sensors ???

cheers

### Post by 42dorian2 on 2015-07-28
Nevermind... Discovered the solution to two of three problems I'm having... Got Sensors running, AND I ended the blurring issue with my Conky.  Now I need to figure out Weather again.  :P

[ATTACH=CONFIG]263451[/ATTACH]

Turns out I DO need to add the compositor controls to Conky for the first time since I have used Conky at all.  :P  (own_window_argb_visual and value)

*EDIT*  Full Data Dump on this setup located here: [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=281865&page=2313&p=13329209#post13329209](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=281865&page=2313&p=13329209#post13329209)

### Post by 42dorian2 on 2015-09-18
Okay, forgive my bluntness here... but chances are you just won't be able to integrate that.  Conky as a whole can grab information in CLI commands, but if you can't find that data with any source of CLI command, then chances are you just have to move on.  It sucks, but... This is Conky for you.

You may want to go ask in an NVidia thread somewhere, see if they know more programs they use for diagnostics.  If you can find something they run in CLI that you never tried before, maybe you can come back here or the big Conky Thread with that info and we can help.  Otherwise, there's not a lot we can do to help you.  Ask somewhere that is talking about NVidia drivers and hardware directly.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-09-19
jdallara, you could look at **man nvidia-settings** and **man nvidia-smi**. If you say it displays both GPUs there, then there should be a way to pull info from the 2nd GPU.

I only have 1 old Geforce 9800 GT and display just the driver version and the GPU temp myself.

```
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color0}${offset 5}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${alignr}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
```

In this if template9 = C it displays Celsius; if it's an F it displays Fahrenheit: 
```
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color0}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#
```

You'll see erroneous error messages when querying anything from nvidia-settings:
```
cavsfan@cavsfan-le-beast:~$ nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp 

[COLOR="#FF0000"]ERROR: Error querying target relations[/COLOR]


[COLOR="#FF0000"]ERROR: Error querying target relations[/COLOR]


  Attribute 'GPUCoreTemp' (cavsfan-le-beast:0.0): 44.
    'GPUCoreTemp' is an integer attribute.
    'GPUCoreTemp' is a read-only attribute.
    'GPUCoreTemp' can use the following target types: X Screen, GPU.
 
```

There is nothing that can be done to get prevent that error from displaying and it's been around for years I believe.

[[img]http://www.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2015-09-1810-25-44.png[/img]](http://www.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2015-09-1810-25-44.php)

### Post by VinDSL on 2015-10-24
That's a strange one !  

I've been running Wily for 6 months, and didn't have any probs like that.  And, I just switched to Xenial - still no probs.

I tried Conky 10.x again, and it was better than it used to be, but the removal of 'pre_exec' frosts my butt.  So, I switched back Conky-all 1.9.1.

Only thing I had to change with Conky 1.9.1 was the logo header - the name wouldn't fit any more...  :D

Conky 1.9.1 ver is actually called Conky 1.9.1_preNONE, and that's the output from '${conky_version}' 


I used 'cut' to truncate it.  Here's the Conky code:

```
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
**[COLOR="#0000CD"]${voffset 0}${goto 185}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}${pre_exec conky --version | sed -n 1p | cut -b1-11}${font}[/COLOR]**
# ${voffset 0}${goto 182}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
```

Here's how it looks in CLI:

```
vindsl@Zuul:~$ conky --version | sed -n 1p | cut -b1-11
Conky 1.9.1

```


That's really all I tweaked...

[INDENT][[img]http://vindsl.com/images/2015-10-23-23:36:37-1280x1024-scrot(650x520).png[/img]](http://vindsl.com/images/2015-10-23-23:36:37-1280x1024-scrot.png)[/INDENT]

### Post by paramvir on 2015-10-24
> **VinDSL said:**
> That's a strange one !  

I've been running Wily for 6 months, and didn't have any probs like that.  And, I just switched to Xenial - still no probs.

I tried Conky 10.x again, and it was better than it used to be, but the removal of 'pre_exec' frosts my butt.  So, I switched back Conky-all 1.9.1.



I did see that message from our amigo @PJRonin but then life got a grip on me - grrrrrrrrr [ Ronin - did anyone see that Keanu Reeves movie 47 Ronin huahahahahahaha - Engrish movie with Nippon Engrish :p lol - but it was good ]

But - then you replied and kewl since both version of your template use the same bargraph - conkywx flavour - colours would have the same issue.

OK I have implemented many short falls of conky 1.10 in lua - it is the better conky by far - stay with it bro - since now officially lua is the scripting language for conky.

Here is the list::
**conky_startup** - this handles the startup issues - if your update interval is 5 seconds - conky updates that conky config in 5 * 2 seconds [10 seconds ] - so we update it such that your conky starts like on steroids 

**conky_pre_exec** - this handles anything that needs to be done only ONCE - or unless updated manually - same as 1.9x

**conky_if_running** - checks the pidof the required process - same as 1.9x

**conky_top_name** - handles the top process name as the workaround patch does not work yet ;-)

and one or two others such as sysinfo but that is different.

These have been already tested by a gang of b... thirsty hooligans - who have no other vocation but to find faults in my work :D - I can say this - these guys are **awesome** !!! 

**Thank you** Tommy, Cavsfan, Florian [ conkywx official German Translator - zsh guru - mpv extremist huahahahaha ], David from warm and Floridian coasts, Hazza from the land down under and not to miss Frankest Frank from Germany but down under for quite some time with an awesome view to the port - Frank and I share the same background in our previous lives at Sea [many thanks to Ubuntu Forums for the meet up] - thank you all.... They assist in the play to go on

After working with conky 1.10 - it has been a stretch to support conky 1.9x

Some times you cannot see a situation unless you face it and yes conky 1.9x is the comfort zone - just take a step out of it and take my hand - perhaps you might just enjoy the thrill of it... ;-)

cheers

Just... an idea here... Have you gone into the code itself and changed the VALUES of those bars? Move a percentage from 50 to 51, or anything like that?  Maybe it's a calculation fluke, is what I'm thinking here.  Your (Insert Value Here) is reaching a warning level just before it gets read by the system, but by the time the NUMBER displays, it's lower than the threshold again, and the colour hasn't shifted back with it in time for the cycle?

(I know, it's crazy talk. It's just a hunch.  May be nothing at all.)

We think alike.   I've tried setting the alert colour to a different colour (works but still wrong), setting the alert value (50) to a range of values from low teens to high nighties and even threw in a couple of negative numbers.   I've also loaded a stress program and put the CPU cores under extreme and constant load and the bar still shows the lowest plot points coloured in the alert colour with the alert colour again appearing at the extreme right hand side (as it should).   Hell, I even thought the code might be chucking a hissie fit over the spelling of "colour" and changed that.   All, unfortunately, to no avail.   I don't think this is a code problem as the Nouveau driver works perfectly whereas the Nvidia driver pukes.

Where's that image of Linus giving the finger to the Nvidia execs?

### Post by VinDSL on 2015-10-25
> **PJs Ronin said:**
> I don't think this is a code problem as the Nouveau driver works perfectly whereas the Nvidia driver pukes.

I was just thinking... look at my post above.  In particular, the OpenGL renderer string:

[INDENT]OpenGL vendor string: nouveau
**[COLOR="#FF0000"]OpenGL renderer string: Gallium 0.4 on NV4B[/COLOR]**
OpenGL version string: 2.1 Mesa 11.0.2[/INDENT]

If yours doesn't say that, you're probably running in llvmpipe mode, which is gawd-awful slow on this machine - smeared screens with artifacts, and all that stuff.

If that's the case, you need to get your machine into NV4B mode, which gets you off the 'pipe' and allows hardware acceleration, et cetera.

Truthfully, the modern Nouveau drivers run circles around the nVidia drivers, on this machine.  The GPU runs about 10C hotter on Nouveau drivers than nVidia drivers, but the increased temps haven't caused any probs yet.

Anyway, ignore this, if you're already off the pipe...  ;)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-10-27
Yeah summer is perpetual here. It will likely cool off a tad more but probably not a whole lot.
We have manatees that come into our canal to keep warm when the water temps get below 70 or so.
They look like they have a lot of blubber like whales but they only have a 1/2 inch of fat insulating them so they suffer if it gets too cold.

I've got Crotons in front of my house that are 5 feet 6 inches high and they don't even grow in the middle of the state as they cannot take frost.
Beachside, we have the ocean a mile away and the intercoastal waterway about 2 miles wide between us and the main land on the other side.

So, it stays warm here. Sometimes we non-snow birds just get sick of it though. But, not when I'm seeing New England getting like 200 inches of snow though. :p

Nice screen there!

Paramvir's getting ready to release version 5 of VinDSL's conky with Paramvir's weather plus some magic of his own. :popcorn:

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-11-21
It's for either version actually. but he provides a conversion script that converts the conkyrc files from 1.9 format to 1.10 format.
The conkyrc files used to be named conkyrc.sh and they still are for version 1.9 but they are named conkyrc.lua for version 1.10.

I tried to manually convert one file myself and didn't get it right so I suggest using his conversion script.

But he is supporting both versions. Once every one has converted over to 1.10 life will be a lot easier for Param IMO!

### Post by 42dorian2 on 2015-11-22
> **PJs Ronin said:**
> Stupid question #132.   Does one need to install Lua5.x to get the Lua functionality working?   I seem to be have some 'issues'.

I don't like this question.  Why?  Because it's NOT stupid.  It's completely valid.  I don't like it at all when the user insults themselves over something like this.

Depending on your distribution, flavour, or whatever other environment variables, what is, or is not, included in your installation can be hugely different.

"Do I Need LUA for LUA Functionality?"  Yes.  Obviously you do.  The problem is, do you have the right VERSION installed, and should you try re-installing what you have?

For THAT... Well... paramvir says LUA 5.1... Check YOUR Distribution channels for the version you've got, and do what's necessary to get it.  Your results may vary, but you are NOT stupid.  I don't like it when users call themselves stupid.

### Post by paramvir on 2015-11-23
> **PJs Ronin said:**
> Ty paramvir.   I'm currently running 14.04 and 15.04 in separate partitions (where I have no issues with this conky setup) and noticed that the only 'lua' installed was liblua5.1-0 and liblua5.2-0.   I was reasonably confident that these would provide the lua functionality but I'm no expert hence my question.   I rsync'd the 15.04 install into a new partition, installed lua5.1 (conky still works fine at this stage) and the conky broke again when I dist-upgraded to Wily. It would appear that installing lua5.1 made no discernible difference.   My quandry remains viz:

OK - from your earlier post 1.10 disaster - you will need to use the conky utility in the conky docs folder **conky-convert.lua **to convert your rc file to the new lua format and then tweak that. conky attempts to convert the 1.9 verion on it's own - but pre conversion works better.

From your messages I cannot tell where the issue is - or for that matter what it is - so can you send me the debug file by email. in a terminal type conkywx and use option 4 - and send me the compressed file.

If you look at the conkywx examples folder - there are changes to the rc files - lua 1.10x files end with *rc.lua the others have *rc.sh - the rest are the same as before. You can see the differences in the files or any changes from earlier rc files.

cheers

Edit:: That conky-convert.lua is actually just **convert.lua**
