### Post by paramvir on 2015-11-24
> **PJs Ronin said:**
> I've done some more testing of the issue I have run in to (that of bargraph alarm colours being displayed as the 'normal' baseline colours... check post #2352 if so inclined) and have narrowed it down to a couple of possibilities.   The first is that there is a bug somewhere in the nvidia drivers or the Ubu kernel.   The second is that my bargraph_small.lua file has become corrupted over time.   I'm thinking the former, but to eliminate the later can someone please post a complete bargraph_small.lua script for me to test.   One along the lines of the original VinDSL conky script would be lovely.

Tyvm.

Try the VinDSL template included with conkywx - take a look in that for the bar graphs and the background usage - conkywx.lua has the bargraph code but is implemented to conform to the usage style in conkywx.lua with command line options.

I know Cavsfan is using this template from conkywx and has no issues with colours.

SO in this way you use your template with a little modification and just call a single lua file.

You can also see the **conkywx lua help** by typing **conkywx --help** in a terminal window - this will open help in the Browser :)

Hope that sorts things with the magic template ;)

cheers

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-11-25
PJs Ronin and possibly RunRickus,

Here is what I have on Arch. I don't display my swap as it is unused and I have a 1TB external USB drive that I display. This is formatted for a 1920x1080 screen so It may need some adjustments.
It seems there is slight differernces between Regular Ubuntu, Mate, and Xubuntu, probably others. I have spent days tweaking the font sizes and placement of each section of VinDSL's conky.
But here it it using conky version 1.10.1 along with the template.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2015-11-2513-56-23.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2015-11-2513-56-23.php")

Here's the **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua** file: (Paramvir recommended we keep everthing in **/usr/share/conkywx/examples** and run it from there or you will have problems.
He gave us a script to copy them from that location to **/home/cavsfan/bin/** so we don't lose our customizations before an upgrade of Conkywx. If you do not copy them you will lose everything.
I use a simple text document to copy them back and forth and manipulate the file permissions. I'll post that too FWIW. 

```
conky.config = {
--##############################################
--# VinDSL | rev. 11-14-15 05:10 | ver. 5.0.0 ##
--##############################################
--#              November 2015 Series         ##
--##############################################
--# ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

--# ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

--###
--# Stable Platforms (current)

--  Arch Linux  (conky 1.10.1)

--# Development Platforms (current)
--  Xubuntu Xenial Xerus 16.04  (Conky 1.10.0)

--  Screen Resolution: 1920x1080 (ASUS VN289QL)

--# Tips n' Tricks: conky 1.8.1 will no longer be supported. RIP :)

--###
--# Prerequisites (required)

--  conky-all 1.10.0
--  Conkywx 5.0.0 (Paramvir Likhari)
--  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor

--###
--# Installed fonts (required)

--  Arrows (JLH Fonts)
--  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
--  ConkySymbols (Cavsfan - not included)
--  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
--  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
--  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
--  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
--  OpenLogos (Icoma)
--  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
--  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
--  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
--  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
--  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva)
--  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
--  Neuropol (Cavsfan Mint systems only - not included)


--# Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
--# Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

--###
--# Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)

    use_xft = true,
    font = 'DroidSans:size=8.75',
    xftalpha = 0.1,

--###
--# Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
--# Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.

    override_utf8_locale = true,

--###
--# Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.

    background = true,

--###
--# Update interval in seconds.

    update_interval = 3.0,
    
--###
--# The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
--# Zero makes Conky run forever.

    total_run_times = 0,

--###
--# Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.

--# used in Xcfe
    background = true,
    own_window = true,
    own_window_transparent = true,
    own_window_type = 'override',
--own_window_type normal
--own_window_type desktop
--### use the next line if not using override
--own_window_hints undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager
--###
--# Some distros require the following lines for true transparency.
--# BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.

-- own_window_argb_visual yes
-- own_window_argb_value 255

--###
--# Force images to redraw when they change.

    imlib_cache_size = 1,

--###
--# Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.

    double_buffer = true,

--###
--# Draw shades?

    draw_shades = true,
    default_shade_color = '#330',

--###
--# Draw outlines?

    draw_outline = false,

--###
--# Draw borders around text?

    draw_borders = false,

--###
--# Draw borders around graphs?

    draw_graph_borders = false,

--###
--# Print text to stdout?
--# Print text in console?

    out_to_ncurses = false,
    out_to_console = false,

--###
--# Text alignment.

    alignment = 'top_right',

--###
--# Minimum size of the text area.
--# Syntax: minimum_size [width] [height]

    minimum_width = 245, minimum_height = 1080,

--###
--# This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
--# Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
--# Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
--# Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
--# Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."

    text_buffer_size = 13936,

--###
--# Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
--# Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
--# Not needed or included in conky version 1.10.0

--max_specials = '1006',

--###added by me
--# Maximum size of user text buffer, i.e. layout below TEXT line in config file (default is 16384 bytes)
-- max_user_text 81920
--max_user_text 168224
    max_user_text = 200000,

--###
--# Maximum width of the text area.
--# Syntax: maximum_width [width]

    maximum_width = 245,

--###
--# Gap between text and screen borders.

    gap_x = 6,-- Left/Right
    gap_y = 33,-- Top/Bottom

--###
--# Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.

    short_units = true,

--###
--# Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.

    pad_percents = 0,

--###
--# Pad spacing between text and borders.

    border_inner_margin = 4,

--###
--# Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".

    top_name_width = 10,

--###
--# Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
--# Set to yes, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.

    no_buffers = true,

--###
--# Set to yes, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.

    uppercase = false,

--###
--# Number of cpu samples to average.
--# Set to 1 to disable averaging.

    cpu_avg_samples = 2,

--###
--# Number of net samples to average.
--# Set to 1 to disable averaging.

    net_avg_samples = 2,

--###
--# Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
--# Only affects certain objects.

    use_spacer = 'right',

--###
--# My colors (suit yourself)

    color0 = 'White',--FFFFFF
    color1 = 'Ivory',--FFFFF0
    color2 = 'Ivory2',--EEEEE0
    color3 = 'Yellow',--CDCDC1
--color4 'DeepSkyBlue',--#3BB9FF
--color4 'DodgerBlue',--#1589FF
--color4 'DodgerBlue2',--#157DEC
--color4 = 'Tan1',--FFA54F
--color4 = 'Gold',
    color4 = 'Yellow',--CDCDC1
--color4 'green',--#00FF00
--color4 'Turquoise1'.--#52F3FF
--color4 'Magenta',--#FF00FF
--color4 'cyan',--#00FFFF
--color4 'red',--#FF0000
    color5 = 'cyan',--EE9A49
    color6 = 'Gray',--7E7E7E
    color7 = 'AntiqueWhite4',--8B8378
--color8 = 'DimGray',--696969
--color8 = '#000000000',--#00FFFFFFF
    color8 = 'DarkSlateGray',
    color9 = 'DeepSkyBlue',--3BB9FF
--color9 = 'Tomato',--#FF6347
--colorX = 'OrangeRed',--#FF4500

--####
--# Load Lua for shading (optional)
--# Set the path to your script here.

    lua_load = '/usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua',
    --lua_load = "$HOME/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/conkywx_1.0.1_source/lib/conkywx.lua",
    lua_startup_hook = 'startup',

--###
--# Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
--# Set the path to your script here.

-- lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua
-- lua_load /home/param/bin/projects/conkywx/conkywx_source/1.0.1/sandbox/VinDSL_templates_fonts/bargraph_small.lua
-- lua_load ~/.conky/bargraph_testing.lua
-- lua_draw_hook_post main_bars

-- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Export path statements for use in *conky* config file
-- if you move this file else where then give the full path for WX_DIR variable
-- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
--do not uncomment
--WX_DIR="/usr/share/conkywx" --export WX_DIR

--###
--# Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
--# Set the path to your .confs and templates here.

    template0 = '/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh',
    template1 = '/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf',
--template1 = '"/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/big-luamix.conf"',
--###
--# Choose Graphic or Dingbat template
    template4 = '/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh',
--###
--template4 = '/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh',
--###
    template7 = '/tmp/wx-cavsfan-vindsl-conkywx.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert',
--template7 = '/tmp/wx-cavsfan-big-luamix.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert',

--# To switch between Imperial and Metric change template9 to F for Imperial or C for Metric values - template4 file does not require any change - it changes based on Units_to_Use in the vindsl-conkywx.conf file
    template9 = 'F',

};

conky.text = [[
#${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 45 -c 0x000000:0.2 -p NA,NA}
${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 25 -c 0x444444:0.4 -s 640,1025 -p 1,5}
##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${voffset -33}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}1${offset 1}2${offset 1}.${offset 0}0${offset 0}4${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=113}${color2}v${font}${voffset -70}${goto 169}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=13}${color4}${exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'} Mate${font}
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=93}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=12.6}${color4}${if_match "${exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '9-11'}" == "LTS"} ${exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/' | awk -F'(' '{print $2}' |  cut -c '1-7'} ${else} ${exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font} ${endif}${font}
####
## Uncomment for Mint systems)
#${voffset 32}${color9}${font conkysymbols:size=32}${voffset -35}u${font}${goto 59}${voffset -8}${font Neuropol:size=10.6}${exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'DESCRIPTION' | awk -F'"' '{print $2}'}${font}
####
##  Uncomment For Arch Linux
${voffset -4}${font conkysymbols:size=52}${color9}t${font}${voffset -40}${goto 70}${font Neuropol:size=24}${color9}${exec cat /etc/*release | grep 'PRETTY_NAME'| sed 's/.*="\(.*\)"/\1/'}${font}
####
## Uncomment For Conky Version
${voffset 8}${alignc}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=9.55}${color4}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
####
## Uncomment to display YOUR custom graphic image (upper-right corner)
#${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -52}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/ABC.jpg -p 180,0 -s 52x52 -n}

##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################
${voffset -5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}SYSTEM${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
# ${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}${exec lsb_release -sd | sed -e 's/development/dev/g'}${alignr}${exec lsb_release -sr}${font}
${voffset 3}${offset -2}${font OpenLogos:size=11}${color2}Z${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=9.5}${color2}${offset 3}${sysname}${offset 6}${kernel}${alignr }${font DroidSans:size=9.3}${machine}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nVidia ID (nvidia-smi version - dynamic)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=9.5}${color2}${offset 5}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${font}${font DroidSans:size=9.3}${alignr}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for 'nvidia-current' driver ID (DPKG version - hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=12}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${exec dpkg --status nvidia-current | grep Version | cut -f 1 -d '-' | sed 's/[^.,0-9]//g'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${exec glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-19'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (any card / any driver - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color2}${offset 5}Video Card Product Name${alignr}Driver Version${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU frequency (hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color2}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}2${offset 3}Quad Q9550${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=9.5}${color2}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Multi${offset 3}Core${alignr -8}${font DroidSans:size=9.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
####
## Uncomment for System Uptime (hybrid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=12}${color2}q${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=9.5}${color2}${offset 5}System${offset 3}Uptime${alignr -7}${font DroidSans:size=9.3}${uptime_short}${font}
## uncoment for grub version
#${voffset 2}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:size=8.6}${color2}${offset 5}Grub${offset 3}Version${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${exec grub-install -V | grep grub-install | cut -c21-42}${font}
##################################
##      PROCESSORS+TEMPS        ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}PROCESSORS+TEMPS${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 3}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 3}${font DroidSans:size=10}${cpu cpu1}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}CPU2${offset 3}${font DroidSans:size=10}${cpu cpu2}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 3}${font DroidSans:size=10}${cpu cpu3}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}CPU4${offset 3}${font DroidSans:size=10}${cpu cpu4}%$alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}k${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}GPU Temperature: $alignr${if_match "${template9}" == "C"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 75,147 -w 140 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 75,159 -w 140 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 75,172 -w 140 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 75,185 -w 140 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4}
##################################
##          FAN SPEEDS          ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}FAN SPEEDS${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=10}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}CPU FAN${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan1' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=10}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}FAN2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan2' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=10}${color2}L${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 2}FAN3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'fan3' | awk '{ print $2 " " $3 }'}${font}
##################################
##            MEMORY            ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}MEMORY${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}l${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 3}RAM${goto 97}${font DroidSans:size=9.4}${mem}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${memmax}${alignr}${memperc}%${font}#
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,279 -w 235 -s 1 -f memperc -a NA -l 1 -x 70}
##################################
##             HDD              ##
##################################
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}HDD${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 4}ROOT${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=9.4}${fs_used /}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /}% / ${fs_used_perc /}%${font}
${voffset 9}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}${color2}${offset 4}FANTOM USB${goto 98}${font DroidSans:size=9.4}${fs_used /media/Fantom/}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /media/Fantom/}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /media/Fantom/}%${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,309 -w 235 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a /home -l 1 -x 70}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20,328 -w 235 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a /media/Fantom/ -l 1 -x 70}
##################################
##      TOP CPU PROCESSES       ##
##################################
${voffset -16}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}TOP CPU PROCESSES${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}Name${goto 100}CPU${goto 163}MEM${alignr}USER${font}
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 1}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 1}${goto 137}${top pid 1}${goto 177}${top user 1}${alignr}${top cpu 1}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 2}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 2}${goto 137}${top pid 2}${goto 177}${top user 2}${alignr}${top cpu 2}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 3}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 3}${goto 137}${top pid 3}${goto 177}${top user 3}${alignr}${top cpu 3}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 4}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 4}${goto 137}${top pid 4}${goto 177}${top user 4}${alignr}${top cpu 4}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 5}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 5}${goto 137}${top pid 5}${goto 177}${top user 5}${alignr}${top cpu 5}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 6}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 6}${goto 137}${top pid 6}${goto 177}${top user 6}${alignr}${top cpu 6}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 7}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 7}${goto 137}${top pid 7}${goto 177}${top user 7}${alignr}${top cpu 7}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 8}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 8}${goto 137}${top pid 8}${goto 177}${top user 8}${alignr}${top cpu 8}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 9}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 9}${goto 137}${top pid 9}${goto 177}${top user 9}${alignr}${top cpu 9}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 7}${lua top_name ${top name 10}}${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=10}${top mem_res 10}${goto 137}${top pid 10}${goto 177}${top user 10}${alignr}${top cpu 10}%${font}
##################################
##          NETWORK             ##
##################################
${voffset 1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}NETWORK${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 2}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=10}${color2}T${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}Download${goto 123}${font DroidSans:size=10}${totaldown eth0}${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}${downspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=10}${color2}N${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}Upload${goto 123}${font DroidSans:size=10}${totalup eth0}${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}${upspeed eth0}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=10}${color2}a${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}LAN${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}${addr eth0}${font}
#${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=10}${color2}a${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}WAN${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
${voffset 1}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=10}${color2}a${font DroidSans:size=10}${color2}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=10}WAN${font DroidSans:size=10}${alignr}Demo Mode
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}CONKYWX ${if_existing ${template7}} ${color5}Alert:${font}${lua main -n scroller -f 11 -l 21 -s 5 -p 98,535 -o ${template7}}${else} ${voffset -2}${color6}${hr 1}${endif}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
${execpi 1230 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -8}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color2}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 18}${font DroidSansMono:size=8}${color0}${voffset -6}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color4}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color4}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=8}${color0}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color0}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -67}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=68}${color4}${alignc 92}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
${voffset -60}${font DroidSansMono:size=8.5}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" (use mono fonts only)
# ${voffset -68}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color0}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color4}&${color0}/'}
##################################
##         Image                ##
##################################
#${if_running audacious} ${else}#
#${voffset -3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9.5}${color4}ICAN HAZ CHEESEBURGER${offset 8}${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
#${offset -10}${image /home/cavsfan/Pictures/icanhascheeseburger3.jpeg -p 10,934 -s 240x200}${endif}
########################################
##  Audacious - VLC - WXMP (Lua)      ##
########################################
###
${if_match "${lua if_running audacious}" == "1"}${voffset 3}${font :bold:size=9.5}${color4}Audacious Playing ${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}${lua main -n music_sort -m audacious -f 10 -c 0xEEEEE0:0.9 -k 2 -s 5 -p 7,995 -e 0xff700f:1 }${font}${else}#
${if_match "${lua if_running mplayer}" == "1"}${voffset 3}${font :bold:size=9.5}${color4}wx media player ${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}${lua main -n music_sort -m mplayer -f 10 -c 0xEEEEE0:0.9 -k 2 -s 5 -p 7,995 -e 0xff700f:1 -t 1 }${else}#
${if_match "${lua if_running vlc}" == "1"}${voffset 3}${font :bold:size=10}${color4}VLC Playing ${color2}${voffset -2}${hr 2}${font}${lua main -n music_sort -g psl -m vlc -f 10 -c 0xEEEEE0:0.9 -k 2 -s 5 -p 7,995 -e 0xff700f:1 -t 1 }${else}#
${voffset 3}${font :bold:size=9.5}${color4}No Media ${color2}${offset 3}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}${endif}${endif}${endif}
######################################
#### This font statement below is required for conky to reset the font before it draws the shades to align the text shadows correctly in *draw_shades yes*
${font}

]];
```

Here's the **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh** file:
```
############################################################
## VinDSL | Paramvir | rev. 15-01-15 22:12 | ver. GRAPHIC ##
############################################################
## WEATHER TEMPLATE for Conkywx 5.0.0                     ##
############################################################
${voffset -7}${font RadioSpace:size=28}${color2}${alignc -15}[PT]${font}${font Arrows:size=28}${voffset -15}${offset 6}${color2}[FCTTF]${font}
${voffset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9.7}${alignc 6}${color4}Feels${offset 3}like${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[FL]${offset 2}${color4}/${offset 3}${color2}[FCTT]${font}
### todays temperature trend e.g. 90/76 
${voffset -20}${goto 205}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8.75}${color2}[D1T]${font}
### today's rain probability
${voffset 0}${goto 205}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.3}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D1P]${font}
### circle around today's temperature trend and rain probability
${voffset -38}${goto 192}${font KRARound:size=43.5}${color2}I${font}
###  Present Conditions Text
${voffset -8}${font Ubuntu:size=28}${color4}${alignc}[CT]${font}
####
#${alignc 20}${voffset 1}${offset 10}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[SR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=8}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[SS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTSSF]${font}
#${voffset -3}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTHF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTPF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTMRF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTMSF]${font}${voffset 10}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[FM]${font}${voffset 11}${goto 75}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=7.55}${color4}U${offset 1}V${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[UV]${font}
####
${voffset 7}${goto 50}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Sunrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[SR]${font}${voffset 0}${offset 2}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTSRF]${font}${voffset -4}${offset 2}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Sunset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[SS]${font}${voffset 0}${offset 3}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTSSF]${font}#
${voffset 10}${goto 90}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Humidity${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[HM]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTHF]${font}#
${voffset 10}${goto 90}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Barometer${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[BR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTPF]${font}#
${voffset 10}${goto 90}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Moonrise${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[MR]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTMRF]${font}#
${voffset 10}${goto 90}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Moonset${offset 1}:${offset 1}${color2}[MS]${font}${voffset -2}${offset 4}${font Arrows:size=14}${color2}[FCTMSF]${font}#
${voffset 10}${goto 90}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Full${offset 3}Moon${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[FM]${font}#
${voffset 10}${goto 90}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}U${offset 1}V${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[UV]${font}
${voffset 0}${goto 90}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}Pollen${offset 1}:${offset 2}${color2}[PLN]${font}
####
####    Windvane (without -i 19 pointing to a windvane image it draws one with lua)
#${lua main -n showcond -p 5, 635 -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
#${lua main -n showcond -p 1, 635 -s 60 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 13 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
### fancy colored windvane
#${lua main -n showcond -p 3, 635 -s 70 -w WI -d [WDD] -f [BF] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 15 -a 1 -l  0xff0000:0.9 }
${lua main -n showcond -p 3, 635 -s 70 -w wi -f [BF] -d [WDD] -j 2 -t 1 -e [WS] -i 15 -a 13 -c 0x0000ff:1 -l 0xff3300:1 -b 0x32a000:1 -o 0x32c000:1 -r 0x000080:1 }
### This leaves out the image so it draws one with lua
#${lua main -n showcond -p 5, 635 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
#${lua main -n showcond -p 1, 635 -s 65 -w WI -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -i 14 -a 1 -l 0xff0000:0.9 }
### Moon percent Illuminated percentage displayed on the moon image
### Wind speed and moon phase
${voffset -55}${font DroidSans:bold:size=10.5}${color4}${alignc -95}[MV]${font}
${voffset 30}${goto 22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.45}${color2}[WS] ${if_match "[UU]" == "F"}mph${else}km/h${endif}${voffset -1}${goto 192}[MP]${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color4}${goto 10}Temp${goto 60}Baro${goto 110}Dew${goto 160}Hum${goto 210}Wspd
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 5,751 -d [GT] -c 0xEE4000:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 55,751 -d [GP] -c 0xFF83FA:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 105,751 -d [GD] -c 0x00FFFF:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 155,751 -d [GH] -c 0x00FF00:1}#
${lua main -n wxgraph -p 205,751 -d [GW] -c 0x00FF00:1}
${voffset 22}${goto 27}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9}${color4}[D2D]${goto 86}[D3D]${goto 145}[D4D]${goto 201}[D5D]${font}
####    Present Condition  WX
${lua main -n showcond -p 5,540 -s 60 -w ci -t 1 -f [PIF] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [PIC] -j 0 }#
####    Present Condition MOON
${lua main -n showcond -p 191, 635 -s 65 -w mi -f [MIF] -d [MROT] -i [MIC] }#
####    Forecast Conditions
${lua main -n showcond -p 22,778 -s 44 -w ci -t 1 -f [D2F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D2I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 82,778 -s 44 -w ci -t 1 -f [D3F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D3I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 141,778 -s 44 -w ci -t 1 -f [D4F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D4I] }#
${lua main -n showcond -p 198,778 -s 44 -w ci -t 1 -f [D5F] -c 0xFFD700:1 -i [D5I] }
${voffset 30}${goto 27}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=9}${color2}[D2T]${goto 87}[D3T]${goto 147}[D4T]${goto 203}[D5T]${font}
${voffset 0}${goto 24}${font DroidSans:bold:size=9}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D2P]${goto 84}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D3P]${goto 144}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D4P]${goto 201}${color4}CoR${offset 3}${color2}[D5P]${font}#
```

And here is my way of copying the files back and forth: (conky files to copy before installing a new version of conkywx) If you don't do #1 before you do #2 you will regret it. ;)
```

# 1 copying the good files before installing

cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.lua /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.lua
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conky-musicrc.lua /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conky-musicrc.lua
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-clockrc.lua /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conkywx-clockrc.lua
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh
cp /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh
cp /usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/conky-restart.sh

# 2 Install the new Conkywx version [COLOR=#FF0000]***[/COLOR]

# 3 setting the file ownership to cavsfan
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.lua
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conky-musicrc.lua
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-clockrc.lua
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh
sudo chown cavsfan:cavsfan /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh

# 4 changing the file permissions
sudo chmod 764 /usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.lua
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conky-musicrc.lua
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-clockrc.lua
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh
sudo chmod 664 /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh

# 5 copying the files back to /etc/share/conkywx
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/conky-restart.sh /usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.lua /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.lua
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh /usr/share/conkywx/examples/big-luamix-template.sh
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conky-musicrc.lua /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conky-musicrc.lua
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conkywx-clockrc.lua /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-clockrc.lua
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-dingbat-template.sh
cp /home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh /usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-weather-graphic-template.sh
```

---

### Post by PJs Ronin on 2015-11-25
Ty Cavsfan, lots of food for thought there. :)

Anyway, I've completed the upgrade to Conkywx5 and conky-all1.10 and the aforementioned problems I was having are being resolved.   I won't go into a full description of what I found, but essentially it looked like my bargraph_small.lua file had a corruption.   This may have been as simple as a non critical, but bad performing, syntax error or perhaps even a wayward ','.   Nonetheless, when I started from scratch with conkywx5 it brought in a new lua handling mechanism and my bargraphs are now behaving themselves.   Wish I could say the same about my cat.

I'm still pottering along trying to get this set up properly, but I really like the way the lua calls are now close-grouped within the conkrc file.   Makes modding on the fly so much easier.   Great job paramvir... once again you've scored a home run!

---

### Post by foodoo on 2015-12-02
Good morning all,

I'm having some problems with the new version !

Installed latest conky-all 1.10v
Installed latest conkywx 5v

Changed to new syntax in the .conkyrc and saved to the home folder

running conky displays everything out of line and no longer displays the thermometer

I've done the RTFM on the help docs but still a little confused on some points.

Installed lua5.1

Changed the name of .conkyrc to .conkyrc.lua but the default conky is displayed instead of conkywx

where am i going wrong ? what am i missing ?
cheers Sean :-)

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2015-12-02
> **foodoo said:**
> Good morning all,

I'm having some problems with the new version !

Installed latest conky-all 1.10v
Installed latest conkywx 5v

Changed to new syntax in the .conkyrc and saved to the home folder

running conky displays everything out of line and no longer displays the thermometer

I've done the RTFM on the help docs but still a little confused on some points.

Installed lua5.1

Changed the name of .conkyrc to .conkyrc.lua but the default conky is displayed instead of conkywx

where am i going wrong ? what am i missing ?
cheers Sean :-)

Did you copy your modified conkyrc.lua files into **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/** after you installed conkywx 5.0 ? 
Otherwise you'll be overwriting your custom files every time you install a new version. 

And take a look at  **/usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh** and make sure the names are spelled right.

A couple of posts back I put "my version" of how I copy the files from that location and keep them in **/home/cavsfan/bin/conkywx/**
You must copy them out of there and then install and then copy them back and you always want to run them out of **/usr/share/conkywx** and no where else.

Here is a little of my **/usr/share/conkywx/conky-restart.sh**: (The files in red are the ones I use.)

```
case "$conky_version" in
1.9)
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/conky-musicrc.sh" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/conkywx-clockrc.sh" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/conkywx-surface-imagerc.sh" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/big-luamix-conkyrc.sh" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.sh" &

	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/conky-system.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/expanded-conkyrc.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/now-conkyrc.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/scroller-conkyrc.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/short-02-conkyrc.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/short-conkyrc.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/short-luamix-conkyrc.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/slim-conkyrc.sh" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.sh" &
	;;
1.10)
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/[COLOR="#FF0000"]conky-musicrc.lua[/COLOR]" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/[COLOR="#FF0000"]conkywx-clockrc.lua[/COLOR]" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/[COLOR="#FF0000"]conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua[/COLOR]" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/[COLOR="#FF0000"]big-luamix-conkyrc.lua[/COLOR]" &
	conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/[COLOR="#FF0000"]vindsl-conkyrc.lua[/COLOR]" &

	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/conky-system.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/expanded-conkyrc.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/now-conkyrc.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/scroller-conkyrc.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/short-02-conkyrc.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/short-conkyrc.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/short-luamix-conkyrc.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/slim-conkyrc.lua" &
	# conky -c "$WX_DIR/examples/vindsl-conkyrc.lua" &
	;;
*)


```

### Post by paramvir on 2015-12-02
> **foodoo said:**
> 
where am i going wrong ? what am i missing ?
cheers Sean :-)

Greetings ole Bier Buddy ;)

Willkommen to the new world ):P

There is a **convert.lua** utility in the /usr/share/docs/extra or near there - use this to convert your old rc file.

You must remember that items in the config section ( above the old TEXT section stuff  **conky.config** ) - 
[LIST]
[*]each line must end with a comma, non-keywords - that is, excluding numbers or true false - need to be in quotes - single quotes are better. 
[*]comments in the config section use 2 dashes - like -- and the comment 
[*]in the TEXT section now known as **conky.text** - comments are standard hash # and the comment 
[/LIST]

If you have a peek at the *rc.lua files in the conkywx/examples folder - you will see the new rc files converted with the convert.lua utility and one is good to go right out of the box - so to speak.

OK another things I noticed in that last couple of days - the if_running function is working in the latest conky release 1.10.1 - but is a little more cpu intensive than my lua version for the same function. Specially if your update interval is like 1 second and you have a number of calls to if_running. I noticed this in conky-musicrc.lua - you do not require audacious built conky - conkywx handles audacious and VLC and of course my very own wx media player ;). 

Good thing I created wx media player - I now realise the music and learning audios I have collected over the years and never heard them while working in silent bliss huahahahaha :lolflag:

I could not get the audio quality from **anytin** else after I created the handling for VLC and audacious - 
[LIST]
[*]I wanted something super light - very low system resources 
[*]able to handle very very **very** large playlists - without blinking an eye 
[*]using standard tools - bash and lua(any version), sed, awk, tail, head etc standard stuff on any distro. 
[*]The musics starts immediately as you start loading tracks 
[*]Watch videos as well - pretty close to what people are going gaga over mpv 
[/LIST]

Ahh I see good Cavsfan has responded already la 

Trust all this helps and we get our Bier points 

cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2015-12-03
> **foodoo said:**
> Hi Paramvir,

Thanks for your help I tried your suggestion but sadly it's still the same

Thanks mate

ok how about this - we never know if conky is unable to parse the magic to our liking ;)

```

${lua main -n thermometer -p 5,705 -t [PT] -s 0.95 -u [UU] }#
${lua main   -n showcond -p 205, 505 -s 90 -w wi -f [BF] -d [WDD] -t 1 -e [WS] -a 3 }

```

**'#'** hash character at the end of a line is a continuation character 

This is much better from readability point as well - since the position '-p' is an absolute position - same as for the image tags - so it does not matter where you place these lines - the result will show up where you state it to be rather than where you place the line.

OK I also banged your lines into my template and they show up as desired.

Perhaps if you show us your template we can do some peekaboo huahahaha


This is my Bier time :guitar:

cheers

### Post by paramvir on 2015-12-03
> **foodoo said:**
> Thanks Paramvir,

Just for fun I stripped out everything else from my template apart from:


Yes that is key word - **FUN** lol - it is just a line of code mucking with us - we shall tame it down - no worries ;)

Dig in again into conkywx.lua

[php]
local function do_thermometer(vala)        ------------ {{{
    local units = vala.u or print("Thermometer Units missing")
    local scale = vala.s
    local temp  = vala.t
    local label = vala.l
    local mx    = vala.lwx*(1/tonum(scale))+30     <--- lines to 
    local my    = vala.lwy*(1/tonum(scale))-25     <--- mess with

[/php]

We just need to convince silly lua that we are indeed giving it numbers.

Oh and Sean this code has been tested on your Ubuntu version as well as the testing stuff for Ubuntu 2016 and many other distros by some blood thirsty ... you get my drift :o

I would like to state that I DO NOT LIKE lua - **it is horrible** - much better to go with Perl !!! The guys from Brazil are weird - they will get me a Bier for saying that huahahaha - been there - lived there - you can really "Blame it on Rio" this time lol

But, hopefully that'll get you started in the right direction.

Enter conkywx in terminal and press 2 to get the wiki to load in your browser. Your answer most probably lies there.

I believe your conf file is not setup properly producing the red line about the stations.txt.
There are many examples in the **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/** directory but the stations.txt file is in **/usr/share/conkywx/stations.txt**.
So that may have not gotten installed when you installed conkywx. Param would know the answer to that question.

That's good!
There are a lot of other things besides just location in the conf file. We all use the **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf** file.
But you have to use a separate one for each conky that calls conkywx.

I use that one for the Big lua mix, and another one for VinDSL's conky - **/usr/share/conkywx/examples/vindsl-conkywx.conf** file.

But if you find one that hasn't been touched yet it might yield some clues as to what you're missing.

Not sure what generates the stations.txt file but you were missing that. It should be here **/usr/share/conkywx/stations.txt**.

Cheers mate

### Post by aquarius18 on 2016-02-02
Since upgrading to conkywx 5.0 I am having a real issue to position the seismic widget to the top of my screen. No matter what I do with the -p switch the widget stays put on the bottom of the screen. It worked fine in conkywx 4.0 - see screenies

before upgrade:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/12493844966193826785218813939511283768901o.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/12493844966193826785218813939511283768901o.php")

after upgrade:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2016-02-02191957.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2016-02-02191957.php")

No idea why I can't get the widget to the top of the screen......

---

### Post by Cavsfan on 2016-02-02
> **aquarius18 said:**
> Since upgrading to conkywx 5.0 I am having a real issue to position the seismic widget to the top of my screen. No matter what I do with the -p switch the widget stays put on the bottom of the screen. It worked fine in conkywx 4.0 - see screenies

before upgrade:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/12493844966193826785218813939511283768901o.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/12493844966193826785218813939511283768901o.php")

after upgrade:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2016-02-02191957.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2016-02-02191957.php")

No idea why I can't get the widget to the top of the screen......

In conkywx-surface-imagerc.lua, I have this, and you may need to play with these numbers: (If I'm not mistaken they seemed to work in reverse from what I would think they should work.)

```
    gap_x = -65,
    gap_y = 1,
```

And this for template7:

```
${if_existing ${template7}}#
${lua main -n scroller -b 3::0x444444:0.4 -f 12 -l 40 -s 15 -p 20,545 -o ${template7} }${endif}

```

This makes my seismic info display in the bottom middle just a little ways up from the bottom because I have Cairo Dock.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2016-02-0211-07-24.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2016-02-0211-07-24.php")

### Post by NM5TF on 2016-02-02
> **aquarius18 said:**
> Since upgrading to conkywx 5.0 I am having a real issue to position the seismic widget to the top of my screen. No matter what I do with the -p switch the widget stays put on the bottom of the screen. It worked fine in conkywx 4.0 - see screenies

before upgrade:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/12493844966193826785218813939511283768901o.jpg[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/12493844966193826785218813939511283768901o.php")

after upgrade:

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshotfrom2016-02-02191957.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshotfrom2016-02-02191957.php")

No idea why I can't get the widget to the top of the screen......

this is an easy one to solve Frank.....look at this: ;)

in your surface-image-lua file

####------------- Earth Quake / Seismic data Example ----------------
#### to align from bottom - set -b to 3 with or without colour
[COLOR="#FF0000"]#### to align from top - set -b to 4 [/COLOR]and what ever colour - as required ...
#### the top value is taken from conky config gap_y setting
#### The Height of the window is important
#### - see above for "minimum_size 900 1050" - 1050 is height of window
#### change the number of records in config file - the widget will auto position itself.
${if_existing ${template7}}#
${lua main -n scroller [COLOR="#FF0000"]-b 4[/COLOR]::0x333333:0.6 -f 12 -l 40 -s 15 -p 12,1 -o ${template7} }${endif}
]];


tommy

### Post by aquarius18 on 2016-02-02
> **NM5TF said:**
> this is an easy one to solve Frank.....look at this: ;)

in your surface-image-lua file

####------------- Earth Quake / Seismic data Example ----------------
#### to align from bottom - set -b to 3 with or without colour
[COLOR=#FF0000]#### to align from top - set -b to 4 [/COLOR]and what ever colour - as required ...
#### the top value is taken from conky config gap_y setting
#### The Height of the window is important
#### - see above for "minimum_size 900 1050" - 1050 is height of window
#### change the number of records in config file - the widget will auto position itself.
${if_existing ${template7}}#
${lua main -n scroller [COLOR=#FF0000]-b 4[/COLOR]::0x333333:0.6 -f 12 -l 40 -s 15 -p 12,1 -o ${template7} }${endif}
]];


tommy
