---

### Post by VinDSL on 2016-08-18
I ran Conky 1.10.2-1 (from the Ubu repos) for a week, and it worked.  Soooo ...

Now, I'm testing Conky 1.10.3-1 backport (from the Debian repos).  Seems to be working okay, too.

Here's the addy:  [https://packages.debian.org/jessie-backports/conky-all](https://packages.debian.org/jessie-backports/conky-all)


[INDENT](Click to expand)

[[IMG]http://vindsl.com/images/VinDSL_2016-08-18_11:57:53(650x520).png[/IMG]]("http://vindsl.com/images/VinDSL_2016-08-18_11:57:53.png")[/INDENT]


And, here's the latest '.conkyrc' ...

```
--##################################################
--## VinDSL | rev 16-08-18 12:27 | v.1.10.3-1-P7  ##
--##################################################
--##             August 2016 Series               ##
--##################################################

--## ¡PLEASE READ THE FINE PRINT! ##

--####
--## Development Platforms (current)
--#
--#  Peppermint Linux OS Seven        (GNOME 3.18 - Conky 1.10.3-1)
--#  Ubuntu 16.10 'Yakkety Yak'       (GNOME-SHELL - UNITY - Conky 1.10.3-1)
--#  Screen Resolution: 1280x1024x24  (DELL UltraSharp 1907FP)
--#
--## Tips n' Tricks: Conky 1.8.x - 1.9.x will no longer be supported. RIP :)

--####
--## Prerequisites (required)
--#
--#  conky-all 1.10.3-1 backport
--#  Conkywx 5.0.0-x (Paramvir Likhari)
--#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor
--#  mesa-utils - Basic GL Utilities

--## DBus toolsets (required for experimental music players below)
--## Install one PAIR or the other PAIR (but NOT both pair) depending on your distro,
--## Examples from Ubuntu PPA - package names are in ('parenthesis'):
--#
--#  Qt 4 D-Bus tool ('qdbus') & Qt 4 development tools ('qt4-dev-tools')
--#  Qt 5 D-Bus tool ('qdbus-qt5') & Qt 5 base development programs ('qtbase5-dev-tools')

--####
--## Installed fonts (required)
--#
--#  Arrows (JLH Fonts - not included in link below)
--#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
--#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
--#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
--#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
--#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
--#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
--#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
--#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
--#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
--#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
--#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva - not included in link below)
--#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
--#
--## Tips n' Tricks from: Mr. Peachy, djyoung4, and 42dorian (Thanks!)
--## Most of the necessary fonts can be downloaded here: http://goo.gl/QPBcPm
--## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
--## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

conky.config = {

--####
--## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
--#
use_xft = true,
font = 'DroidSansFallback:size=9.55',
xftalpha = 0.1,

--####
--## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
--## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
--#
override_utf8_locale = true,

--####
--## This buffer is used for single lines, text, output from $exec, and other variables.
--## Increasing the 'text_buffer_size' (too high) will drastically reduce Conky's performance.
--## Decreasing the buffer size (too low) will truncate content and cause strange display output.
--## Standard text buffer size is 256 bytes (cannot be less). Adjust YOUR buffer wisely!
--## Tips n' Tricks from dk75:"You don't need to create a 12KiB buffer for every Conky config line."
--#
text_buffer_size = 4352,

--####
--## Maximum number of special things, e.g. fonts, offsets, aligns, etc. (default is 512)
--## Tips n' Tricks from: sparker256 (Thanks, Bill!)
--## (Currently not working in Conky 1.10.3-1 - Disabled)
--#
--max_specials 600,

--####
--## Maximum size of user text buffer, e.g. for layout below TEXT line (default is 16384)
--## Increase this, as needed, to accommodate large config files with a great deal of text.
--## Tips n' Tricks from: Cavsfan
--#
max_user_text = 32768,

--####
--## Daemonize Conky, aka 'fork to background'.
--#
background = true,

--####
--## Update interval in seconds.
--#
update_interval = 2.0,

--####
--## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
--## Zero makes Conky run forever.
--#
total_run_times = 0,

--####
--## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
--#
own_window = true,
own_window_transparent = true,
own_window_type = 'normal',
own_window_hints = 'undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager',
--####
--## Some distros require the following lines for TRUE transparency.
--## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
--#
--own_window_argb_visual = true,
--own_window_argb_value = 255,
--####
--## Don't want TRUE transparency? (icons look janky on certain walls)
--## Comment BOTH of the lines above and Uncomment the line below.
--#
own_window_argb_visual = false,

--####
--## Force images to redraw when they change.
--#
imlib_cache_size = 1,

--####
--## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
--#
double_buffer = true,

--####
--## Draw shades?
--#
draw_shades = false,
default_shade_color = '333300',
--# default_shade_color = '3c3c3c',
--# default_shade_color = '494949',
--# default_shade_color = '565656',
--# default_shade_color = '626262',

--####
--## Draw outlines?
--#
draw_outline = false,

--####
--## Draw borders around text?
--#
draw_borders = false,

--####
--## Draw borders around graphs?
--#
draw_graph_borders = false,

--####
--## Print text in X?
--## Print text in console?
--## Print text to stdout?
--#
out_to_x = true,
out_to_console = false,
out_to_ncurses = false,

--####
--## Text alignment.
--#
alignment = 'top_right',

--####
--## Minimum size of the text area.
--#
minimum_width = 245,
minimum_height = 1394,

--####
--## Maximum width of the text area.
--#
maximum_width = 245,

--####
--## Gap between text and screen borders.
--#
gap_x = 6,  --# Left/Right
gap_y = 32, --# Top/Bottom

--####
--## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
--#
short_units = true,

--####
--## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
--#
pad_percents = 0,

--####
--## Pad spacing between text and borders.
--#
border_inner_margin = 4,

--####
--## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
--#
top_name_width = 10,

--####
--## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
--## Set to true, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
--#
no_buffers = true,

--####
--## Set to true, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
--#
uppercase = false,

--####
--## Number of cpu samples to average.
--## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
--#
cpu_avg_samples = 2,

--####
--## Number of net samples to average.
--## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
--#
net_avg_samples = 2,

--####
--## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
--## Only affects certain objects.
--#
use_spacer = 'right',

--####
--## My colors (suit yourself)
--#
color0 = 'white',		--#FFFFFF
color1 = 'ivory',		--#FFFFF0
color2 = 'ivory2',		--#EEEEE0
color3 = 'ivory3',		--#CDCDC1
color4 = 'ffcc66',		--#FFCC66
color5 = 'ivory3',		--#CDCDC1
color6 = 'gray',		--#BEBEBE
color7 = 'antiquewhite4',	--#8B8378
color8 = 'dimgray',		--#696969
color9 = 'tomato',		--#FF6347

--#####
--## Load Lua for shading (optional)
--## Set the path to your script here.
--## (Currently not working in Conky 1.10.3-1 - Disabled)
--#
-- lua_load = '~/.conky/draw_bg.lua',
-- lua_draw_hook_pre = 'draw_bg',

--####
--## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
--## Set the path to your script here.
--#
lua_load = '~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua',
lua_draw_hook_post = 'main_bars',

--####
--## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
--## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
--#
template0 = '/usr/share/conkywx/conkywx.sh',
template1 = '/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/vindsl-imperial-conkywx.conf',
template2 = '/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/vindsl-metric-conkywx.conf',
template3 = '/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-imperial-weather-dingbat-template.sh',
template4 = '/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-imperial-weather-graphic-template.sh',
template5 = '/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-metric-weather-dingbat-template.sh',
template6 = '/home/vindsl/.config/conkywx/wx-wu-vindsl-metric-weather-graphic-template.sh',
--##
--## Tips n' Tricks: type 'route' in CLI and check for interface names in the 'Iface' column .
--## Define the Wireless interface here - wlan0, wlan1, etc. (required)
--##
template7 = 'wlan0',
--##
--## Define the Wired interface name here - eth0, eth1, etc. (required)
--#
template8 = 'eno1'

} --end conky.config
conky.text = [[

##################################
##             LOGO             ##
##################################
## Uncomment for hard-coded ID (static)
# ${offset -3}${voffset -34}${font OpenLogos:size=106}${color2}v${font}${voffset -78}${goto 180}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.55}${color2}${offset 4}1${offset 2}6${offset 1}.${offset 0}1${offset 1}0${font}
####
## Uncomment for soft-coded ID (dynamic)
# ${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -75}${goto 179}${font UbuntuTitleBold:size=19.6}${color4}${execpi 1800 cat /etc/*release | grep 'RELEASE' | awk -F'=' '{print $2}'}${font}
## Additional ID (branch version, code name, release date, etc.)
# ${voffset 0}${goto 185}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}${execpi 1800 conky --version | sed -n 1p | cut -b1-13}${font}
# ${voffset 0}${goto 182}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}Conky ${conky_version}${font}
# ${voffset 0}${goto 182}${font Ubuntu-B:size=8}${color4}Pre-Alpha${font}
####
## Uncomment to display YOUR custom graphic image (upper-right corner)
# ${voffset -30}${font OpenLogos:size=103}${color2}v${font}${voffset -52}${image /home/vindsl/Pictures/BDC/Avatar(Transparent-50)55x55.png -p 180,0 -s 52x52 -n}
${voffset -1}${image /home/vindsl/.conky/logos/peppermint_logo_small.png -n}${voffset 18}${goto 45}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}${font Ubuntu-B:bold:size=7.55}${color5}Conkywx ${execpi 1800 conkywx -v}${goto 180}Conky ${conky_version}${font}${voffset -5}
##################################
##            SYSTEM            ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}SYSTEM${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}${execpi 1800 lsb_release -sd | sed -e 's/Ubuntu/Ubuntu/g' -e 's/development/dev/g'}${alignr}${execpi 1800 lsb_release -sr}${font}
# ${voffset 4}${font OpenLogos:size=10}${color2}u${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}${execpi 1800 lsb_release -sd}${alignr}32 Bit${font}
${voffset 0}${offset -2}${font OpenLogos:size=12}${color2}Z${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 3}${sysname}${offset 3}${kernel}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${machine}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nVidia ID (nvidia-smi version - dynamic)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}${execpi 1800 nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${alignr}${execpi 1800 nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for 'nvidia-current' driver ID (DPKG version - hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execpi 1800 dpkg --status nvidia-current | grep Version | cut -f 1 -d '-' | sed 's/[^.,0-9]//g'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for nvidia driver ID (OpenGL version - hydrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${offset 3}(${offset 2}${execpi 2 nvidia-settings -q gpucoretemp -t --verbose=none | awk '{s+=$1}END{print s/NR}' RS=" "}°${offset 1})${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execpi 1800 glxinfo | grep 'OpenGL version string' | sed -e 's/OpenGL.*: //' | cut -c '14-20'}${font}
## Uncomment when running Nouveau drivers
# ${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -1}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce GT 710${voffset -2}${alignr}nouveau${font}
## Uncomment when running nVidia drivers with GPU temp
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce GT 710${offset 3}(${offset 2}${execpi 2 nvidia-smi --query-gpu=temperature.gpu --format=csv,noheader,nounits}°${offset 1})${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${execpi 1800 nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (Nouveau version - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}nVidia GeForce 7600 GT${offset 2}${alignr}nouveau${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for hard-coded video ID (Generic version / any card / any driver - static)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}d${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}Video Card Product Name${alignr}Driver Version${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${font}
####
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU Temp / CPU frequency (hybrid)
# ${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset 0}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}P4${offset 3}Extreme${offset 3}Edition${offset 3}(${offset 2}${execpi 2 sensors | grep 'temp1' | cut -c16-17}°${offset 1})${alignr 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${offset 1}GHz${font}
## Uncomment for CPU ID / CPU Temp / CPU frequency (hybrid)
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}A${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}Intel${offset 3}Core${offset 3}i5-3470${offset 4}(${offset 2}${execpi 2 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed -n 1p | cut -b18-19}°${voffset -1}${offset 1})${alignr -3}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${freq_g cpu0}${voffset -1}${offset 2}GHz${font}
####
## Uncomment for System Uptime (hybdid)
${voffset 2}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}q${voffset -2}${font DroidSans:size=8.55}${color3}${offset 5}System${offset 3}Uptime${voffset -1}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${uptime_short}${font}
##################################
##          PROCESSORS          ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}PROCESSORS${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 5}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color3}${offset 2}CPU1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8}${cpu cpu1}%${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${goto 137}${voffset -1}CPU2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8}${cpu cpu2}%${font}
${voffset 1}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}k${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color3}${offset 2}CPU3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8}${cpu cpu3}%${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${goto 137}${voffset -1}CPU4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8}${cpu cpu4}%${font}
##################################
##            MEMORY            ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}MEMORY${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 6}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color2}l${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color3}${offset 3}RAM${goto 97}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${mem}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${memmax}${alignr}${memperc}%${font}
##################################
##             HDD              ##
##################################
${voffset 14}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}HDD${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 7}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color3}${offset 4}ROOT${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${fs_used /}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /}%${font}
${voffset 16}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}x${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color3}${offset 4}HOME${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${fs_used /home}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${fs_size /home}${alignr}${fs_free_perc /home}%${font}
${voffset 16}${font StyleBats:size=9.9}${color2}4${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8}${color3}${offset 4}SWAP${goto 95}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${swap}${goto 133}/${offset 5}${swapmax}${alignr}${swapperc}%${font}
##################################
##         TOP PROCESSES        ##
##################################
${voffset 15}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TOP PROCESSES${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 2}${top_mem name 1}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${top_mem mem_res 1}${alignr}${top_mem mem 1}%${font}
${voffset 0}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 2}${top_mem name 2}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${top_mem mem_res 2}${alignr}${top_mem mem 2}%${font}
${voffset 0}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 2}${top_mem name 3}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${top_mem mem_res 3}${alignr}${top_mem mem 3}%${font}
# ${voffset 0}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 2}${top_mem name 4}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${top_mem mem_res 4}${alignr}${top_mem mem 4}%${font}
# ${voffset 0}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 2}${top_mem name 5}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${top_mem mem_res 5}${alignr}${top_mem mem 5}%${font}
# ${voffset 0}${font StyleBats:size=10}${color1}h${voffset -1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 2}${top_mem name 6}${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${top_mem mem_res 6}${alignr}${top_mem mem 6}%${font}
##################################
##   NETWORK - AUTO DETECTION   ##
##################################
## WIRELESS/WIFI SECTION ##
${if_existing /proc/net/route ${template7}}
${voffset -11}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}WIFI NET${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}T${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 5}Download${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totaldown ${template7}}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${downspeed ${template7}}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}N${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 5}Upload${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totalup ${template7}}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${upspeed ${template7}}${font}
${voffset 4}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}LAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${addr ${template7}}${font}
#
## Uncomment [THIS] to display your Public/WAN URL (WIFI) - via checkip.dyndns.org
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}${else}
#
## Uncomment [OR THIS] to obscure your Public/WAN URL (WIFI) - for privacy, screenshots, etc
# ${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${font DroidSans:size=8.65}${color3}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${alignr}Demo Mode${font}${else}
#
## WIRED/ETHERNET SECTION ##
${if_existing /proc/net/route ${template8}}
${voffset -23}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}WIRED NET${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 4}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}T${voffset 1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.2}${color3}${offset 5}Download${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totaldown ${template8}}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${downspeed ${template8}}${font}
${voffset -3}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}N${voffset 1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.2}${color3}${offset 5}Upload${goto 120}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${totalup ${template8}}${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${upspeed ${template8}}${font}
${voffset 0}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${voffset 1}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.2}${color3}${offset 5}Private${offset 3}IP${goto 123}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}LAN${alignr}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${addr ${template8}}${font}
#
## Uncomment [THIS] to display your Public/WAN URL (WIRED) - via checkip.dyndns.org
# ${voffset -3}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${voffset 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${color3}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.3}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${voffset -1}${alignr}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}${else}
#
## Uncomment [OR THIS] to obscure your Public/WAN URL (WIRED) - for privacy, screenshots, etc
${voffset -3}${font PizzaDudeBullets:size=9.5}${color6}a${voffset 1}${font DroidSans:size=8.2}${color3}${offset 5}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.5}WAN${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${voffset -1}${alignr}Demo Mode${font}${else}
#
## COMMON DISCONNECTED/OFFLINE SECTION ##
${voffset -22}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}NETWORK${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 25}${goto 77}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}NO CONNECTION${voffset 23}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##      CONKYWX WEATHER         ##
##################################
${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8.25}${color4}CONKYWX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (DINGBAT font set).
# ${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template3}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for IMPERIAL Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template1}" -t "${template4}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for METRIC Weather Stats (DINGBAT font set).
# ${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template2}" -t "${template5}"}${font}
####
## Uncomment for METRIC Weather Stats (GRAPHIC icon set).
# ${execpi 900 ${template0} -c "${template2}" -t "${template6}"}${font}
##################################
##           TIME               ##
##################################
${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}TIME${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset -6}${font RadioSpace:size=32}${color3}${if_match ${time %l}<=9}${alignc 7}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${else}${if_match ${time %l}>=10}${alignc -1}${time %l:%M}${offset 3}${time %p}${endif}${endif}${font}
##################################
##         CALENDAR             ##
##################################
${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}DATE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 10}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %A}${font}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSansFallback:bold:size=18}${if_match ${time %e}<=9}${color3}${alignc 65}${time %e}${font}${else}${if_match ${time %e}>=10}${color3}${alignc 60}${time %e}${endif}${endif}${font}
${voffset 0}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %B}${font}
${voffset -2}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.6}${color2}${alignc 60}${time %Y}${font}
${voffset -78}${font CutOutsFor3DFX:size=67}${color6}${alignc 95}2${font}
####
## Uncomment for "SUNDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2015"}${voffset -66}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -66}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jan 2016"}${voffset -66}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Jul 2016"}${voffset -66}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -82}${else}${voffset -60}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; cal -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}
####
## Uncomment for "MONDAY = First Day-of-the-Week" valid Jan.2015 - Dec.2016 (use mono fonts only)
## Tweaked for proper alignment of annoying months with six calendar weeks.
# ${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Mar 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Aug 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Nov 2015"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="May 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${if_match "${time %b %G}"=="Oct 2016"}${voffset -76}${else}${voffset -70}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${endif}${font DroidSansMono:size=7.55}${color3}${execpi 60 VinDSL_Cal_9=`date +%-d`; ncal -M -C -h | sed -e 's/\r//g' -e 's/^/ /g' -e '1d' -e s/^/"\$\{offset 100"\}/ -e 's/\<'"$VinDSL_Cal_9"'\>/${color9}&${color3}/'}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 1 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
# ${if_running rhythmbox}
# ${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
# ${voffset 4}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt | head -n 1`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 rhythmbox-client --print-playing-format %tt --no-start}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##  RHYTHMBOX 2 (Experimental)  ##
##################################
# ${if_running rhythmbox}
# ${voffset -13}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}RHYTHMBOX${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
# ${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=7.85}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.rhythmbox /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title | cut -c 14-""}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##    BANSHEE (Experimental)    ##
##################################
# ${if_running banshee}
# ${voffset -25}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}BANSHEE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
# ${voffset 3}${font DroidSans:size=7.85}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`/usr/bin/banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f1- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 banshee --query-title --no-present | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##   GUAYADEQUE (Experimental)  ##
##################################
# ${if_running guayadeque}
# ${voffset -15}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}GUAYADEQUE${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
# ${voffset 2}${font DroidSans:size=7.85}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.guayadeque /\Player org.freedesktop.MediaPlayer.GetMetadata | grep title | cut -f2- -d" "}${font}${endif}${endif}
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 6}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement}${font}${endif}${endif}
]] --end conky.text
```

### Post by VinDSL on 2016-08-20
> **paramvir said:**
> Example: ${include_file path to file}

you can see - there has been a bit of a discussion here [https://github.com/brndnmtthws/conky/pull/195](https://github.com/brndnmtthws/conky/pull/195) - go down to where OP asks if his thing will get merged ?

Lets put it this way - once you see what I am getting at - would that be on your wishlist for conky?

I've been thinking about this all week.  :D

I don't know if the OP's pulls were finally merged or not (I suspect not), but I found a practical application for the 'cat' variable (GNU coreutil).

Regardless, 'cat' worked, and it fixed a nagging concern for me.

Conky 1.10.x seems to have a problem recognizing and/or handling nested 'if' variables.  As a result, my experimental music player scripts are constantly looping error/warning/notifications in the background such as (for instance) "Service 'org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc' does not exist." when the VLC player is not open and running.

By making a separate text file and including it via the 'cat' variable, it keeps the nested 'if' variables out of harm's way, until they're needed.

Does that make sense ?

As an example of what I'm talking about ...

The last section in my script is:

```
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}
${voffset -4}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 6}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement}${font}${endif}${endif}
```

While this never caused a problem in the past, if you start Conky 1.10.x in CLI and watch what's going on in the background, it is constantly looping a notification that "Service 'org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc' does not exist." when VLC is not playing.

Technically speaking, of course, this dbus service doesn't exist when VLC is NOT playing.  And, when you start playing something with VLC, the notice disappears until you close VLC, then the notifications start looping again.

What I did was get the nested 'if' variables out of harm's way by putting them in a text file and calling them with the 'cat' variable.  

Here's how the VLC section in my .conkyrc file looks now:


```
##################################
##     VLC (Experimental)       ##
##################################
${if_running vlc}${execpi 2 cat ~/.conky/vlctrack.txt}${else}${endif}
```


And, here's how the ~/.conky/vlctrack.txt file looks:


```
${voffset 7}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}VLC${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}${font}
${voffset 7}${font DroidSans:size=8.25}${color3}${if_match "${execpi 2 expr length "`qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement`"}" >= "48"}${alignr 15}${scroll 38 4* ${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement}}${font}${else}${alignc}${execi 2 qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep xesam:artist: | cut -c -13 --complement | tr '\n' ' ' && qdbus org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.vlc /\org/mpris/MediaPlayer2 org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.Player.Metadata | grep title: | cut -c -13 --complement}${font}${endif}
```


BINGO !  Problem solved !  Amazingly, 'cat' parses the code correctly !

Now, I only get a handful of residual notifications in CLI after closing VLC, while the dbus service is stopping, and the nested 'if' variables go back to sleep.

### Post by paramvir on 2016-08-21
> **VinDSL said:**
> I've been thinking about this all week.  :D

I don't know if the OP's pulls were finally merged or not (I suspect not), but I found a practical application for the 'cat' variable (GNU coreutil).



Yes this was my point to OP there - but - you do not need to use soreutil cat - you can use conky head or tail - since you are not looking at using conky variables - but I think you are doing much more with cat...

There was an issue with nested ifs and it was settled - but it needs more looking into ...

Also I found the catp more interesting - where you have a choice to use the full functionality of variables as well.

OK - since we are talking about music - I took a different course - I guess you figured me by now huahahaha 

With conkywx FIVE - I also released **wx media player **- uses mplayer as back end [ though Florian my esteemed conkywx German Translator feels I need to build mpv support into it :P ] - but vlc and audacious are also supported besides **wx media player** - **without** audacious being compiled into conky :D !!! - mpv is tricky but yes - support shall come ;-)

[ATTACH=CONFIG]270793[/ATTACH]

OK - in this screen you can see VLC playing - it is using conkywx to show you the magic of vlc - conkywx --help has all the stuff.

By the way - the Earth you see - I realised Flat Earth could display some interesting things - This widget will show you actual positions of the sun and the moon and twilight and sun rise and sun set - you will see eclipses as they happen ;-) - just using information we already calculate with Magellan.

But SIX is some time to come - many things happening ;-)

### Post by Cavsfan on 2016-08-25
> **NM5TF said:**

I believe Temp2 is referring to the IOH or X58 Chip.....IOH = Input/Output Hub.....go here:

Thanks but, that appears to be for an i7. Mine is an old Intel Core2 Quad purchased around March 2009.

But, I did find from looking up the datasheet on this [Intel® G33 Express Chipset]("http://ark.intel.com/products/31914/Intel-82G33-Graphics-and-Memory-Controller"),
it may be the MCH Temperature (Memory control hub). It says it was made in Q2 of 2007, so that makes sense.

It's actually a G31, but it is a 82G33 and MCH makes as much sense as anything else I reckon.

My brain hurts from looking at all this stuff!!!

With any additional Pulse packages installed **wx media player** would not work.

I had a little startup sound set to play with paplay and noticed it never worked; I never heard anything so I played the command in terminal and it could not even find paplay.
 I used mplayer and it works fine and I do hear the startup sound.

Guess my head is fine now. :P

What I have currently:

[ATTACH=CONFIG]270995[/ATTACH]

You will only need 1 of these that have **imperial** or **metric** mentioned in them. Imperial is for us backwards Americans who can't understand Celsius and prefer Fahrenheit instead. :p

And one of the 2 **dingbat-template** or **graphic-template**, but not both. They are different looks as you will learn.
I always seemed to like the graphic template the best, but that's entirely up to you.

When you installed conkywx, it put all the files in **/usr/share/conkywx/examples** and they will be root owned. But, you can copy them from there into your directory,
Just make sure you point to where they are.

If you type conkwx in terminal option 2 will bring up a conkywx WIKI Manual in your browser. There you will find most of what you are looking for.
There you will find VinDSL's fonts, a conkywx-assistant and help with everything you could possibly need. Paramvir is pretty thorough on his help documentation.

It's hard starting from the first page as much has changed since then. Then we went from conky 1.9 to 1.10 and everything changed...

VinDSL came up with a new one that he posted back a few pages, lemme see...
Here is his latest post but, you may want to look up a bit and down a bit from here: 
[https://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=246&p=13529745#post13529745](https://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1771033&page=246&p=13529745#post13529745)

I'm in Arch Linux right now and cannot provide a screenshot of VinDSL's conky.

See how far that takes you and post back with any questions.

Welcome to conky and VinDSL's conky has always been one of my favorites.
I learned much from this thread and spent a couple lifetimes messing with it. :p

Here is a screenshot of my Arch Linux system with 5 conkys going on: (BTW this is version 6, which is still being tested but, will be released shortly.
After it has been through a fine toothed comb and has all the additions that Param wants to put into it. Part of it is the tilted pictures and the day/night picture
It will also be leaner and meaner than version 5 and it's predecessors)

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2016-09-0717-47-38.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2016-09-0717-47-38.php")

I run mine from **/usr/share/conkywx**, and I've been warned several times that you are not supposed to do that. I don't listen very well. :P

### Post by foodoo on 2017-01-13
Hello al14,

Here's my quad core code plus the code for the CPU temps as well !

> ${voffset 4}${font RadioSpace:bold:size=8}${color4}PROCESSORS${offset 8}${color4}${voffset -2}${hr 2}${font}
${voffset 6}${font conkysymbols:size=8}${color2}F${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2} CPU 1${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu1}${lua main -n bargraph -p 95,165 -w 188 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}%${alignr}${execi 30 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | cut -c16-17}°C${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=8}${color2}F${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2} CPU 2${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu2}${lua main -n bargraph -p 95,180 -w 188 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}%${alignr}${execi 30 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | cut -c16-17}°C${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=8}${color2}F${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2} CPU 3${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu3}${lua main -n bargraph -p 95,195 -w 188 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}%${alignr}${execi 30 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | cut -c16-17}°C${font}
${voffset 2}${font conkysymbols:size=8}${color2}F${voffset -2}${font DroidSansFallback:size=8.39}${color3}${offset 2} CPU 4${offset 5}${font DroidSans:size=8.3}${cpu cpu4}${lua main -n bargraph -p 95,210 -w 188 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4}%${alignr}${execi 30 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | cut -c16-17}°C${font}


You will probably need to mess around with the '-P' values to correctly align the bar graph's plus you will need to install 'lm-sensors' then run 'sensors-detect' and simply type 'yes' to all of the questions.
Once you have done this you can run 'sensors' in your terminal which will show you all of your core temps plus fan speeds and the relevant voltage's of your system

Cheers Sean

### Post by foodoo on 2017-01-13
Ok take a look inside the bar graph Lua script you could simply copy the code from one of the cores then paste it under the second core to make the third core and again under the third core
to make the fourth core

I just found the small bar graph script:

```
function conky_main_bars()	local bars_settings={
		{	--[ Graph for CPU1 ]--
			name="cpu",
			arg="cpu1",
			max=100,
			alarm=50,
			alarm_colour={0xFF0000,0.72},
			bg_colour={0xFFFFFF,0.25},
			fg_colour={0x00FF00,0.55},
			mid_colour={{0.45,0xFFFF00,0.70}},
			x=82,y=158,
			blocks=74,
			space=1,
			height=2,width=5,
			angle=90,
			smooth=true
			},
		{	--[ Graph for CPU2 ]--
			name="cpu",
			arg="cpu2",
			max=100,
			alarm=50,
			alarm_colour={0xFF0000,0.72},
			bg_colour={0xFFFFFF,0.25},
			fg_colour={0x00FF00,0.55},
			mid_colour={{0.45,0xFFFF00,0.70}},
			x=82,y=172,
			blocks=74,
			space=1,
			height=2,width=5,
			angle=90,
			smooth=true
			},
		{	--[ Graph for CPU2 ]--
			name="cpu",
			arg="cpu3",
			max=100,
			alarm=50,
			alarm_colour={0xFF0000,0.72},
			bg_colour={0xFFFFFF,0.25},
			fg_colour={0x00FF00,0.55},
			mid_colour={{0.45,0xFFFF00,0.70}},
			x=82,y=172,
			blocks=74,
			space=1,
			height=2,width=5,
			angle=90,
			smooth=true
			},
		{	--[ Graph for CPU2 ]--
			name="cpu",
			arg="cpu4",
			max=100,
			alarm=50,
			alarm_colour={0xFF0000,0.72},
			bg_colour={0xFFFFFF,0.25},
			fg_colour={0x00FF00,0.55},
			mid_colour={{0.45,0xFFFF00,0.70}},
			x=82,y=172,
			blocks=74,
			space=1,
			height=2,width=5,
			angle=90,
			smooth=true
			},
```

Make sure you number the cores 1 2 3 4 and because I have simply copied the code from core 2 and used it for the third and fourth you will need
to mess around with the X and Y values in particular the Y values to be able to see them both otherwise, cores 3 and 4 will be sitting on top of core 2 so you will not be able to see them.

I remember running my conky and opening the small bar graph script in an editor, messing with those values saving the changes in my editor then I could see those changes in my conky :-)

Try this I'm sure it will make you smile

Cheers Sean

### Post by ajgreeny on 2017-01-27
> **al14 said:**
> I managed to put together a Conky with vinDSLs' 'HOWTO: VinDSL Conky Script' Thread, with some minor modifications, but I am perplexed that both 'ROOT' & 'HOME' show the exact same amount _used_ and _available_, including the _percentages_.... 

Anyone know why?

Thanks!!
Do you have a separate filesystem, ie, partition for /home?

If not, home is simply a folder in the root partition, hence your identical values.

### Post by deadflowr on 2017-01-28
You can plus minus the alignr options to move a particular line over one way or the other
${alignr +5} will move 5 over one way
${alignr -5} will move 5 the other way.

Unlike other options such as offset or color or font the align# option only runs for that line.
where as the other options run until specified to end with an escape.

Typically something like ${font some-font-name-here:size=10} will have a ${font} at the end of whatever particular text output used that font.
Otherwise that font would run as the outputted font for all text/output for the rest of the script.
If that make sense
 

Alternatively you can also use ${goto ###} replacing ### with the position number counted from the left.
so ${goto 50} would be 50 over from the left.
goto should work just like align# where it should not need an escape.

---

where as the other options run until specified to end with an escape.

Typically something like ${font some-font-name-here:size=10} will have a ${font} at the end of whatever particular text output used that font.
Otherwise that font would run as the outputted font for all text/output for the rest of the script.
If that make sense
 

Alternatively you can also use ${goto ###} replacing ### with the position number counted from the left.
so ${goto 50} would be 50 over from the left.
goto should work just like align# where it should not need an escape.

### Post by NM5TF on 2017-01-30
> **al14 said:**
> Works like a charm, you're a genius!

Thanks!!

FYI....the unit of measure in this case is in Pixels....alignr +5 is 5 pixels from right margin....

a very handy helper app for aligning things on the desktop is "Screenruler"...
an actual ruller shows up on the screen and can be moved horiz/vertical and
the units can be changed...pixels, inches, cm/mm, etc....

sudo apt-get install screenruler

### Post by deadflowr on 2017-02-02
your new network interface name is eno1,
so you need to replace all the entries in the conkyrc from eth1 to eno1.

i use gedit to simplify this,
as gedit can find and replace.
(i'm sure other text editors have this function, but i do not use others enough)

to open gedit's find and replace press ctrl + H, (or look in the menu item search >> find and replace)
then type in the name eth1 in the Find box, then press the Find button in the bottom right corner.
then type eno1 in the Replace box, and then click on the Replace All button.
then click on Close and save the file.

---

### Post by al14 on 2017-02-02
That fixed it, thank-you!!

I changed the Template = 'eno1' , in another distro (same) conky from 'eno1' to 'eth1', replacing all the other 'eno1's' with 'eth1' and it worked fine, just not with this distro-

Anyway, you're terrific, thanks again!

EDIT: Any ideas about a new problem I mentioned in post #2509 (please see thumbnail)-

---

### Post by deadflowr on 2017-02-02
> **al14 said:**
> Any ideas about a new problem I mentioned in post #2509 (please see thumbnail)-

One of two things come to mind that are typical.

First is a configuration setting is off.
Post the top section of the conkyrc, the stuff before TEXT.

Second the blurring can happen sometimes when you accidentally have multiple conkys running inadvertently.
Comically I had this when I ran Kubuntu with the default restore session setting on, and thus my conky ended up a big wall of blur.

Of the two, though, I'd go with the first as something to look into.
But without the config settings we would be at a loss.
So post it.

---

### Post by al14 on 2017-02-02
I had the same thought that when it loaded at bootup at the upper left and jumped across to the upper right that it was two conky's that was causing the blur....

Slightly modified by me...

Thanks!!

```
--#  conky-all 1.10.1-3
--#  UTF-8 Compatible Text Editor
--#  mesa-utils - Basic GL Utilities
--####
--## Installed fonts (required)
--#
--#  Arrows (JLH Fonts - not included in link below)
--#  ConkyWeather (Stanko Metodiev)
--#  Cut Outs for 3D FX (Fonts & Things)
--#  Droid Font Family (Google Android SDK)
--#  KR A Round (Kat's Fun Fonts)
--#  Moon Phases (Curtis Clark)
--#  OpenLogos (Icoma)
--#  PizzaDude Bullets (Jakob Fischer)
--#  Radio Space (Iconian Fonts)
--#  StyleBats (Vinterstille)
--#  Ubuntu Font Family (Canonical Ltd)
--#  Ubuntu Title Bold (Paulo Silva - not included in link below)
--#  Weather (Jonathan Macagba)
--## Most of the necessary fonts can be downloaded here: http://goo.gl/QPBcPm
--## Unzip the fonts into your font folder, for example: /home/username/.fonts
--## Run this command in a terminal (rebuilds font cache file): sudo fc-cache -fv

conky.config = {

--####
--## Use XFT? Required to Force UTF8 (see below)
--#
use_xft = true,
font = 'DroidSansFallback:size=9.55',
xftalpha = 0.1,

--####
--## Force UTF8? Requires XFT (see above)
--## Displays degree symbol, instead of Â°, etc.
--#
override_utf8_locale = true,

--#
background = true,

--####
--## Update interval in seconds.
--#
update_interval = 2.0,

--####
--## The number of times Conky will update before quitting.
--## Zero makes Conky run forever.
--#
total_run_times = 0,

--####
--## Create 'own_window' type. Makes Conky behave like other panels.
--#
own_window = true,
own_window_transparent = true,
own_window_type = 'normal',
own_window_hints = 'undecorated,below,sticky,skip_taskbar,skip_pager',
--####
--## Some distros require the following lines for TRUE transparency.
--## BOTH of these lines need to be Commented/Uncommented in tandem.
--#
--own_window_argb_visual = true,
--own_window_argb_value = 255,
--####
--## Don't want TRUE transparency? (icons look janky on certain walls)
--## Comment BOTH of the lines above and Uncomment the line below.
--#
own_window_argb_visual = false,

--####
--## Force images to redraw when they change.
--#
imlib_cache_size = 1,

--####
--## Use double buffering? Reduces flicker.
--#
double_buffer = true,

--####
--## Draw shades?
--#
draw_shades = false,
default_shade_color = '333300',
--# default_shade_color = '3c3c3c',
--# default_shade_color = '494949',
--# default_shade_color = '565656',
--# default_shade_color = '626262',

--####
--## Draw outlines?
--#
draw_outline = false,

--####
--## Draw borders around text?
--#
draw_borders = false,

--####
--## Draw borders around graphs?
--#
draw_graph_borders = true,

--####
--## Print text to stdout?
--## Print text in console?
--#
out_to_ncurses = false,
out_to_console = false,

--####
--## Text alignment.
--#
alignment = 'top_right',

--####
--## Minimum size of the text area.
--#
minimum_width = 245,
minimum_height = 1394,

--####
--## Maximum width of the text area.
--#
maximum_width = 245,

--####
--## Gap between text and screen borders.
--#
gap_x = 6,  --# Left/Right
gap_y = 32, --# Top/Bottom

--####
--## Shorten MiB/GiB to M/G in stats.
--#
short_units = true,

--####
--## Pad % symbol spacing after numbers.
--#
pad_percents = 0,

--####
--## Pad spacing between text and borders.
--#
border_inner_margin = 4,

--####
--## Limit the length of names in "Top Processes".
--#
top_name_width = 10,

--####
--## Subtract file system -/+buffers/cache from used memory?
--## Set to true, to produce meaningful physical memory stats.
--#
no_buffers = true,

--####
--## Set to true, if you want all text to be in UPPERCASE.
--#
uppercase = false,

--####
--## Number of cpu samples to average.
--## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
--#
cpu_avg_samples = 2,

--####
--## Number of net samples to average.
--## Set to 1 to disable averaging.
--#
net_avg_samples = 2,

--####
--## Add spaces to keep things from moving around?
--## Only affects certain objects.
--#
use_spacer = 'right',

--####
--## My colors (suit yourself)
--#
--## color0 = 'white',        --#FFFFFF
color10 = 'white',            --#000000
color1 = 'ivory',            --#FFFFF0
color2 = 'ivory2',            --#EEEEE0
color3 = 'ivory3',            --#CDCDC1
color4 = 'mint4',           --#C7F994
color5 = 'ivory3',            --#CDCDC1
color6 = 'gray',            --#BEBEBE
color7 = 'antiquewhite4',    --#8B8378
color8 = 'dimgray',            --#696969
color9 = 'tomato',            --#FF6347

--#####
--## Load Lua for shading (optional)
--## Set the path to your script here.
--## (Currently not working in Conky 1.10.1-3 - Disabled)
--#
--# lua_load = '~/.conky/draw_bg.lua',
--# lua_draw_hook_pre = 'draw_bg',

--####
--## Load Lua for bargraphs (required)
--## Set the path to your script here.
--#
--## lua_load = '~/.conky/bargraph_small.lua',
--## lua_draw_hook_post = 'main_bars',

--####
--## Define the locations of Conkywx files (required)
--## Set the path to your .confs and templates here.
--##
--##
--##
--## Tips n' Tricks: type 'route' in CLI and check for interface names in the 'Iface' column .
--## Define the Wireless interface here - wlan0, wlan1, etc. (required)
--##
template7 = 'wlan0',
--##
--## Define the Wired interface name here - eth0, eth1, etc. (required)
--#
template8 = 'eno1',

} --end conky.config
conky.text = [[
```

```

I believe this *could* help you, This is a conky on Xubuntu 16.04. It's not VinDSL's conky but, it's a working conky that is working well.

[[img]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2017-02-0315-00-54.png[/img]](http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2017-02-0315-00-54.php)

And like          deadflowr said it could be saving an old session causing the blurring and having multiple conkys running at the same time.
I had that problem on Arch Linux with Xfce when I first installed it because the checkmark was there to save the session at logoff.

Here is the conky. Paramvir created it for Arch Linux but, with a slight mod. it's working on Xubuntu:

```
conky.config = {
	alignment = 'bottom_right',
	cpu_avg_samples = 3,
	net_avg_samples = 3,

	default_color = 'white',
	default_outline_color = 'black',
	default_shade_color = 'black',
	double_buffer = true,

	draw_borders = false,
	draw_graph_borders = false,
	draw_outline = false,
	draw_shades = false,

	gap_x = 7,
	gap_y = -108,
	--good gap_y = -83,
	--gap_y = -43,

	maximum_width = 315,
	minimum_width = 315,
	minimum_height = 500,

	no_buffers = true,
	override_utf8_locale = true,

	own_window = true,
	own_window_type = 'override',
	own_window_transparent = true,
	own_window_class = 'Conky',

	-- ARGB can be used for real transparency,
	-- note that a composite manager is required for real transparency
	-- own_window_argb_visual true

	-- Set the transparency alpha value from 0 to 255
	-- own_window_argb_value 250


	text_buffer_size = 512,
	total_run_times = 0,
	update_interval = 4,
	uppercase = false,
	use_spacer = 'right',
	-- top_name_verbose = false,
	-- top_name_width = 9,

	-- hddtemp_host = '127.0.0.1',
	-- hddtemp_port = '7634',


	use_xft = true,
	xftalpha = 0.8,
	font = ':size=9',

	color1 = 'Tan1',
	color2 = 'DimGray',
	color3 = 'White',
	color4 = 'DarkSlateGray',
	color5 = 'LightGray',
	color6 = 'yellow',
	color7 = 'darkgrey',

	short_units = true,

	lua_load = './lib/conkywx.lua',
	lua_startup_hook = 'startup',

	template6 = '/sys/class/block/sdc1/',
	template7 = '/sys/devices/platform/coretemp.0/hwmon/hwmon0/temp2_input',
	template8 = 'f',
	template9 = 'f71882fg.2560',

};

conky.text = [[
${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 20 -c 0x444444:0.4 -s NA,NA }
${font  Mono:size=9}${execpi 6000 my_cal=$(date +%-d); cal -h | sed 's/\<'$my_cal'\>/${color1}&${color3}/g'}
#${voffset -107}  ${font Arial:bold:size=18}$alignr${color3}Arch ${color1}LINUX${font}
${voffset -115}  ${font Arial:bold:size=16}$alignr${color3}Xubuntu ${color1}16.04${font}
${voffset 0}$alignr${font Arial:bold:size=36}${color5}${time %H:%M}${font}${color5}${font}
${voffset 0}${font Arial:bold:size=16}${color3}$alignr${time %a} ${color1}${time %d} ${color3}${time %Y}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# world time - present time in diff cities
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${voffset 14}${font :bold:size=10}${color1}WORLD TIME ${color4}${hr 2}${font}${color3}
${color3}UTC:${goto 36}${color1}${tztime UTC %H:%M-%d}${goto 100}${color3}BKK:${goto 133}${color1}${tztime Asia/Bangkok %H:%M-%d}${goto 198}${color3}TKO:${goto 230}${color1}${tztime Asia/Tokyo  %H:%M-%d}
${color3}LAX:${goto 36}${color1}${tztime America/Los_Angeles %H:%M-%d}${goto 100}${color3}HOU:${goto 133}${color1}${tztime America/Mexico_City %H:%M-%d}${goto 198}${color3}NYC:${goto 230}${color1}${tztime America/New_York  %H:%M-%d}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# SYSTEM info
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}SYSTEM ${color4} ${hr 2}${font}
${color3}$sysname ${kernel}${color1}x64${alignr}${color3}Pkgs: ${color1}${lua pacman_update}
${color3}Load Last 1 Min: ${loadavg 1} $alignr${loadgraph 9,100 00fff3 ff0029 scale -l}
${color3}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${color1}${alignr}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# PROCESSORS cpu and gpu info
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}PROCESSORS ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}CPU1 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu1}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,243 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}$alignr${cpugraph cpu1 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU2 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu2}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,257 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}$alignr${cpugraph cpu2 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU3 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu3}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,270 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}$alignr${cpugraph cpu3 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU4 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu4}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,284 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4}$alignr${cpugraph cpu4 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}MCH:${offset 2}${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'temp2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'temp2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${alignc -45} Fan: ${platform f71882fg.2560 fan 2} rpm ${alignr}Freq: ${freq_g cpu0}Ghz
${color3}GPU:${offset 2}${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}${alignc -30} Fan:${execi 60 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="fan.speed" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}%${alignr}Mem:${execi 60 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="memory.used" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}MB
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# NETWORK - internet traffic
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color Tan2}NETWORK ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}IP on ens33 $alignr ${addr ens33}
${color3}Dn:${offset 18}T:${totaldown ens33} ${color3}$alignr${downspeed ens33} ${alignr}${downspeedgraph ens33 12,100 006964 00fff3 scale -l}
${color3}Up:${offset 18}T:${totalup ens33} ${color3}$alignr${upspeed ens33} ${alignr}${upspeedgraph ens33 12,100 006964 00fff3 scale -l}
#${color3}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${alignr}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
${color3}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${alignr}127.0.0.1${font}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# MEMORY - ram
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}MEMORY ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}$mem $memperc% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 100,413 -w 100 -s 1 -f memperc -a NA -l 1 -x 70}${color1}${alignr}${memgraph 12,85 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# HDD space on hard drive partitions
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}HDD ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
#${color3}/system${color5} ${alignr 175}${fs_size /}${offset -4}B${alignr 170}${fs_used_perc /}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 145,444 -w 163 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a / -l 1 -x 70}
${color3}/system${color5} ${alignr 175}${fs_size /}${offset -4}B${alignr 170}${fs_used_perc /}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 160,441 -w 165 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a / -l 1 -x 70}
# ${color1}/home${color5}${alignr 145}${fs_used /home}${alignr 132}${fs_used_perc /home}%${lua main bargraph 190 517 115 1 fs_used_perc /home 1 70}
# ${color1}/video_music${color5}${alignr 145}${fs_used /home/cavsfan/media/video_music}${alignr 132}${fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/video_music}%${lua main bargraph 190 531 115 1 fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/video_music 1 70}
# ${color1}/image${color5}${alignr 145}${fs_used /home/cavsfan/media/image}${alignr 132}${fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/image}%${lua main bargraph 190 545 115 1 fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/image 1 70}
#${color3}/swap${color5}${alignr 160}${swap}${alignr 152}${swapperc}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 170,456 -w 125 -s 1 -f swapperc -a swapperc -l 1 -x 70}
#${color3}/swap${color5}${alignr 172}${swapmax}B${alignr 152}${swapperc}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 160,459 -w 130 -s 1 -f swapperc -a / -l 1 -x 70}
#${color3}/Fantom${color5}${if_mounted /media/Fantom} ${alignr 175}${fs_size /media/Fantom/}${offset -4}B${alignr 170}${fs_used_perc /media/Fantom/}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 145,458 -w 167 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a /media/Fantom/ -l 1 -x 70}${else} is not mounted${endif}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# HDD activity
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}HDD ACTIVITY${color4}${hr 2}#
${if_match "${lua if_running hddtemp}" != "1"}${lua pre_exec hddtemp -d /dev/sda /dev/sdb}${endif}
${font}${color3}/dev/sda ${alignr}Read: ${diskiograph_read /dev/sda 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t} Write: ${diskiograph_write /dev/sda 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t -l}
#${color3}/dev/sdb ${alignr}Read: ${diskiograph_read /dev/sdb 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t} Write: ${diskiograph_write /dev/sdb 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t -l}
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
# TOP PROCESSES top 3 running process sorted by cpu usage
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}TOP PROCESSES ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}${goto 5}Process Name ${alignr}${goto 130}PID ${goto 190}MEM% ${goto 255}CPU%
${top name 1}${goto 120}${top pid 1}${goto 185}${top mem 1}${goto 250}${top cpu 1}
${top name 2}${goto 120}${top pid 2}${goto 185}${top mem 2}${goto 250}${top cpu 2}
${top name 3}${goto 120}${top pid 3}${goto 185}${top mem 3}${goto 250}${top cpu 3}
${top name 4}${goto 120}${top pid 4}${goto 185}${top mem 4}${goto 250}${top cpu 4}
#${top name 5}${goto 120}${top pid 5}${goto 185}${top mem 5}${goto 250}${top cpu 5}
#${top name 6}${goto 120}${top pid 6}${goto 185}${top mem 6}${goto 250}${top cpu 6}
#${top name 7}${goto 120}${top pid 7}${goto 185}${top mem 7}${goto 250}${top cpu 7}
#${top name 8}${goto 120}${top pid 8}${goto 185}${top mem 8}${goto 250}${top cpu 8}
#${top name 9}${goto 120}${top pid 9}${goto 185}${top mem 9}${goto 250}${top cpu 9}
#${top name 10}${goto 120}${top pid 10}${goto 185}${top mem 10}${goto 250}${top cpu 10}

]];

```


BTW, it would be a good idea to setup an account on the site like I used to post pictures on. Seeing a big picture is much better that a little one.

Cheers and HTH

### Post by al14 on 2017-02-04
> **Cavsfan said:**
> I believe this *could* help you, This is a conky on Xubuntu 16.04. It's not VinDSL's conky but, it's a working conky that is working well.

[[IMG]http://en.zimagez.com/miniature/screenshot2017-02-0315-00-54.png[/IMG]]("http://en.zimagez.com/zimage/screenshot2017-02-0315-00-54.php")

And like          deadflowr said it could be saving an old session causing the blurring and having multiple conkys running at the same time.
I had that problem on Arch Linux with Xfce when I first installed it because the checkmark was there to save the session at logoff.

Here is the conky. Paramvir created it for Arch Linux but, with a slight mod. it's working on Xubuntu:

```
conky.config = {
    alignment = 'bottom_right',
    cpu_avg_samples = 3,
    net_avg_samples = 3,

    default_color = 'white',
    default_outline_color = 'black',
    default_shade_color = 'black',
    double_buffer = true,

    draw_borders = false,
    draw_graph_borders = false,
    draw_outline = false,
    draw_shades = false,

    gap_x = 7,
    gap_y = -108,
    --good gap_y = -83,
    --gap_y = -43,

    maximum_width = 315,
    minimum_width = 315,
    minimum_height = 500,

    no_buffers = true,
    override_utf8_locale = true,

    own_window = true,
    own_window_type = 'override',
    own_window_transparent = true,
    own_window_class = 'Conky',

    -- ARGB can be used for real transparency,
    -- note that a composite manager is required for real transparency
    -- own_window_argb_visual true

    -- Set the transparency alpha value from 0 to 255
    -- own_window_argb_value 250


    text_buffer_size = 512,
    total_run_times = 0,
    update_interval = 4,
    uppercase = false,
    use_spacer = 'right',
    -- top_name_verbose = false,
    -- top_name_width = 9,

    -- hddtemp_host = '127.0.0.1',
    -- hddtemp_port = '7634',


    use_xft = true,
    xftalpha = 0.8,
    font = ':size=9',

    color1 = 'Tan1',
    color2 = 'DimGray',
    color3 = 'White',
    color4 = 'DarkSlateGray',
    color5 = 'LightGray',
    color6 = 'yellow',
    color7 = 'darkgrey',

    short_units = true,

    lua_load = './lib/conkywx.lua',
    lua_startup_hook = 'startup',

    template6 = '/sys/class/block/sdc1/',
    template7 = '/sys/devices/platform/coretemp.0/hwmon/hwmon0/temp2_input',
    template8 = 'f',
    template9 = 'f71882fg.2560',

};

conky.text = [[
${voffset -9}${lua main -n background -r 20 -c 0x444444:0.4 -s NA,NA }
${font  Mono:size=9}${execpi 6000 my_cal=$(date +%-d); cal -h | sed 's/\<'$my_cal'\>/${color1}&${color3}/g'}
#${voffset -107}  ${font Arial:bold:size=18}$alignr${color3}Arch ${color1}LINUX${font}
${voffset -115}  ${font Arial:bold:size=16}$alignr${color3}Xubuntu ${color1}16.04${font}
${voffset 0}$alignr${font Arial:bold:size=36}${color5}${time %H:%M}${font}${color5}${font}
${voffset 0}${font Arial:bold:size=16}${color3}$alignr${time %a} ${color1}${time %d} ${color3}${time %Y}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# world time - present time in diff cities
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${voffset 14}${font :bold:size=10}${color1}WORLD TIME ${color4}${hr 2}${font}${color3}
${color3}UTC:${goto 36}${color1}${tztime UTC %H:%M-%d}${goto 100}${color3}BKK:${goto 133}${color1}${tztime Asia/Bangkok %H:%M-%d}${goto 198}${color3}TKO:${goto 230}${color1}${tztime Asia/Tokyo  %H:%M-%d}
${color3}LAX:${goto 36}${color1}${tztime America/Los_Angeles %H:%M-%d}${goto 100}${color3}HOU:${goto 133}${color1}${tztime America/Mexico_City %H:%M-%d}${goto 198}${color3}NYC:${goto 230}${color1}${tztime America/New_York  %H:%M-%d}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# SYSTEM info
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}SYSTEM ${color4} ${hr 2}${font}
${color3}$sysname ${kernel}${color1}x64${alignr}${color3}Pkgs: ${color1}${lua pacman_update}
${color3}Load Last 1 Min: ${loadavg 1} $alignr${loadgraph 9,100 00fff3 ff0029 scale -l}
${color3}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Product Name" | sed -e 's/.*: /nVidia /'}${color1}${alignr}${exec nvidia-smi -q | grep "Driver Version" | awk '{print $4}'}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# PROCESSORS cpu and gpu info
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}PROCESSORS ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}CPU1 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 0' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu1}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,243 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1}$alignr${cpugraph cpu1 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU2 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 1' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu2}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,257 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2}$alignr${cpugraph cpu2 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU3 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu3}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,270 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3}$alignr${cpugraph cpu3 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}CPU4 ${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'Core 3' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif} ${cpu cpu4}% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 130,284 -w 80 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4}$alignr${cpugraph cpu4 12,82 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
${color3}MCH:${offset 2}${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 sensors | grep 'temp2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${else}${execi 1 sensors -f | grep 'temp2' | sed 's/.*+\(.*\)  \(.*\)/\1/'}${endif}${alignc -45} Fan: ${platform f71882fg.2560 fan 2} rpm ${alignr}Freq: ${freq_g cpu0}Ghz
${color3}GPU:${offset 2}${if_match "${template8}" == "c"}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*1)}}'}°C${else}${execi 1 nvidia-settings -q GPUCoreTemp | awk '{if (NR==2) {print ($4*9)/5+32}}'}°F${endif}${alignc -30} Fan:${execi 60 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="fan.speed" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}%${alignr}Mem:${execi 60 nvidia-smi --query-gpu="memory.used" --format=csv,noheader,nounits}MB
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# NETWORK - internet traffic
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color Tan2}NETWORK ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}IP on ens33 $alignr ${addr ens33}
${color3}Dn:${offset 18}T:${totaldown ens33} ${color3}$alignr${downspeed ens33} ${alignr}${downspeedgraph ens33 12,100 006964 00fff3 scale -l}
${color3}Up:${offset 18}T:${totalup ens33} ${color3}$alignr${upspeed ens33} ${alignr}${upspeedgraph ens33 12,100 006964 00fff3 scale -l}
#${color3}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${alignr}${execi 1800 wget -q -O - checkip.dyndns.org | sed -e 's/[^[:digit:]\|.]//g'}${font}
${color3}Public${offset 7}IP${goto 121}${alignr}127.0.0.1${font}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# MEMORY - ram
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}MEMORY ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}$mem $memperc% ${lua main -n bargraph -p 100,413 -w 100 -s 1 -f memperc -a NA -l 1 -x 70}${color1}${alignr}${memgraph 12,85 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# HDD space on hard drive partitions
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}HDD ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
#${color3}/system${color5} ${alignr 175}${fs_size /}${offset -4}B${alignr 170}${fs_used_perc /}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 145,444 -w 163 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a / -l 1 -x 70}
${color3}/system${color5} ${alignr 175}${fs_size /}${offset -4}B${alignr 170}${fs_used_perc /}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 160,441 -w 165 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a / -l 1 -x 70}
# ${color1}/home${color5}${alignr 145}${fs_used /home}${alignr 132}${fs_used_perc /home}%${lua main bargraph 190 517 115 1 fs_used_perc /home 1 70}
# ${color1}/video_music${color5}${alignr 145}${fs_used /home/cavsfan/media/video_music}${alignr 132}${fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/video_music}%${lua main bargraph 190 531 115 1 fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/video_music 1 70}
# ${color1}/image${color5}${alignr 145}${fs_used /home/cavsfan/media/image}${alignr 132}${fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/image}%${lua main bargraph 190 545 115 1 fs_used_perc /home/cavsfan/media/image 1 70}
#${color3}/swap${color5}${alignr 160}${swap}${alignr 152}${swapperc}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 170,456 -w 125 -s 1 -f swapperc -a swapperc -l 1 -x 70}
#${color3}/swap${color5}${alignr 172}${swapmax}B${alignr 152}${swapperc}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 160,459 -w 130 -s 1 -f swapperc -a / -l 1 -x 70}
#${color3}/Fantom${color5}${if_mounted /media/Fantom} ${alignr 175}${fs_size /media/Fantom/}${offset -4}B${alignr 170}${fs_used_perc /media/Fantom/}%${lua main -n bargraph -p 145,458 -w 167 -s 1 -f fs_used_perc -a /media/Fantom/ -l 1 -x 70}${else} is not mounted${endif}
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# HDD activity
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}HDD ACTIVITY${color4}${hr 2}#
${if_match "${lua if_running hddtemp}" != "1"}${lua pre_exec hddtemp -d /dev/sda /dev/sdb}${endif}
${font}${color3}/dev/sda ${alignr}Read: ${diskiograph_read /dev/sda 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t} Write: ${diskiograph_write /dev/sda 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t -l}
#${color3}/dev/sdb ${alignr}Read: ${diskiograph_read /dev/sdb 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t} Write: ${diskiograph_write /dev/sdb 12,70 00fff3 ff0029 scale -t -l}
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
# TOP PROCESSES top 3 running process sorted by cpu usage
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${font :bold:size=10}${color1}TOP PROCESSES ${color4}${hr 2}${font}
${color3}${goto 5}Process Name ${alignr}${goto 130}PID ${goto 190}MEM% ${goto 255}CPU%
${top name 1}${goto 120}${top pid 1}${goto 185}${top mem 1}${goto 250}${top cpu 1}
${top name 2}${goto 120}${top pid 2}${goto 185}${top mem 2}${goto 250}${top cpu 2}
${top name 3}${goto 120}${top pid 3}${goto 185}${top mem 3}${goto 250}${top cpu 3}
${top name 4}${goto 120}${top pid 4}${goto 185}${top mem 4}${goto 250}${top cpu 4}
#${top name 5}${goto 120}${top pid 5}${goto 185}${top mem 5}${goto 250}${top cpu 5}

]];
