Looking good Tommy!

I've checked the file names on the scroller weather but, right now I'm trying to figure out Grub on a UEFI SSD.
I've got Grub installed on Arch and having been trying to figure out how to customize it.

Even installing Grub on another system is a huge challenge. My son gave me this beast and all the drives were formatted legacy MBR partitioning, probably because it came with 2 2TB RAID drives that were SATA.
So, he added a 480 GB SSD (which he has given me) and a 1TB SSD which he shall keep :P and none of them were formatted like they should be.

On Arch for example, instead of the simple
 ```
sudo grub-install /dev/sda

``` it has become 
```
sudo grub-install --target=x86_64-efi --efi-directory=/boot/EFI/Arch_grub --bootloader-id=Arch_grub
```

So, I plug along. 
Surely you have seen my new 4K 2560x1440 monitor conkys on Arch? Oh no I guess I forgot to post it after I finished resizing it.

### Post by NM5TF on 2018-05-16
look at this file.../usr/share/conkywx/lib/thermometer.lua

here is a copy of my working thermometer...compare it to his

```
--[[
    Paramvir Likhari - conkywx 2011 - 2016 - GPL3
    bargraph library for use with conkywx lua implementation
--]]

local w = {}

local PI        = 3.14159265358979323846
local TORADIANS = 3.14159265358979323846/180
local sprintf   = string.format
local tconcat   = table.concat
local tonum     = tonumber
local supper    = string.upper

local function ScriptPath()
    local f1=io.popen('pwd')
    if f1 then
        local rinfo = f1:read("*a")
        f1:close()
        return rinfo:match( "^%s*(.-)%s*$" )
    end
end
package.path = tconcat({package.path,";",ScriptPath(),"/lib/?.lua;"})

local FC = require("luafunc")

local wxsplit     = FC.wxsplit
local to_rgba     = FC.to_rgba
local get_points  = FC.get_points
local do_set_font = FC.set_font
local make_txt    = FC.make_txt

local function set_font(p)
    do_set_font(p)
end


function w.thermometer(p)
    local cr = p.cr
    local th = {}
    th.units = p.u or print("Thermometer Units missing")
    th.scale = p.s or 1
    th.temp  = p.t or nil
    th.label = p.l or nil
    th.scale = tonum(th.scale)

    th.scale_color        = p.c or '0xcd950c:1'
    th.scale_lines        = p.d or '0xffd700:0.5'
    th.scale_text_colour  = p.e or '0x4169e1:1'
    th.temp_readout       = p.f or '0x4169e1:1'

    th.scale_color        = wxsplit(th.scale_color,":")
    th.scale_lines        = wxsplit(th.scale_lines,":")
    th.scale_text_colour  = wxsplit(th.scale_text_colour,":")
    th.temp_readout       = wxsplit(th.temp_readout,":")

    if not th.temp then
        th.temp = 0
    else
        local wxx1, wxx2, wxx3 = th.temp:find('(%-?[%d%.]+)')
        th.temp  = tonum(wxx3)
        --th.temp = 100
    end

    --local height,mid,th.UnitFacT,th.UnitFacM,num,scale_factor,scale_spacer
    th.ThermVals = {
        F = {
            height       = 150,
            mid          = 74,
            TFactor      = 33,
            ScFactor     = 137 / 160,
            scale_factor = 207,
            scale_spacer = 10,
            num          = {-20,tconcat({"0°",th.units}),20,40,60,80,100,120,140},
        },
        C = {
            height       = 160,
            mid          = 21,
            TFactor      = 30,
            ScFactor     = 154 / 90,
            scale_factor = 217,
            scale_spacer = 0,
            num          = {-30,-20,-10,tconcat({"0°",th.units}),10,20,30,40,50,60},
        },
    }

    th.UnitFacT = (th.temp + th.ThermVals[th.units].TFactor) * th.ThermVals[th.units].ScFactor
    th.UnitFacM = (th.ThermVals[th.units].mid  + th.ThermVals[th.units].TFactor) * th.ThermVals[th.units].ScFactor

    if th.scale > 10 then th.scale = th.scale / th.ThermVals[th.units].scale_factor end
    th.mx    = p.lwx*(1/tonum(th.scale))+30
    th.my    = p.lwy*(1/tonum(th.scale))-25

    th.fsize=10
    cairo_scale(cr,th.scale,th.scale)
    cairo_set_line_width(cr,1.5)
    cairo_set_source_rgba(cr, to_rgba(th.scale_color))
    --graphics outer
    --bottom circle
    th.r_outer    = 25
    th.lang_outer = 335
    th.rang_outer = 0+(360-th.lang_outer)
    th.h_outer    = th.ThermVals[th.units].height-4--maybe make this a percentage?###########
    cairo_arc(cr,th.mx,th.my,th.r_outer,TORADIANS*(th.rang_outer-90),TORADIANS*(th.lang_outer-90))
    --coordinates,left line
    --local th.lxo, th.lyo = get_points(startpt,degval,degfact,sizefact,opsign)
    th.lxo, th.lyo = get_points(th.lang_outer,0,0,th.r_outer)
    --coordinates,left line
    th.rxo, th.ryo = get_points(th.rang_outer,0,0,th.r_outer)
    --top circle
    cairo_arc(cr,th.mx+th.lxo+((th.rxo-th.lxo)*0.5),th.my+th.lyo-th.h_outer,(th.rxo-th.lxo)*0.5,TORADIANS*180,0)
    --right line
    cairo_line_to(cr,th.mx+th.lxo+((th.rxo-th.lxo)),th.my+th.lyo)
    cairo_stroke(cr)
    ----------------------------------------------
    --graphics inner
    th.sr,th.sg,th.sb,th.sa = 0,1,1,1
    th.mr,th.mg,th.mb,th.ma = 1,1,0,1
    th.fr,th.fg,th.fb,th.fa = 1,0,0,1
    if th.UnitFacT<(th.UnitFacM) then
        th.colr = ((th.mr-th.sr)*(th.UnitFacT/th.UnitFacM))+th.sr
        th.colg = ((th.mg-th.sg)*(th.UnitFacT/th.UnitFacM))+th.sg
        th.colb = ((th.mb-th.sb)*(th.UnitFacT/th.UnitFacM))+th.sb
        th.cola = ((th.ma-th.sa)*(th.UnitFacT/th.UnitFacM))+th.sa
    elseif th.UnitFacT>=(th.UnitFacM) then
        th.colr = ((th.fr-th.mr)*((th.UnitFacT-th.UnitFacM)/(th.ThermVals[th.units].height-th.UnitFacM)))+th.mr
        th.colg = ((th.fg-th.mg)*((th.UnitFacT-th.UnitFacM)/(th.ThermVals[th.units].height-th.UnitFacM)))+th.mg
        th.colb = ((th.fb-th.mb)*((th.UnitFacT-th.UnitFacM)/(th.ThermVals[th.units].height-th.UnitFacM)))+th.mb
        th.cola = ((th.fa-th.ma)*((th.UnitFacT-th.UnitFacM)/(th.ThermVals[th.units].height-th.UnitFacM)))+th.ma
    end
    cairo_set_source_rgba(cr,th.colr,th.colg,th.colb,th.cola)
    --bottom circle
    th.r_inner    = th.r_outer-6
    th.lang_inner = th.lang_outer+9
    th.rang_inner = 0+(360-th.lang_inner)
    th.h_inner    = th.UnitFacT
    cairo_arc(cr,th.mx,th.my,th.r_inner,TORADIANS*(th.rang_inner-90),TORADIANS*(th.lang_inner-90))
    --coordinates,left line
    th.lxi, th.lyi = get_points(th.lang_inner,0,0,th.r_inner)
    cairo_line_to(cr,th.mx+th.lxi,th.my+th.lyi-th.h_inner)
    th.rxi, th.ryi = get_points(th.rang_inner,0,0,th.r_inner)
    cairo_arc(cr,th.mx+th.lxi+((th.rxi-th.lxi)*0.5),th.my+th.lyi-th.h_inner,(th.rxi-th.lxi)*0.5,TORADIANS*180,0)
    --right line
    cairo_line_to(cr,th.mx+th.lxi+((th.rxi-th.lxi)),th.my+th.lyi)
    cairo_fill(cr)

    ----------------------------
    cairo_set_line_width(cr,1)
    cairo_set_source_rgba(cr, to_rgba(th.scale_lines))
    th.grad = 8.5
    --th.lnn = (#th.ThermVals[th.units].num*2)-1
    th.lnx  = th.mx+th.lxo
    th.lnw  = (th.rxo-th.lxo)
    for i=1,(#th.ThermVals[th.units].num*2)-1 do
        lny=th.my-th.r_inner-(th.ThermVals[th.units].scale_spacer+((i-1)*th.grad))-((th.rxi-th.lxi)/2)
        if i==(#th.ThermVals[th.units].num*2)-1 then
            th.lnx=th.lnx+2
            th.lnw=th.lnw-4
        end
        cairo_move_to(cr,th.lnx,lny)
        cairo_rel_line_to(cr,th.lnw,0)
        cairo_stroke(cr)
    end
    --numbers
    cairo_set_source_rgba(cr, to_rgba(th.scale_text_colour))
    --cairo_select_font_face(cr, font, CAIRO_FONT_SLANT_NORMAL, CAIRO_FONT_WEIGHT_NORMAL);
    --cairo_set_font_size(cr, th.fsize)
    cairo_save(cr)
    set_font({ cr, tconcat({'Mono:',th.fsize,':normal'}) })
    cairo_restore(cr)
    th.grad=17
    --th.lnn = #th.ThermVals[th.units].num
    th.lnx=th.mx+th.lxo+(th.rxo-th.lxo)+4
    for i=1,#th.ThermVals[th.units].num do
        lny=th.my-th.r_inner-(th.ThermVals[th.units].scale_spacer+((i-1)*th.grad))-((th.rxi-th.lxi)/2)+(th.fsize/3)
        cairo_move_to(cr,th.lnx,lny)
        cairo_show_text(cr,th.ThermVals[th.units].num[i])
        cairo_stroke(cr)
    end

    th.fsize=12.5
    cairo_save(cr)
    set_font({ cr, tconcat({'Mono:',th.fsize,':bold'})})
    --local lbx=th.mx+th.lxo-5
    --local lby=th.my-th.r_inner-10-((th.rxi-th.lxi)/2)
    cairo_move_to(cr, th.mx+th.lxo-5, th.my-th.r_inner-10-((th.rxi-th.lxi)/2))
    cairo_rotate(cr,TORADIANS*(-90))
    cairo_show_text(cr,th.label)
    cairo_stroke(cr)
    cairo_rotate(cr,TORADIANS*(90))
    cairo_restore(cr)
    --temperature readout
    cairo_set_source_rgba(cr, to_rgba(th.temp_readout))
    --th.text=tconcat({th.temp,"°",th.units})
    cairo_save(cr)
    make_txt({ cr = cr, text=tconcat({th.temp,"°",th.units}), px=th.mx, py=th.my-3, align='bc' })
    cairo_restore(cr)
    ------------------------------------
    cairo_scale(cr,1/th.scale,1/th.scale)
end

return w

```

### Post by Cavsfan on 2018-05-16
Good deal! The files in your ~/config/conkywx folder should be the only ones you should be editing etc. and they are working good right?

In a nutshell conky 1.10 conkyrc files are like this.
The top part uses double dashes as the comment identifier:
```
conky.config = {
    alignment = 'bottom_right',
    --alignment = 'top_right',
    default_color = 'white',
    double_buffer = true,
        blah, blah, blah
};
```

Then the bottom section is like this.
With the # as the comment character.
```
conky.text = [[
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# Music info
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
${texecpi 3 if [ -s ${template1} ]; then ${template2}; fi }#
${voffset 0}${lua main -n background -r 1 -c 0x444444:0.3 -s NA,NA }#
#### below lua if_running
blah, blah, blah
]];


```

I feel I've spent nearly a year of my life messing with conkys now. ](*,)
So, I guess everyone does maybe a tad faster and maybe a tad slower.

### Post by foodoo on 2018-05-29
Hi Guys,

it looks like this now :
${lua main -n daynight -s 150  -p 80, 1200 }

but sadly still not working.
it will display in the correct place by using a space after the comma like this -p 80, 1200
but if I don't have that space after the comma it displays at the top of my screen and you can't move anywhere!
I have noticed there is an extra space after 150 in your example like 150  -p 80, 1200
but that is not making any difference with or without !

The thermometer, compass, barometer and anemometer all have to have that space after the comma to get them to display
in their correct places, without the space after the comma they all land at the top of my screen with no way to move them around!

The needle on the anemometer stays at zero even though it's been windy and gusty, I have tried it with and without the settings below.
I can get the bezel ring to appear on the barometer by using -j 2  but not the anemometer ! 
The bargraphs don't need that extra space after the comma and work just fine :P 

${lua main -n barometer -p 10, 10 -s 300 -r [BR] -u [UU] -j 0 -c 0x00ff00:0 -e 0xFFA54F:1 }${font}

${lua main -n anemometer -p 10, 310 -s 300 -r [WS] -v [WG] -u [UU] -w 0 -k km -j 0 -c 0x00ff00:0 -e 0xFFA54F:1 }${font}

${lua main -n showcond -p 192, 670 -s 110 -w wi -f [BF] -d [WDD] -e [WS] -o 0x00ff00:0 -b 0x00ff00:0 -r 0xFFA54F:1 -a 3 }${font}

${lua main -n thermometer -p 5, 890 -t [PT] -s 1 -c 0xFFA54F:1 -d 0xFFD700:0.2 -e 0xFDFCFC:1 -u [UU] }${font}


I'm going to install some other Ubuntu versions like kubuntu or lubuntu 16.04
just like I did with mine and see if its the same problem !



Many thanks Sean
P.S That big hole at the bottom of my conky is where mrpeachy's sun moon plotter normally appears but sadly it starts up then shuts down
but it worked correctly using conky-all 1.9 version ! I'm quite sad about that too !

### Post by foodoo on 2018-05-30
Hi Paramvir,

Thanks for your help.

I had to change to this to get it to display something:

> conky.config = {

	
	alignment = 'top_middle',


	default_color = 'white',
	double_buffer = true,
	draw_borders = false,
	draw_graph_borders = false,
	draw_outline = false,
	draw_shades = false,


	no_buffers = true,


	own_window = true,
	own_window_transparent = true,
	own_window_type = 'normal',
	own_window_class = 'Conky',
	own_window_hints = 'below,sticky,undecorated,skip_taskbar,skip_pager',


	own_window_argb_visual = true,
	own_window_argb_value = 100,


	maximum_width = 631,
	minimum_width = 631,
	minimum_height = 1029,
	
	gap_x = -1,
	gap_y = 50,


	use_xft = true,
	xftalpha = 5,
	font = 'DejaVu Sans:size=10:style=bold',
	text_buffer_size = 2000,
	update_interval = 10,


	color0 = 'White',
	color1 = 'Tan1',


	lua_load = './lib/conkywx.lua',
	lua_startup_hook = 'startup',


-------------------------------------------------------------
-- location of image file - conkywx tmp directory for the used conkywx config file
-- IMPORTANT do not comment this line - even if you do not use it
-------------------------------------------------------------
	template1 = '/tmp/wx-foo-conkywx-05.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gsi_image.gif',
	template2 = '/tmp/wx-foo-conkywx-05.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gwsi_image.gif',
	template3 = '/tmp/wx-foo-conkywx-05.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/gisi_image.jpg',
	template4 = '/tmp/wx-foo-conkywx-05-test.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/seismic',
	template5 = '/tmp/wx-foo-conkywx-05.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/wxalert',
	template6 = './examples/alert-smscrollrc',
	template7 = '/tmp/surface',


};


conky.text = [[
# ${if_existing ${template7}}#
${texecpi 11 if [ -s ${template5} ]; then conky -c ${template6}; fi }#
${if_existing ${template1}} ${image ${template1} -p 0, 0 -s 200x150}${else}No CIMSS Surface Analysis Image${endif}
# ${if_existing ${template3}} ${image ${template3} -p 205, 0 -s 200x150}${else}${goto 510}No Intellicast Surface Analysis Image${endif}
${if_existing ${template2}} ${image ${template2} -p 205, 0 -s 200x150}${else}${goto 510}No Intellicast Surface Analysis Image${endif}
# ${if_existing ${template2}} ${image ${template2} -p 0, 155 -s 200x125}${else}${goto 310}No Wund Surface Analysis Image${endif}
# ####------------Daynight Example ------------------------------------
# ${pid_nice ${top pid 1}}
# ${color0}${head /tmp/mag 2}#
# ==========================
# ${color0}${head /tmp/mag 2}#
# ==========================
# ${color0}${lines /tmp/mag}
# ${color0}${lines /tmp/preloadlist.txt}
# ==========================
# ${color0}${head /tmp/mag 3}
# ==========================
# ${color0}${head /tmp/mag 1}
##
# ${lua main -n daynight -s 600 -p 0,150 }
# ${voffset 200}${rss ${template7}}
# ${voffset 200}${template7}
##
# ${lua main -n daynight -s 400  -p 180,200  }
# ${lua main -n daynight -s 200 -p 410,0 }
${lua main -n daynight -s 170 -p 410, 0 }
###Tide testing
# ${voffset 150}${goto 10}${color3}Tides:${lua main -n scroller -b 2 -c 0xffff00:1  -l 40 -s 15 -o ${template7} }#
# ${lua main -n calendar -p 20,200 -f 20 -r b -w 2 }#
#
####------------- Earth Quake / Seismic data Example ----------------
#### to align from bottom - set -b to 3 with or without colour
#### to align from top - set -b to 4 and what ever colour - as required ...
#### the top value is taken from conky config gap_y setting
#### The Height of the window is important
#### - see above for "minimum_size 900 1050" - 1050 is height of window
#### change the number of records in config file - the widget will auto position itself.
${if_existing ${template4}}#
${lua main -n scroller -b 3::0x333333:0.6 -j Wx Seismic Table:DejaVu Sans:14:1 -k 3 -f 11 -l 40 -s 15 -p 12,1 -o ${template4} }#
# ${endif}#
${endif}
]];

 I didn't touch anything else and it was the only conky being called !

${lua main -n daynight -p 80, 1200 -s 150 }${font}
${lua main -n showcond -p 77, 820 -s 40 -w ci -i [D2I] }${font}
${lua main -n showcond -p 134, 820 -s 40 -w ci -i [D3I] }${font}
${lua main -n showcond -p 194, 820 -s 40 -w ci -i [D4I] }${font}
${lua main -n showcond -p 254, 820 -s 40 -w ci -i [D5I] }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 93, 163 -w 185 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu1 }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 93, 180 -w 185 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu2 }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 93, 197 -w 185 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu3 }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 93, 214 -w 185 -s 3 -f cpu -a cpu4 }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20, 395 -w 280 -s 3 -f memperc -a NA -l 1 -x 70 }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20, 442 -w 280 -s 3 -f fs_used_perc -a /boot -l 1 -x 70 }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20, 475 -w 280 -s 3 -f fs_used_perc -a /home/foo -l 1 -x 70 }${font}
${lua main -n bargraph -p 20, 507 -w 280 -s 3 -f fs_used_perc -a /media/foo/CORSAIR/ -l 1 -x 70 }${font}
${voffset -70}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color5}Sunrise: ${color5}${offset 2}${alignr}[SR]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color5}Sunset: ${color5}${offset 2}${alignr}[SS]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}Moonrise: ${color0}${offset 2}${alignr}[MR]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}Moonset: ${color0}${offset 2}${alignr}[MS]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}New Moon: ${color0}${offset 2}${alignr}[NM]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}First Quarter: ${color0}${offset 2}${alignr}[MFQ]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}Full Moon: ${color0}${offset 2}${alignr}[FM]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}Last Quarter: ${color0}${offset 2}${alignr}[MLQ]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}Illuminated: ${offset 2}${color0}${alignr}[MV]${font Arrows:size=12}${color0} [FCTMIF]${font}
${voffset -5}${goto 8}${font DroidSans:size=8}${color0}Moon Phase: ${color0}${offset 2}${alignr}[MP]${font}
${voffset -5}${font RadioSpace:bold:size=8}${color4}LAST UPDATE${offset 8}${color4}${voffset -2}${hr 2}${font}
${voffset -5}${alignc }${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color5}${color1}[LU]${font}
```

Sean, what we all did was install Xfce DE only. It is much like Xubuntu. 
Plus we do not use any DM, we just login to TTY1 and it kicks off the startup process.

If you'd like help with any of this PM me or Param, or better yet you should have our email addresses from testing Conkywx.
Param is the most knowledgeable  but, I can tell you how to do the 2 things I mentioned and you want to add xfce4 and xfce4-goodies after you got the base system installed on Arch.
Don't mess with Gnome or any other DE. I first installed Gnome and everything was huge. I couldn't figure out how to make it smaller and then Param mentioned Xfce.

I installed Arch EFI on this newer PC I have with a 440GB SSD. That plus 3 Xubuntus and 1 Debian Sid distro.

[https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/xfce](https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/xfce)

### Post by NM5TF on 2018-06-25
@Mister-e2....

open [COLOR="#FF0000"]/usr/share/conkywx/lib/conkywx.lua [/COLOR]and look at the 1st 2 data entries called "require"...
these are required to run the lua functions in conkywx....

it should look similiar to this:  

```

--[[
	Paramvir Likhari - conkywx 2011 - 2018 - GPL3
	common library for use with conkywx lua implementation
--]]

require 'cairo'
require 'imlib2'
```

note: my testing version may not look exactly like yours, but should be similiar

the other displays, scrolling wx & seismic also depend on a functioning lua app...fix
your surface image problem & the others should work...

BTW...the[COLOR="#FF0000"] preferred[/COLOR] way to start conkywx is to use  [COLOR="#FF0000"]/usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh[/COLOR]

you never stated what templates you are using....you said that your wx is working, so the config file is ok for your location...did you enable seismic
function in the config ???

tommy

### Post by paramvir on 2018-12-07
Looking good Tommy ;-) 

By the way this version has some new things as usual - the way the data is displayed on the conky window is using a new widget **textformat** . This is LUA driven. 

IMO :p the way that conky is displaying data is wee bit out of date and we need to be able to format data rather smartly. It is perhaps not the ideal solution - but the data we want to display is mostly in 'row, column' format. Keeping that as focus - you start to get a picture ion your mind. The result is that data is uniformly formatted and you do not need spacing formats like goto, voffset, offset etc. This saves the perfectionist in us from using graphical rulers to align pesky text and then land up with exotic errors or missing text as the text buffers has gone hitch hiking or something.

There has been some discussion on the conky github re integration of lua 5.3 with current conky - which is stuck with lua 5.1 - we have had a clear understanding and that perhaps may happen in a future version. The performance gains are not expressible in a written form - they have to be experienced. I have done the spade work on Arch Linux - and is available for all on the AUR thing.

If you are using conkywx on a low spec configuration - then use as few options as possible such as rss, scrollable views etc.

There has been a fair bit of concern regarding air quality - so have incorporated the WAQI data - we can sing that old song - 'Killing me softly' in our case with the air huahahahahaha 

Waiting to hear some experience

cheers ):P

---

### Post by paramvir on 2018-12-07
Oh and yes - I would like to THANK all the gentlemen who during times of unrealistic duress were not as gentle while testing the program to make it as workable as it is today. :)

Thank you

### Post by damnitdan on 2019-02-12
Thanks very much for the newest iteration of conkywx.  I was able to get it set up (to my liking) but I still have some issue(s) with this release.
1) I believe there's an issue with the default paths defiined in conky-restart.sh.
2) After "fixing" the the paths, weather data is displayed.  But, weather data does not get displayed after a system restart/pwr cycle. The only way (I've found) to get things working again is to remove & re-install conkywx and fix everything once again.

I'm running Manjaro with openbox wm.
```

&#9484;&#9472; 13:33:49 (obxdan) » < ~ >
&#9492;&#9472;&#9472;&#9508; yay -Q conky-cairo  tolua++_5.3 conky conkywx linux
conky-cairo 1.11.2-1
tolua++_5.3 1.0.93-1
conky-cairo 1.11.2-1
conkywx 181207-1
linux419 4.19.20-1
```
I launched from the cmd-line and got on screen text: "No CIMSS Surface Analysis Image" and "No Intellicast Su"  and the following messages in the terminal:
```

&#9492;&#9472; 16:57:14 (obxdan) » < ~ >
&#9492;&#9472;&#9472;&#9508; /usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh 
&#9484;&#9472; 17:20:18 (obxdan) » < ~ >
&#9492;&#9472;&#9472;&#9508; conky: cannot open /home/obxdan/.config/conkywx/examples181207/examples/big-luamix-gauge-conkyrc: No such file or directory
conky: cannot open /home/obxdan/.config/conkywx/examples181207/examples/seismicrc: No such file or directory
conky: cannot open /home/obxdan/.config/conkywx/examples181207/conkywx-weather-wrings-systemrc: No such file or directory
conky: desktop window (710) is root window
conky: window type - override
conky: drawing to created window (0x3e00001)
conky: drawing to double buffer
```
In the default  /usr/share/conky-restart.sh I modified the following:
Commented surface-imagerc & seismicrc lines
mv "$WX_DIR/**examples**/big-luamix-gauge-conkyrc" "$WX_DIR/big-luamix-gauge-conkyrc"
mv "$WX_DIR/conkywx-weather-**wrings**-systemrc" "$WX_DIR/conkywx-weather-**rings**-systemrc"
That got things working (with big-luamix-gauge & conkywx-weather-rings)

My config uses a modified short-conkyrc and  a modified vindsl-org-conkyrc (as linked below)
[https://scrot.moe/image/amiRL]("https://scrot.moe/image/amiRL")

Here's an example when weather data does not get displayed (after restart/pwr-up):
Note: vindsl is displayed just no weather data
```

&#9484;&#9472; 16:54:00 (obxdan) » < ~ >
&#9492;&#9472;&#9472;&#9508; /usr/share/conkywx/run-conkywx.sh 
&#9484;&#9472; 16:54:15 (obxdan) » < ~ >
&#9492;&#9472;&#9472;&#9508; conky: desktop window (710) is root window
conky: window type - normal
conky: drawing to created window (0x2e00001)
conky: drawing to double buffer

conky: drawing to created window (0x3000001)
conky: drawing to double buffer
conky: forked to background, pid is 4602

  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed

Downloading main data file...
100    13  100    13    0     0     23      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--    23

Weather update download completed.

### Post by NM5TF on 2019-02-12
@damnitdan...

we are aware of the issues, we have verified them during testing, and Paramvir is working on a fix....

to get the debug info do this....

open a terminal and key in [COLOR="#FF0000"]conkywx[/COLOR]....select option [COLOR="#FF0000"]4[/COLOR]
and select [COLOR="#FF0000"]0[/COLOR] to get the correct config file....[COLOR="#FF0000"]restart conkywx[/COLOR].......

it will generate a  [COLOR="#FF0000"]wxdebug.log[/COLOR] debug file in directory [COLOR="#FF0000"]/tmp/wx-[COLOR="#00FF00"]your-username[/COLOR]-conkywx-02.conf-pChA_r_27u0-m39/[/COLOR]

email this file to the dev at [COLOR="#40E0D0"]plikhari@gmail.com[/COLOR]

tommy

2) After "fixing" the paths, weather data is displayed. But, weather data does not get displayed after a system restart/pwr cycle. The only way (I've found) to get things working again is to remove & re-install conkywx and fix everything once again.

### Post by Cavsfan on 2019-02-20
> **damnitdan said:**
> I solved item 2 above. I was using a conkywx.config file from the previous release! (Duh)

There still remains the issue of the default paths in conky-restart.sh.

Looking pretty good!

Yes, that is usually the problem when you get a new release. You have to remember to change the release number to the new one.
I use Catfish. It's on Thunar file manager that comes with Xfce. It's under File > Find in this folder. You have to remember to click the gear and put a check mark besides **Search File Contents **so it will find it. 

[ATTACH=CONFIG]282598[/ATTACH]

Then after I have copied all of the files I use to the new directory I use ^ to find any file with the old version number and change every occurrence to the new one.
I believe the conkys that comes with the version are setup for a 1920x1080 monitor. I recently got a 4k monitor 3840x2160 and I refuse to repeat the process of enlarging it pixel by pixel.

```
Plus my startup file in my home directory:
#!/bin/sh
sleep 10
/home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/examples190121/conky-restart.sh

```

Catfish comes pre-installed on Xubuntu 18.04, which I am currently on:
```
cavsfan@bionic:~$ apt-cache policy catfish
catfish:
  Installed: 1.4.4-1
  Candidate: 1.4.4-1
  Version table:
 *** 1.4.4-1 500
        500 http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu bionic/universe amd64 Packages
        500 http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu bionic/universe i386 Packages
        100 /var/lib/dpkg/status

```

But, I also have Arch Linux  and Manjaro is[COLOR=#000000] in the Arch Linux family so to speak [/COLOR][COLOR=#000000]and it can be installed like this:
```
sudo pacman -S catfish
```
There's a way you can configure Thunar on Arch to have the same capability in Xfce under File. It's been so long since I've used anything besides Xfce that I do not remember if they have Catfish as well or not.

If you're using Compiz on [/COLOR][COLOR=#000000]Manjaro, which I don't know why anyone wouldn't, a lot of us set up commands in the Command Plugin of CCSM to restart our conkys:

The top one is set to use **Cntl+Shift+F12** and executes this: [/COLOR][B]killall conky && sleep 5 && /home/cavsfan/.config/conkywx/examples190121/conky-restart.sh

[/B][COLOR=#000000]Param has been working on the problem you have brought up and I need to help test it if it's not already done by the other testers.
I have Arch Linux, openSUSE Tumbleweed, Fedora 29 and Xubuntu 18.04 LTS on this machine.

I installed in on Arch yesterday but, got sidetracked and didn't finish.
I'm installing it on Xubuntu right now.

[/COLOR]

### Post by paramvir on 2019-02-23
OK here is a game changer - conky is also now available as a stand alone Linux program. This means we do not have to wait catch up for the next release.

Will be working to get conkywx compatible with this version as well.

[https://github.com/brndnmtthws/conky/releases/download/v1.11.3/conky-x86_64.AppImage](https://github.com/brndnmtthws/conky/releases/download/v1.11.3/conky-x86_64.AppImage)

To test you will need to make the program executable and the ```
./conky-x86_64.AppImage --help
``` - this will give you all the conky command line options.

In simple words you use this as you used conky the installed version - but use the app name with path where you have placed this file.

example - ```
~/mybin/conky-x86_64.AppImage -c your config
```

cheers:guitar:

### Post by VinDSL on 2019-03-15
> **paramvir said:**
> Wow that is awesome - been firefighting [...] 

It sent me scrambling, too, when Intellicast | Weather Underground turned off their formerly free forecasting API.

After trying this n' that, I finally ended up salvaging ConkyWX 5.0.0-1 by scraping the weather forecasts from MSN:

```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main weather data source - building redundancy
## For current conditions use ::
#  Wunderground [w], METAR [m]
## For forecast and/or current conditions use ::
#   Intellicast [1] or Weather [2] or MSN [3]
## Use of XML data remains unchanged. See Note below at xml_ID
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
default_wx=3
```

Wunderground is still working fine for everything else,  except the forecasts.

Sometimes less is more, you know? :)

### Post by damnitdan on 2019-04-03
> **freebird54 said:**
> As soon as I figure out where that is set, I'll try that. The current setup really *IS* a mess! Conkywx appears to be working (without success) as it notifies as updating regularly - but I haven't even found where it's killing the conky I had running (or why). Somehow, I'm just not as quick as I once was :)

Thanks for the suggestions though, I'll need all the help I can get as I go along...

freebird

Set it in your conkywx-02.conf. (This file is referenced in the all conky instances that you see in the restart-conky.sh file.)
> grep "examples/conkywx-02.conf" /usr/share/conkywx/examples/*
Just edit /usr/share/conkywx/examples/conkywx-02.conf and set weather data source(s). Personally I use "default_wx=3:2"  which pulls the forecast from MSN & the current conditions from Weather.com
> 
## main weather data source - building redundancy
## use colon : character to separate current condition and forecast
## for current conditions use ::
##     METAR [m]
## for forecast and/or current conditions use ::
##     intellicast [1] or weather [2] or msn [3] or accuweather [a]
##     dark sky    [d] or wunderground [w]
## use of xml data remains unchanged See Note below at xml_ID
## w, w:1, w:2, w:3, w:a, w:d
## m:1, m:2, m:3, m:a, m:d
...
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------
default_wx=3:2
As well (in this same file), you'll need to set your location for whichever weather data souce you've picked. Ignore anything to do with intellicast (as it's gone).

What do you mean when you say, "- but I haven't even found where it's killing **the conky I had running** (or why)" ? What conky you had running? When conkywx is launched, it will killall conky. 

FWIW...
My display was blank until I removed/commented references to nVidia (as I have no nVidia). 
If you change stuff in /usr/share/conkywx/* and restart conkywx they may not appear/take effect unless you rm the local copy:
> rm -rf ~/.config/conkywx

cheers...
Here's my lastest conkywx tweak: big-luamix-gauge-conkyrc & vindsl-org-conkyrc
[https://scrot.moe/image/aBvUp]("https://scrot.moe/image/aBvUp")
Go SENS go! LOL

### Post by NM5TF on 2019-04-03
assuming you are working with version 181207...or whatever version you have.....I am using 190312 which has not been released yet...

look at ~/.config/conkywx/examples181207/conkywx-02.conf [COLOR="#FF0000"]or it might be in /usr/share/conkywx/examples181207/conkywx-02.conf
[/COLOR]
here is where you can choose what WX source you want....this file also determines your location, LAT/LONG, elevation, etc....

```
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## Conkywx Config File | Paramvir | rev. 12 Mar 2019 | ver. 190312_build_190312
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## THIS PROGRAM IS FOR PERSONAL AND PRIVATE USE ONLY
## CONTACT WUNDERGROUND.COM FOR PROFESSIONAL SOLUTIONS
## CAVEAT: No guarantee that this program will work on all platforms,
## Use At Your Own Risk.
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------

## main weather data source - building redundancy
## use colon : character to separate current condition and forecast
## for current conditions use ::
##     METAR [m]
## for forecast and/or current conditions use ::
##     aerisweather [1] or weather [2] or msn [3] or accuweather [a]
##     dark sky    [d] or wunderground [w]
## use of xml data remains unchanged See Note below at xml_ID
## w, w:2, w:3, w:a, w:d
## m:2, m:3, m:a, m:d
## 2,   2:1, 2:3, 2:a, 2:d
## 3,   3:1, 3:2, 3:a, 3:d
## a,   a:1, a:2, a:3, a:d
## d,   d:1, d:2, d:3, d:a
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------
[COLOR="#FF0000"]default_wx=w[/COLOR]

```

as you can see, I have wunderground selected....which is working fine today....

tommy

### Post by NM5TF on 2019-04-04
conkywx ships with WX from Florida....CavsFan home....don't know why Paramvir picked that tho...

look in the conkywx-02.conf file...line by line enter YOUR info so it will find your location...this is the most important section...

here is MY position info...your should be similar

```
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## User position and Elevation in Feet
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Enter Latitude, Longitude in decimal format
## standard convention South and West co-ordinates are minus
##
## Your location is used to locate a pws nearest to you
##
## Comment the position if you want to use weather site data co-ordinates
##
## To get more accurate information for your precise location the coordinates
## can be picked by using Google
## or http://www.daftlogic.com/sandbox-google-maps-find-altitude.htm
##
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
Latitude=34.06782792975
Longitude=-107.23579801068

elevation=7200
```

I am a little confused tho...I believe you said you downloaded the latest version...which would be 181207 and NOT version 7

here is latest released version  [http://foreverquest.blogspot.com/](http://foreverquest.blogspot.com/)

### Post by NM5TF on 2019-04-05
OK...here we go....

the file that tells which conkys to run is located here  [COLOR="#FF0000"]~/.congfig/conkywx/examples181207/conky-restart.sh[/COLOR]

should look similar to mine...this is just a partial view...

```
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# run conky instances
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/conky-musicrc" &

    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/vertical-scroller-gdacs-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/vertical-scroller-news-conkyrc" &

    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/conkywx-clockrc" &

    conky -c "$WX_DIR/conkywx-weather-rings-systemrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/conky-systemrc" &

    conky -c "$WX_DIR/conkywx-surface-imagerc" &
    conky -c "$WX_DIR/seismicrc" &

    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/vertical-scroller-seismic-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/vertical-scroller-weather-conkyrc" &


    conky -c "$WX_DIR/big-luamix-gauge-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/big-luamix-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/flip-big-luamix-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/flip-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/expanded-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "/home/tommy/.config/conkywx/examples190222/short-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/hartley-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/now-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/slim-conkyrc" &

    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/vertical-scroller-vindsl-weather-conkyrc" &
    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/horizontal-scroller-weather-conkyrc" &

    # conky -c "$WX_DIR/vindsl-org-conkyrc" &


exit 0
```

just unselect the # in front of whatever display you want.....in your case I believe you said the original vindsl...[COLOR="#FF0000"]at the very bottom it resides.[/COLOR]..

and yes, Paramvir makes extensive use of the lua language...it's how he makes all those instruments, graphs, & other eye-kandy.....

but you mostly don't have to deal with that because the .lua resides in the templates he uses.....[COLOR="#FF0000"]each xxxxrc file references a specific template[/COLOR]

I don't know what text editor you use, but we all use either VisualStudioCode which is free or SublimeText3.0 which is not free, but has a free evaluation mode...you WILL have
to edit these files to replace paramvirs username with yours....most likely why some are not working for you....

also there is always RTFM :D:D:D

open a terminal & type conkywx....select option 2 which will open the help in your browser....

```
[tommy@tommy ~]$ conkywx


			Conkywx Assistant
	-----------------------------------------------------
	Current version of conkywx on your system is: 190312
	-----------------------------------------------------
	Choice :  Option
	-----------------------------------------------------
	   1   :  Quick HELP with conkywx weather program
	   2   :  Refer to offline conkywx WIKI Manual in your browser
	   3   :  FORCE update conkywx data
	   4   :  Get detailed DEBUG information for conkywx
	   5   :  Reset Forecast trend information
	   6   :  Reset graph information
	   7   :  List Template Labels 
	-----------------------------------------------------
	  [[ q to Quit ]]
	  Enter your choice followed by [ENTER]: 



```

maybe best if you just nuke all your old conkywx's....[COLOR="#FF0000"]after saving your favorites from the old versions of course.[/COLOR]...and do a clean install....your choice of course..:P:P

oh yeah...I have been playing with conkywx since version 2 or 3...it's addictive....but frustrating at times also...:(:(:(

have fun & remember...we are here....

tommy

### Post by NM5TF on 2019-04-07
the wunderground issues have nothing to do with geographical location...2 of our testers are in Australia, and Paramvir is in New Delhi, India :D:D

IBM owns wunderground, and also has merged intellicast into wunderground lately....they are in the process of "improving" some features....;);)
have you gone to the wunderground page for Toronto yet ??....does it display correct information ??? if so, it should work with conkywx...

as to your question regarding WX sources.....it depends upon what YOU have specified in your  [COLOR="#FF0000"]~/.config/conkywx/examples181207/conkywx-02.conf[/COLOR] file

yours should look similar to mine...

```
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## Conkywx Config File | Paramvir | rev. 12 Mar 2019 | ver. 190312_build_190312
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## THIS PROGRAM IS FOR PERSONAL AND PRIVATE USE ONLY
## CONTACT WUNDERGROUND.COM FOR PROFESSIONAL SOLUTIONS
## CAVEAT: No guarantee that this program will work on all platforms,
## Use At Your Own Risk.
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------

## main weather data source - building redundancy
## use colon : character to separate current condition and forecast
## for current conditions use ::
##     METAR [m]
## for forecast and/or current conditions use ::
##     aerisweather [1] or weather [2] or msn [3] or accuweather [a]
##     dark sky    [d] or wunderground [w]
## use of xml data remains unchanged See Note below at xml_ID
## w, w:2, w:3, w:a, w:d
## m:2, m:3, m:a, m:d
## 2,   2:1, 2:3, 2:a, 2:d
## 3,   3:1, 3:2, 3:a, 3:d
## a,   a:1, a:2, a:3, a:d
## d,   d:1, d:2, d:3, d:a
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------
default_wx=w
```

in my case I have just [COLOR="#FF0000"]w[/COLOR] selected...I get both forecast & current data from wunderground....another section of the [COLOR="#FF0000"]conkywx-02.conf[/COLOR]
file further divides this into forecast data from wunderground based on my location information, and the current WX data from my neighbor's PWS ( Personal
Weather Station ) via wunderground that is selected via an xml_ID in the [COLOR="#FF0000"]conkywx-02.conf[/COLOR] file

do you have[COLOR="#FF0000"] conky-all[/COLOR] installed ?? if not, you might considering installing it....

have you edited [COLOR="#FF0000"]conky-02.conf[/COLOR] file so all variables pertain to your location....I believe it ships with Paramvir's location data if memory serves...lol

as you can see, the [COLOR="#FF0000"]conkywx-02.conf[/COLOR] file is the heart of conkywx....:lolflag:

tommy

I put my fonts in my home directory. I create a directory .fonts and copy the fonts from another partition into that folder. I keep a copy of those fonts and some other fonts like Buuf in a partition called Media.
That way I only download them once. Took a while to get through my dense skull to do that instead of having to find them on the internet and download them every time I need another copy. #-o

[ATTACH=CONFIG]282994[/ATTACH]

I'm currently on Fedora 29, which has no Nvidia support in their conky nor compositing, or something is wrong with the compositing. I can't use override, etc.

#3....in the [COLOR="#FF0000"]short-conkyrc[/COLOR] file, have you tried changing the default position from [COLOR="#00FF00"]alignment_ = bottom_left[/COLOR] to[COLOR="#FF0000"] alignment = bottom_middle[/COLOR] ???
to further adjust the position of the window, [COLOR="#FF0000"]not the text[/COLOR], use [COLOR="#FF0000"]gap x & y.[/COLOR]....gap_x= a + number will move to the right x number of pixels....a - number will move to the left x number of pixels....same for gap_y...+ moves up & - moves down....

#4... the musicrc file is a little trickier to align.....[COLOR="#FF0000"]there are 2 parts to position....the music info & the scroller info.[/COLOR]....same alignment procedure as above.....it can be done see here [https://imgur.com/a/IKRGR5V](https://imgur.com/a/IKRGR5V)

#5....yeah, we know....sometimes it will display album info, but usually it's like you describe...don't know why, but it's not a design fault I believe....

tommy

### Post by freebird54 on 2019-05-03
Hmm - sudden silence around here - is there a holiday somewhere? :)

Anyway - I am undeterred - and will post some progress notes in case of interest...

I have (I think) improved the appearance and functionality of my conky setup - though it is far from finished. My sizing issues have been dealt with, and a couple of minor changes/additions have been set in. I've been lucky enough to get a partial update of conkywx, so the scrolling complaints and weather parsing errors have gone! I am still trying to learn enough lua to fix up the audacious scroller, but in the meantime I simply work around it - with what I CAN handle (bash script). This just (statically) posts the song, artist, album, year, track number, total time and elapsed time in a block on my side conky. Not exactly elegant, but it works for me! Here it is, if anyone wants it for temporary use until final resolution is upon us.

```
#!/bin/bash

# variable declarations and settings
check=`audtool --current-song`

playback_status=`audtool --playback-status`

aud="Audacious is now"
status=`audtool --playback-status`
title=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data title`
artist=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data artist`
album=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data album`
year=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data year`
track_no=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data track-number`
track_length=`audtool --current-song-length`
track_play='    <->        '
old_title=' '    # to check for song change
# conditional statements start

if [ "${check}" == "No song playing." ]; # Check if audacious is running, skip out if not
then
    echo "Audacious is not running"
    rm /tmp/trackPlaying 2> /dev/null # clean up
    exit 1
fi

case ${playback_status} in
    "paused")
        echo ${aud} ${status}
        echo " "
        #  Switch the comment block to shorter output
        #  in both locations (paused and playing) if
        #  your output area is narrow or your font big
        #  echo  "   Title: " ${title}
        # echo  "  Artist: " ${artist}
        # echo  "   Album: " ${album}
        # echo  "    Year: " ${year}
        # echo  "   Track: " ${track_no}
        # echo  "  Length: " ${track_length}
        # echo  "Position:  "`audtool current-song-output-length`
        echo  ${title}
        echo  ${artist}
        echo  ${album}
        echo  ${year}
        echo  ${track_no}
        echo  ${track_length}
        echo  `audtool current-song-output-length`
        ;;
    "stopped")
        echo ${aud} ${status}
        if [[ -e '/tmp/trackPlaying' ]];   #if scroller file exists
            then
            rm /tmp/trackPlaying # dump it ( it gets rebuilt on restart )
        fi
        ;;
    "playing")
        if  [ "${title}" != "${old_title}" ];   # song change, kill /tmp/trackPlaying
            then
            rm /tmp/trackPlaying 2> /dev/null   # ensure new one can get created
        fi
        echo ${aud} ${status}
        echo " "
        # echo  "   Title: " ${title}
        # echo  "  Artist: " ${artist}
        # echo  "   Album: " ${album}
        # echo  "    Year: " ${year}
        # echo  "   Track: " ${track_no}
        # echo  "  Length: " ${track_length}
        # echo  "Position:  "`audtool current-song-output-length`
        echo  ${title}
        echo  ${artist}
        echo  ${album}
        echo  ${year}
        echo  ${track_no}
        echo  ${track_length}
        echo  `audtool current-song-output-length`
        old_title=title        #to keep /tmp/trackPlaying alive
        if [[ ! -e '/tmp/trackPlaying' ]];   #if scroller file not yet written
            then
            # uncomment your preferred choice for scrolling
            # echo > /tmp/trackPlaying ${track_play}${title}"  -  "${artist}"  -  "${album}"  -  "${year}
            echo > /tmp/trackPlaying ${track_play}"Title: "${title}"  -  Artist: "${artist}"  -  Album: "${album}"  -  Year: "${year}
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        ;;
esac
 
```
and just called with:
```
${if_match "${lua if_running audacious}" == "1"}${voffset 10}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious Running${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}
${voffset 10}${font DroidSansMono:size=9}${execi 1 ~/conky_aud.sh}
${else}#
${voffset 8}${font :bold:size=8}${color4}No Media Playing ${color1}${hr 1}${font}#
${image ${template5} -p 55,865 }#
${endif}#

```

As you may have noticed, it also created a file **/tmp/trackPlaying** so perhaps I can get a scroller call going without having to go through music_sort.lua

I'll throw up a few screenies so you can see how its going - and hi to all you silent people!

keep smiling

freebird

Edit: wrong pic, and a note: No more Leafs in the playoffs, so back to normal avatar... :(
freebird

### Post by freebird54 on 2019-05-08
Thanks, I guess. :redface: It is just a little step on the way to what I really want it to do, and to verify that audacious can do what it's supposed to! Actually, I have goone a little further on it, and added in a scroller call for title, artist and album. I see that you have the lua calendar from param, so you probably could do that too, if you want. Here's the revised **conky_aud2.sh**:

```
#!/bin/bash

# variable declarations and settings
check=`audtool --current-song`

playback_status=`audtool --playback-status`

aud="Audacious is now"
status=`audtool --playback-status`
title=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data title`
artist=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data artist`
album=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data album`
year=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data year`
track_no=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data track-number`
track_length=`audtool --current-song-length`
track_play=' '
old_title=' '    # to check for song change
# conditional statements start

if [ "${check}" == "No song playing." ]; # Check if audacious is running, skip out if not
then
    # echo "Audacious is not running"
    rm /tmp/trackPlaying 2> /dev/null # clean up
    exit 1
fi

case ${playback_status} in
    "paused")
        # echo ${aud} ${status}
        # echo " "
        #  Switch the comment block to shorter output
        #  in both locations (paused and playing) if
        #  your output area is narrow or your font big
        # echo  "   Title: " ${title}
        # echo  "  Artist: " ${artist}
        # echo  "   Album: " ${album}
        echo  "    Year: " ${year}
        echo  "   Track: " ${track_no}
        echo " "
        echo  "  Length: " ${track_length}
        echo  "Position:  "`audtool current-song-output-length`
        # echo  ${title}
        # echo  ${artist}
        # echo  ${album}
        # echo  ${year}
        # echo  ${track_no}
        # echo  ${track_length}
        # echo  `audtool current-song-output-length`
        echo " "
        echo "Audacious is paused"
        ;;
    "stopped")
        echo ${aud} ${status}
        if [[ -e '/tmp/trackPlaying' ]];   #if scroller file exists
            then
            rm /tmp/trackPlaying # dump it ( it gets rebuilt on restart )
        fi
        ;;
    "playing")
        if  [ "${title}" != "${old_title}" ];   # song change, kill /tmp/trackPlaying
            then
            rm /tmp/trackPlaying 2> /dev/null   # ensure new one can get created
        fi
        # echo ${aud} ${status}
        # echo " "
        # echo  "   Title: " ${title}
        # echo  "  Artist: " ${artist}
        # echo  "   Album: " ${album}
        echo  "    Year: " ${year}
        echo  "   Track: " ${track_no}
        echo " "
        echo  "  Length: " ${track_length}
        echo  "Position:  "`audtool current-song-output-length`

        # echo  ${title}
        # echo  ${artist}
        # echo  ${album}
        # echo  ${year}
        # echo  ${track_no}
        # echo  ${track_length}
        # echo  `audtool current-song-output-length`
        old_title=title        #to keep /tmp/trackPlaying alive
        if [[ ! -e '/tmp/trackPlaying' ]];   #if scroller file not yet written
            then
            # uncomment your preferred choice for scrolling
            # echo > /tmp/trackPlaying ${track_play}${title}"  -  "${artist}"  -  "${album}"  -  "${year}
            echo > /tmp/trackPlaying ${track_play}"    Title: "${title}"   *  Artist: "${artist}"   *   Album: "${album}"     "
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        ;;
esac

```

and I have it called like this:

```
# ##################################
# ##     Audacious                ##
# ##################################
${if_match "${lua if_running audacious}" == "1"}${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious Running${offset}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}
${voffset 50}${font DroidSansMono:size=11}${execi 1 ~/conky_aud2.sh}
${lua main -n scroller -f 12 -l 50 -s 1 -p 2,885 -o ${template8}}#
${else}#
${voffset 8}${font :bold:size=8}${color4}No Media Playing ${color1}${hr 1}${font}#
${image ${template5} -p 55,885 }#
${endif}#

```
Obviously positioning will differ, and 	**template8 = '/tmp/trackPlaying',** will need to be inserted in your conkyrc with the other templates as appropriate. The scrolling is not the smooth scrolling, but it does solve the problem of LONG titles and album names :)
I'll throw a partial screenie up for you with the revisions going..

Enjoy! (or not - as you choose) :)

freebird

### Post by freebird54 on 2019-05-09
I made an oops in the script I posted earlier - the scroller wants the file to be there when it gets called! It is a small change though - so here it is:
```
    "stopped")
        echo ${aud} ${status}
        if [[ -e '/tmp/trackPlaying' ]];   #if scroller file exists
            then
            [COLOR="#008000"]echo >/tmp/trackPlaying " " # clear file to avoid error[/COLOR]
            [COLOR="#FF0000"]# rm /tmp/trackPlaying # dump it ( it gets rebuilt on restart )[/COLOR]
        fi
        ;;

```
The line in red needs to be commented out (as shown) and replaced with the line in green. :redface:

enjoy

freebird

### Post by freebird54 on 2019-05-20
Gee, I feel that have become the default owner of an entire thread by default and abandonment :)  Here goes with an update anyway...

I have achieved functionality! My 2 conky's now show everything I need them to, in a manner that keeps them out of my way - we;ll at least leaves enough room for any 1 program I run. I do miss the spinning cube from Beryl/compiz in the old days - or even compiz from Unity (which was the best workflow I ever found). Anyway - I decided to go with the 'mainstream' now that we've (Ubuntu) gone Gnome so as to get used to it...

Well - the updates. I have NO errors spewing into the terminal after starting/running conky's. I have a reasonably good-looking setup (well it makes an old-school sense to me :) ). I improved the bottom weather setup with a  barometer, and preferred icons. My side panel clock feature now looks sleeker and clearer - and even gives a penguin a place to sit. By means of wasteful effort I have gotten Audacious to give me wjat I want - with scrolling title/artist/album - Year from the tags in file - track number, length and updating position in the song while playing. I have also gotten the album art to display, along with a progress bar - and the whole lot disappears under a restful picture when the music player isn't playing. There is more that I considered - and a conky is NEVER really done - but it isn't urgent to solve and add on now. I even got to remind myself of a bit of bash scripting again. I still maintain I was best at 68000 assembly, C, and AmigaDOS scripting - but I got the job done anyway :)

I'll add in a couple of details and mini-screenies, and a big one from imgur as well for your delectation...

[IMG]  [https://imgur.com/9J4d0ei](https://imgur.com/9J4d0ei)  [/IMG]

[IMG][https://imgur.com/EC4gZ0o](https://imgur.com/EC4gZ0o)  [/IMG]

AS you can probably guess, my next main project will be to clean up my music collection a bit (make sure of album art, fill some holes, dump some uninteresting dupes - try to integrate some mp3's with my ogg's etc. It is amazing how good tools for helping with that are getting (kid3-qt). - that's all folks for now :)

keep smiling

freebird

PS: -f any one wants code fragments for anything here, just ask. You are allowed to use MY thread! hee hee

fb

Looking good there freebird54. Glad to see that you have what you want as everything Linux is is pretty much customizable to suit your needs.

I've got a ton of Linux systems on my 1TB SSD and there still are systems out there supporting and maintaining compiz, emerald, even the Fusion Icon.

I've been trying to stick with those. Although I'll always have a working Xubuntu system: now I have Xubuntu Bionic Beaver 18.04.2 and have all of the above just no Fusion Icon or fish tank (experimental plugins).
Arch Linux, OpenSUSE Tumbleweed, Fedora 30, MX Linux 18.2 all have working compiz, emerald, and Fusion Icon. Even Debian Buster has compiz-reloaded which has all the good stuff.
I'd bet that could be installed on Ubuntu, although I haven't checked.

I reinstalled openSUSE TW yesterday and got everything installed almost. I still need to install Conkywx. But, here's a screenie of the cube with the fish tank.
I always change how many fish, etc. are displayed. I edit it and put a 1 beside the number that is there, like if it's 5, I make it 15, 8 I make it 18 and have 4 air thingies, the default is one.

You know because more fish need more air. :P

[[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/Br00uwrl.png[/IMG]]("https://imgur.com/Br00uwr")

Here's Arch Linux with 7 conkys

[[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/lqrDCFql.jpg[/IMG]]("https://imgur.com/lqrDCFq")

The one on the right where VinDSL's conky goes can have one line commented out and it does not display weather info. just system info.

OpenSUSE is keeping up with Arch Linux fairly closely, at least as far as the kernel goes:
```
cavsfan@openSUSE:~> uname -r
5.1.2-1-default
```

I quit getting notifications for posts on threads that come after mine for some reason. Sometimes I get one and most of the time nothing.

Compiz is not really back on Ubuntu. The last version that supported compiz-reloaded was 16.04 and it would be really hard to get it on 18.04 or 19.04.

I had to get Emerald window decorator from 2 old Trusty 14.04 deb files via this thread I posted in January 2018. [https://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2381499](https://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2381499)

You might look at MX Linux 18.2, it has all of the old compiz stuff, Emerald and the Fusion Icon. It's Debian based so, it's not unlike Ubuntu.

Surprisingly, Debian Buster also has all of the above too, which is what will be Debian 10.

### Post by freebird54 on 2019-05-27
As I said - I need to try out some alternatives again - but I'm not sure I'm ready for Arch! I'm having enough trouble trying to learn Gnome ways without having to keep up on ALL of it :)

Anyway - I thought I would post some tidy ups managed since the last update - maybe even my little bash script to make it all work!
Here's the pix:

[https://imgur.com/rRBITRj](https://imgur.com/rRBITRj)  =  Playing

[https://imgur.com/bLHmHnL](https://imgur.com/bLHmHnL)  =  Paused

[https://imgur.com/0Kdy7lw](https://imgur.com/0Kdy7lw)  =  Stopped

[https://imgur.com/5MfXKTI](https://imgur.com/5MfXKTI)  =  Not Running

[https://imgur.com/2eioZod](https://imgur.com/2eioZod)  =  Overview

the bash script:
```

#!/bin/bash

# This script assumes the following:
# the directory for each song contains folder.jpg containing album art
# and your home dir has a directory Work with a substitute image for
# when folder.jpg is not found.
# It also assumes that imagemagick is installed
# (source of the convert command to resize and change to cover.png for display)
# This script is called conky_aud3.sh in my conky script
################################################################################
# variable declarations and settings
check=`audtool --current-song`
playback_status=`audtool --playback-status`
aud="Audacious is now"
status=`audtool --playback-status`
title=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data title`
artist=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data artist`
album=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data album`
year=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data year`
track_no=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data track-number`
track_length=`audtool --current-song-length`
track_position=`audtool --current-song-output-length`
file_path=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data file-path`

# functions for later for clarity and easier scripting
ShowInfo ()
    {
    # If the scroller is not used, uncomment the title/artist/album lines that suit your use
    # Switch the comment block to shorter output
    # works in both locations (paused and playing)
    # if your output area is narrow or your font big
    # echo  "   Title: " ${title}
    # echo  "  Artist: " ${artist}
    # echo  "   Album: " ${album}
    echo  "    Year: " ${year}
    echo  "   Track: " ${track_no}
    echo " "
    echo  "  Length: " ${track_length}
    echo  "Position: " ${track_position}                    # Updates with frequency you set with execi
    # echo  ${title}
    # echo  ${artist}
    # echo  ${album}
    # echo  ${year}
    # echo  ${track_no}
    # echo  ${track_length}
    # echo  `audtool current-song-output-length`
    }

GetArt ()
    {
    file_path=`audtool --current-song-tuple-data file-path` # get the path to the song
    file_path=$(eval echo "${file_path}")                   # pre-expand to full path
    cd "${file_path}"
    if [[ ! -e "folder.jpg" ]];                             # if no art work found
        then
        cp ~/Work/headphone.png /tmp/cover.png              # put in placeholder
    else
        convert "${file_path}""/folder.jpg" -resize 120x120 /tmp/cover.png # ready for showing
    fi
    }

    # conditional statements start

if [ "${check}" == "No song playing." ];                    # Check if audacious is running
then
    rm /tmp/trackPlaying 2> /dev/null                       # clean up
    exit 1                                                  # done this pass
fi

case ${playback_status} in
    "paused")
        echo " "
        echo "    "${status}                                # report status
        echo " "
        ShowInfo                                            # Display song info
        ;;
    "stopped")
        echo " "
        echo ${aud} ${status}                               # report status
        if [[ -e '/tmp/trackPlaying' ]];                    # if scroller file exists
            then
            echo >/tmp/trackPlaying " "                     # clear file to avoid error and stop scroll
            cp ~/Work/blank.png /tmp/cover.png              # blank out cover art
        fi
        ;;
    "playing")
        if  [  "${track_position}" == "0:00" ];            # song beginning
            then
            GetArt                                         # Retrieve and display cover art
            echo > /tmp/trackPlaying ${track_play}"  Title: "${title}"   *  Artist: "${artist}"   *   Album: "${album}"  "
        fi
        echo " "
        echo " "
        ShowInfo                                           # Display song info
        ;;
    *)
        ;;
esac


```

and the "Audacious" section of the main conky:

```

# ##################################
# ##     Audacious                ##
# ##################################
${if_match "${lua if_running audacious}" == "1"}${voffset 5}${font DroidSans:bold:size=8}${color4}Audacious Running${offset 8}${color6}${voffset -2}${hr 1}#
${voffset 150}${if_match "${audacious_status}" != "Stopped"}${goto 8}${color3}${audacious_bar 6,277}${font}${endif}#
${voffset -150}${font DroidSansMono:size=10}${color2}${execi 2 ~/conky_aud3.sh}#
${image /tmp/cover.png -p 160,855}#
${else}#
${voffset 8}${font :bold:size=8}${color4}No Media Playing ${color1}${hr 1}${font}#
${image ${template5} -p 55,855 }#
${endif}#


```

Well - there you go. I guess I'll drop by the Cafe as well with this - should be able to leave it alone for while now (a day or two?)

freebird

### Post by Cavsfan on 2019-06-06
> **freebird54 said:**
> As for Arch - I would consider running one - but I suspect I would need a CloneZilla image to start with rather than building from scratch! I also already know how to spell RTFM - one reason I am here rather than staying with Debian back in the day... These forums may or may not have the most knowledge, but usually you can get help appropriate to the problem! I am respectful of anyone who navigates Arch successfully though...

Arch is not impossible to install, although it seems that way at first. 
My first install went like this: I tried for about 3-4 days and gave up. Let a few days pass, then I tried it again and got it right. This was on my old 10 year old computer.

When I got this newer computer, it had a 480GB OCZ VERTEX460 SSD that was formatted Legacy/MBR thus limited to 4 partitions. So I formatted it UEFI/GPT and installed Windows 10 first. BTW I think GPT can have up to 1000 partitions.

Then I installed Arch Linux in EFI mode. It took me a little while but, wasn't too bad. But, I ended up installing it a couple of more times until I got it just like I wanted it. Plus that helped me learn the process better.
There is quite a bit of action between you typing stuff into a blank screen but, if you have a smart phone there is a Arch Linux Wiki app you can install. Or just print out the instructions up to and after when you chroot into the system and finalize the installation.
I think I did that and just checked things off one at a time.

I'm just saying don't let yourself believe that you cannot do it. That which is worth it just takes a little longer and Arch Linux is worth it. Just ask Param.

Here's my openSUSE TW 190530 version of Conkywx, almost perfect but, not quite. The 5.1.7-1-default kernel was just installed but, I haven't rebooted yet.

[[IMG]http://i.imgur.com/NtI2Wijl.png[/IMG]]("https://imgur.com/NtI2Wij")

### Post by freebird54 on 2019-06-07
Don't worry - I am not offended by the suggestion :) I am just in doubt as to the advantages of the system for my current useage patterns. I have just been lurking on the newbies boards in the Arch forums to see what goes wrong, and how the help is for when it does. I think, for the same reasons I am now on the LTS release of Ubuntu that I would go nuts with tracking down the things that break with new kernels etc - and I feel too old to learn new tricks! As for the install, I doubt that would give me any trouble - no matter how long since I last had to look at fdisk. Arch HAS to be much easier than TAMU was to install - for one thing there aren't 20-odd floppies to feed in, for another I wouldn't have to specify vblank intervals and frequencies to get my monitor as far as VESA specs, and I wouldn't have to spec up a Motif instance from scratch either! For that matter, I chose TAMU (Texas A&M Uni) because it was reputed to work with ATI cards - and as a good Canadian of course that was what I had in my hi-horsepower 386 tower....

As for layout of disks - I currently run with / and /home separately, then soft links for Music, Downloads, Videos etc to data drives (3tb and 2tb) accessible from multiple distros. I didn't split boot off this time, as it seems unnecessary now, but this setup would work well with Arch I am sure.

BTW - I really like the way that the bezels look on your newest setup - they give almost a 3D effect to things that seems to increase their 'authority' in some way. Good job with that!

BCNU

freebird

Thanks Freebird! I thought the rings looked the best of the 3. I had to make those gauges smaller to allow for the silver rings that Param came up with. If I wanted to also make it slanted plus the rings, I would have had to make them smaller yet. 
As far as Arch goes, it is the most documented distro there is. It is surprisingly stable too. I think I've been using it for about 4-5 years, mind has gotten foggy, and it has amazed me with the it's resilience. I have never encountered anything that I couldn't recover from without much difficulty.

Arch keeps older packages on your system for a period of time and if something goes haywire you can rollback a single package or even every package by rolling back a day or 2, or whatever date you want as long as it's not too long ago.
Like the xfdesktop package, it should be version 4.12 something but, they allowed it to go to 4.13, [[COLOR=blue]this post[/COLOR]]("https://bbs.archlinux.org/viewtopic.php?id=244801") explains it. We've all got it on hold.
But, 4.13 was a developmental release and was not supposed to hit the mainstream until it hit 4.14. I know 4.13 hit Fedora 29,30 and that is why I cannot use **own_window_type = 'override', **in it. I have to use this in every conky:
```
    own_window = true,
    own_window_type = 'normal',
    own_window_transparent = false,
    own_window_hints = 'undecorated,sticky,below,skip_taskbar,skip_pager',
    own_window_argb_visual = true,
    own_window_argb_value = 0,

```
While Arch Linux is the most bleeding edge distro, it is unbelievably stable too. 

So, one day when you've got nothing better to do, you may give it a try.

I just tried to install the RC for MX Linux 19 and mucked that up. It won't even boot so I could re-install it but, I've already got 18.2 working nicely and it will turn into 19 eventually. So, I'm not going to mess with it.

I for sure need to stay off my computer when I am very sleepy like I have been lately. No installing systems, no posting to forums...
